Agatha stepped back with a dangerous smirk on her perfect, dark-red lips. 

Dinosaur nerds are not supposed to be this fucking sexy. Rio’s brain stuttered.

“I’ll see you this evening at the panel, baby girl.” Agatha purred with a wink before gracefully turning on a heel and walking away. “I didn’t know anyone else knew how to curse in Old Norse,” she teased as she flipped her hair and opened the door.

“I hate you,” Rio called after her, face red and flustered. All she heard in reply was a melodic cackle.

Rio dug her nails into the palms of her hands, trying to calm herself. She was a cosmic entity. She could fling Agatha across the world if she wanted to, turn her into a prehistoric mosquito. The way Agatha had gotten under her skin in less than eighteen hours was infuriating, yet she still felt drawn to her. The comet hurtling toward Earth. 

She just didn’t know who was going to be the destroyer, but everything about this felt doomed.

And Rio Vidal, Death herself, loved it.  

🦕🦖🦕

Ellie Sattler leaned against a wall in the conference and convention part of the hotel, waiting for Agatha. She wasn’t necessarily a jealous ex, but she didn’t want to see Agatha parading around with anyone else, either. The current situation was bad enough, and the way things had been left wasn’t ideal.

Agatha fucking someone was one thing. Her being in love? That was crossing a line.

So, Ellie kept drawing Agatha back into the darker side of paleontology and archeology. 

Her Aggie was charismatic, manipulative, and only cared about a handful of people. If anyone could get information, specific artifacts, or steal research, it was Agatha. Especially if the price was right, and this time, Ellie knew exactly what to give her. It was risky, selfish, and absolutely horrible of her to dangle it in front of her the way she had, but Ellie had done it anyway. 

Now, there was the Rio of it all. The blonde scientist knew Agatha hated the woman from afar, and she expected Agatha to use her charms, but watching the two of them all day, she hadn’t expected this hate-lust thing they had going on.

So maybe she was jealous.

“Don’t you have an alley to skulk about?” Agatha’s voice sliced through her thoughts as she met the woman’s icy blue glare.

“Good to see you, too.”

Agatha scoffed and leaned against the wall next to her. The slightest hint of a smile ghosted at the corner of her mouth.

Ellie glanced around and made sure no one paid attention to them. “Did you get it?”

Agatha nodded and slipped her the flash drive she had swapped out of Rio’s computer bag. Ellie pocketed it and handed Agatha a thick manila envelope from her bag. 

“Everything you need and requested is in here.”

Agatha quickly glanced at Ellie before turning back to face forward. “What’s your plan?”

“Ian and I want to use her research for something remarkable. I’ll show you once it’s ready. Thank you, Aggie.”

Agatha clenched her jaw at the name. “Don’t call me that in public. I’ll see you around, Dr. Sattler.”

🦕🦖🦕

Rio was sick. Rio was never sick. It was supposed to be biologically impossible for her to become ill, yet here she was, shivering, coughing, vomiting, and with a fever, even. 

Had she lived as a human for so long that she was now susceptible to certain human conditions? She did have feelings after all. 

This was the part of the weekend she was looking forward to. The panel, going toe to toe with Dr. Harkness. 

There were other scientists, sure, but Agatha was the one she wanted to verbally spar with, and now, she was too sick to do it. She had called Dr. Jones, the man in charge of the event, and told him she couldn’t make it. Now, freshly showered and medicated, she crawled into bed, trying to figure out what had happened that even her self-healing wasn’t working.

 

Agatha looked at Rio’s seat after Dr. Jones had announced that she was unable to attend. She found Ian and Ellie in the crowd and winked as the two-hour panel on dinosaur characteristics and DNA began, knowing she would now control the narrative without Dr. Vidal interfering.

 

Rio felt, more than heard, the three crisp knocks on the door.  Her headache had lessened, but was still pounding behind her eyes. She grabbed two more pills and downed them with a few gulps of water before opening the door to find Agatha standing there with soup in one hand and a shopping tote in the other.

“Go away.” Rio shook her head and began to close the door.

Agatha darted her arm out and blocked it. “Look, I heard you were sick. So I brought you medicine, soup, Gatorade, crackers, and everything else you need. May I please come in?”

Rio let out a heavy sigh and crawled back into the bed, ignoring Agatha.

Agatha set the bag on the dresser and locked the door behind her. She made Rio list her symptoms and asked when she last took anything. Eyeing the bottle of medicine on the nightstand, she raised an eyebrow, examining it and turning back to the supposed adult with a disapproving look.

“This is what you took?”

“Yes, Agatha. See? I am perfectly capable of taking care of myself.” Rio nodded proudly.

“This is infant Tylenol. You’ll never feel better with this.”

Rio groaned and buried her face in the pillows. “I’ve never been sick before.” She mumbled.  

Ignoring the confession, the healthier adult placed the drinks in the mini fridge, leaving one out for Rio to have with the soup. “You’re going to take two of these every four hours; they won’t counteract what you just took. You also are going to drink that whole Gatorade, and you’re going to eat this soup.” Agatha placed the items on the nightstand and settled next to Rio on the bed. 

“Why should I trust you?”

“I’m a doctor.” Agatha’s lips twitched upward.

“I don’t think extinct species count, Agatha.”

“Well, I didn’t kill them.” She opened the soup container and handed it to Rio. “And of the two of us, I have experience with colds. Now eat.”

Rio eyed the chicken noodle soup and looked up at Agatha. “I can’t eat chicken! They’re evolved from dinosaurs!”

“They are also healing, and overpopulated, eat the damn soup, Rio, before I shove it down your throat.”

“Yes, mommy,” Rio smirked, taking the soup with a grimace.

“You do know we’re the same age?” 

Rio laughed to herself. That’s what you think. “Yes, but you just love being in charge, don’t you, Dr. Harkness?” 

“You love it.” Agatha breathed.

“Why are you doing this? Taking care of me? After I told you I hated you?” Rio asked quietly, taking another bite of soup.

 

“First, you don’t actually hate me. I don’t think you’re capable of hating anyone.” Agatha had meant to be cocky, but something quietly softened. “I know I would hate being sick when I’m supposed to be presenting and networking. This means nothing. We can go back to pretending to hate each other in the morning.” Agatha turned around, kicking off her heels, and settled against the headboard next to Rio. “Want to watch trash reality TV?”

Agatha looked over at a snoring Rio a few hours later. She was medicated, hydrated, and nourished. Her work here was almost done. She carefully removed herself from the bed and tucked the covers in around the sleeping woman. She leaned over, stopped herself, then stood back up and gathered trash to tidy up the space a bit. She wrote out a quick note with the times Rio needed to re-medicate and left out a fresh bottle of water.

She once again gathered her heels in her hand and slipped out of Rio’s room, but first stopped to pick up the small pouch she had dropped behind the dresser the night before, and replaced the flash drive and room key she had taken prior.

Agatha tossed her heels toward her suitcase and huffed in frustration. What was she doing? She didn’t even like Dr. Rio Vidal or any of the women who came before her. She was still suspicious of that long line of scientists who looked nearly identical, and while she respected the paths they had blazed for women in the field, she didn’t fully respect their views or research. 

Then, there was the guilt and remorse she felt. Agatha had done many morally questionable things, yet this was the first time she actually questioned it. No, she hadn’t taken the research for herself; she would never, but something didn’t sit right with her.

Her phone rang, bringing her out of her thoughts, and she smiled upon hearing the voice on the other end.

 

🦕🦖🦕

Saturday morning featured a more convention-style format. There was an exhibition hall, demonstrations, and displays. It was truly a nerd's dream. Geologists, paleontologists of all disciplines, archaeologists, anthropologists, and others in adjacent fields were in attendance as both exhibitors and attendees.

Agatha couldn’t help but be slightly bored even though she was surrounded by some of the brightest minds in the scientific world and having her ego stroked. She was there representing the school where she currently taught, USC, while doing what most other scientists at the top of their field were doing: selling their own books. She was an aisle over from Rio, who was currently teaching at NYU. Agatha leaned against the table, taking down notes, when a bag landed next to her.

“Here are Vidal’s children’s books. All signed, I know how much they worship her.” Ellie smiled at Agatha.

Agatha rolled her eyes and placed the books in her bag, though she still smiled.

“Even found stuffed animals, they make sounds.” Ellie squeezed one, the so-ugly-it-was-cute creature letting out a high-pitched screech.

Agatha laughed quietly. “Oh, those are going to your side of the country.” Shaking her head, she turned more serious. 

“Did you get everything you needed?” Dr. Harkness signed a book on prehistoric amphibious mammals and handed it to a young teen.

“To move to the next step.” Ellie sneered at a passing man who looked at her a little too long.

“Fantastic, leave me out of it, Sattler.”

“This could make us famous, Agatha.”

“I’ll pass. The History and Science channels already have both of us on retainer.” She sighed, “Just don’t drag anyone else into it.” Giving her a warning look, Agatha turned from the permanent thorn in her side to discuss graduate programs, effectively dismissing the other scientist.

 

Rio knew she was leaving in the morning, along with most of the Americans, to give herself more time to readjust once stateside. She sauntered over to where Agatha’s booth, like hers, was now empty, and stood behind her. “Whatever you gave me last night worked beautifully, Harkness. Maybe you should be a doctor since you can’t get your dinosaur facts right.” 

Agatha pivoted, slightly taller in her heels. “Is that so? Seems the panel was a hit. I’ve been invited to an archaeological dig in two years. My…seventh?” 

 

“Do you even know your brontosaurus fossils from your bronchiasaurus ones?” Rio jabbed. She had her sights set on that dig in South Africa that was currently being planned, staffed, and funded. Not being on that panel last night had potentially cost her the opportunity, and she would have to work even harder over the next two years to even get on the waitlist for it.

“Can you distinguish your Triassic from your Jurassic dinosaurs?” Agatha stepped closer to Rio, those blue eyes narrowing.

Rio could feel it as they exchanged jabs, the electricity between them, how Agatha made Rio feel so dangerously mortal and reckless all at the same time. Her eyes fell to Agatha’s lips, her tongue darted across her bottom lip. Memories of Thursday night and how she didn’t get to lay a finger on Agatha replayed in her head. She had taken hold of Agatha’s wrist and was guiding her to a bathroom in the back hallway as they continued insulting each other, each insult going straight to Rio’s core. She had a fleeting thought that she should see a therapist. A mortal one.

“How did you even get your Ph.D? Just mention Mommy’s name?” Agatha sneered, shoving Rio up against the hallway wall and kissing her harshly, one hand in her hair.

Rio spotted the private bathroom and shoved Agatha toward it, not breaking the kiss. They crashed through the door. Rio kicked it shut, throwing the lock closed. She broke the kiss to breathe. 

“How did you get yours? Intimidation and sex appeal?”

“It worked, didn’t it?” Agatha grinned sarcastically before she pulled Rio back into her, kissing her urgently. Hastily unbuttoning Rio’s shirt. She let it fall to the ground, followed by the green lace bra she wore underneath. Agatha licked into Rio’s mouth and backed her into the counter. She quickly undid the button and zipper of Rio’s pants and let them fall before she lifted her onto the counter. Agatha pulled back and let her eyes roam over Rio’s body. “You’re beautiful.”

Rio’s mouth quirked up on one side. “Careful, Harkness, you almost sounded sincere.”

“Fuck you.”

“Please.” Rio fisted her hand in Agatha’s shirt and pulled her back to her, kissing her with a renewed hunger. She trailed her hands down to Agatha’s breasts and ran her thumb across her nipple over her bra and shirt. Agatha moaned before grabbing Rio’s wrist. 

“No,” Agatha growled in Rio’s ear.

 

Rio’s whine turned into a gasp when Agatha’s lips wrapped around her left nipple. Her hands ached to touch Agatha, but with the attention she was receiving from the woman in front of her, she could concede for the moment.

Rio gripped the edge of the counter with one hand and the back of Agatha’s head with the other, arching into Agatha’s mouth. Agatha licked circles around her nipple and grazed it with her teeth before nipping it slightly. She brought her hand up to copy her movements on the right nipple before switching breasts. Rio curled over to bury her face in Agatha’s neck and bit down on her pulse point, muffling her moans. She never knew she was this sensitive; every movement Agatha made set her nerves on fire and shot down to her core. If Agatha continued like this, it wouldn’t take long for her to come.

“Agatha, my god—”

“You can build me a temple later,” Agatha quipped against her chest, the vibrations adding to the sensations. Rio’s nails dug into Agatha’s scalp, and she swore in Sumerian, causing Agatha to chuckle.

Agatha moved her free hand that was gripping Rio’s thigh, up toward Rio’s now useless green, lacy underwear, and ran one finger slowly up and back down.

Rio shivered, her hips rolling into Agatha’s hand, seeking friction. 

Agatha released Rio’s nipple with a pop and kissed across her collarbone, up her neck, and back to her mouth. “So wet, so needy, and so close.” She whispered against Rio’s mouth.

“You infuriate me,” Rio whispered back, though it held no real bite.

Agatha scoffed, wrapping an arm around Rio’s waist. “Push your panties aside,” she growled. 

 

Rio did as she was told and held them there. Something was thrilling about letting this woman boss her around. Agatha once again ran one finger up and down Rio, kissing her deeply, drinking her in. 

“I can tell how close you are.” She circled her clit and then swiped across it. “Just from having your tits sucked.”

Rio whined and thrust her hips with purpose. “Please, Agatha.” She begged.

Finally, Agatha entered her with one finger, thrusting and curling in on that soft spot. It took less than a minute for Rio to come undone. Gasping and moaning Agatha’s name right into her mouth. Rio stared at Agatha in awe and slight confusion. That was unlike anything she had ever experienced. She usually needed more, longer. What the hell had this woman done to her? Agatha had slowly removed herself and was washing her hands. She had returned to the paragon of control that she was while Rio redressed herself in a haze. Rio looked at the woman who was checking her lipstick in the mirror and met her eyes. “Do you want to grab something to eat? Maybe come back to my room?”

“Sorry, baby girl, I have to get packed and make some calls. I have an early flight.”

Rio’s eyes darted to the floor. “Oh, okay.”

“Coffee in the morning?”

Rio perked up slightly. “Yes. Six? In the lobby.”

Agatha nodded and gave Rio another searing kiss before walking out of the bathroom and back to the exhibition hall to pack her things.    

 🦕🦖🦕

Agatha stared at the ceiling of her hotel room. It was three a.m., and she couldn’t sleep. The woman had been drawn to Rio Vidal since the second she saw her Thursday night, and kept being pulled to her. Telling herself she was in control, she wasn’t sure she believed it anymore. She was still shocked at herself that she had gotten partially naked Thursday night, but thankfully, it was dark.

It had taken her a decade into her career to meet Rio Vidal, and less than thirty minutes in, she was fucking her against a door. 

What a weekend.

It all ended tomorrow, though. 

Tomorrow, when she went back to California, and returned to her normal life. The life she only partially wanted. The one that had gotten so complicated, so quickly. 

The one that Ellie could never understand. 

The reason she had agreed to do Ellie and Ian’s bidding for them so they could do whatever it was they were doing. 

She ran her hand down her face and groaned.

Rolling over and closing her eyes, she tried to will herself to sleep. Yet all she saw was Rio. How the marks Agatha had left would peek out from under her clothes. The way Rio reacted to Agatha’s touch. How her lips somehow tasted like rain, and how addictive they were. The sounds that fell from her lips when she came for Agatha.

Agatha was suddenly thankful they lived on opposite sides of the country. 

 

Around the corner and down the hall, Rio also stared at the ceiling, replaying the weekend.  Death had made a habit of sleeping as the humans did; she even had a routine. 

She needed to make a trip back to her realm, talk to her siblings, the other cosmic entities like Eternity and Infinity, and regain some godsdamned sense. The tips of her fingers absentmindedly traced a bruise on her collarbone, remembering Agatha’s teeth and lips on her skin. 

This cosmic entity, older than time itself, still had so many unanswered questions about the other woman who had fallen into her orbit this weekend. She still couldn’t believe she held on to outdated beliefs, especially given how intelligent she was, but no one was perfect.

Who had Agatha been calling every night? Not that it mattered. They weren’t anything more than two top researchers in their field with differing schools of thought that were technically rivals. Who happened to have great chemistry and amazing hate sex. If you could call it that. 

She squeezed her eyes shut, trying to will herself to sleep, only to replay the weekend in her head.

Agatha had been right about one thing.

Rio Vidal could never hate Agatha Harkness.

🦕🦖🦕

Rio had already checked out of the hotel and sat in the cafe at a quarter until six, waiting for Agatha, when Dr. Sattler sat down across from her. They had worked together in the past and were at least friendly with each other.

“Hey, Rio, I haven’t gotten to talk to you this weekend.” Ellie smiled sincerely.

Rio returned the smile and fiddled with her latte. “It was a busy weekend.”

“Busy with Agatha Harkness?” Ellie raised an eyebrow.

Rio blushed and squirmed in her seat, avoiding Ellie’s gaze.

“You do know she’s married?” 

Rio’s head snapped up, her mouth suddenly dry, pulse in her ears. “What? She wasn’t wearing a ring, she didn’t say…” Rio was searching around frantically for Agatha, her mind racing.

“She doesn’t wear it at events. His name is Ralph. Great guy. Married for ten years, four-year-old twins.” Ellie’s face was full of concern.

“I…what?” Rio had gone pale, her heart was racing, and she was breathing too fast. Mortal reactions.

 

“Ellie? What are you doing?” Agatha’s cold voice cut in as she looked between the two women. Her brow furrowed, dread filling her body.

 

Rio’s eyes drifted to Agatha’s left hand, where her wedding set now sat.

“I can’t believe you.” Rio spat as she stood and gathered her things as quickly as possible.

“Wait, Rio, I can explain.” Agatha reached for her as Rio walked away.

“Save it, Harkness.” 

Agatha watched her disappear out the doors and slowly turned her head to the smug-looking blonde demon still sitting at the table.

“Happy?”

“If only she had let you explain,” Ellie mused, looking at her nails.

Agatha blew out a breath, picked up her own bags, and walked away, back to her life at home.

Her career. Her husband. Her kids.

Chapter 2: She Blinded Me With Science

Chapter Summary

After their explosive meeting in London in 2009, six years pass before our two paleontologists meet again, this time at Oxford. What happens when they're coworkers for eight months, and Wanda tags along with Agatha? How will Rio handle being near Agatha and hearing the truth™? What will Ralph say? What happens when Ellie re-enters the scene?

Chapter Notes

Warning: SMUT. Right off the bat.

I believe this will be four parts, with the potential for 5.
This chapter strays a bit from the dino aspect, but goes hard with it in the next chapter. This is an emotional roller coaster, though.

I'm not completely happy with it (I think I rewrote this a total of five times) and may come back and edit it.

See the end of the chapter for more notes

May 10, 2015 - Mother’s Day

10:23 PM, Oxford University, Oxford, England

 

Six years. It had been six years since Rio Vidal had walked away from Agatha Harkness in London. Now, here she was, a visiting professor at Oxford University, her front pressed up against the wet tile wall of the shower in her temporary apartment, screaming Agatha’s name, with Agatha pressed up against her back, three fingers fucking into her.

 

Five Hours Earlier

 

Rio walked into the lobby of the designated apartment building at Oxford University and straight to the check-in desk. It wasn’t bad for university-run housing. Much nicer than student dorms, she gave them that. It had nothing on her building in New York, of course, but it would do. It was only until December, and less hassle than finding a temporary place to stay. She had just exchanged her paperwork for the key with the young man at the desk when her muscles tensed, sensing the woman’s presence behind her.

 

“If it isn’t Dr. Vidal herself.” 

“Dr. Harkness.” Rio turned slowly to see Agatha, standing a few feet behind her, her long hair pulled up, wearing a tank top and shorts, toned arms and legs on full display, with a locket around her neck. Rio desperately tried to school her expression and not visibly check her out. Agatha’s raised eyebrow and smirk said that she noticed the way Rio’s eyes flicked down her body. Rio looked to the younger redhead next to Agatha. Rio knew vaguely who she was, but waited for an introduction anyway.

Agatha’s smirk widened slightly. “Dr. Vidal, this is Wanda Maximoff, my…research assistant.” 

Wanda rolled her eyes and stepped forward, hand outstretched. “Dr. Vidal, it’s a pleasure to meet you.”

Rio took Wanda’s hand with a polite smile, briefly wondering if the young woman knew the brief history between the two older women. She turned back to Agatha. “I assume you’re also here until December?”

 

Agatha nodded as she retrieved their keys from the desk clerk, having already taken care of the paperwork ahead of time. She handed Wanda the key to her apartment, “You’re right next to me, in case you need anything.” She gave her an almost motherly smile before turning back to Rio, her expression returning to a neutral one. “Vidal, lovely to see you again. I’m sure we’ll see you around.” Her voice was clipped, clearly wanting to avoid any further conversation.

 

Agatha led the way to the elevators, both women close behind, and pressed the button for the third floor once they stepped in.

 

“I’m also on the third floor,” Rio confirmed with a sense of dread.

“Of course you are,” Agatha grumbled.

They stepped out and moved down the hall, slowing as they approached their door numbers, and glanced at each other when they stopped at the same part of the hallway.

 

Laughter bubbled out of Wanda when Agatha and Rio realized they were directly across the hall from each other, both older women glaring at her.

That answered that question.

🦕🦖🦕

Rio stepped into the place she would call home for the next eight months and dropped her duffel bag. The rest of her luggage had been delivered while she took care of getting everything situated downstairs. She scrubbed her hands over her face and groaned. She hadn’t seen Agatha in person since 2009, and now they would be neighbors and coworkers for the rest of the year.

That one weekend had been on her mind nearly every day over the years. Death, an all-powerful being, had been hung up on this mortal for a pathetically long time. If anyone other than her best friend knew, they would think she was obsessed. Rio told herself it was curiosity and unanswered questions. 

She couldn’t ask Ellie Sattler any of the questions that had plagued her, as there was obviously history there that Rio dared not touch, and she refused to reach out to Agatha. Instead, she monitored Agatha’s social media pages, using her magick to bypass privacy measures. 

She saw the pictures of Agatha with her husband, Ralph. A prestigious doctor at Cedars-Sinai in LA. She watched Agatha’s young twins, Nicholas and Lilith, who looked just like miniature versions of Dr. Harkness herself, grow up. Rio mused over Agatha’s small list of friends that she had carefully curated. Noting that most of her engagement came from her family, specifically her mother, a well-known and somewhat unethical billionaire by the name of Evanora Harkness.

As the years passed, the questions accumulated.

Why did Agatha work so hard? Why did she work at all? What exactly was the relationship with Wanda and her twin brother, Pietro? They seemed to be like adopted children to Agatha and Ralph, and upon seeing Wanda in the lobby, that was the theory she was going with. Why did Ian Malcolm spend time with the family, and why was Agatha’s mother commenting but absent from pictures?

Rio could have meddled magickally and answered her questions that way, but refused. It felt like a violation of Agatha’s privacy to do so, and while social media was one thing, using magick to answer questions thousands of miles away was a completely different ballgame. 

They never spoke to each other like adults, choosing to spar through academic journals and websites, refuting each other’s work and research. They never appeared at the same conferences or conventions. Instead, they tore into each other as often as they could from afar, appearing separately on television programs with their opposing views and refuting the other’s theories. They had been at war ever since that weekend.

Now, Agatha was right across the hall for the foreseeable future. The woman who had consumed her thoughts, haunted her dreams, and been at the forefront of her memory for over half a decade. Rio could simply walk across the hall and ask her all of her questions, but then, Agatha would know that Rio had looked her up and kept tabs on her all these years. Agatha would tease her mercilessly and never let her live it down. Rio also doubted she would tell anything resembling the truth. 

No, Rio would not be doing that. She continued unpacking, trying to keep her mind off the breathtaking brunette with piercing blue eyes across the hallway, and fighting the feelings that had come over her the moment she laid eyes on her.

“Get it together, Vidal. She’s just a human who you’ve been stuck on, that’s all.” She mumbled to herself as she shoved clothes into a drawer and slid it closed a little harder than necessary. 

It was going to be a long eight months.

🦕🦖🦕

Agatha had unpacked quickly and now sat on the black sofa, looking at Wanda, with her arms crossed.

“You never did tell me what happened that weekend.” Wanda took a bite of the Thai takeout they had ordered and looked at Agatha, waiting for the details. “I figured it out for myself.”

“You were twenty and busy with studies of your own.” 

“It was summer, Aggie.”

“You were playing big sister to the little ones, had started your own research projects, and you were a kid. Hell, you’re still a kid.”

“I’m twenty-six, married, and have toddlers of my own.” Wanda reminded. 

“I still kick myself for taking you on that expedition. You never would have met that mediocre man if you hadn’t gone.” Agatha mentally chided herself for the hundredth time.

 

“Stop trying to change the subject. You’ve been long-distance flirting with Rio since London. You were giving each other fuck me eyes downstairs. What’s the real story? I can guess, but it’s better coming from you.” Wanda leaned in closer, ready for Agatha to finally fill in the blanks.

“It was one weekend. I was doing a favor…”

“A con. I’m a big girl.”

“Fine, I was doing an exchange for Ellie. It happened to involve Rio.”

Wanda sighed and sank against the cushions. She now had a clear picture of how things happened. “You slept with her, got what you needed while she was distracted. Whatever Ellie had must have been important, because you dete — fuck.” Wanda cut herself off, her brain catching up with her mouth as the memories of the days that followed Agatha’s return came back to her. She looked at her pseudo-mom, mouth agape, eyes wide in shock. “I don’t want to know how Ellie got it, but that’s why we went to New York! That trip changed everything for us. All because you slept with someone?” 

“Well, no, that was a benefit. I stole her research, Wanda, and gave it to Ellie. I feel awful, and I don’t think she knows. I also went on that expedition in South Africa, and she didn’t. Something else that came from that trip. But, Ellie got very jealous of Rio.”

“Of course she did. She gets jealous of any woman you even think of looking at.” Wanda gave Agatha a look as if it were the most obvious thing about the blonde.

“Well, I was going to explain some things to Rio when the ex from hell got to her first and told her I have a husband and kids. She walked away without letting me defend myself. We haven’t spoken since.” Agatha shrugged, something like regret in her eyes.

“Yes, but that dig saved us, you mainly.” Wanda’s eyes dipped to Agatha’s locket.

“For now, and I can’t tell her that.” Agatha pursed her lips and looked away.

Wanda shook her head. “No, but you could rekindle that flame, and by the way you’ve acted since the last time you saw her, there was a flame. You’ve got eight months, you’re working on a permanent solution.” Her lips turned upward as she suggested it.

“Have you talked to Ralph?” Agatha took a sip of her red wine, letting the strong taste calm her.

“He may have told me a few things over the years.” 

Agatha groaned. “That man.”

“We just want you to be happy.” Wanda stood and took her dishes into the kitchen. “I am going back next door to call the boys and prepare for the week. You should think about it.”

“It was one weekend! It was nothing!” Agatha called after her.

“Tell that to the feelings in your eyes.”

“That’s the wine!”



Agatha was three glasses in, mulling over the past six years, the back and forth with Rio, the way the other woman had looked at her, and then tried to hide it. The past years had been a type of intellectual foreplay, and now, the real woman was so close she could almost taste her. Something she craved to do again. Agatha hadn’t contacted her after that weekend. She hadn’t known what to say and wasn’t one to apologize, ever. She also rarely explained herself. 

She remembered returning home that weekend, only to have Ralph tease her. Ian had told him about Rio, and once Agatha explained her very complicated thoughts, Ralph had stopped the teasing and immediately understood. Unfortunately, Agatha had more pressing matters than the conundrum that was Rio Vidal, and she went back to her life as usual. Teaching, parenting, pretending to be the perfect wife and heiress when Evanora demanded it. Keeping her children, biological and not, safe. Repressing the feelings she swore she didn’t have. 

They also had made a very impromptu trip to New York, the six of them. Having taken the items she had gotten from Ellie to someone who knew what to do with them. That trip had kicked off a new life for them. Combined with the expedition, it was a turning point.

Now, she was here, a visiting professor, separated from most of her family for the majority of the year, with temptation right across the hallway.

She was pulled from her thoughts when her phone rang, lighting up with a picture of Ralph and the kids.

“Hello, my loves.” She answered the FaceTime call with a bright smile. 

“Happy Mother’s Day!” The three of them said in unison, with Pietro, Wanda’s twin brother, chiming in from the background. 

She let out a genuine laugh. “It’s not Mother’s Day in the UK, but thank you.” She spent a few minutes speaking with them, reminding the kids to be good and to help out with Wanda’s twins. She then spoke to Lilia, her long-time friend, who was helping with the kids while she was gone, before the phone was handed back to Ralph. 

She noticed Ralph move to another room before he looked into the camera. “What’s wrong?”

Ralph knew her better than anyone, and it came as no surprise that he could tell that she had been overthinking. “She’s here.” 

He nodded. “Called it. What did I tell you?” He tried to hide his smile and failed.

“I hate when you’re right.”

He let his smile win out.

“We’re working together, Ralph.” 

He shrugged and ran a hand through his blonde hair, and she glanced at the way his bicep flexed. His good looks were completely wasted on her, and they always had been. She knew they had always made a picture-perfect couple, and their genetics had produced two beautiful children. She was thankful for that, knowing her mother would have lost it if everything and everyone hadn’t turned out perfectly. Most women would kill for what she had, yet here she was, Agatha and her perfect husband, discussing the woman she had slept with like it was a normal topic of discussion.

Because it was.

“What did Wanda say? I’m sure she picked up on it.” He asked, cutting through her thoughts.

“To go for it,” Agatha grumbled around the fingernail she was chewing on.

“She’s perceptive, and I agree with her. I love you fiercely and support you, just as you support me. The kids and I have plans. Go have fun.”

“I love you, too, enabler.”

“Like you aren’t. Have fun, use protection.” The screen returned to her background picture. She let his and Wanda’s words swirl together before pushing herself off the couch and grabbing her key.

🦕🦖🦕

Rio was about to step into the shower when there was an insistent knock on the door. She knew it was Agatha and, for a brief moment, considered not answering it. Desire and curiosity for what Agatha wanted won. She hastily pulled on a robe and stormed to the door as Agatha’s knocking grew more impatient. 

“What is it, Harkness?” She asked gruffly, opening the door wide.

Agatha pushed past her into the apartment, not waiting for an invitation.

“By all means, come right in,” She mumbled, closing the door and following Agatha further into the room.

 

“You’re infuriating, do you know that?” Agatha started, turning around to face Rio, her eyes trailing down and back up when she noticed what Rio was writing.

“I’m infuriating? Me? I was about to take a shower, and you come barging in like you own the place.”

“Yes! You! You never let me explain, just walked away and never reached out!” Agatha’s voice rose, taking a step closer to Rio, ignoring the shower comment.

“That goes both ways, Agatha.” Rio ran a hand roughly through her hair. “What was I supposed to do? Let you continue lying?” She gritted out.

“I never lied to you.” Agatha gravitated closer to Rio, her jaw clenched.. 

“You’re MARRIED! With kids! Two things you failed to mention.” Rio’s voice rose, and her eyes narrowed, focused solely on Agatha.

Agatha turned away from Rio’s scrutinizing gaze. “You’ve had zero problem mentioning me in articles or on TV. Clearly, I made an impression.” Agatha looked back at her, meeting the volume of her voice.

“Neither have you!” She spat back. “I don’t fuck married women.” She added, steering the conversation back.

“If you remember correctly, you didn’t.” 

“I don’t facilitate cheating.” Rio’s breath mingled with Agatha’s, their faces close, voice dipping low..

“I. Wasn’t. Cheating.” Agatha finally allowed her eyes to dip to Rio’s mouth.

Rio stared back at Agatha, eyes wide, mind reeling. “Enlighten me then.”

Agatha closed the small gap between them and captured Rio in a desperate kiss.   

Despite Rio’s better judgment, she grabbed Agatha’s hips and pulled her into her forcefully, returning the kiss. Rio bit down on Agatha’s bottom lip and slipped her tongue past her lips when Agatha gasped.

Rio grasped the back of Agatha’s thighs and lifted her easily. Agatha wrapped one arm around Rio’s shoulders, her other hand slightly tugging on her hair. Her legs took hold around Rio’s waist. 

“Where are you taking me?” She asked between breaths when Rio started walking.

“I told you, I’m going to take a shower.”

“You do smell like desperation and bad decisions.” Agatha mouthed against Rio’s neck.

“And here I thought that was you,” Rio bit back a moan and placed Agatha back on her feet in front of the shower, reaching around to fumble with the faucet.

Agatha tugged on the poorly tied belt of Rio’s robe and pushed it off her shoulders, onto the floor. “Tits against the wall, Vidal.” She hissed in her ear.

Rio reached for the waistband of Agatha’s shorts. “Don’t I get to…”

Agatha slapped her hands away. “I told you before, no touching. Tits to the wall.” She punctuated with a slap to Rio’s ass.

 

Rio moaned and obeyed, quickly realizing that she would do whatever Agatha told her to do. The thought didn’t frighten Death nearly as much as it should.

 

Agatha quickly undressed, running her fingers over her locket and murmuring an ancient and forgotten language under her breath before stepping into the shower behind Rio. She grabbed the other woman’s wrists and placed her hands above her head, palms flat against the tile. The water cascaded over both of them in the walk-in shower as she moved Rio’s dark hair over one shoulder and licked down the column of her neck, and bit her shoulder, dragging her nails down her back. 

 

Rio shuddered at the mix of sensations, biting back her moans in an attempt to deny the smug woman behind her the satisfaction of knowing she was driving her wild. Nails ran up her thighs, ghosting over where she so desperately wanted Agatha’s experienced fingers. Rio knew Agatha was marking her neck and back, and she willed the evidence not to heal immediately. 

It took everything Rio had not to give in to the teasing between her legs or at her tits. She was clenching around nothing, and Agatha had barely touched her, heat building in her stomach, hands grasping futilely at the tile.

 

“You’re holding back from me,” Agatha murmured in her ear, before licking the shell of it.

Rio smirked toward the wall. “Whatever do you mean?” She forced out as normally as possible.

Agatha ran a finger over Rio’s clit and was rewarded with a low moan. “You think I can’t feel you dripping down your leg? Or how you’re tensing against me? I’ve barely touched you, and you’re trying not to come.” She punctuated her statement with a sharp pinch to Rio’s clit.

Rio finally cried out, her hips bucking back towards Agatha. “There she is,” Agatha smirked into her shoulder and began to circle where she had just pinched. “Are you going to be a good girl for me?”

Rio nodded with a whimper at the excruciatingly slow pace.

“Words, doctor.”

“Yes, I’ll be good.” 

She increased the speed and pressure of her circles, Rio dropping her head forward, bracing against the tile, ass pressed against Agatha. The volume of her moans increased, and she spread her legs wider apart for her, biting her lip to muffle a scream when Agatha slammed three fingers into her without warning. Rio rutted against Agatha’s hand, hot water against her skin, steam enveloping them, Agatha’s palm against her clit, with three fingers fucking into her. Agatha snaked her other arm around Rio and pulled her tight against her. 

“Let me hear you while I make you come, babygirl.” Agatha’s breath was warm against her ear as she curled her fingers, ripping a scream from Rio’s lips, and clenching around Agatha’s digits. 

“Yes, scream my name.” Agatha moaned, pressing herself flush against Rio’s back, pupils blown, taking everything she could from what contact she was getting. 

Agatha’s name was like a chant for Rio as she gave into the pleasure, Agatha not slowing down until Rio’s head fell back against her shoulder and she put her hand on her wrist. 

“Agatha.” She whispered shakily.

“Yes?” Agatha kissed into Rio’s hair and then mentally scolded herself for slipping into a softer version of herself, just like she had done the last time.

“Water’s getting cold, need to get clean.” She mumbled, completely and utterly fucked.

 

Agatha laughed internally at how Rio didn’t realize she had spoken in five different languages and filed that information away for later. “Let’s get clean then, lightweight.” Agatha tried to tease, but it fell short when the other scientist shivered against her from another aftershock.

Rio Vidal was going to be her ruin.

The shower was too intimate for Agatha, and she hated that she was leaning into it instinctually. She refused to let Rio wash or look at her, insisting that the water was getting too cold and they needed to hurry. Although she took her time with Rio, and craved her touch. What the fuck is happening to me? She thought, as her nails lightly scratched against Rio’s scalp, drawing a soft moan from the other woman. She hurriedly redressed and kissed Rio before she went back across the hall. 

 

“I’ll see you tomorrow,” Rio said softly, fighting not to ask her to stay.

Agatha’s mouth curled into an amused smirk. “Ah, yes, so you can prepare to fill young scientists with the wrong information, and I can correct them. It’s going to be a great time, Doctor Vidal.”

Rio watched her go and tilted her head slightly when something on Agatha’s back glowed slightly purple through her shirt. “Get it together, you’re imagining things.” She chided herself, locking the door behind Agatha and turning in for the night.

Surely, Death would know if something odd or magickal were going on around her. She wasn’t that integrated with the mortal world. 

Was she?    

🦕🦖🦕

“Everything is fine here, Ags. Kids are okay, I’m okay, the old crone hasn’t reared her ugly head. May I please go back to sleep?” Ralph sleepily reassured her through the phone. “Don’t you have a class? Where’s Wanda?”

“She’s in a lab, and classes haven’t started yet.” She chewed nervously on her thumbnail. “Ralph, I’m not myself around her. It’s just like last time. I briefly thought about staying, bringing her coffee. That’s not me.”

He yawned, and the sheets rustled when he rolled over. “It is when you’re not being an abrasive bitch who thinks she’s better than everyone else. Even El—”

“Don’t bring the devil into this.”

“Okay, well, you were like this before, is all I’m saying.”

“Yeah, and look where that got me.” She ran her hand over her face and leaned back in her chair. “She’s going to start asking questions. I didn’t have my ring on yesterday, but I wear it at work, and she did accuse me of cheating in London.”

 

Ralph sat up against the headboard, realizing the chances of going back to sleep were slim to none. “What did you say?”

“I told her I didn’t cheat, she said to enlighten her, we fucked in the shower.”

“One of my favorite ways to be enlightened.” 

“There’s just something about her. I kissed the top of her head, Ralph. Washed her hair. It was shockingly intimate.”

“How scandalous.” He said with an exaggerated gasp. “Agatha, we’re protected now, you could probably break the contract and still be fine. Relax, go wild.”

She smirked at her reflection in the window. “Thanks for the pep talk and the permission, Hubby.” 

“Happy wife, happy life. Can I please go back to sleep now?”

“Please. I know Ian is in that bed with you. You’re not going back to sleep. Let the kids know I’ll call them later. Love you, and thank you.”

“Of course, love you too.” He looked over where Ian cracked an eye open and huffed. “Ian sends his love, too.”

Agatha laughed and hung up the phone, placing it on her desk. 

She looked back at her computer screen and tried to reply to emails, her mind continuing to wander back to Rio and the circumstances surrounding her situation. The best course of action would be to stay away from the other paleontologist until the end of the program. They clearly couldn’t control themselves around one another, and Agatha couldn’t avoid Rio’s questions forever. It was not lost on Agatha that Rio hadn’t mentioned anything about her research, and therefore clearly didn’t know that Ellie, and Ian, by proxy, had a copy of it. 

The best thing for Agatha to do was to continue her banter and light bullying. Tear into Rio’s theories, remain rivals, and drive a wedge between them. It was safe, and no one would get hurt. Whatever magnetism and potential feelings were there would fade away. Anything that was there surely wasn’t permanent. 

That’s what she told herself for the rest of the day, including when she went to question Rio in her office later that afternoon, only to end up straddling Rio’s lap with her hand between the other woman’s legs.

“I can’t believe you stole my lab hours.” Agatha bit down on Rio’s bottom lip, dragging one finger slowly through her heat.

“I didn’t steal them, I merely repurposed them.” Rio struggled to get out.

“Liar.” Agatha pushed into her slowly. “What do you even need it for? Testing your birdbrained theories?”

“Why do you need it? Can’t you just use mommy’s money and buy a lab?” Rio gasped and bucked her hips when Agatha’s thumb touched her just right.

The woman straddling her smirked. “Did someone Google me?”

“Harkness isn’t…a common…last…name.” Rio gasped out as Agatha’s thrusts sped up.

“I need this lab and those hours, and you’re not going to come until I get what I want.” She stilled her movements when Rio’s moans became louder.

“Are you using sex as a weapon, Dr. Harkness?” Rio tried to sound serious, but it came out as a whimper instead.

“Think of it as a bargaining tool.” Agatha pressed against Rio’s G-spot and kissed her, swallowing the groan she let out.

“Not fair.” 

“Well? Can I have my hours back?” Agatha began to stroke against the sensitive area inside Rio.

“God, Agatha, please…”

Agatha stopped her movements again. Rio growled in frustration.

“Fine, yes. We can figure it out. Just let me come, you manipulative demon.”

Agatha grinned devilishly. “Thank you, babygirl.”

Rio moaned at the moniker and pulled her closer when Agatha continued her movements.

Pleasure coursed through her like an electrical current as she came with a silent scream into Agatha’s neck. She knew her pants and boxer briefs were ruined, and didn’t care. She fell back into the chair and stared up at Agatha, dazed. “You’re a fucking menace, Harkness.”

 

Agatha removed her hand and brought her fingers to her lips, sucking the long digits in a way that could only be described as obscene, and released them with a pop. “That I am.” She gave Rio one last kiss before standing up. “Pleasure doing business with you, Vidal. Thank you for being so cooperative.” She winked, smoothed down her skirt, hiding her soaked panties and slick thighs, and walked out the door, leaving Rio alone to deal with her own aftermath.

 

“That woman is going to kill me,” Rio mumbled to herself. 

 

🦕🦖🦕

Beginning of August

 

Death found the routine invigorating. Teaching, researching, arguing with Agatha Harkness, being absolutely ruined by her. No amount of insults, teasing, or mild threats could keep the two of them apart. They still ended up in varying states of undress most days, with Rio moaning Agatha’s name. It didn’t matter the place, an apartment, office, classroom, lab, the supply closet next to the Dean’s office, the one time in the library, — nowhere was sacred.

Except their beds. The two places Agatha refused to have Rio. 

Yet, for some reason, Rio was still forbidden to touch Agatha, and it had nothing to do with her period. They had been sleeping together all summer, and Agatha had yet to have one. No, there was something else, and Rio was determined to find out the reason. She had also noticed other patterns in Agatha’s behavior.

She was rarely allowed to see Agatha fully naked, and if she was, it was after Agatha had already completely undressed herself. Even then, it was usually pitch black, Rio was turned the opposite direction, or Rio had been blindfolded. Something she liked very much, but she desperately wanted to drink in the entire sight of Agatha. Explore her body in its entirety and make Agatha feel as good as she made Rio feel. She also noticed that Agatha’s locket always stayed on. Rio had once heard parts of what seemed to be an incantation in a mix of Latin and Greek. 

“Hide us…”

“...the chains”

“Protect us from evil.”

Rio had questions.

 

She had also noticed that she was losing her connection with her own cosmic world. For nearly two hundred years, Rio had been blending in with human society and rarely used her magick. There had been a time when she was aware of all reapers and magick users around her, yet when a professor had died from a heart attack on campus, she hadn’t even noticed one of her reapers was there. 

She was losing her touch, and it was becoming a concern. How could she protect herself, or those she cared about, not that she cared about anyone, if she wasn’t in tune with her surroundings?

She was also becoming more susceptible to illness and general human ailments. While she usually needed a few minutes to recover after an invigorating session with Agatha, she found herself actually feeling fatigued and needing sleep. While she had trained herself to sleep, it wasn’t something she needed until now. She was having aches and pain, something was happening, and she wasn’t sure what it was.

 

Those specific lines of thought were for another time, and she pushed them down for the more pressing issue of the Agatha situation. They rarely talked outside of the jabs they threw at each other, and never spent time together that wasn’t sexual or academic. That wasn’t who Rio was, usually. Yet, she wanted, no, needed, to know more about Agatha, not just what she saw online, or through articles and video clips. 

Fortunately, Rio knew the perfect young redhead to talk to.

 

As a research assistant and completing her own doctorate, Wanda Maximoff was working with the entire department. Not just Agatha. She knew everyone on campus attending the summer program and was a crucial part of everyone’s research projects. She had made friends with everyone with her warm smile and warmer demeanor. She listened to the grad students and their woes, proved herself indispensable to the other scientists, and was easy to talk to. 

That particular morning, Rio found her working with a cloned prehistoric shrubbery. “Good Morning, Dr. Vidal. I’m just finishing with these samples, and I’ve already started on the data.” The younger woman took clippings from the shrubbery and put them onto various microscope slides.

 

“Thank you, but that’s not what I wanted to talk to you about.” Rio looked around the lab nervously to make sure they were alone. She was on edge, and while she should’ve started with something light, like asking about Wanda’s sons, she wanted to get straight to the woman on her mind.

Wanda stopped what she was doing and glanced up, “I haven’t done —”

Rio waved a hand in front of herself, “Oh no, nothing like that. You’re doing great and should be running your own research. No, it’s about…it’s about…” Rio sighed and looked down, sliding onto a stool next to Wanda and crossing her arms across her chest. “...Agatha. It’s about Agatha.”

Wanda nodded and bit back her smirk, turning it into a sympathetic smile. “I’m aware of the situation.” She said carefully. “Though I’m sure you know that, which is why you’re coming to me.” 

Rio nodded. “Right. Exactly how long have you known her?” Rio had only inferred the basics from her snooping and observation and needed confirmation.

Wanda sat down on her stool, abandoning the slides, and turned to Rio. “Sixteen years now. My twin brother, Pietro, and I were ten when we went to live with Agatha and Ralph, her husband.”

Rio nodded. “She and I don’t talk much about anything that’s not work-related. It’s weird to, well,” 

“Have a sexual relationship with someone that’s basically a stranger?” Wanda finished for her.

Rio flinched, “Right. We don’t go out, she doesn’t stay the night, I can’t touch her, she says she’s not cheating, but it feels like she is.” The realization that Agatha basically raised Wanda finally hit Rio, and she snapped her head up, eyes wide. “Shit. She’s like your mom. I should not have this conversation with you.” 

Wanda began to laugh. “Trust me, I am the best person to have this conversation with. Yes, she is legally my mom. She did adopt us. Aggie’s mom, Evanora, made it this huge PR thing. Anyway, I know Agatha almost as well as Ralph does. If you were to have this conversation with anyone, it would need to be Ralph or me. Well, there’s another person, but she’s busy with the younger kids,” she explained as her laughter faded.

Rio gave her an amused smile. “So wait, how was it a PR thing?”

Wanda leaned on her hand. “After I explain, you have to spill, got it?”

Rio nodded slightly in agreement.

“My country, Sokovia, was at war. Pietro and I had survived multiple attacks, although our parents hadn’t. We were ten when Evanora Harkness, as an act of goodwill,” Wanda rolled her eyes, “visited the orphanage we were in. She saw my brother and me and decided we were to be the perfect poster children for her cause and for her corporation to get behind. She had just forced Agatha to get married to appease stockholders. Something about ‘the future of the company’ and what better to reinforce that, AND make her look like the good Samaritan, than her daughter adopting the two orphans?”

Rio stared at Wanda, mouth slightly agape. Rio herself hated Evanora and her company, Salem Seven Holdings, for professional and environmental reasons, but this took it to a whole new level. “Wait, did you say Agatha was forced to marry Ralph?”

“Did I?” The corner of Wanda’s mouth twitched upwards, and she shrugged. 

Rio moved her focus back to Wanda and her brother. “How did you and Pietro feel?”

“Honestly? We were just glad to have a real home and a family. Most people would be in awe of the money, but that’s not what we cared about. We were terrified that they would be mean, like Evanora is. Agatha certainly can come off that way to most everyone. However, we were taken in, and they reminded us of our parents. They have supported us through everything. Well, Agatha isn’t completely supportive of my marriage, but that’s because she doesn’t like my husband. 

“She has done so much to keep us protected and safe. Nothing changed when they had the twins, and we’re one big happy family, for the most part. I have twin boys now, and she’ll say she hates it when they call her grandma, but she secretly loves it.” 

Rio took it all in. The great Agatha Harkness, this woman who came off as hardheaded, stubborn, judgmental, stuck-up, and just plain arrogant, wasn’t actually any of those things, in private. She had seen cracks in the mask, but here was Wanda, not just confirming it, but living proof. Wanda had been raised and shaped by the woman in question, and from what Rio had seen, was a good human and someone people enjoyed being around. 

“So why is she being so distant?” Rio asked out loud.

“That’s something you have to ask her, but I will tell you, she doesn’t always like it,” the younger woman smiled knowingly. 

“Why doesn’t she like your husband?” 

Wanda laughed again. “She says a dishwasher has more personality. He’s just guarded, is all. I met him in South Africa on that mass expedition in 2011. She blames herself for dragging me along.” 

Rio soaked in the information, focusing on this unseen domestic side of Agatha and the knowledge that she didn’t like how distant she was being. 

“What are your feelings about my mother?” Wanda probed.

“I…uh…well, you are definitely blunt like she is.” Rio attempted to dodge the question, eyebrows knitted together from hearing Wanda using the title for Agatha.

 

Wanda tilted her head slightly, waiting for the answer and measuring Rio’s reaction to what she had called Agatha. She didn’t always refer to the woman as her mom, but she was curious to see how the professor reacted. Rio seemed to be processing it as though it made sense, instead of it shocking or upsetting her. Wanda observed every change in the professor’s body language and watched for any sign of hesitation or discomfort.

“It takes everything in me not to ask her to stay every night. I hate her rules. I want to go on a date, watch trash reality TV, and make fun of it. I want to do the stupid things couples do, I want to…” Rio turned away from Wanda’s probing green eyes, focusing on a petri dish with some unknown bacteria growing in it. She was being extremely vulnerable, words tumbling out of her mouth at a stupidly fast rate, and even she was surprised at her own confession.

“You want to date Agatha, for real. A little slow for lesbians, but, wow.” Wanda’s eyes widened, processing the information from the distinguished woman in front of her.

Rio laughed nervously. “I feel like I’m in high school, confessing a crush.” It felt like a natural comparison for Rio, even though she had never had that experience. 

“To one of the people closest to the crushee, no less,” Wanda added.

“Well, check one off for the nerdy scientist stereotypes, I guess. I can talk about dinosaurs for days, present results of experiments in front of an auditorium of strangers, stare dinosaurs in the face, replicas of course,” Rio caught herself when Wanda gave her a funny look, “but I can’t tell the beautiful woman that I am literally having sex with that I want more.”

Wanda put a comforting hand on the older woman’s knee. “Trust me, she’s not much better.”

“Okay.” Rio let out a shaky breath. “Promise you won’t say a word of this to her?”

“Of course not.” The woman raised by Agatha Harkness vowed. 

Rio stood and wrapped her arms around herself. “Now what do I do?”

“You ask her to dinner. You get to know each other with your clothes on,” Wanda deadpanned.

“You mean talk about something that’s not work-related?” Rio lifted an eyebrow.

Wanda buried her head in her hands with a groan. “You’re both helpless.”     

🦕🦖🦕

Once Rio left the lab, Wanda headed straight back to the apartment building and used her extra key to let herself into Agatha’s apartment. It was Friday, the day Agatha refused to have classes or office hours. 

“A certain fling of yours just visited me.” She flung herself on the couch, where the older woman was grading essays and listening to music that sounded a lot like something one would listen to when they were yearning. 

“Oh? What about?” Agatha didn’t look up, but did raise an eyebrow in interest.

“You and your circumstances.”

“Did you promise you wouldn’t say a word to me?”

Wanda nodded.

Agatha looked up and locked eyes with Wanda for a few minutes before she finally looked away, brow furrowed.

Wanda reached forward and took Agatha’s hand. “Ags, I think it’s time. You’ve been focused on her for years.”

Agatha opened her mouth in protest, but the words died in her throat.

“Exactly.” Wanda reaffirmed. “You’ve gone back and forth with both Ralph and me since May about her. Stringing her along isn’t going to help.”

Agatha finally moved her stack of half-graded papers to the coffee table and pulled Wanda close, tucking her daughter’s head under her chin. 

“You’re extremely wise for your age, you know that?” 

“I seem to remember a feisty brunette who was just as wise at my age. I learned from the best.”

They sat there for a few more moments before Agatha sighed and unwrapped her arms from Wanda and nudged her to move. “Well, I'd better make some calls before Dr. Vidal comes knocking.”

Wanda grinned, “Good luck!”

 

Agatha watched Wanda head back to her own space before making three very specific phone calls about her highly specialized situation. The first to a specialist in New York, Jennifer Kale, the second to Lilia, and the third to Ralph. After receiving clearance and instructions from Dr. Kale, reassurance and more direction from Lilia, and very ecstatic screams from Ralph, she finally felt prepared for if and when Rio made a move. She could always take the risk and ask Rio out first. Agatha was a take-charge kind of person and an independent woman; however, that would look suspicious if she asked her right after Rio had spoken with Wanda.

She opened her laptop and pulled up the stupid document that controlled her life, rereading the clauses over and over. She had dissected them, maliciously complied, found out every way to get around them, and was now on a mission to break the damn thing without destroying herself or her family. It was worse than a curse. It was legally binding. Staring at it, she scoffed. Most people would have loved growing up as the heiress of a multi-billion-dollar corporation. The only heir to a billionaire. Not Agatha Harkness. Not when her mother was Evanora Harkness.

The bigoted, unethical, loud-mouthed, two-faced, destroy-the-planet-for-her-own-gain, Evanora Harkness. Agatha had everything she wanted given to her on a blood diamond-encrusted platter, yet was somehow born with a sense of right or wrong. Not much, but some. She didn’t always abide by it, but it was there, at least. “Just a little longer,” She muttered, closing the computer and taking out her phone. 

She couldn’t wait around for Rio to text her. She didn’t know if or when she would. Though knowing Rio, she wouldn’t wait very long. Agatha groaned and leaned back on the couch. “Woman,” she said with a frustrated sigh. 

She nearly jumped out of her skin when she received the text.

Feathers

Dinner?

 

Agatha smirked at the name she had given Rio in her phone before typing a reply.

 

Why, doctor, are you asking me on a date, or is this casual?

 

She watched the typing bubbles appear and disappear multiple times with an amused grin.

 

Feathers

I’ll be at your door at seven. Wear something nice. Menace.

 

Her smirk deepened when Rio didn’t answer the question. It was most definitely a date.

 

Sweats it is. See you at 7, babygirl.

 

Agatha padded into her room, still smirking at the exchange, and looked in her closet. For the first time in what seemed like lifetimes, she didn’t know what to wear.   

 

🦕🦖🦕

 

Rio didn’t take the bait with Agatha’s last message. Instead, she made the wise decision to ignore it and get dressed. She had already found a restaurant to take Agatha to, a suggestion from a colleague, and was trying to calm her nerves. In her eons of existence, death had never been nervous in the presence of a mortal, or any being, for that matter. She had guided queens, kings, celebrities, heroes, and all types of people to the afterlife. She’d even been in rooms and in conversation with those people when they had been living.

Yet Agatha Harkness made her heart race and her knees shake. It was a rare phenomenon, and Rio didn’t try to think about it, afraid of what she would discover if she did. She had once spoken about the concept of love and attraction to her sisters, Eternity and Infinity, shortly after returning from London. They told her to stop the ruse of being mortal and return to their realm. She discussed it with her best friend in New York, Alice Wu, who had been all dreamy-eyed and talked about love at first sight and her wife.

It was absolutely not that. She had talked to one other person about it, and death had absolutely rejected what she had said. No, it was just hormones or something. Maybe Rio did need to take a sabbatical and recharge in her realm, especially if she was walking around and not sensing reapers.

Rio looked at herself in the mirror, trying to clear the racing thoughts. She smoothed the lapels of her jacket; the tailored suit looked perfect. She undid the top two buttons of the dark green shirt she wore underneath, showing just the right amount of collarbone. Her hair was up, a few strands hanging loose, and her makeup was light. She wore boots with a low heel, and as she looked at herself in the mirror, she thought she looked hot enough to date. “Here goes roughly everything,” She whispered.



Agatha ran her hands over her hair for what must have been the sixty-fifth time. She had allowed Wanda to dress her, and while she admitted she looked great, she kept tugging at different parts of her dress. The fitted sleeveless black dress fell mid-thigh, the deep V-neck showing ample cleavage. Her locket ever present, stilettos giving her a few extra inches of height. “Well damn, I clean up nicely.” She had told Wanda when she saw herself in the mirror. Wanda had rolled her eyes playfully. 

“Please, as though you didn’t already know that.” 

 

Agatha whipped her head around toward the door when Rio’s knocking cut through the silence. She took two deep breaths as she closed the distance between her and the door, her heels clacking against the wooden floor. Agatha took another calming breath and opened it, her breath catching in her throat when she laid eyes on the woman in front of her. Dr. Rio Vidal truly was beautiful. Agatha’s instincts screamed at her to say something sarcastic and catty.

“You’re beautiful,” she said breathily. Damnit Harkness.

Rio stood there, eyes raking over Agatha, speechless.

Agatha leaned against the doorframe, “I know I’m hot, but geeze, Vidal, way to inflate my ego.” She drawled, proud of her quick recovery.

“I think I preferred the Agatha that was paying me a compliment outside of sex.” Rio snapped back.

Agatha reached for her bag, closing and locking the door behind her. “Where are you taking me for dinner? Somewhere with dino nuggets and fries?” 

Rio held out her arm, and Agatha took it without hesitation. “Something like that. There’s a McDonald’s up the street.”

“Sounds like your idea of a fancy date, feathers.”

Once outside of their building, Agatha dropped Rio’s arm and took a step away, careful not to bump into Rio or look overly friendly as they got into their Uber. Rio looked at her questioningly, to which she returned a tight smile. 

 

Agatha would be lying if she said she wasn’t slightly impressed at the upscale establishment Rio had brought them to. They had discussed theories and actually agreed on a few things on the Uber ride over, and now found themselves in comfortable silence over wine and Greek salads. 

“I talked to Wanda today,” Rio admitted, eyes focused on her fork.

“Oh?”

“She told me her story. How you and Ralph took her and Pietro in, how you were forced to marry Ralph.” Her voice dropped, making sure no one around them heard, in case anyone recognized Agatha. 

“Questions you’ve had for a while, I assume.” 

Rio nodded, finishing her salad and placing her fork on her plate as she waited for the main course.

“First outing and already bringing out the serious topics, I see.” Agatha sipped her wine and waited for their plates to be swapped out before continuing. “I guess it is time to answer a few questions.”

“I think I deserve an explanation by now, don’t you?” Rio finally met Agatha’s eyes.

Agatha inhaled sharply, almost choking on her asparagus, by the intensity of the amber eyes that stared at her, paired with Rio’s words. The scientist was right. 

“You do.” She took another bite of her dinner. “You know what I’m set to inherit.” It was a statement, not a question.

“Obviously.”

“How much have you read about my mother?” She asked casually between bites.

Rio scrunched her nose. “Just her company. Why?” 

“Look up some of her beliefs and some things she’s done while we finish eating,” Agatha commanded.

Rio obeyed immediately, as if conditioned, and Agatha watched her facial expressions carefully as they finished eating. She waved off a refill of her wineglass when Rio laid her phone down and looked at Agatha, both plates removed.

“I knew she was unethical and had terrible business practices, but shit.”

“Dessert?” Agatha asked as the waiter approached. 

Rio shook her head. “No, would you like to walk back? It’s not far.”

Rio insisted on paying the bill, and the minute they hit the sidewalk, Agatha continued the conversation. “She’s always been cruel, ultra conservative, judgmental. I kind’ve became a scientist to spite her. Except I fell in love with paleontology. She hated that I didn’t go into business or finance to take over the empire. Trust me, I can run that place myself.” Agatha walked closer to Rio, though she made sure they didn’t appear as anything more than friends. Despite it being early August, there was a slight breeze, and she shivered when there was a particularly hard gust. Rio chuckled, stepping back, taking off her suit jacket, and handing it to her. “How chivalrous.” Agatha deadpanned.

“You’re welcome, Harkness.” 

Agatha smiled at her, genuinely. The type that met her eyes. She stepped closer to Rio’s side and resumed her story. “She hoped that I’d marry a man, and that’s how the business would be passed down. Imagine her fury when she caught me with a girl during my sophomore year of high school.” Agatha scoffed at the memory. “She sent me to a conversion camp; it didn’t go well.”

“Did you break out?”

“Ralph and I burned it down, actually.” 

Rio stopped and turned. “Okay, Wednesday Addams. Your husband, Ralph?”

Agatha nodded and tugged Rio to continue walking. “We became very close. His parents were also terrible people and sent him to camp when they found out about his love of musicals. So, we planned to go to the same college, exchanged addresses and phone numbers, and kept in touch. The joke was on our parents when they sent us to the same boarding school for junior and senior years.

“I met Ellie freshman year of college. We were together for a very long time, off and on. My mother found out, and I was forced to get married. At the time, I needed her money, her name. It would all be gone if I didn’t. So, Ralph and I married. I was finishing my doctorate, he was in med school. Then Wanda and Pietro. My mother was trying to indebt me to her further by forcing me to adopt these orphaned kids…money can buy anything, but honestly? It was one of the best things that happened to us. Then we had Nicky and Lily, they’re ten now. Science, of course. Ralph and I would never.” Agatha made a face of disgust.

“Why have biological children?” 

Agatha chewed on the inside of her cheek. She had never told anyone who hadn’t been in her life at the time about any of this, yet here she was, uncharacteristically baring her soul to this woman whom she liked way more than she was supposed to. “When Evanora caught me at sixteen, she made me sign a contract, another at eighteen, and another on my wedding day. It’s legitimate, notarized, and legally binding. It stated that we had to have biological children. Thankfully, it didn’t state how they had to be conceived. Evanora never put much faith in the sciences.”

“Your whole life was mapped out for you.” 

Agatha hummed and then looked around, not realizing that they had made it all the way back to their apartments. “When did we even take the elevator?” 

“We took the stairs,” Rio answered.

“I’ve never been so engrossed talking to someone that I wasn’t aware of my surroundings.” Agatha blinked at Rio. “Damn. Starting to realize why I kept it the way I had. You’re dangerous, Vidal.”

“I could say the same for you, Harkness.”

Agatha fished her key out of her bag, unlocked the door, and looked over her shoulder to see Rio in front of her own door. “Hey, feathers, you coming in?” 

Rio turned, eyebrows raised to her hairline. “You want me in your apartment?”

“No, just asked for shits and giggles.” She walked in, leaving the door open.

 

Rio hesitated for a moment before following Agatha, closing the door behind her. She had been in Agatha’s apartment before, but never casually, without a heated entrance. Rio was already perplexed with how the night had gone. Agatha had never said that much to her without it being full of a heated back-and-forth. Agatha actually wanted Rio to know the truth, and it tugged at something in her chest. Not going there. Rio told herself. 

Rio watched Agatha hang her jacket up in the coat closet and take her stilettos off. She tracked the woman across the room and watched her sit gracefully on the couch and turn those ocean blue eyes, full of amusement, on her.

“Are you just going to stare, or are you going to have a seat?”

Rio felt the heat creep up her cheeks as she took off her boots and sat on the opposite end of the sofa, turning to face Agatha, resting her arm on the back of the couch, and propping her cheek on her fist. “Better, your highness?”

“Yes. Now that I’ve shared entirely too much, your turn.”

“I still have questions.”

“The book of Harkness is closed for the night, hot stuff.” Agatha moved closer and put a hand on Rio’s knee. “I want to know about you. This goes both ways. Any exes I need to know about? Spouses? Kids? I know you're a science nepo baby.”

Rio chuckled. “Your favorite topic. No serious exes.” Or any in this realm, anyway. “Never married, no children. Just a best friend back in New York who’s house-sitting for me. Oh, and pet-sitting.”

“Cat?”

“Bearded dragon. Victoria.”

“Oh, Nicky would love a house dinosaur.”

“She thinks she’s a dinosaur. Alice is spoiling her, giving her entirely too many bugs, I’m sure.”

“That’s what aunts are for. Lily is constantly giving Wanda’s boys sweets. She’s somewhere between aunt and much nicer big sister.”

“What about your childhood? Must’ve been a little confusing having a mom with the exact same name as yours.”

Rio’s brain stuttered for a moment, forgetting her cover story. “Oh, right. No, it was just normal. Plus, she was gone so much that it really didn’t matter. I stayed with Alice a lot.” 



They talked a bit longer before Agatha glanced at the clock, a little past midnight. “It’s getting late, I’ll see you tomorrow?”

“Are you kicking me out?”

“I am. If we’re going to date, then we’re going to date. Now kiss me and go home.”

 

Rio couldn’t help but smile at the beautiful menace in front of her. She leaned forward and gave Agatha a chaste kiss, jumping up when Agatha tried to grab her and pull her in. “Two can play this game, Mommy.”

 

Agatha watched her walk out with a wicked grin. She knew exactly what she was doing.

🦕🦖🦕

Beginning of October

Agatha and Rio hadn’t had sex since August, and everyone else was suffering because of it. Wanda sat in Agatha’s office, listening to her rant, when she finally lost it. “For the love of all things holy and unholy, just fuck already.”

Agatha sat straight up. Wanda rarely spoke to her like that. “Now that I finally can, I planned on it. Were you not listening?”

“Wait, what? You couldn’t?” 

“No. Did I not tell you? Part of the instructions was to sustain for a month, and then I held out a bit extra to make sure.”

“You didn’t mention that. So you fed Rio a line of bullshit?”

Agatha returned to her work.

“Wouldn’t this all be easier if you just told her the truth?”

Agatha ignored her.

Wanda groaned. She knew the answer to that. “You’ve been moody and irritable. I need this for you.”

Agatha looked at her sideways. “It’s not that easy, angel.”

“What do you…you’re starting to really like her with all this domestic stuff you’ve been doing.”

Agatha made a sound of acknowledgement. 

“So, what now?”

“We continue what we’re doing, I stick to the plan, and I just see what happens?”

Wanda covered her eyes with her hands. “For the first time, Agatha Harkness doesn’t have a plan.”

“I have the concept of a plan.”

Wanda grabbed a pillow and screamed into it. “Aggie, you’re emotionally stupid when it comes to relationships, you know that? The two of you have spent the last month learning about each other. She has Ralph’s number. Don’t look at me like that. I gave it to her. She Facetimed the kids with you last weekend. You are in this, and you know it. That woman is your girlfriend; you just haven’t caught up. You’ve managed to keep up your discourse, and you have bickered like a nerdy married couple since you met. So tonight, after your date, have sex in a bed, let her finally touch you, and discuss what you are. Something. The sexual tension is too thick. I’m about to go find a girlfriend just to restore balance to the universe.

Agatha stared wide-eyed at Wanda, whose chest was rising and falling rapidly.

“She’s meeting all four kids in December, Ags. How long are you going to wait?” Wanda prodded when Agatha failed to respond.

Agatha returned to her work. “I know that. It’s just…this wasn’t part of the plan, and you know it.”

“Then tell her.”

Agatha shook her head. “I’m in too deep now. I have to see it through.”

Wanda bit her lip. “I hope she forgives you.”

“Me too.”

 

They had ordered in and watched movies, pointing out discrepancies in an old dinosaur thriller. “Why are all long-necked dinosaurs automatically labeled as a brontosaurus?” Rio mused.

“Because anything else would require research or hiring a consultant,” Agatha answered, glancing over at her. “Dr. Malcolm moonlights as a consultant. He makes good money at it.” 

Rio turned her head and looked back at her. “Speaking of Ian, what do the kids call him? Do they know he’s Ralph’s boyfriend? Will they know I’m your girlfriend?”

Agatha froze. “Girlfriend?” There was that word again, and this time from the source. Agatha had started holding Rio’s hand in public, as long as she didn’t have that ‘being watched’ feeling. They did act like a couple, but disagreed on seventy-five different fronts. There was so much that Agatha was avoiding, like the reality of the situation. 

Rio turned to face Agatha fully, taking both of her hands in hers. Her eyes searched Agatha’s, her face full of panic when she realized Agatha was overthinking. “I mean, I consider you to be my girlfriend, but if you don’t, then…”

Rio’s words were cut off when Agatha surged forward and captured Rio’s lips with hers, cupping her cheek. “If you will have me, complications, different theories, and all, then yes.”

Rio smiled softly and pressed her forehead to Agatha’s. “Yes. I’ll take it all.” 

“The kids call him Uncle Ian, and while they don’t know, I think they suspect it. They’re smart kids, and they know about all types of families. I wouldn’t explicitly tell them, and they can call you whatever you want.”
