Wanda had bid them goodnight with a knowing smirk and walked off toward her door while they entered Agatha’s apartment. Rio closed the door and locked it behind her while Agatha kicked off her shoes and took off her jacket. “I can’t believe you’re still so adamant about the feather theory!”

Rio scoffed, taking off her own shoes and jacket. “Why can’t you just admit that not only proof, but that you’re wrong about something?”

Agatha put her hand on her chest and gasped, stepping closer to her girlfriend. “Excuse you! I am not wrong. While there is evidence for some species, the larger species absolutely did not have down feathers like some baby penguin!”

Rio grasped Agatha’s hips and pulled her flush against her. “You don’t know that, you weren’t there!”

Every time she woke, startled by the unfamiliar presence, she was automatically calmed by the sight of the other woman. 🦕🦖🦕

Time seemed to speed up as they hurtled toward the end of the semester. Each night was spent in the comfort of the other’s arms as their conversations grew deeper and they tiptoed around certain feelings. Agatha had never felt this pull towards another person. While she had been in love with Ellie when they were young, it had never been like this, and it terrified her. That was not part of the plan. 

It was December, and Agatha sat in her office, staring out the window at the lightly falling snow, when Wanda marched in, slammed the door shut, and flopped into a chair on the other side of the desk. 

“I have it. It’s secured.”

Agatha turned to face the redhead and blinked. “That’s amazing.”

“Pietro and Ian are on their way to retrieve it and take it back home.”

“Did you…”

“Yes. You need to go…”

“I will.” Agatha stayed seated, though she knew she needed to go then. “Thank you, I know this hasn’t been an ideal situation.”

Wanda laughed harshly. “Not at all. I miss my boys, Ags.”

“I know, Angel. I miss everyone, too—especially Nicky and Lilith. It’s hard to be away from family, especially your kids, for such long periods of time. I hated doing it with you and Pietro when you were young, too. It made me feel like such a horrible pretend mom.”

“We never viewed it as pretend, Aggie. You were, and are, an amazing mom. Now, go, so we can put an end to this thing and you can grovel for forgiveness.”

Three days later, Agatha had suitcases and boxes surrounding her. Everything except the essentials were already neatly packed and ready to go. She sat on the couch, grading final papers, with Ralph on speaker phone. 

“We have the banquet in two days. I know she’s spending the winter break with us, but why do I feel like this? Like being across the country from her will destroy me?”

“You know why, Sweetie.”

“You and Ian do just fine.”

“Yeah, but you’re a lesbian. You people are clingy.”

Agatha ran a hand through her hair and thought about it. She and Rio were usually always together. “Okay, you’ve got me there.”

“You’re going to need the distance when the other shoe drops,” Ralph said hesitantly.

“You’re not wrong.”

“By the way, Ellie will be at the banquet. Oxford granted them a research grant for a project they’re working on, and they’re announcing it there. Ian said you might not want Rio to see it.”

“Why does the love of your life have to partner with Satan’s mistress?” Agatha groaned.

“First, darling, you’re the love of my life, we just happen to be strictly platonic. Secondly, I thought you were Satan?”

“Satan could never.”

Ralph chuckled, “I love you.”

“I love you, too.” Agatha smiled. She had once vowed to love Ralph, and only Ralph, and she had meant that. Love burned her once, plus, it was complicated. Now, she was rethinking it.

“Ralph?”

“Agatha?”

“You’ll always love me, right? Even if everything goes down in flames?”

“‘Til death do us part.”

 

🦕🦖🦕

 

End of the Semester Banquet

December 11, 2015

 

Agatha lightly trailed her fingers over Rio’s exposed collarbones, watching their reflections in the full-length mirror in front of them. “We make one sexy couple. I say we skip the dinner and fly back tonight. We can stop in New York and have a few days to ourselves before we head back to LA.” She bent down and kissed her shoulder.

Rio laughed and turned to kiss Agatha properly. “What has gotten into you? You love to hear people praise you. You're being recognized for the new species you discovered in South Africa. You know? The dig you stole from me?”

“I didn’t steal it! You got sick!” Agatha defended.

“I still don’t know what happened. That was the first time in my entire life that I got sick, and it was gone in less than a day. Probably the stress of being around you.” She smirked and kissed Agatha again, careful not to smudge their lipstick. “Speaking of, I have been feeling increasingly off again over the past few months.”

“Pregnant?” Agatha asked with a raised eyebrow.

“That would be a scientific breakthrough.” Rio turned back to face the mirror to check her hair one more time, mentally calculating her chances of actually conceiving. No, Agatha would have to be an extremely powerful witch, sorcerer, or cosmic being for that. There was a specific day. It was a whole process.

“Why are you so insistent on skipping it?” Rio pushed.

Agatha sighed and sat on the edge of her bed. “A certain blonde will be there to present some type of breakthrough. I don’t know exactly what it is. Ian will be there too, Ralph warned me.”

Rio shrugged. “We’ll face her together, Ian will be a buffer. You have an award to receive, and we look amazing together. Let’s make people jealous.

 

Agatha’s heart skipped a beat, and her stomach flipped simultaneously. Here was Rio, supporting her in everything, while Agatha was holding back. The guilt was gnawing at Agatha. She couldn’t possibly tell Rio how she felt without confessing everything, and how would Rio react to that?

Not well. Agatha was positive she wouldn’t survive it. Not to mention how betrayed Rio would feel. 

Then there was Ellie Fucking Sattler. Ellie’s mere presence was a threat, and Agatha would have to keep her and Rio separated at all costs. Ellie could absolutely ruin everything, and would, given the chance. Ian was trustworthy. Ellie was as trustworthy as a starving raptor. If Agatha could take it all back and be honest with Rio from the start, she would at least consider it. She would probably still do it her way, but she would at least consider honesty.



The banquet was going well until Agatha felt the familiar skin-crawling sensation. Nothing intimidated Agatha, so to speak. However, knowing that Ellie could destroy what she was building with Rio didn’t exactly sit well with her. She sat at a table with Rio, Wanda, and several other faculty members and research assistants. She caught Wanda’s glance and cut her eyes slightly upwards. Wanda nodded subtly and spoke loudly. “Ellie Sattler, so lovely to see you again.” Everyone turned and engaged her in conversation while Agatha and Rio quickly slipped away and toward Ian Malcolm.

“Ian, you were supposed to be protecting me,” Agatha joked as she drew up beside him.

“I wanted to watch the inevitable fist fight. I think Rio here could take her.” Ian winked.

Rio rolled her eyes and put an arm around Agatha’s waist. “Thankfully, Wanda provided a distraction.”

“Oh, it’s only temporary, I’m afraid.” Ian took a drink and looked at Agatha, communicating silently. Her jaw tightened, and she gave a slight nod.

 

Ellie found them a few minutes later. “Aggie, I am so proud of you.” She greeted, walking up to the couple of scientists. 

“Ellie.” Agatha greeted curtly.

“Ah, Rio, I see you and Agatha have become close after she lied to you all those years ago.”

Agatha winced, though Rio took it in stride. “A miscommunication. We’re on the same page now, personally, at least.” 

Ellie raised an eyebrow, intrigued. “Oh? Aggie, are you officially dating? Does Mommy Dearest know?”

Agatha turned to Rio and put a hand on her bicep, “Honey, will you excuse me for a moment? I need to speak to Dr. Sattler.”

Hurt quickly flashed across Rio’s eyes before she nodded. “Of course. We could both use another drink as it is.”

Agatha’s stomach clenched as Rio walked away. She knew she shouldn’t have asked her to leave. That she could trust Rio, yet this was a side of her that Rio didn’t know about. 

Ellie scoffed. “If I didn’t know you better, I’d say you were in love.”

Agatha turned back to the offending presence with a look meant to kill. 

“Holy shit. You are.” Ellie bit the inside of her cheek. “That’s rich. I bet she doesn’t know you used her, or that you’re most likely using her now.”

“What the fuck do you want, Ellie?” Agatha spat.

“So Evanora doesn’t know you’re breaking the contract? Because if you’re officially dating, I’m sure you’re breaking all those little loopholes. Is that what our little exchange in London was for? That’s why you needed an appointment with Kale?”

Agatha began to tap her foot impatiently.

Ellie was bursting at the seams with excitement. “Oh, this is just thrilling, Aggie.”

“Stop calling me that.”

“Oh, you don’t get to make demands right now. The way I see it is if Evanora finds out about your little tryst, well, you know the consequences. If Rio finds out what you’ve done and the other people at play, well, she’ll never forgive you.” Ellie wore a cruel smile and shook her head slowly. “I told you when you broke my heart. You belong to me.”

Agatha’s head whipped around to meet Ellie’s mocking gaze. “You broke up with me, if I recall correctly. You refused to play by the rules. You don’t understand what I’m capable of now, Ellie. I may have been powerless a decade ago, but I’m not now. Leave Rio out of this and go have a rebound relationship like a normal person. Leave my family out of this.” Agatha turned to walk back to her seat, only to see Rio returning with drinks. “Baby, just in time. I was just telling Ellie that we will sadly miss her announcement since we are leaving after my award.”

Rio’s jaw tightened, and she turned to Ellie. “Ah, yes, so sorry, I’m sure we’ll see it in the association newsletter.”

Agatha wrapped her arm around Rio’s waist and quickly led her back to their table. 

“Ags, what was that about?”

Agatha pulled Rio’s chair out for her before taking her own seat. “Oh, you know, just bitter exes. Now, you’re okay if I don’t mention you in my speech, right?”

Rio tilted her head with a smile. “Nice try, Harkness. I wasn’t there. I’ve helped with some of your analysis over the past few months about the species, but I wasn’t there in the initial discovery. Now, what’s up with Ellie?”

Wanda took her seat and looked over at them, having heard part of the conversation. “I’m so sorry, Vision called, boys are sad it’s not tomorrow. Ellie’s problem is that she’s a bitch who can’t move on.” 

“What Wanda said.” Agatha echoed.

The lights dimmed as the presentation started. Rio sighed exasperatedly. “This isn’t over, Harkness.”

“I know, baby. On the plane tomorrow.”

 

The acknowledgements and presentations went quickly. Agatha accepted her award for Paleontological Progress and Discovery, and at the end of her speech, even looked at Rio and added, “I’d also like to thank Dr. Vidal, who, despite our differences, has been indispensable during the data collection process of this discovery. I truly couldn’t have finished this without her.” 

She returned to the table as the lights brightened, several people moving tables and replicas to the stage. Agatha glanced at Wanda and Rio. “Let’s go, early start tomorrow.”

Rio stood and embraced Agatha. “Thank you. You didn’t have to do that.”

“I wanted to.”

Wanda made a sound of disgust and walked past them. “Let’s go.”

 

The video started when they were close to the door, and Ian’s voice boomed over the speakers.

“Imagine if we could bring back the dinosaurs from fossilized DNA.”

Rio froze, Agatha tried to pull her forward, and Wanda looked at Agatha uneasily. 

 

Rio kept listening as her research was quoted back to her. The research she had marked as ‘too dangerous’. The research she had never shown anyone. The research she kept on a very secure flash drive. She glanced at Agatha, “That’s my research.”

 

Agatha had a choice to make. She either confessed right then or she acted innocent. “What?”

Rio nodded. “I just don’t know how they got it.” 

“Let’s head back before we have to listen to Ellie and go over ideas there.”

Rio tilted her head. “Why are you so twitchy?”

The video stopped, and Ellie’s voice took over. “Since late 2009, Dr. Malcolm and I have been working tirelessly on Jurassic Park. We are currently growing and raising multiple species of real dinosaurs on the island thanks to multiple investors.”

Agatha’s head snapped up, and murmurs and gasps went through the ballroom. What did she mean by multiple investors? Real dinosaurs? She felt the anxiety radiating from Wanda and the rage from Rio. 

Wanda’s phone vibrated in her hand, and she stepped away to take the call.

Rio gripped Agatha’s hand, shaking her head. “No, it’s too dangerous.”

Ellie looked at the back of the room, seemingly right at Agatha and Rio.

“Originally deemed ‘too dangerous’ by one of the original researchers on the project, we have put extra security measures into place and have employed animal behavioralists. The island is secluded, and coordinates are currently kept hidden so that we may do our work in private.”

Agatha shook her head. “Ian spends most of his time teaching at Yale or in LA with Ralph. Ralph certainly doesn’t know about this. I didn’t know about this.”

 

Ellie’s professional smile turned into a wide grin as she seemed focused on the couple near the exit. “The park will be ready for visitors in 2020. I would personally like to thank Dr. Agatha Harkness for her incomparable contribution to the research that went into this research. If not for her, Jurassic Park would be nothing but a dream.”

 

Rio turned and looked at Agatha, mouth agape, and walked out of the ballroom.

Agatha followed behind her.

“Rio, it’s not what it looks like.”

“It’s exactly what it looks like, Agatha!” Rio turned around once they were outside of the building, standing on the sidewalk in the snow. “London! God. I’ve been so blind! You stole my research after we fucked the first time. I can’t believe I trusted you or asked you to stay. Then you acted so nice when I got sick. You probably did that too!”

“That’s not, exactly…”

“No! You don’t get to defend yourself! You had a reputation for stealing artifacts from sites, for dealing in secrets, working with the black market, and I ignored that because I had some stupid crush on you! And here I am in love with you!” Rio’s hands immediately flew up to cover her mouth.

Agatha’s heart deflated. This is not how she wanted to hear those three words.

“You’re in love with me?” Agatha’s voice was soft, gentle, afraid she would startle Rio.

Rio wiped tears from her cheeks that she didn’t even know had fallen. “Yes, Agatha. I have been. For months. But it doesn’t matter. You betrayed me. You could have told me at any point in the past six years, or hell, the past eight months! What else have you been keeping from me?”

Agatha’s eyes dropped to the snow. So much. She raised her eyes again to meet Rio’s. “Please, Rio, I can explain.” 

“I don’t want your half-truths and perfectly curated explanations.”

They both turned when Wanda came running out of the building. “Aggie, check your phone. The kids. We have to go.”

Agatha pulled out her phone to a text from Evanora. There were photos and videos of her and Rio being rather close during the banquet, around campus, kissing in the lab, and the end of her speech. Then the actual text.

 

Evil Old Hag

At least someone is loyal to this family.

You know the consequences.

 

Agatha looked back up at Rio. “We have to fix this, but I have to protect my family and kids.”

“Can you tell the whole truth?”

Agatha chewed on her lip.

“Goodbye, Agatha.” She turned and stormed away.

“I love you.” It was quiet, watery. Agatha hadn’t realized she said it. If the circumstances were different, if it wasn’t her kids, she would have run after Rio. She would have gone down swinging. 

 

But they weren’t different, and it was her kids.

 

Rio’s steps faltered, her tears falling harder, as she swallowed a sob and kept walking.

 

Wanda put a hand on Agatha’s shoulder. “We’ll fix it, but right now, Ian has already taken off to intercept your mother, Pietro too. Ralph started emergency treatments on the kids and called Jen. We have to go. I froze everyone in the ballroom so we can portal, might as well use the big magick now.”

Agatha nodded. “I’ve portaled all of our belongings, the last of the vials have been sent to Jen, and you and I have what we need.”

Wanda took Agatha’s hand. “How does it feel to be at full power?”

“Awful.”

With a flash of purple and scarlet, they were gone.

       

🦕🦖🦕

 

Rio entered her apartment and flipped on the light, walking through the barren home. All of her boxes had already been sent back to New York. A single duffel and her backpack sat in her bedroom. Tears still occasionally rolled down her cheeks, though she wasn’t sure how. She walked across the hall and tried the knob. The door swung open easily. Rio stepped in, noticing everything was gone. The clothes from just a few hours before, makeup that had been on the bathroom counter, shampoo bottles from the shower, even the toothbrushes. 

“Wanda must have called someone.” She said out loud. She looked over at the bed where they had made love, she guessed, since they had finally said the words. Though it was too late for that. Her brows furrowed when she saw a neatly folded shirt on top of Agatha’s pillow, with a note. 

Rio cautiously walked over and picked up the note. For Rio was scrawled across it. She unfolded it and, despite her best efforts, smiled. 

I wore this stupid shirt so it would smell like me. We’re so gay.

It was an oversized heathered gray shirt that said BIRDS in colorful letters with various bird silhouettes around the letters. Then there were various dinosaurs everywhere. Rio had bought it for Agatha as a joke, and Agatha wore it on occasion, joking that she was going to give it back. 

Rio clutched the shirt to her chest and made the short trip back to her apartment, where a diabolical blonde sat on the couch.

“Your door was open.”

“Haven’t you done enough damage tonight? I’m surprised you aren’t chasing after her.” Rio sneered.

“Too early. Besides, all she does is lie and break hearts. What were you hoping would happen, Rio? She’s never going to leave her family, and that contract is binding unless her mother dies.”

“She has a plan,” Rio responded, voice shaky.

“Sure, did she tell you this plan?”

“No.”

Ellie laughed mockingly. “Do you want to know the truth?”

“Give it to me, Ellie.” She doubted Ellie knew the whole truth either, but so far, Ellie had been mostly honest.

“She’s a witch.”

 

Chapter End Notes

Fucking Agatha.

Rio, just hear her out.

Evanora sucks.

Chapter 3: The Magick From My Hand

Chapter Summary

The fallout. Rio learns some of the truth. Agatha and Wanda handle some family business, and Agatha tries to explain herself.

Chapter Notes

I had to split this chapter into two; more dino content coming next chapter!

Eternity is played by Mila Kunis.
I absolutely use Ian and Malcolm interchangeably for him. He has two first names for a name.

Comments are nice and motivate me and stuff...

See the end of the chapter for more notes

“A witch?” Rio regarded Ellie skeptically, “I am a woman of science.”

“So am I!” Ellie insisted. “But I’ve seen it! She uses herbs and incantations to make things happen!” 

Rio shook her head, exasperated. “She practiced Wicca and had an obsession with the Craft when she was younger. Now she enjoys TikTok witchery,” she scoffed. Glancing at her phone when it vibrated.

Agatha

Whenever you need the jet, one is there for you.

 

The number came immediately after. Rio considered declining the offer; her magick wasn’t strong enough to portal and Agatha had stranded her. She refused to respond, deciding to schedule her flight as soon as she got rid of Ellie.

“It’s more than that. Runes, hex bags, wards.” Ellie continued

Rio kept her face neutral. That was slightly more advanced for your run-of-the-mill middle-aged woman who never got over her obsession with the Owens sisters. Then again, Agatha was anything but average or run-of-the-mill.

“Don’t you want to know what was so important that she stole research?” Ellie asked.

“No.” Rio answered with finality. “I want to hear it from her. If I decide to ever speak to her again.” Rio glanced around the apartment, memories of Agatha overtaking her. 

“Now, please leave. I have a flight to catch.”

Ellie left with a sigh. “I’m just trying to save you the years of pain I went through.”

Rio scoffed, “Too late.”

Once the door was closed behind her, she dialed the number for the private jet service. 

She had to put Oxford behind her, and quickly. She needed the two people on Earth whom she had known the longest. She needed to break down in the comfort of her own home, call out to her sisters to see what was wrong with her, and most importantly—she wanted to wrap herself in the stupid shirt Agatha had left, eat ice cream, and cry. 

She really had become human.  

 

🦕🦖🦕

Los Angeles, California

Roughly 2 p.m. (time zones)

Harkness Mansion

 

The two witches stepped out of the portal into the enchanted basement of the sprawling mansion, greeted by a spitfire of an old woman. 

“I’ve seen slugs move faster.” Lilia Calderu assessed in a judgmental but loving tone from where she sat in the corner. “How’d Rio take the news?”

Agatha’s expression was remorseful before rage at the situation took over. She stormed forward, still wearing the dress from the banquet, the runes etched into her skin flaring violet. She flitted between different shelves, disappearing into the dark shadows of the basement, gathering herbs, ancient bowls, and various vials. 

Wanda followed shortly behind, sidling up next to Lilia. “Not well. What happened here?”

“It was maybe an hour ago?” Lilia recounted. “The boys were in the playroom when they collapsed. The school called Ralph at work.” Lilia’s words were watery. “Nicholas and Lilith had seizures during recess and were unresponsive. All four children are at Cedars now. Kale came immediately.” Lilia stood next to Wanda, watching Agatha lay out various ingredients on the alchemist's table. 

Wanda arched an eyebrow. “Jen portaled? She hates portaling.”

“The things we do in emergencies,” Agatha mumbled, furiously grinding herbs together with a worn mortar and pestle.

“Yes, well.” Lilia gestured around them. “Jen made Alice stay back. Told her it was an important client in Seattle. She still doesn’t know.” Lilia watched the runes on Agatha’s skin burn brighter through her clothing. “How did you keep those hidden for so long?”

“Between Wanda and me, the place was heavily warded. Combined with the glamour I was using—meds from Jen to restore my own magick—it just came together. Rio’s own power diminishing.” Agatha grimaced, mumbling in guilt. She flicked her fingers—the bowl bursting into flames while she chanted in Latin. “Alright, Superstar, summon it.”  

Wanda closed her eyes and reached her hand out. Lilia couldn’t suppress a smile as she watched the book floating from the shadows. These two women she helped raise, having come to this point in their magickal journeys.

“You recovered it?” Lilia’s voice was threaded with pride.

“In South Africa.” Wanda answered, sending the book to the table. “Oxford laid claim before Agatha could.”

“Wanda here,” Agatha flashed a smile at the younger witch. “Did an amazing job of locating and rescuing the feisty thing. Couldn’t have done it better myself.” Her smile turned into a smirk. “Even seduced a pretty girl in the meantime.”

Agatha took the book, letting it fall open to the page it deemed necessary. She quickly absorbed the words before placing the cursed book in a pocket dimension for safekeeping. 

“We found a way to activate her powers as the Scarlet Witch and bring mine to their full potential, essentially sharing the powers of the Darkhold. 

Granted,” she strained the liquid. “We won’t be fully juiced up until the ritual is performed.” She explained, pouring the potion into eight separate vials, packed them into a bag, and led the way out of the basement.

“Lilia, go to Rio when she lands. She needs you.” She glanced nervously over her shoulder, wishing she could be the one to comfort the woman. 

“Remember, she doesn’t know that we’re connected. When she does connect the dots, make sure she knows that you weren’t helping me with this and that you never betrayed her.” Agatha’s lip trembled as the adrenaline wavered.

“I can’t hurt her anymore.” Her stilettos tapped harshly with each step as she crossed the ground floor to the stairs.

“She told you, didn’t she?” Lilia called after her softly.

Agatha nodded. “After she found out that I gave her research to Ellie, it just came out.”

She took a sharp breath. “I said it back, but it was too late. She probably doesn’t believe me.” She hurried up the stairs, somehow graceful in the otherwise inconvenient footwear.

“I can’t just randomly show up when she hasn’t told me yet.” Lilia finally responded to Agatha’s earlier order.

Wanda shrugged behind them, “She’ll call. I can even tell you exactly when, if you want.” She gloated.

“You’re going to be insufferable, little witch.” Lilia scoffed.

“You would be too, if you were a cosmic being,” Wanda smirked.

“That’s a misuse of your powers, Red.” Agatha scolded.

 

They could have easily snapped their fingers to change clothes, but they knew they needed to settle their nerves first—before chaos broke out at one of the world’s most prestigious hospitals. 

Agatha looked at her phone for the seventeenth time. She let out a shaky breath and ran a hand through her hair. Falling for Rio hadn’t been the plan—or had it? She sat on the edge of the bed and stared at the infernal device, thinking back on words Lilia had said to her over the years, prophesies, readings, always the same.

She practically flew out of her room. Now dressed casually in sneakers, she jogged down the stairs, clutching her bag. “Lilia!” She shouted for the ancient woman. “Where are you, you old bat?” She skidded to a halt in front of Wanda and Lilia, waiting at the door. 

“Yes, dear?” Lilia glanced up, eyes gleaming with that pretentious all-knowing look.

“What did you mean when you said my soul was marked for death?”

The left corner of Lilia’s lips twitched. “Well, the contract spells that out quite clearly. You'd better go, the children…”

“No! It had a double meaning, didn’t it? I’m marked for her.”

Lilia hummed, opening the door with a flick of her wrist. “Go, save the day. We’ll worry about mending hearts later.”

Agatha shot her a look as Wanda dragged her to the car, the door closing behind them.

 

All of those nights whispering ‘mine’ into Rio’s hair, asking Rio who she belonged to, had been a lie. A delusion. Rio didn’t belong to Agatha any more than animals belong to people. 

No, she was the one who belonged to Rio, and Jen hadn’t bothered picking up on that particular mark. She switched lanes and cut someone off, the sound of a horn blaring at her. As much as she craved control in all situations, was that really a bad thing? Did she really hate the idea of belonging to her lover?

That was something to be unpacked when lives were not on the line.  

Agatha sped toward the hospital, knuckles white on the steering wheel. “I know I’m running on adrenaline, but I thought I’d be more panicky. Still, I’m worried about the kids.”

Wanda put a comforting hand on Agatha’s shoulder. “You’ve always been good in a crisis,” she reassured. “Beyond that, it’s anti-climactic. We’re almost fully powered. We have antidotes that are powered by Death herself. Kale is working her magick. We won.” 

Wanda looked at her phone when it buzzed. “Lilia just got the call from Rio. She’s headed to New York. Ian and Pietro intercepted Evanora, so she’s detained in Munich. We’re in, we do witchy things, and we’re done.” Wanda reached over and squeezed Agatha’s hand. “And you can grovel for your woman to forgive you. At least you know she can’t kill you…herself.”

They breezed through the hospital easily, recognized by most of the staff, as they journeyed toward the private surgical suite their family had taken over. A few sympathetic glances thrown their way. 

“How are they?” Agatha asked, stepping into the surgical theater and over to where the kids lay on hospital beds in a circle, their heads centered, monitors and IVs attached.

Wanda did the same.

Ralph joined the two witches in the center of the circle. “They’re currently comatose, but not fully. Kind of like a Sleeping Beauty state.”

Agatha looked to the corner where Vision sat frozen in a chair, a slight improvement from normal if she said so herself. 

Wanda followed her stare and quickly turned to Jennifer. “What did you do to my husband?”

Jen, who had taken Agatha’s bag and begun mixing the vials with her own concoctions, looked up at Wanda. “I don’t trust him. He has the personality of dry paint.” 

“I told you,” Agatha responded dryly, hands rubbing soothing circles on a shoulder of each of her offspring. “He’s hiding something. No one is that robotic.”

“By the way, when my wife finds out about this. You’re paying for the divorce, AND helping me win her back.” Jen glared while filling eight syringes.

“Please, when she finds out how much I’ve been paying you, she’ll forget about it.” Agatha flipped her hair behind her shoulder, more nervous tick than anything.

“Money doesn’t buy trust, Agatha.” Jen looked straight at Agatha, willing her to understand how serious of a betrayal this was. 

“Moving on, we’re going to inject each of you with this, and Pietro, when he gets here. Right now, you need to conjure the original contract and do your spellwork.” Jen held up a syringe before setting it down. “Once the spell is said, the antidote is activated and the contract breaks,” she rushed. “Marks,” she handed two antidotes to Ralph. “Dissolve from your very souls, new ones replace them; etched into your souls and bones this time.”

“This room is already masked—runed too. Anyone who comes in or looks inside, it looks like we’re doing a routine appendectomy.” Ralph added. “Just in case.”

Jen nodded. “With this part done, you’re free to move on to the last step.”

 

Agatha sucked in a breath at the thought of it. Her jaw clenched, lips forming a thin line as she rolled them together. “I know, and it’s already in motion. It’s just going to take some time.” 

“Which you’ll have. Now, let’s get started.” Jen moved from her and stepped toward the Maximoff twins. 

Jen and Ralph administered the glowing potion to the four sleeping children first, monitoring them as it spread under their skin. They mapped the veins as the potion worked its way into each child’s system, watching closely for any reaction. 

“Now, you three,” Jen instructed, handing them out. “And the fourth for Pietro.”

“Cheers,” Ralph said sarcastically before injecting his own and then Agatha’s.

“Alright, Ms.Cosmic Being, this might sting.” Jen took Wanda’s arm, finding a vein with ease. Wanda winced as the formula burned her veins, flexing her hand to help the circulation, turning to Agatha. 

 

Agatha stepped to a cleared area of the room, gesturing to Wanda with a flourish of her hands. “Let’s see what you’ve got, kid.”

They chanted together, and with a bright flash of blue, a parchment appeared.

“Is that it?” Jen asked, surprised by the ancient-looking scroll.

“Yes.” The other three conscious adults answered simultaneously.

“Mother has a flair for the dramatic.” Agatha quipped.

“All this for a bigoted narcissist’s need for control.” Jen grumbled.

“Control issues run in the family.” Agatha muttered before beginning to recite the contract-breaking spell, the runes on her skin flaring again. Wanda joined in, hands mimicking Agatha’s motions. 

 

The parchment began to glow, a slight wind starting to blow around them. The incantation penetrating the room and agitating the parchment. Purple magick burst forth from Agatha’s hand and directly into the contract; scarlet from Wanda joined and intertwined with the other strands.

Words began to glow on the paper, as if removing themselves from existence one by one.

“Agatha Harkness will thus be married to Ralph Bohner…”

“Agatha Harkness will bear the children…”

“Agatha Harkness will not know the sexual touch of another woman…”

Agatha watched that one go, smirking. Though it was one of many she’d have to explain to Rio about this whole ordeal. 

“It’s working!” Jen raised her voice to be heard over the growing noise.

“William Maximoff,” the two chanting witches shouted out.

Ralph rushed to Billy’s small body and watched as his entire body was bathed in blue light. His lips parted in surprise as something black rose out of his body and vanished.

Waves of scarlet and purple started wrapping around him, his body stilling. 

Jen hurried over and ran glowing pink hands over him. “Marks are dissolved, new marks in place. He’s no longer marked and will wake soon.” She called to the powerful forces wielding dangerous magick. 

Agatha focused on the paper floating in between them, noticing how it had ripped slightly when Billy was released. She was ready to call Tommy’s name when she felt her veins run cold. They were running out of time. “Let’s kick this into gear, kid. Something wicked this way comes, and it’s not me.” 

“Thomas Maximoff, Nicholas Scratch Harkness, Lilith Harkness, Ralph Bohner.” They rattled off in unison, setting off a shockwave through the room. 

Ralph passed out, convulsing on the floor as the spell worked through him, tendrils of magick enveloping him. Jen regarded him and scoffed in disgust. “Lightweight,” she turned back to the main event. “Wait until Ralph recovers. I can step in while the two of you switch out.” She shouted over the growing chaos. 

 

With those four names called, the curse fought back, mist rose around them. The wind picked up, violet and scarlet magick swirling throughout the room—cutting through like lightning. Words flying off the paper: The consequences, the addenda about the Maximoffs, the details about Agatha’s own expiration date. 

She cackled maniacally, the rush of power overtaking her as she felt the literal bonds that had held her captive for so long began to tear and stretch against her soul. Her eyes darted to Wanda, who wore a devilish grin as the final locks on her own powers started to break.

 

Outside, a nurse walked past, peering through the window. She tilted her head and shrugged at the unusual number of people in the large theater for a routine appendectomy.

 

Ralph took a sharp breath and scrambled back to his feet. “Well, that reminded me of college. Not in a bad way. I feel lighter, a whole new man. I could…”

“Shut the fuck up, Ralph, and keep an eye out.” Agatha turned her attention back to Wanda, the parchment almost completely destroyed.

 “You’re up, Angel.”

“You first!” Wanda shouted back.

 

The doors burst open, Pietro speeding through in a blur. “She’s coming,” he rasped out before collapsing in front of Ralph, blood pooling beneath him.

Wanda screamed, trying to keep her attention on the spellwork. Jen slammed the doors shut and strengthened the wards with a snap, grabbing the final syringe and scrambling over to Pietro to jab it into the first vein she could find. “Agatha, now!” 

 

“Ralph! Save our son!” Agatha shouted before turning back, both hands toward the tattered object that had given her nothing but grief. Heartbreak. Sorrow. 

“Pietro Maximoff! Wanda Maximoff!” 

“No!” Wanda yelled before convulsing and falling to her knees. 

A bolt of pink magick replaced Wanda’s, causing Agatha to smirk. “You’re no Scarlet Witch, but I guess you’ll do in a pinch.”

“Funny, I think I told you something similar in the nineties.” Jen retorted.

 

The doors rattled as the runes on the walls began to glow and crack. Agatha eyed Wanda, willing her to recover quicker. She glanced over at where Ralph laid Pietro on a spare surgical table, cutting his shirt off, yelling at Jen to float him various tools or medications while she maintained the lock on the contract. The doors burst open with a flash of blue, and for the first time in years, Agatha was in the same room as her mother. 

Evanora stepped into the room, closing the doors behind her. She didn’t need any casualties; couldn’t afford the bad publicity. “You’ve been a naughty girl, Agatha Harkness,” her voice dropped condescendingly. “I do what’s best for you, and here you are undoing it all.” She raised a hand, ready to stop the matriarchal coup d’etat, when she was thrown into a wall by Wanda. 

 

“AGATHA HARKNESS!” Wanda roared, rising to her feet. She kept one hand focused on the blasted, overly powered piece of paper, and the other she used to pin Evanora against the wall. 

Evanora cackled. “Oh, Wanda, do you think you can do much? I took you in, gave you both a home.”

“You stole our birthright. Dropped us on your daughter as a punishment, bound us, and then tried to dictate our lives—just like you did with Agatha.” Wanda corrected. 

“To-may-to, to-mah-to,” The elder Harkness mocked.

“If I could kill you, I would.” Wanda hissed.

 

Behind them, Agatha stood, ignoring the pain of the spell and the rewriting happening within her body. She sent another powerful thread of violet magick at the contract, causing it to explode into ashes and disappear. The room quieted, the mist and the wind disappearing. Vision sat in the corner, unscathed, the four kids unharmed. Ralph and Jen working on Pietro. 

Agatha raised her hand, and with a flick, she, Wanda, and Evanora were transported to a forest clearing, no longer needing to use a portal. 

Wanda still had Evanora in her magickal grasp as Agatha circled both of them. “Well, mother.” Agatha’s voice was devoid of emotion. “It looks like I finally found a way out.”

“You always were wicked.” She hissed like the snake she was. “Disobeying your mother like this.” She regained her composure. Her usual arrogance returning. “You may have broken it. May no longer be sealed, but as long as I’m alive.” She gave Agatha a knowing smirk. “I still own you. Contract or no.” She stared at Agatha, smug as ever, letting her lips twitch upward.

Agatha stopped directly in front of the old hag, crossing one arm over her stomach and bringing one finger to her chin. “While there is still that pesky detail, your days are numbered, Evanora.” Her finger twitched, forcing Evanora to her knees.

Evanora laughed, clearly amused at Agatha’s display. “You can’t kill me, not without very hefty consequences.” 

Agatha ignored the implied threat. “You see, mother,” she spat, “Not only am I the hottest bitch in every room I walk into, I’m also the smartest. I’ve done my research. Your hold on me? Your immortality? Its shelf life just decreased drastically. You no longer have the ability to harm any member of my family.” She crowded into her mother’s space, tipping her chin with two fingers. “No more threatening me to end their lives before their designated time, no more putting them into comas. Or magick controlling diseases. Or discreetly draining their energy or life forces. They are no longer available to you.” 

The elder Harkness had narrowed her eyes, looking up at Agatha in growing fury. 

“I will find a way to regain control, and when I do—”

“You won’t,” Agatha cut her off, stepping away. “But I’ll have eyes on you, just in case.” She looked back at the cause of her misery with cruel glee. “I have also set up separate accounts that you can’t access, so don’t even think about punishing me financially, mother.”

“You don’t need my money, you brat.”

“No, but I enjoy taking it and donating it to causes you hate, in your name. Or spending it on sex toys.” Agatha goaded.

“You’re heathens. The both of you!” She shouted out in frustration.

“Yes, well. We enjoy it.” Her eyes gleaming, watching her mother in her frazzled state. “As for you, mother,” she clicked her tongue. “I’m going to send you back to Munich.” She jabbed a pointed fingernail into her mother’s chest. “Where you will not, I repeat, not, be telling anyone what happened.” Agatha’s eyes flared violet. “I will absolutely begin a smear campaign against the company and tank the worth of your shares if necessary.”

“You wouldn’t!”

“Knowing you can’t retaliate by killing someone I love?” Agatha laughed. “I absolutely will.” 

Evanora opened her mouth to add something, but Agatha snapped her back to Munich before she could respond.

 

Agatha finally turned to Wanda with a satisfied smile that didn’t quite reach her eyes, every muscle still tense, and opened her arms without a word. Wanda took a few steps forward and closed the distance, melting into the embrace and letting herself finally crumble with the weight of everything. A daughter seeking a mother’s comfort.

“Seeing them like that, not knowing if we were right—” she hiccuped into Agatha’s hair, before pulling away in sudden realization. “Pietro! Aggie, we have to…” 

Agatha’s jaw clenched, a slight tick in the muscle when she stepped back and squeezed Wanda’s biceps, the sound of monitors filling their ears. The coppery smell of blood, sweat, and antiseptic lingered in the air. 

Agatha was silent, her face guarded. 

She stepped away from Wanda, striding toward the operating table. 

Ralph looked nervously toward her. 

Jennifer began chanting various healing spells in Latin. 

Wanda nearly tripped over her own feet trying to get there. 

“Where are the kids?” Agatha asked first, trying to find the right words to address the potentially dying elephant lying before them.

“Safely at home with Sharon, Vision, and Ian.” Ralph attempted to place a calming hand on Wanda’s shoulder, only for her to shrug it away. “Lilia portalled and retrieved his dumbass and got the story before returning to Rio’s.” He took Agatha’s hand and squeezed it. “Then she called Sharon over to sit with them.” 

 

Ralph stared at his eldest son, taking a step back so Wanda could take Pietro’s limp hand. “Story goes, Ian disarmed one of Evanora’s guards and shot at her, trying to injure her enough to get her back here. Pietro saw the rune etched on the side of the gun and pushed the bitch out of the way so she wouldn’t die. Reckless fucker got hit instead.” 

“Witchkiller bullets,” Agatha breathed out, trembling. It was bold of her mother to use something that could, in turn, kill her. Evanora had to know something that Agatha was not privy to.

Wanda looked up, brows furrowed together. “Pietro isn’t a witch.”

“No,” the exhaustion was clear in Jen’s voice. “But, he does have powers, and witch blood. Hence,” she waved her hand over him, gesturing.

Wanda sobbed. “Even though we…” her words trailed off as her eyes flicked up to the monitors, watching his vitals drop, as if in slow motion.

Jen briefly glanced from Ralph to Agatha and back to the young man lying on the table. The wound had been stitched, the cursed poison was spreading through his body, the dark green and black visible in his veins. 

“Just because we’re no longer bound doesn’t mean we’re immortal. Eventually, some of us will be, but not everyone.” 

Agatha brought a finger to her chin, running through her knowledge of the types of toxins that may have been used, knowing Jen had done the same. Technically, Wanda could heal him, but Agatha knew she was still learning and that could go terribly wrong. She looked up to Jen, who mouthed ‘no’, confirming Agatha’s theory. She sighed internally; even the great Jennifer Kale couldn’t fix this one. For the first time that entire evening, she feared they would lose someone.

 

“Do something!” Wanda screeched, eyes flaring crimson, hands flaring violently. “Aggie, call out to her!”

“She won’t do anything but take him,” Jen said, matter-of-factly.

Wanda’s hands flew to Pietro’s chest, but Agatha was faster. She put two fingers on the redhead’s temple and whispered, “Dormi.” Ralph caught her mid-fall.

“I’m going to kill your boyfriend for this.” Agatha gritted out. She laid a hand over his heart and called the Darkhold from its hiding place with the other. Jen’s eyes widened at the sight of it.  

 

The only way to safely save Pietro was to absorb the poison into her own body, shaving centuries off of her own life. The very life she was fighting to preserve. While she still had another step to go, she could see the fire at the end of the bottom of the stake and knew she was almost there. Now, she was about to put it all in jeopardy. 

Her own veins now coursed with the stolen magick of the original Green Witch that she had spent the better part of the past year carefully siphoning. It would negate and destroy the majority of the poison meant to kill Pietro, neutralizing the threat, but still enough left within her to be a nuisance. A bridge she would cross at a later time.

“Holy shit, you actually have it.” Jen exclaimed, her disbelief bringing Agatha out of her thoughts.

“If you breathe a word of this, I’ll cut out your tongue.”

“Got it.”

“I’m not telling a soul.” Ralph replied from the floor, cradling Wanda in his lap.

 

Once again, Agatha uttered the spell she needed, the black in Pietro’s veins receding, and instead running up her arm. She yelled out in pain, yet forced her hand to stay anchored to his chest, willing every drop of poison into her body. She gasped when she felt Lady Death’s magick flow forward and grip onto the invading toxins.

Pietro’s vitals began to stabilize once his body was free from the cursed magick, and the woman who had raised him fell to her knees. Jen rushed to her side. Ralph stayed trapped with Wanda still in his lap, an arm reaching towards the only woman he would ever love. 

Agatha’s screams echoed throughout the room, tears brimming in the corners of her eyes—hair sticking to her skin from sweat. Her body throbbed with pain, her stomach churned. She swallowed hard, breath shallow. “Jen,” she breathed. 

Jen extended her hand and called the nearest trash can to them just in time for Agatha to expel thick, black goo.

“Well, there’s the poison,” Jen announced.

Agatha sucked in deep breaths, still convulsing as her body calmed. “It’s what I could fight off; there’s still a lingering curse that is a problem for future me.”

“Yes, but the final potion that completely severs Evanora’s grip on you will be strong enough to undo that damage as well.” Jen carefully checked Agatha’s vitals while trying to reassure her. “Plus, you’re the most powerful witch that isn’t some cosmic or prophesied being.” 

“You make it sound easy,” Agatha scoffed weakly, glancing at Wanda and then up to Pietro. “Let’s just get home and take it from there.”

 

🦕🦖🦕

 

Rio had known Lilia Calderu for over four hundred years. She was one of the very few people who knew Rio’s true identity. 

Texting her and Alice from the plane, telling them to meet her at her apartment when she landed. Rio let out another shaky breath. Agatha’s private jet was nice, though she was tempted to destroy it, even if that wasn’t necessary. She blew her nose and wiped her tears. Did Agatha have more than one plane already waiting?

How long had this been planned out?

Rio shook her head, choosing to believe that Agatha really had been planning to spend the break with her. She thought about the look on Agatha’s face when Rio confessed. Agatha had looked pained as though that was the last thing she wanted to hear. Another tear rolled down her cheek at the memory. Yet, that infuriatingly beautiful woman had still said it back. 

 

Did Agatha really mean it? Rio downed yet another bottle of water, determined to remain hydrated. 

Was she really in love with her? Rio had so many questions, and the further they flew away from Oxford, the more clearly she was able to think about the past eight months. 

The time she had sworn Agatha had glowed purple. How she always wore and touched her locket, even during sex. How it had taken forever for her to allow Rio to touch or even fully see her. 

She knew it all went back to that contract, yet she wasn’t sure how, exactly.

Ellie had said that Agatha was a witch, but could she really trust Agatha’s ex? Then there was Rio’s own fading magick; needing sleep, becoming less of a cosmic being and more human than anything. Her sister, Eternity, once warned her about it. She sighed and leaned back in the seat, unable to relax. Willing the jet to fly faster.

She had barely crossed the threshold, bag half-set down, when Alice pulled her into a hug. “I’m so sorry she turned out to be a lying bitch.”

“I knew her reputation. I should have been more careful.” Rio tried to say it confidently, but the crack in her voice betrayed her.

Lilia stood a few feet behind Alice, face full of concern. “My dear, you’ve talked to both of us this whole time about her. We know what she means to you.” Lilia’s face softened into a sympathetic and motherly smile. “In your entire existence, you have never loved someone. Not like this.”

“Well, I wish I didn’t.” She broke from Alice’s arms and stormed past, dramatically flinging herself to the couch. 

Lilia raised an eyebrow while Alice tilted her head. “Very tantruming toddler of you,” Lilia added, sitting next to Rio and rubbing her back. “Even death has a heart, you know?”

“It’d be much easier if I didn’t.”

 

“That’s for damn sure. Reapers have gone missing, souls have been stolen, your magick has been drained. All because you decided to play human for a few hundred years.”

The temperature in the room had gone cold. Everything, even the city outside, was completely silent. The smell of snow, lilies, and the wine your ex-mother-in-law gifted you, hoping you’d have an allergic reaction, filled the air. Rio groaned, her eyes rolling. “Come to gloat?”

Eternity stepped out of thin air, a bright smile across her face. Dark hair falling in curls around her shoulders, long white gown sweeping across the floor. “Of course not, sister. I merely want to help.” 

Lilia scoffed, “Sure you do.”

Eternity narrowed her eyes at the Sicilian woman. “Shouldn’t you be elsewhere, Calderu?”

“Shouldn’t you be assigning people to their endless paradise, or hell, or whatever it is you meddle in?” She shot back, undeterred.

The other cosmic being looked toward Alice, “Detective, do you even know where your wife is?” 

“Seattle, with a high-priority client, why?”

Eternity chuckled. “Sure she is.” 

She looked back at Rio. “You don’t know the woman you were sleeping with, and you don’t know your friends. You really did ace the human experience.” She said sarcastically.

Rio narrowed her eyes in question. “What do you mean?”

“You’re not going to believe me if I tell you, so let me show you.” The smug immortal flicked her wrist, transporting them to another time and place. Leaving Alice and Lilia alone.

 

Alice turned to Lilia, “What was she talking about?”

Lilia sighed deeply. “Jen has been helping Agatha with something. For years.”

Alice’s eyes were the size of saucers. “What? How do you know Agatha?” Lilia’s words shocking her more than the sudden disappearance of the cosmic wonders.

Lilia sighed as she launched into the explanation.




Rio blinked at what Eternity was showing her. It was a blur, a montage really. First, they were in a hospital in New York.

“Where are we?” Rio asked, trying to pinpoint it.

“WHEN are we.” Eternity clarified. “Right after London, 2009. Pay attention.”

 

Agatha and Jen stood in an empty corridor. Agatha handed Jen a small titanium cooler with multiple vials in it. Jen looked up at her in shock and shook her head in disbelief. “How did you get stolen souls?”

Agatha looked away. “Unethically,” she answered, the tension obvious in her voice.

“Fuck, Agatha. Ellie is involved, isn’t she? She’s the only person who would go to these lengths for you.” She shrugged, rethinking it. “Well, Ralph and Wanda, but Ralph wouldn’t know what to do with a reaper, and Wanda is only 20 with analog magick.”

Something like shame crossed Agatha’s face as she continued to avoid looking at Jen. “I will do anything to save the lives of my family, Jen. Can you help or not?”

“I can. We ran all the tests, and we can get rid of the current curse and then start on the unbinding. I also know how you can get the magick we need to power you up and create the mixture we need.”

Agatha’s head snapped back to look straight at the doctor in front of her. “I can’t absorb her powers; it will kill me.”

“That’s why it’s imperative to go on the dig. Other than that dusty old book. Isn’t that where your mother hid that rusty old pendant?” 

Agatha nodded.

“We’re going to use it as a middleman, a type of conduit, if you will. You’re also going to use your more…traditional, succubus powers.” 

The scene faded away, surrounding them in darkness—the lightest glow around them. Rio’s mind was racing, thoughts and Agatha’s words floating around her brain, tears forming behind her eyes as she looked at her sister. “What? She…she…” the words wouldn’t come, falling to her knees, her breathing heavy. Agatha had used her. More than once. She had been draining her of her powers, this entire time, through sex. That’s why she couldn’t touch her. That’s why she didn’t want Rio to see her naked. Agatha Harkness never cared. 

 

Rio looked up at Eternity, tears now falling down her cheeks, she begged, “Please, take me home. You know I can’t do it myself. I have to know if Alice knew.”

“There’s more.” Eternity deadpanned.

They were at the faculty apartments at Oxford now, the day before she had arrived. She watched as Agatha and Wanda entered her apartment. “What are they doing?”

“Just watch.”

 

“Aggie, are you sure this is going to work?” Wanda looked at her skeptically as she started drawing runes in various places and then covering them up, while Agatha followed suit and hid hex bags as well.

“Of course. The campus is  warded, and I’ll be glamoured so my runes won’t show.”

“I knew I saw Agatha glow! She’s runed!” Rio exclaimed through tears.

Eternity hummed, freezing the scene and waving a hand to show Rio the runes everywhere. Rio gasped at the sheer amount of runes that covered her apartment and the runes on Agatha.

“Why didn’t I notice or see any of these?”

 

“Agatha is very skilled in analog magick and runework. She made sure you didn’t see them.” Eternity looked at her sister with fake pity. “She also knew that after hundreds of years of spending more and more time as a human, and then living full-time as one, you were losing your connections and Sight.” She hummed unsympathetically. “You had your magick, but it was less likely to kill her, especially with her protections, locket, and the fact that you’re less a cosmic entity and more a demigod at this point.”

Rio nodded, thinking back on the memories, tears gathering on her lashes, red blotches taking over her face once again. The too-familiar sobs began to rise in her throat and she fought to swallow them down. This entire time, she thought it was real. She had fallen in love with this insufferable scientist, and the whole time, Agatha was faking it.

“What about Wanda?” Rio asked meekly.

Rio, wrapped in grief, missed the slow grin spread across Eternity’s face. “You were so caught up in your work and Agatha that you didn’t notice the power emanating from the Darkhold.”

“The book of the damned is at Oxford?” Rio swiped at the tears flowing down her cheeks.

“Was. Wanda stole it, and now she and Agatha control it.”

“No. Agatha must have a good reason for doing this.” Rio argued back.

 

The scene around them dissolved, Rio’s living room reappearing. The air now smelled like rage, protection, and betrayal. Alice now curled into a ball on the couch, sobbing. Lilia was nowhere to be found.

“Where’s Lilia?” Rio’s eyes darted around the apartment, looking for the Sicilian.

“She knows Agatha, Rio. She swore she wasn’t working with her, but I couldn’t trust her. I sent her back to her. Jen…” Alice broke into another sob, unable to say anything else.

“I know. I saw it.” Rio’s voice full of sympathy for her friend.

“I didn’t know,” Alice whispered.

“I believe you, and Lilia too.”

 

“Disgusting.” Eternity spat. “I just proved that you can’t trust humans, yet you believe two of them.”

Rio turned on her. “Yes, because if Agatha knew who I was, then she knew about my relationships with Lilia and Alice before I told her about Alice. Yes, she used me. Yes, she broke my trust and my heart, but I have to believe there’s some good in her, I have to.” Rio stayed on the floor, tears mixing with snot, wiping at it halfheartedly with her forearm.

Alice slid off the sofa and joined her friend on the hardwood, enveloping her in her arms.

 

“You disappoint me, sister.” Eternity’s voice was harsh. “You are Death. You command legions, yet you have walked amongst humans for centuries, blending in with them.” She threw her hands into the air in frustration. “You were losing your abilities and then allowed a witch to outsmart you, and worst of all, you fell in love with her. You’re still defending her when you should be seeking your revenge.”

Rio slowly lifted her head. “What do I need to do to regain my powers? My connection?”

A Cheshire cat-like smile lit up Eternity’s face. “Simple, come back to your realm. Spend some time there, forget about all of this unpleasantness.”

“How long?” Alice sniffed, untrusting.

“Oh, just a few centuries.” Eternity answered nonchalantly.

Rio narrowed her eyes, focusing on one word: “What do you mean, forget?”

“Oh, your time on Earth would be erased from your memories, of course.” Eternity’s voice was full of glee.

 

“You do realize that when you have an evil plan, you’re not supposed to reveal it?” Agatha’s voice, full of exhaustion and something Rio couldn’t quite pin, cut through the air. “Terrible security, by the way, my love.” She turned to Alice. “Your wife needs to speak with you.” With a snap, Alice was gone.

“Agatha Harkness. Step away from my sister.” Eternity growled.

Agatha bared her teeth and snapped at her in a feral display of dominance. “Hela says ‘fuck you’.” She recited a few words in a nameless language; causing Eternity to begin to fade.

Eternity looked at the witch, disbelief and rage in her eyes. “How dare you! You are mortal!”

Agatha hummed, head tilted to the side, “Somewhat. Which is why I needed Hela’s help, and boy, does she hate you. Enjoy your time in the Asgardian Dungeons.” She stepped closer, whispering to only where she could hear. “I may not know everything you’ve been up to, but when I find out, you will pay for this.” 

Agatha stepped back, flicking her wrist. Eternity finally disappearing. 

 

Agatha reached down and hoisted Rio up by the elbows, leading her to the plush, dark green couch. Urging her to sit next to her, facing each other.

Rio couldn’t look away, taking the tissues Agatha offered, and leaning into her touch when the witch caressed her cheek. Helpless when it came to the conniving witch. 

Then, Rio remembered. She jumped to her feet quickly. “What are you doing here, Agatha?” She barked. Mustering all of her anger and hurt.

 

Agatha winced. “I needed to explain myself, apologize, fix thing—”

“You lied to me!”

“Yes.”

“Used me!”

“Yes.”

“Left me.”

Agatha stood and reached for Rio, holding back tears when the entity wrenched away. 

”I never meant it to go this far, Rio. I never meant to hurt you. I feel guilty and terrible.”

Rio laughed harshly. 

“Are you even capable of feelings?”

Agatha took a step back, as if she had just been sucker punched, the wind knocked out of her. “Everything I did was because of my feelings, Lady Death!” She retorted, voice rising. “Because of my love for my family. That contract? That contract put a timer on their lives; bound their magick, dictated all of us, even Wanda and Pietro. I did what I had to do to survive. If that meant using people, then so be it. The only thing I regret is hurting you in the process.” Agatha’s chest tightened, her stomach lurched. 

“Don’t get me wrong, with as much as it kills me to see you like this?” She ran her hands through her hair roughly.  “If I had to do it again? I would. I would do anything for my children.” Her eyes swirled like a storm on the ocean. Her face set in solid resolve.

Rio blinked and looked away. “You never loved me.” A statement.

Agatha’s face crumbled. “Oh, Rio,” she stepped forward and cupped her face with both her hands. “I will never stop loving you.” Agatha brought their lips together, kissing her in a way that completely took Rio off guard. There was no ulterior motive, no animosity or sinister plot. Rio was the oxygen Agatha needed to exist in that moment.

Rio gripped Agatha’s wrists, holding on, wanting to remember this moment, knowing it was their last. Their tears mixed on Rio’s lips, Agatha’s tasting like hope and forgiveness, Rio’s tasting like finality and heartbreak. 

Rio swallowed Agatha’s moans.

Agatha swallowed Rio’s sobs.

The kiss felt like it lasted forever, yet not long enough, over before it began. 

Rio stood there, clutching the air where Agatha had been, tears running down her face, blinking into her empty apartment. Underneath her foot was the activated hex bag, the one she had used to send Agatha back to Los Angeles. 

The former cosmic being, now reduced to a shell of her former glory, sobbed and fell back to her knees with a guttural cry and a broken whisper.

“I don’t wanna see your face.”  

Chapter End Notes

I am SO EXCITED about the next chapter!

AND we meet a certain recent JW character...

How're we feeling?

Chapter 4: Jurassic Park is Frightening in the Dark

Chapter Summary

It's been five years. The anticipated Jurassic Park has opened on Isla Dunblar. Agatha and a few others head out to spend a week observing, collecting data, and ensuring everything is ready for public viewing.

What happens when Rio is there, and they are forced to face their feelings? What happened in the past five years, and why is Rio so different?

What happens when everything goes so horribly wrong within hours?

Chapter Notes

IT'S HERE!

IT'S A LONG ONE, FOLKS!

First, THANK YOU SO MUCH, Hannah-0730/CrystalsandLavender, for being my sounding board. Validating choices, sacrificing her emotional well-being, reading parts of this, etc. Every time she said it hurt and was devastating...I made it worse.

A few reminders: Lilith/Lily is Nicky's twin sister/Agatha's youngest.
Agatha legally adopted Wanda/Pietro when they were ten.
Ian Malcolm goes by Ian, Malcolm, Dr. Malcolm...I switch it interchangeably because...I do.

Take water breaks, not for spicy content, but due to dehydration from crying. Also, check the new tags.

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Late June 2020

Isla Nublar, 120 miles off the coast of Costa Rica

 

Agatha stepped off the ship and onto the dock of the Island that housed the soon-to-be-open Jurassic Park, followed by her daughter Lilith, Wanda, and Vision. 

Five years had passed since the day they had regained their magick and broken the majority of the ties Evanora had over them. Five years since Oxford and the announcement of the experimental park.

The witch threw her backpack over her shoulder and strolled to where the dock met the beach, only to be met by the blonde from hell, bathed in the early-afternoon sunlight, glowing as though backlit by flames.

Fitting. 

Ellie Sattler leaned against a green Jeep painted with the Jurassic Park logo, eyes glued to Agatha. “Hey Aggie, last time I saw you, you were chasing after Dr. Vidal, about to have your heart broken.” 

“I’m going to feed you to a Gigantosaurus and enjoy every minute of it,” Agatha gritted out.

“I don’t think we’re allowed to poison the wildlife,” Wanda smirked, looking around at the scenery.

“Hello, Dr. Sattler, so nice to see you.” Vision greeted, oblivious to everything.

Lilith looked around before her attention returned to her phone and texted furiously.

Ellie leaned in to Agatha when she had her chance, “I’m still willing to help heal your bruised ego.”

“I’d rather make out with a triceratops.” She tossed her hair behind her shoulder and headed toward the vehicle. She stopped for a moment, taking in the island. She had felt a pull toward the hunk of land the closer their glorified boat had gotten, and still felt the thrum of it. She chalked it up to the true purpose of her trip, yet something else kept tugging at her. She rolled her shoulders back and tried to tamp the feeling down for the time being.

Wanda threw her pack in the Jeep and climbed in, gesturing for her husband to do the same. “Can we please get to camp before the dick measuring contest?” 

Lilith’s face twisted in disgust as she continued to text. 

“Lily, we’re surrounded by literal dinosaurs. Shouldn’t you put your phone away?” Vision looked over at her, half scolding.

“Don’t tell her what to do, Viz.” Wanda reprimanded, voice laced with annoyance.

Ellie cleared her throat as they all climbed into the Jeep. “Right. Well, Aggie—”

“Stop calling me that,” Agatha growled.

“Fine, Agatha,” Ellie sighed. “I was surprised when you moved your visit up a week.”

“Ian told us to come a week earlier.” Agatha shrugged.

“Of course he did,” Ellie muttered under her breath.

Agatha ignored the bane of her adult life and focused on the scenery and the feeling in her chest.

The ride to the compound was silent, punctuated by Vision pointing out dinosaurs and Wanda taking occasional pictures. Lily continued texting, and Agatha was lost in her thoughts.

Memories of the park's announcement played in her head, reminding her of how the relationship with Rio had ended. They hadn’t even been together for long, yet it had seemed like years.

It was only normal for the park to remind her of the woman. Hell, a day hadn’t gone by that she hadn’t thought about Lady Death. Agatha knew she had fucked up. She knew she should’ve explained.

Yet, her family was safe, and she was the closest she’d ever been to being completely free that she could taste it. That was the goal, and she needed to keep her eye on the prize.

The pull, combined with the constant ache and emptiness in her chest, wasn’t helping matters at all. 

While she was on the island to oversee progress, check all enclosures for maximum safety, and give the green light for public visitors, there were two specific items she needed to collect.

The final components to end the curse and truly free her from Evanora Harkness.

It was the true reason behind her visit.  

Maybe, just maybe, Agatha could win her soulmate back once she was no longer on these little missions that involved her deceiving said soulmate.

Lilia had kept Agatha updated on all things related to the cosmic entity. Something the overpowered witch assumed Rio allowed, or else Death would have promise spelled Lilia.

Rio had forgiven Jen, Lilia, and even Wanda. Yet she refused to speak or see Agatha. Jen explained over and over that Agatha made sure the potions witch could restore Rio’s magick before she began siphoning. It was a long, arduous process, but it had worked.

Death’s powers were restored without losing her memory or returning to her realm. She remained mostly who she was, she kept her friendships, and she remained somewhat true to herself.

She had, however, returned to her original cosmic settings when it came to the virtues and moral codes she followed. Which also meant refusing to see or communicate with Agatha, someone who continuously disrupted the Sacred Balance. 

Eternity had been lying about the process, though Rio had yet to go to Asgard to ask why. Hell, she wouldn’t even ask Hela.

Death chose to live in her bubble of ignorant bliss and sadness, letting Agatha writhe in pain.

For half a decade.

Agatha stared blankly at the scenery as they drove to the main base. She knew that once her mission was completed, she could go to Rio in New York and try to talk to her, for the umpteenth time. Maybe, just maybe, she would listen to her then. Agatha had never wanted to cause any harm to the woman. She was merely doing what she needed to do to survive.

“Ow!” She jumped, her skin stinging from where Wanda had pinched her arm. 

“You weren’t paying attention, we’re here!” Wanda exclaimed. “Lily didn’t even wait for the car to stop, probably excited to see Ian.”

Ellie was uncharacteristically silent as they unloaded, for which Agatha was thankful. 

A relatively strong blonde came up behind Wanda, effortlessly slung a duffel over her shoulder, and grabbed a supply crate. Going by the weapon strapped across her other shoulder, she was one of the mercenaries hired as protection.

Wanda turned around and stared up at the woman, a few inches taller than her. Agatha smirked next to her.

Vision was oblivious.

“Dr. Maximoff, Dr. Harkness, Dr. Jarvis, this is Zora Bennett. She’s one of our guards.” Ellie introduced, “She’ll be accompanying you around the island during your stay.” Ellie’s voice remained solemn, as though she were about to lead them into a funeral.

“I look forward to working with all of you,” Zora replied, nodding before walking off toward the building.

Wanda watched her, eyes zeroed in on a particular asset of hers. 

Agatha reached over and wiped a bit of drool from her chin. “Down girl. He’s right there.” She whispered.

“Like I fucking care.” She shot back.

Vision had been acting suspiciously ever since the contract had been destroyed, and, frankly, Wanda had begun to see him for what he was. A brilliant man whom she had grown apart from. 

There was something else that Wanda couldn’t quite put her finger on, and she refused to read his mind, though Agatha had suggested it once or twice.

Wanda had decided, however, that once Evanora was dealt with, she would file for divorce. 

“She seems lovely.” Vision remarked, heading off in the same direction.

The Scarlet Witch hummed in agreement.

“Well, no more delays.” Ellie sighed, leading them to headquarters.

Agatha looked over at Wanda and shrugged. She wasn’t sure what Ellie meant by that; all the updates they had gotten had been perfect, and Ian had been chomping at the bit for them to arrive a few hours prior.

That’s when she felt it. The pull she had felt all day tugged harder. Deeper. At a more molecular level, as though it was trying to guide her entire body toward something.

Or someone.

Rio.

She gripped Wanda’s arm as they entered the building. “She’s here.”

“Your mother?” Wanda’s eyebrows flew into her hairline.

Death. She thought.

Wanda turned to her, lips slightly parted in surprise.

“That’s why he told you to come.” She stated.

Agatha nodded as they passed through secure passageways, half listening to Ellie explain that their fingerprints, retinal scans, DNA, and badges were already coded into the database. 

He and Lily. Together. 

Wanda’s mouth curled into a smirk. Not a bad idea.

Thanks for the support, Red.

They stepped into the main room in the middle of the base. Screens monitoring cameras all over the island and several computers, remotely tracking various creatures via trackers and sensors, lined the walls. Dozens of scientists, ensconced in multiple tasks, were set at different stations throughout. Corridors led off from numerous points of the room. There was a second level, a balcony encircling the room, where more observational equipment was set up. It reminded Agatha of various sci-fi and action movies she had seen growing up. Knowing that Ian had partially designed it, she wasn’t surprised.

At what looked like the main control panel, Ian stood, already headed to greet them.

Next to him, wearing khaki cargo pants and a black shirt, there she stood, just as breathtaking as Agatha remembered her.

Rio Vidal. Death herself.

With Lily next to her, talking excitedly.

Lily? The fuck?

Why was Rio looking at her like a loving aunt? 

The texting. The questions when Agatha surprised Lily two days ago by moving the trip up a week. Her daughter had some major explaining to do.

Agatha tried to walk over as casually as possible, tried to stay calm. She was confused, furious, devastated. She wanted to question, yell, kiss Rio. Part of her was elated to see the woman only feet away from her, and at the same time, she felt like she could crumble into a thousand pieces.

The well-respected, professional scientist glared at Ian as she passed him, flashing her favorite finger.

Rio pushed her tongue into her cheek with a crooked smile and a small laugh. 

Agatha wanted to run to her. 

Wanted to fall to her knees and sob.

What did you say to the woman you so horribly betrayed? 

To the only woman who could save you from an untimely demise?

“Dr. Vidal.” Her voice was hoarse, a large stone having taken up residence in her throat.

Rio’s eyes finally flicked over to Agatha for a millisecond, her face quickly changing into a neutral expression before looking back at Lily. “Lily, go get settled. I’ll take you to the nursery later.”

A flash of yellow erupted from Rio’s cargo pocket and scurried up her body, coming to a stop on Rio’s shoulder, stretching toward Agatha, and hissed, a small flame erupting from its mouth.

Rio suppressed her grin and removed the bearded dragon from her shoulder. “Take Victoria with you. Wouldn’t want her to singe your Mom’s hair.”

Brow raised, Agatha looked down at the reptile, now perched on her daughter’s shoulder, judging her. “I don’t remember bearded dragons producing fire.” 

Rio shrugged. “I’ve heard plenty of stories about a certain rabbit. Want to share?”

“I do not.”



Lily looked back and forth between the two of them. The slight banter was better than she had hoped for.  Inhaling deeply, she turned to her mother first, “Just listen to each other, okay?” She hugged her tightly. “You did what you had to do.” She whispered in her ear before turning and hugging Rio. “I’m so glad you didn’t break contact with me. Please listen to her.” 

Stepping away, she smiled and waved to Wanda, who had been watching intensely, next to Ian. The teenager walked over to them, hugged her older sister, and fell into easy conversation.

 

 

Agatha turned back to Rio, hugging herself around the waist. “Are they trying to ‘Parent Trap’ us?”

Rio raised an eyebrow, not looking at her old rival. “That would explain why she made me watch that movie a few years ago.” A deep sigh escaped her lips. “Walk with me where everyone isn’t staring?”

The witch nodded, hand extended for Death to lead the way. 

Rio led the way to the garden, still within the walls of the compound, her back still to Agatha.

“I see you and Lily stayed in contact.” 

Rio didn’t turn around. “Yes. She didn’t remember that night; she just knew we had broken up. Until you told her what happened when her magick came in.”

“That explains why she was furious. I gave the twins the PG version, of course. Nicky wasn’t exactly pleased either.” Agatha began to pace, even though Rio made sure they never faced each other.

“Yes, well. The kids and I did talk a bit that summer, Agatha. They reached out back in 2016, though.” She chuckled. “Lily took an interest in my research.”

“Teenage girls will do anything to spite their mothers.”

“Did you not know that they spent time with me last summer, and at the beginning of this month? Hell, every time they’re in New York, unless they’re with you.” Rio questioned, still not looking at her former lover.

“Jen said you weren’t in town. That lying bitch.” Agatha smirked. If she were honest with herself, she was jealous. Jealous that her children were able to spend time with her soulmate, and she wasn’t. Envious that they had a relationship with her. Screams had nearly erupted from her lungs when Lily hugged Rio. 

Rio would barely look at her.

Then she remembered the barely there words when Rio had banished her back to Los Angeles.

“I don’t wanna see your face.” 

“You still don’t want to look at me,” she stated dryly. 

“No, Agatha.” Rio’s words were firm.

“Will you let me explain?” She swore the sound of another part of her heart shattering was audible.

“Lilia, Jen, the twins, even Wanda, Pietro, Ian, AND Ralph have tried to for almost five years, Agatha. What can you possibly say that they haven’t?” Rio clenched her jaw, still not turning around.

“Just let me explain, Rio. Please. Can’t you see I’m still trying to make this right?”

“You have two minutes, Harkness.” Rio’s voice was cold.

“Will you please look at me?” Agatha begged, something she never did. “You can make sure I’m telling the truth that way.” She brushed Rio’s shoulder with the back of her hand, feeling the muscles go rigid at her touch. “Please, fea…feathers.”



It was the way Agatha’s voice broke on Rio’s old nickname, and the desperation that changed her mind. The witch was right. Rio had her magick back and could tell if lies were being told, but looking at Agatha would make it easier. 

She inhaled deeply, knowing that actually seeing this woman for more than a quick glimpse may destroy her. She clenched her right fist, digging her nails into the palm of her hand so the pain grounded her.

Slowly, she turned around, breath hitching when she finally allowed herself to see the woman who had plagued her every waking thought since she first met her. 

Thick, silky brown hair was beginning to be affected by humidity, reverting to its natural wave, with a white streak emerging on the right. The area beneath cerulean eyes was darkened from lack of sleep. Close to five years of it, if Rio were to hazard a guess. 

A short-sleeved dark green shirt, camouflage cargo pants, and hiking boots fit nicely, but showed she had been working out quite a bit. A coping mechanism, she was sure. The canvas backpack was still slung over her shoulder, and most notably, the tips of her fingers were black.

She had removed her glamor. 

Rio knew that the two powerful witches were sharing the Darkhold. She also knew that they didn’t use it often, which explained the minimal blackening of her fingers that Rio found beautiful. 

“You haven’t been sleeping.” The entity stated.

“Neither have you.” The human retorted.

“I don’t have to sleep anymore.”

“No, but I always slept better curled up next to you.” She murmured.

Memories of nights spent together, skin against skin, tangled in each other’s arms, flooded Rio’s mind. A tear rolled down her cheek as she quickly looked away from the endless blue eyes she once found comfort in, flicking the offending saline mixture away. “Get out of my head, Agatha.”

“Whatever you’re seeing, it’s not me.”

Large brown eyes slammed shut, scanning for any invasion. Only to realize, it was her own mind doing this.

Traitor. She thought.

“Okay, Agatha. Two minutes. We need to get this over with. Time starts now.” Rio knew that the longer this woman talked, the easier it would be to forget everything and fall back into her arms and her bed. 

Death was supposed to be invincible. The existence of Agatha Harkness disproved that theory.

Agatha swore she heard ticking as she took a deep breath to fight for Rio. “I know what the others told you, and you know that’s all true.”

Rio’s nod was barely noticeable. 

“I made sure Jen could restore your Magick. I know what it’s like to have that taken away. I wouldn’t permanently do that to you.” She began, face hardened.
