“I always knew who you were; there was never a time I didn’t.” She raised her hand, fingers hovering next to Rio’s cheek, mere inches between them. 

“I guess because of the connection between us?”

Rio leaned into Agatha’s palm, eyelids fluttering at the contact. 

“I met Hela. I was determined to break the contract, kill my mother, save my family. The twins' health was under my mother’s control, so many things were interconnected. As Wanda has told you, my mother knew who and what she and Pietro were. We had to find the Darkhold. We had to find the locket.” Agatha drew her locket out from inside her shirt with her free hand. 

“I knew you weren’t completely happy being Death, and I knew you were on more of a hiatus.” She was closing the small gap between them as she spoke. “Hela told me, but I knew. I just did.” Her hand flexed to cup Rio’s cheek, her free hand taking Rio’s in hers. 

Agatha sucked in a breath and kept going.

“I didn’t know I was stealing your research for Ellie. I knew I was getting something important, but I didn’t know what. I never meant to use sex to do it, though I will admit that it’s sometimes a bonus when I’m running a con. 

“I’m a succubus, Rio. Sex is one of the ways I feed. Involuntarily at times. However, I usually can’t feed from you—you would kill me without a conduit, which is where the locket came in. I needed your Magick to break the contract and most of the curse.” The eye contact never broke between them, only adding to the tension and the feelings rising in both of them.

Agatha knew her time was up. “May I keep going?”

Rio cleared her throat. “Why wouldn’t you let me touch you?” The question had been on her mind for years, and she had convinced herself that it was due to Agatha faking everything.

Agatha scoffed. “My mother was very literal in the writing of the contract. I was not allowed to enjoy the sexual touch of a woman. In other words, after my lawyer, Lilia, and my coven looked over it, meant that I could give…”

“But not receive.” Rio finished the thought.

“Right. Kissing isn’t inherently sexual. So that was a loophole. However, the runes carved into my skin are to hide me from her, other witches, and once I had enough Magick, then it no longer mattered, especially with the locket. I just had to say a spell each time. I’m sure you heard it.”

“It counteracted whatever magickal chastity belt she had on you.” Rio threaded their fingers together, blinking back tears at the contact.

“Right. If I had allowed it too early, then harm would come to the kids, me, Ralph…anyone I loved that she had a magickal grip on.” The hand on Rio’s cheek trailed down to her waist and settled there.

“Evil bitch.” Rio spat, pushing Agatha’s hair behind her ear.

 

 

Agatha let out a small sigh at the contact, relieved to feel Rio on her skin again. “When I siphoned from you, I wasn’t turning you human.  You were already losing that connection on your own. I always had a plan, my love. I couldn’t just kill my mother. If I did that, I would die. The bitch stays alive by draining my family.” Her lips twitched with the hint of a genuine smile. “I was able to cut that cord when I broke the contract.” She pulled her cosmic entity closer and pressed their foreheads together.

“You and I are made for each other. Our names are literally etched into the other’s genetic makeup. I have always been able to feel you, the pull from you, and pushed it aside. That night, it hit me like a truck. I was ripped into pieces when you sent me away without allowing me to explain.” Her tongue darted out to wet her lips, anticipating the kiss she had waited years for.

“Agatha, I didn’t understand the pull we had towards each other until Lilia explained it to me. It doesn’t even scratch the surface, and won’t until we both accept each other. The bond.”

Agatha squeezed her eyes shut. So that was why she didn’t feel everything from Rio. She had reluctantly accepted her fate. Rio hadn’t. 

 

 

Rio knew Agatha had read between the lines. “There’s still so much to discuss. I…” She stopped suddenly and pulled back, searching the blue eyes she had missed so much.

“You’re dying.”

Agatha smirked. “Not for long. Wanda told you what happened, right? With the poison? The poison for Pietro? Well, I still have a smidge of that in my veins—your magick killed the majority of it. That’s mostly why I’m here, other than the scientific breakthroughs, of course.”

“Of course.” 

“I need two more ingredients for the final antidote and ritual. Once completed, I evolve to my final form, if you will, the poison completely clears out, Evanora dies, and the family and I are immortal after a few words from the Darkhold.” She leaned back into her, whispering as though saying it too loudly would make it untrue. “I’ll be completely free.” 

Rio stepped back, breaking physical contact and distancing herself from the one who was made for her. “Agatha. You’re disrupting the sacred balance.”

 

 

The fucking Sacred Balance. Death’s moral compass and her rigid black and white thinking. Lilia had warned Agatha that the closer Rio became to her cosmic form, the stricter she was about her main rule, one of her main reasons for her existence.

The finality of death; the checks and balances of the universe. 

“Oh, sweetheart. Surely you know that by being your soulmate, and not having poison running through my veins, I’m immortal anyway, once you accept the damn bond. As a siphon in possession of the Darkhold, I’m already close. Our dear sweet Scarlet Witch is immortal, and do you really think that Wands and I would live forever without our families?” Her smile was wolfish, baring teeth that she wanted to sink into Rio’s throat to mark her.

“You did know about this place then, if what you need is here. You did know about my research.” Death growled in a commanding and haunting voice.

“I didn’t know it was yours.” She replied honestly. “What do you think Wanda was doing with all those plants? Or me with the DNA samples? We were finding the right ones. They had to cultivate, my love. I knew they were opening the park. Did you forget that Dr. Malcolm is basically my sister wife?”

Dark clouds rolled in quickly, lightning cracking overhead. “You can’t just disrupt life like this, Agatha.”

“Plenty of people die every day, my sweet death. What’s a few immortals? I’ve been tossing the afterlife a few bodies here and there. I’m about to drop my mother and everyone in cahoots with her. Your legion and Sacred Balance will be just fine, Rio. Think of me as your personal Santa.” The scientist’s hair whipped around her as the wind grew stronger.

“No. Agatha. I can’t do this.” Death moved further away, untouched, decision made. 

 

 

Agatha Harkness gasped in shock, not used to rejection, yet Rio was creating a pattern. “You would walk away from me to protect this Sacred Balance of yours? Throw away a life together? A family?” Mouth agape, she stared at Rio—the audacity of choosing a concept over her.

Rio hugged her middle and looked away. “Yes.” 

The Green Witch vanished in a cloud of black and green, the storm dissipating as quickly as it had appeared.

Agatha fell to her knees, letting out a feral scream and a shockwave of Magick that shook the ground and everything in its path.

🦕🦖🦕

She hadn’t been sitting in the dirt for longer than a minute and thirty-two seconds, wallowing in her own pity, when she felt the familiar chaotic energy behind her.

 

“Sacred Balance?” Wanda asked quietly, lowering herself next to the older witch.

“Sacred fucking Balance.”

Wanda wrapped her arms around her. “You’ll still get your skeleton, I know you will.”

A small smile emerged at the redhead’s attempted joke. “I hope you’re right, Superstar. In the meantime, I want off this fucking island.” She stood, dusting off her pants. “I’m going to collect what I need. You stay here. I’ll be back.”

“Aggie, are you su—”

The woman she considered her mother didn’t even wait, just left in a cloud of violet. 

“Right. Mother knows best.” She grumbled, heading back toward the building.

“Dr. Maximoff, what are you doing out here? Could be dangerous, you know?” The taller woman winked, leaning against the building.

Well, there’s something better to do. Wanda thought to herself. She flashed a smile at Zora. “Please, call me Wanda. I was just headed back inside, but I’m not too worried about danger, Ms. Bennett.”

“It’s Zora, and if you’re not, do you want a proper tour of the island?” She smirked, taking a step towards the scientist.

Wanda knew she should say no, go inside, spend time with Lily, pretend to do her job, ignore her husband. 

“I would love one. Know a good tour guide? A beautiful, muscular blonde with captivating green eyes, perhaps?” She smiled up at her, something dangerous glinting in her gaze. 



Zora glanced down, biting the inside of her bottom lip. Wanda was a married woman, one who was standing close enough for the guard to feel the heat radiating from her. “It seems you create your own danger, Wanda.” Peering down at Wanda through half-lidded eyes, she smiled playfully. “Meet me up front in ten, Vela.” The newly coined tour guide turned and headed off to get supplies. 

 

 

“Vela?” Wanda called after her.

“Velociraptor. Cute but deadly.” Zora smirked over her shoulder.

Wanda smiled at herself, then headed back into the compound to reapply sunscreen and bug spray and grab her pack. Only to run straight into Vision.

“Where have you been?” He asked anxiously.

“Mom and Dr. Vidal had a bit of an argument over their views. I was trying to calm Mom down, but you know how she is.” Wanda ran a hand through her hair.

Vision hummed, unconvinced. “Where are you off to now?”

“Zora is going to take me on a tour of the island. I was just headed to get my backpack.” Her eyes narrowed. Vision had been caring and doting once; however, since the events of five years ago, he had become nosy. Suspicious even. None of the adults in the family liked it much. Even the teenagers were growing suspicious of his actions. Their sons, however, were too busy bickering with each other to notice.

She continued walking to the domestic side of the compound, Vision on her heels.

“I think I’ll come with you. I’d love to see more of the island.”

“No need, dear. You’re safer here. I wouldn’t want you to be bitten by something, or heaven forbid, find a prehistoric plant you’re allergic to.” She faked a bright smile, changed into her hiking boots, and scooped up her bag. Magick was rising under her skin, and she was suddenly on very high alert.

Vision returned the same fake smile and caught her upper am, right below her shoulder. “No, darling. I will be going with you.”

“Cute.” Her magick flared, flooding his body with heat. He yelped with pain and stepped back. “Jealousy looks awful on you, dear.” Scarlet tendrils of power wrapped around him, causing him to go limp. “Take a nap, my dear husband. Aggie and I will deal with you later.” Depositing him on the bed, she turned and left the room, making sure the door locked behind her as she stepped back into the corridor. 

She walked briskly to meet Zora, knowing she should immediately find the other witch and tell her what had happened. They needed to find out precisely what Vision was doing, why he had been acting so out of character recently. 

Honestly, she didn’t care. It would be handled. Agatha could feed off him after they interrogated him. They had both suspected he was cheating, and she didn’t want to bother with that right now. Wanda was busy, her mind turning back to the woman she was headed to meet. 

The woman she wanted to get to know better, with what limited time she had. She knew once Agatha had what she came for, it was go time. How did you tell someone you just met that you were a being of legend?

They would cross that bridge when they got there.

The smile from earlier returned when she found Zora loading a Jeep, though this one was smaller and made for more rugged terrain. “Sorry it took me longer.”

“I was wondering if you were going to back out. I’d have to give you a new nickname.”

“Never,” Wanda dropped the bag into the back and slid into the passenger seat, sliding her sunglasses into place. Maybe they’d come across the other paleontologist while they were out. Two pterodactyls with one boulder, or whatever that saying was.

The guards at the gate didn’t ask questions as they left the compound, only remaining on the main paths for a mile or so before Zora veered off onto lesser-used ones.

“So, Vela, what’s your story?” 

“It’s a bit long, muscles.”

Zora covered her mouth, attempting to hide her smile. “It’s a long tour.”

Wanda turned in her seat to face the other woman and began to tell her the non-magickal parts, falling into comfortable conversation.

This was much better than waiting for Agatha back at the base.  

🦕🦖🦕

Lilith Harkness was not an idiot. In fact, she was almost as intelligent as her parents, and if you asked her, she would say she was indeed smarter than her twin. She was younger than Nicholas by exactly thirteen minutes. A fact which she often followed up with ‘perfection took a bit longer, you insufferable man.’

No one else was allowed to bully her brother.

With her mother’s looks, intelligence, and tongue, she was almost a carbon copy. Fortunately for her, she understood and expressed her emotions much better than her mother ever could. 

Which was why she had stayed in touch with Rio. She had talked to her Nonna Lilia enough to know that Rio, Death herself, was supposed to be her mother’s soulmate. 

Lily had gagged at that idea at first. Her parents kissed each other on the cheek. Their love was platonic. The then ten-year-old found actual love disgusting.  

Seeing them on video calls when they were together at Oxford, she could tell how in love they were, even if the great Agatha Harkness couldn’t. They snuck kisses when they didn’t think the kids were watching, completely encapsulated by each other.

Lily had witnessed how devastated and changed her mother was afterwards. Rio would subtly ask about her. 

Now, while she thought the idea of soulmates was magical and dreamy, it was also devastating and heartbreaking. 

It was also not for the emotionally inept.

If she were ever to need relationship advice, she would not be getting it from Agatha Harkness. Unless, of course, she were to marry her gay best friend and have a lavender marriage. Then her Mom would be the first person she would talk to.

While Agatha hadn’t told Nicky and Lily what had happened that day until they were thirteen, they already knew. Not only had Nonna told them, but their older siblings had as well. Wanda and Pietro rarely kept anything from them. 

Besides, Lily heard everything that was said in their home, and often out of it.

Agatha Harkness was not the only witch who taught herself magick as a child.

Lilith Harkness, living up to both her first and last names, knew advanced magick by the age of seven and had eyes and ears everywhere. She tapped into coven meetings, her mother’s phone conversations, USC board meetings. 

Anything and everything. She had been caught on many occasions, and her mother knew she was doing it, of course, but she would never discourage learning. 

Now, at fifteen, she was interested in what she had deemed the family business. Paleontology. Not whatever Evanora’s businesses were. 

She was fascinated by every aspect of that particular scientific discipline: zoology, archaeology, geology, botany, the behavior of dinosaurs, and other prehistoric species. Any science related to prehistoric times fascinated her, especially when she could infuse her magick into it. 

Nicky was more interested in human medicine and magick, remembering how they were kept magickally ill as children. He chose to shadow their father, older brother, and Aunt Jen during the summers and was currently back in LA, helping with their twin nephews.

She shadowed their mom, older sister, and Uncle Ian. However, she was also trying to get their mom and Rio back together.

So she and Uncle Ian formed a plan. Move the trip up a week while Rio was still on the island and force them together.

Going by the quick storm, rumbling of her mother’s magick, and disappearance of both of them, their plan was dead. 

The baby Sinosauropteryx drank clumsily from the bottle Lily held to its mouth, cradled safely in her arms. Brand new feathers were soft against her skin as she sat in an oversized chair in the nursery, Ian Malcolm leaning against a counter across from her, watching closely. 

While she and Nicky had always called him ‘Uncle Ian’, they had always been subconsciously aware of his relationship with their dad. They didn’t have the words for it when they were younger, and she vaguely remembered the actual conversation when they were five, but the arrangement never bothered any of the four siblings. She always knew her Grandmother Evanora was evil. She could feel it at a molecular level, so what she knew of the ‘rules’ as her mom had called them, hadn’t surprised her, even as a young child. Nicky had accepted it easily as well. 

It had helped that Agatha had been able to easily show them a kid-friendly memory.

Now that she was older, she knew it had been heavily sanitized for kids.

Stroking the feathers of the future highly dangerous dinosaur in her arms, she looked up at the scientist. “Our plan is dead. How are we going to get them back together?” She kept her voice quiet and soothing so as not to startle the prehistoric creature she was cradling as though it were a baby or a cat.

“Like life, lesbians uh, find a way.” Ian gestured.

Lily gave him a look very reminiscent of her mother.

“Lilypad,” One leg crossed over the other at the ankle. “Like the currently small creature in your arms, their love shouldn’t exist. Your mother is…somewhat human, though now immortal, and Dr. Vidal is a cosmic entity. We’re all bound to secrecy, a forbidden knowledge.” Worry spread across his charge’s face. “The binding spells cause the cameras to static out when we say anything along those lines.” Pushing off the counter, he took the empty bottle from the teenager. 

“These plants, animals, nearly everything on this island was created by forbidden knowledge, you know the story.” Gesturing with one hand, he rinsed out the bottle with the other, careful to make as little noise as possible so as not to arouse the sleeping beast in Lily’s arms. 

 

“They live by two completely different codes. Rio lives by the very rigid belief that there is a Sacred Balance for everything. Your mother lives by a morally grey code of her very own.” Turning back around, he laid the bottle out to finish air drying and paced closer, making sure to stay near the sleeping dinosaur in case anything were to startle it.

“She is always after power of the magickal sort, and knowledge. However, her highest priorities are to keep her family safe and to see the demise of Evanora.” Wise brown eyes met a copy of Agatha’s blues, though seeking information, knowledge, and guidance. “She is also guided by chaos, much as we are, in her quest.”

Lily’s phone was next to her bag, where Ian had previously stood. He picked it up and took a few pictures of her holding the feathered dinosaur with a long, banded tail asleep in the girl’s arms and returned the phone to her bag.

“When you have clashing beliefs and priorities like that, someone has to compromise, and I’m not sure if you’ve noticed, but cosmic entities don’t exactly like compromising. Have you tried doing that with your sister lately?”  

Lily furrowed her eyebrows. “I never have to compromise with Wanda. We usually either agree, or she lets me have my way.”

Ian rolled his eyes and huffed. “Of course. Well, it usually doesn’t work that way. She fights the rest of us tooth and nail. So does Dr. Vidal. Does she compromise?”

Her eyes looked toward the ceiling, thinking, before returning to her uncle’s. “Not exactly. She pushes me to problem-solve, and while it is a form of compromise, I often reach the same conclusion as her or present a full counterargument. We debate, compare notes, go from there. Last time, it was Nicky, Rio, and me choosing where to eat when we were in New York earlier this summer. It doesn’t have to be big things.”

“Your mother would just choose what she wanted.” The chuckle died on his lips when the young dinosaur flexed its fingers, claws shining in the fluorescent lights overhead, before curling them into Lily’s shirt. His hand darted forward to shield her, though he knew the claws weren’t dangerous yet. “Time to put this guy away.”

 

 

The look he received was straight from Agatha. “Uncle Ian, I’ve had worse from Señor Scratchy and Victoria.” Her mother’s familiar was often a bunny, and could be very aggressive when playing. Rio’s bearded dragon would fight anyone for a blueberry, and Lily had the scar to prove it, and was currently sporting a bandage on her hand from where the little shit had burned her for not sharing a banana earlier. 

Victoria had been put back in her ‘house’, as Lily called it, for a time out. She swore the lizard called her a bitch. She needed to have a talk with Rio about her overpowered familiar.

 

 

“Yes, but I am currently responsible for you, and I do not want to be on the receiving end of your mother’s wrath. I still haven’t lived down the last time someone got hurt.” Wincing from the memory, he carefully removed the feathered theropod.

“Oh, you mean when Pietro almost died?” Lily raised an eyebrow, rising to retrieve her phone to devise a new plan. 

Chewing on her lip in concentration, a bad habit she had inherited from her mother, she looked down at her notes. “So obviously their first talk didn’t work. What now?”

Malcolm came back, carrying another tiny creature, wrapped in a towel and a blanket, and brandishing a smaller bottle. “Behold, the results of a beaver, otter, and platypus having too much to drink.”

“A castorocauda? Bitchin’,” Returning to her seat, she settled in again to feed the prehistoric mammal with one arm, and took a selfie with the other. “Now, back to my question.”

“No more shows set in the 80s for you. Back to what I was saying. Chaos.” Returning to the other chair, he also took a picture of the teen feeding the mammal and sent it to Ralph. He had not sent the photo of her feeding something with sharp teeth and claws. 

“Much like the somewhat controlled chaos we are creating, love is chaos.” Sitting in a chair opposite the teen, he rested his elbows on his thighs and clasped his hands together. “Even when they’re magickally marked, or whatever they have going on.”

Adjusting her position as whiskers tickled her arm, she nodded for him to go on.

“Knowing Agatha as long as I have, she’s off searching for what she needs. Rio will find her, and they’ll fight more. The ball is truly in their court right now.”

Eyebrows knitted together in deep concentration as the meddling teenager tried to think about other options. “What about all the various barriers and fencing? Could we corner them somewhere?” 

“They can apparate themselves all over the place, or portal.”

Chewing on the inside of her cheek, she tried to think of something else before Ian interrupted her, taking the fuzzy creature out of her arms. “Hey! That’s my emotional support platyottver!”

Chuckling, he unwrapped the squeaking animal from the blanket and towel. “That’s certainly better than its scientific name.”

“I shall name him Ottver. Or she.” A proud smile crossed her face as she snapped another picture.

“Sure, Lilypad. Now, what is my favorite genre of thing?”

 

 

“Something that looks like it shouldn’t exist, but does.” The budding scientist rolled her eyes. He was basically her second dad; she knew him well.

“Right. Life is messy. It breaks its own categories like this little guy and your claw-happy friend from earlier. Just like your mom, Rio, your siblings, you. None of you should exist, yet you do.” Gesturing wildly, he moved Ottver over to a tank so the critter could get a swim in.

“Chaos.” Her eyes widened as a text from Nicky popped up on her phone, just as what Ian was saying clicked.

“Uncle Ian, have you noticed Viz acting strange lately?” 

“Your father and I have—” 

 

 

The beep of the door reached his ears seconds after the teenage witch went through it, the doors closing behind her.

“Prehistoric theme park, cosmic beings, superpowered witch, baby witch, lesbian drama, Viz being shifty, and who the hell knows what Ellie is doing.” Moving swiftly toward the door, he sent texts out to locate the other supposed responsible adults. “Here’s that chaos I was talking about,” he grumbled, stepping into the hallway. He looked to the left and then to the right, where, on the ground, was Lily’s bag, its contents spilling out.

“That’s not good.” Looking around frantically, his phone lifted to his ear, kneeling to gather the contents of the bag. “Check the cameras outside of the nursery.” His voice shook as he said the words. 

“Checking, sir.” Came the guard's voice. “It looks like Dr. Jarvis was walking down the hall, Lily Harkness came out of the nursery, and the cameras went dark.”

“Fucking Vision.” Dr. Malcolm attempted to control the rage building within him. “Where’s Bennett?”

“Taking Dr. Maximoff for a tour of the island, sir.”

“Dr. Sattler?” 

“She left about an hour ago, sir, possibly for her medical data rounds, sir.”

“Start the search for Lily and Dr. Jarvis, find Bennett, anyone, really, before something else happens.” Standing, he jogged to the security room, where he could cycle through all the cameras. He also needed a radio, a set of keys, and a weapon for good measure. 

He ended the call, immediately dialing Agatha.

Straight to voicemail.

Wanda.

Voicemail.

Ellie.

Voicemail.

Zora.

Voicemail.

Rio.

Voicemail.

“These sapphics are going to kill me.” He mumbled, reached security, and grabbed what he needed. Kidnapping went deeper than whatever Agatha was doing, and it had Evanora Harkness written all over it.

Agatha blinked, allowing her eyes to adjust to the rainforest around her and exactly where she had zapped herself to. The air was thinner, which told her she was higher up on the mountain, but not too close to the rocky mountainside where the plant and dinosaur nest she needed would be.

She breathed in the air, grounding herself and truly taking in the scientific efforts around her. They had done it. They had actually regenerated plants and trees from billions of years ago. 

As much as she wanted to study and enjoy the various vegetation that the highly dedicated team of scientists had resurrected, Agatha knew she needed to collect her clippings and be on her way.

Carefully marching through the undergrowth, careful not to harm anything, she replayed the conversation back. Rio had not been interested in repairing their relationship, and Agatha was not one to beg, usually. She had already displayed abnormal behaviors for her, and needed to get over it. Onward and upward.

Easier said than done, of course, but she had to try.

For now, she had to focus. Retrieve the plant first, egg DNA second. Getting the clippings was dangerous; extracting the DNA was purely suicidal. However, when she succeeded, because she could not afford to fail, she would finally be released from the clutches of the old hag and everything that had come with it.

It wasn’t just her, she was doing this for. She and Ralph could divorce, and he would be free to marry Ian. Wanda and Pietro could live their lives without the worry that Evanora would recruit them into whatever horrible futures she had in store. The same went for her own children. She would be able to potentially move forward with Rio.

If Rio wanted her. 

The woman she had fallen in love with was not the same. Lilia had warned her this could happen. Her words played back in Agatha’s mind. “When you do finally speak again, just remember, Rio didn’t think you knew she was Death. She didn’t know you were a witch. There’s a lot you don’t know about each other. She’s going to have walls up with you. She’s not going to be the woman you remember. 

Pushing the thought away, she reminded herself that after tonight, she would be the wealthiest woman in the world. She didn’t plan to stay that way for long. Evanora Harkness had done many purely evil things to get where she was, and Agatha was chaotic neutral, not chaotic evil. There were some things she needed to set right. 

Turning her thoughts back to the task at hand, she knew using her magick would be preferable, but it wasn’t ideal given the situation and her uncertainty about where the outcroppings or the dinosaurs were. It was better to do this the old-fashioned way. 

She had taken a chance apparating here as it was.

Luckily, she hadn’t landed in the middle of a pack of rexes. 

Wanda also needed to be with her. Only the Scarlet Witch had the reality-warping skills to distract a pack of highly intelligent dinosaurs. Whoever said reptiles had small brains and were incapable of intelligent thought clearly didn’t know shit about science or dinosaurs in general. 

Revisiting the plan, it wasn’t her best, but she hadn’t precisely extracted DNA from a Troodon before. Let alone from an egg sitting in a highly protected nest. 

There was a first time for everything.

Glancing at her phone, she realized she had roughly two and a half hours until sunset, being this close to the equator. She could at least apparate closer to the mountain; it would save time. Sighing, purple mist surrounded her before she could think better of it.

The smell of more trees hit her. Opening her eyes, she found herself in a clearing this time, a clear trail leading from it. Frustrated, she yelled at nothing.

“You’re closer to the mountain. It’s only five minutes away.” Rio’s silken voice filled her ears.

Agatha pivoted around, eyes searching for the woman she had already argued with once today.

“You came back?” 

“You came to me, actually,” Rio answered, stepping out from between the trees.    

“Absolutely not. You made your choice, and I’m respecting it.” Arms crossed over her chest.

“Agatha, I’m still Death, I still have to follow—’

‘NO YOU DON’T!” The heiress yelled, frustrated. “You’re a cosmic being, a god, basically. You can change the rules, make your own.” Stalking toward Rio, her face flushed from her rising anger. “Gods, old and new, knows Eternity was trying to.” She rolled her eyes at the mention of the other cosmic being. 

“I know you feel the pull between us.” She threw her hands in the air. “You think we can ignore this forever?” Veined hands wrapped around the straps on Rio’s pack, pulling her flush against her. “We couldn’t ignore it for fifteen minutes when we first met.” 

Death dropped down and slid out of her backpack, Agatha angrily tossing it to land at the bottom of a nearby tree trunk, her own following.

Her hand came back and slapped Agatha’s left cheek, her head turning sideways. “You think it’s so easy for me?” Her voice rose. “I do feel it, Agatha. Everyday. In the fabric of my existence.” Using a gust of wind, she forced her witch to sit on a fallen log.

Agatha’s mouth hung slightly agape from the impact of Rio’s hand. Her fingers brushed lightly across her stinging cheek, still shocked that Vidal had it in her. Flipping her hair behind her, she leveled a glare at the woman a few yards away. “Could’ve fooled me, since you never reached out.”   

“I was busy restoring the Magick drained from me, remember?” The leaves crunched beneath Rio’s boots, taking a seat opposite Agatha. She sat with her legs spread apart, elbows propped on her knees. “What exactly was Eternity trying to do?” Her breaths were still slowing, running a hand through her hair, steering the conversation into safer waters. 

“Still is, as best as she can in a magickally sealed dungeon in Asgard, anyway. Something we need to take care of after disposing of my mother. You should really speak with your ex.” The emotionally exhausted scientist ran a hand through her hair. “Long story short, Eternity wants more power, specifically of the cosmic variety. She wasn’t sending you to your realm to recharge, my love. She was going to steal your powers and step into your role.”

She shivered in pleasure and sighed contentedly at the skeletal fingers that ran down her spine and over her hip. Bony digits had just begun to press against her sensitive clit when another voice entered her head.

AGATHA! Stop fucking Death. Lily is missing. Vision has her. No one knows where Ellie is either. And now I need a lobotomy.

Leaping to her feet, she snapped her clothes back on, ever thankful for magick. “We have to go. Vision kidnapped Lily, and Ellie is missing. Aftercare later.”

Returning to her human form, fully dressed, Rio grasped Agatha’s hand. “Wanda?” Rio didn’t give a single fuck about anything else at that moment. They could continue what they were doing later. 

“She used our link to tell me, which means she knows about this, considering she asked for a lobotomy.” Rummaging through her backpack for her phone, she checked the time and swore. “The sun is going to set soon.” Chewing on a nail, she looked up at Rio. “I have to get those components, or this whole thing is for naught. I need you to find Wanda, switch places with her, and find Lily.” 

Throwing her backpack over her shoulder, she pulled Rio to her, kissing her as if her life depended on it.

Breaking the kiss, Agatha pressed her forehead against Rio’s. “He knows everything. He’s been acting stranger than usual for years; we thought he was cheating on Wanda. I also feared he was working with my mother. Starting to think it’s the latter.” She kissed her again, slower. 

“Keep the storm up, it gives us the upper hand. It makes things difficult as shit for me, but it gets my baby back. We’ll talk more after all this, okay? Find Wanda. Don’t get eaten.”

 

Rio watched, stunned, as Agatha began to walk away. “You’re NOT going to go search for your daughter? You’re taking the OTHER cosmic entity on a witchy treasure hunt with you?” Her voice rose with anger.

Tired. Agatha was tired. Tired of having to explain her motives. Tired of chasing after rare objects. Tired of her mother and her curses. 

Turning back to Rio, the person who was supposed to automatically love her unconditionally due to a fairytale connection, her shoulders slumped. Rio was full of anger and hurt, as though the past two hours hadn’t even happened.

“Rio, my love. I know that we haven’t actually been together much over the eleven years we’ve known each other, but understand this.” Her voice was resolute. “I will always do what is best for my family. Currently, that is finally ending the hold that Evanora has. The sooner the better. Then, I don’t constantly have to worry about wild shit like my children being kidnapped. So yes, right now, sending the love of my life, who is a cosmic being, to save my daughter is my backup plan.” Tying her hair up, she added, “ If I can’t do it myself, then who else am I going to trust to go in my place? Malcolm will be with you.” She turned back to the trail and kept walking, the rain not touching her due to a spell.

 

“I love you too,” Rio whispered.

🦕🦖🦕

Ellie watched Agatha follow Rio out of the building, felt the shockwave, saw Wanda leave. She knew Rio and Agatha had disappeared somewhere, even though she longed to be the one to comfort Agatha.

They had been kids when they met. Eighteen years old at Stanford. Ralph and Agatha had been a package deal; they met Ian shortly after. The four of them became inseparable almost immediately. Agatha trusted the three of them with her secrets. It wasn’t long until they met her coven. 

Ellie and Agatha fell in love quietly over late-night study sessions, heated debates over long-held beliefs, stolen moments in the library, and dissecting the books of the Vidal family. 

For Ellie, Agatha had been it. The woman she had planned on spending the rest of her life with. So what if Evanora had a different future planned for her only heir? Fuck the contract. The sex stipulation hadn’t been put in there at the time. That hadn’t happened until the old crone found out about them. 

That’s when the problems began. 

Ellie was a scholarship student, and Evanora was a board member. Even if she hadn’t been, she still would’ve pulled strings. That was the major problem. If Ellie stayed with Agatha, she could kiss her entire future goodbye. The elder Harkness would throw her money wherever it was needed to solve the problem.

Sinking into a chair to monitor cameras for hints of the other women, her thoughts continued, much to her displeasure.

When Evanora discovered them, Agatha was entirely financially dependent on the billionaire, not to mention bound in ways Ellie didn’t fully understand.

The 90s weren’t the best time to be out, but she didn’t want to be in a secret relationship either. She wanted to be a strong, queer woman in STEM. A role model for others like them; she wanted it all.

They fought. They argued. Dishes were broken. Agatha leveled a chateau that belonged to her mother. Ellie burned a spellbook that refused to burn. 

They dreamed of advancing science to the point they had now reached. They believed they could make medical advances, technological breakthroughs—the possibilities were endless.  

Then she finally broke up with Agatha when the heiress refused to go no contact with Evanora.

It was one of the biggest regrets in her life so far. 

Watching Ian and Ralph over the years, she was practically overcome with a deep-seated rage at how easily their relationship was. At the same time, Ralph didn’t have the stipulations Agatha did.

The jealousy that she had been filled with that first time Agatha met Dr. Vidal nearly killed her. Agatha had never even looked at her the way she looked at Rio. Yes, she teased her about it, but Ellie knew then that something was different about Rio Vidal.

It’s why she contacted Evanora. She had other reasons; the billionaire had always funded her research because, in the old witch’s mind, Ellie was obedient and loyal. 

The blonde had been the one to end things. Ellie had already been feeding Evanora information for years: nothing serious, but little things. The money the old crone had offered her was alluring, and something she couldn’t ignore. Just the idea of how far the various fields of paleontology could be pushed fueled the scientist, and it clouded her judgment. 

Driving through a gate and waving to the guard, she headed into the veterinary center, miles from the main compound, to check on a few ill animals. 

Trips down memory lane were always unwelcome, even if they had been happening more often as though the ghost of lesbian relationships past were visiting her. 

Because of their working relationship and their partnership with Ian at Yale, Ellie watched both sets of twins grow up—an extension of the family by proximity. Wanda had shadowed them, following in Agatha’s footsteps, and now Lily was doing the same through a program USC had set up. Self-flagellation by watching the family she could have had with Agatha.

She stepped into a room where a pack of compsognathi was being studied after falling ill. The one she had named Chirp reached her first, lingering near her boot as she made notes on the previous recordings.  

Ian had been telling her to move on for years, the thought bouncing in her mind. It wasn’t for her lack of trying. No one interested or intellectually stimulated her, no matter the gender.

Until Agatha had started circling, she had flirted with Rio, somewhat innocently. They had met in the early 2000s—then along came Agatha and that immediate connection.

Apparently, the married with kids and entire stolen research debacles weren’t the dealbreakers she had hoped.

Zora was interesting and gorgeous; however, the minute Wanda and Zora even looked at each other, Ellie knew that wasn’t happening.

Perhaps she should adopt a dog, or something prehistoric. Looking down at Chirp, she tilted her head, considering how practical a pet he would make. Compsognathi were relatively loyal, somewhat intelligent. 

Chirp was alerted by the pack's call and scampered back.

Loyal to their pack. Like someone else she knew.

Scoffing at the comparison, she finished recording the data and made a note to release the pack in the morning. 

Walking back to her vehicle, she saw the monitoring app on her phone alert, showing a storm cell forming on the island. Huffing, she put in the coordinates to the area in her GPS and headed in that direction, sensing that it wasn’t completely natural.

Knowing enough about Agatha and with Evanora dropping hints occasionally, Ellie had picked up that Dr. Vidal was magickal herself. That bearded dragon she carried around was a dead giveaway. 

Driving across the grassy plains, passing various species of creatures that were once extinct, she forced her brain to quiet down. Reaching forward to flip on the radio, Ian’s voice filled the cabin in short bursts.

“Harkness…missing…BOLO”

Coming to a stop and placing the car in park, she fished out her phone to the text from Dr. Malcolm, sent too long ago for her liking. 

Dr. Chaos

Lilith has been kidnapped. Most likely Vision. 

A group text. He had sent a group text that went unanswered.

Dr. Chaos

 

Guys? I have no idea where they’ve gone. Stop fucking, yearning, fighting, sulking, whatever the fuck you five lesbians are doing, and help me find our Lily.

Zora, I really hope you’re with Wanda so she can explain.

 

Ellie slumped forward, pressing her forehead to the steering wheel before rearing back with a feral scream, startling a flock of confuciousorni nearby. 

“NO, NO, NO!” She yelled, punctuating each repeated word with a punch to the dashboard.

One of the things she hated most about Evanora was that she used the children as pawns. She always had. Agatha’s morals were loose.

Evanora’s were missing.

Ellie had three options.

One: Find Malcolm and help him find Vision.

Two: Find Agatha and help her find Lily.

Three: Find Vision, and double-cross him.

Knowing Vision was also working for Evanora, and that he had Lilith, who was just as impulsive as her mother, made three the best option, especially with the Magickal first-aid kit she carried for emergencies. She just hoped Lily didn’t need it.

Retrieving another phone from her bag, she opened up her texts to rendezvous coordinates from Vision. He hadn’t told her anything about kidnapping Lilith. He was doing this completely off the cuff.

Fucking men and their anger.

He also knew he wasn’t supposed to text, only call. Ellie hadn’t even saved his number. He was terrible at this whole backstabbing business. Inputting the coordinates in the GPS, she sped off, phone to her ear. 

🦕🦖🦕

Vision hadn’t exactly meant to kidnap Lilith. He had known the spitfire of a teenager since she was six years old. His sons adored her. However, he was ready for this to be over. Having been the brunt of family jokes for years, knowing Wanda was planning on divorcing him, he was done. It was why he had accepted Evanora’s offer. 

Help her terminate Agatha and join her in the actual Harkness family business. After all, he was more an archaeologist than anything, and the wealthiest woman in the world had some very interesting dealings in that department. 

No more coven, no more witches other than the crone. He could even sue Wanda for custody if he felt like it. Evanora had supplied him with protection against witchcraft—magickal blockers, the witchkiller bullets that almost killed Pietro.

The only rule? Don’t kill Agatha, Wanda, or Rio. Depower, do not kill.

The only problem was that he hadn’t had any of that on him when he argued with Wanda. He had been slow to wake, cursing himself for being so careless. Calling Evanora would only lead to berating. Swinging his legs over the bed, he stood, slipping the runed platinum bracelet on, careful to cover it with a shirt sleeve. It was hot and humid on this godforsaken island, but he couldn’t risk being bitten by anything.

Holstering the weapons he had practiced with in secret, he strapped on his backpack and set out in search of Dr. Sattler. They needed to formulate a plan, and quickly. 

That’s when it happened. Luck had smiled upon him.

Somewhat.

As soon as he saw her, he hit the remote in his pocket, causing the cameras to glitch for a short amount of time.

 

Lilith Harkness had been looking down at her phone, thumbs flying over the screen, when she bumped into him. Her eyes hardened as soon as she saw him, quickly murmuring Latin. The syringe of sedatives plunged into her neck at the same time the phone and evidence vanished.

 

No mind, he had a bargaining chip. He threw her over his shoulder and ran out of the nearest exit as quickly as he could, repeatedly saying “Rio Vidal is death herself. Wanda Maximoff is the Scarlet Witch,” and other truths they weren’t allowed to speak. The binding spells made the cameras turn to static.

Thankfully, no one had been in the halls.

They burst through the door, one arm clutching his sister-in-law to his shoulder. Looking around, he spotted the motorbike and sidecar he had stowed earlier before running into Wanda. When he had found the thing, he had been annoyed by the sidecar. Now, he was thankful for it. He went over the blueprints of the grounds in his memory. Recalling where the foot gate was, he carefully placed Lily in the sidecar, covering her with a blanket that had conveniently been thrown in there.

Turning off the cameras with the remote, he pushed the bike toward the gate. Once on the otherside, he clicked the button again. Constantly clicking the thing every few yards was going to become extremely annoying, he thought. When they were far enough away, he texted Ellie coordinates to a small clearing five miles away from base.

Looking down at the motorcycle he was pushing, he took a deep breath, knowing it was time actually to use it. 

He had no idea what he was doing.

Situating himself, he inserted the key and turned it, thankful it was automatic. Shifting into gear, he lowered a boot onto the accelerator, and the machine rocketed forward. Clicking the remote vigorously, he attempted to steer around trees. It was the Unidentifiable dinosaur corpse that spooked him. He veered down a slight slope, frightening the scavengers participating in their part of the food chain.

The bike was stopped by a tree stump, dislodging its rider. The archaeologist groaned, lying on his stomach. Spitting out a mouthful of dirt and jumping to his feet, he ran to the sidecar to check on Lily. 

Ripping the blanket off, he sighed in relief to see that she was unharmed, still knocked out from the sedative. Tossing her back over his shoulder, he knew they were in danger of being caught.

He began trudging toward the meeting point.

Ringing cut through the air. They were still a good two miles out, and it was a miracle they hadn’t been caught. He answered to Ellie yelling in his ear.

“YOU KIDNAPPED LILY?! HOW THE FUCK IS AGATHA NOT ON YOUR ASS RIGHT NOW?”

Shifting the deadweight against him, he stopped for a second. “I don’t think she knows.”

“Obviously! Where are you?” 

“I crashed my ride. I’m still two miles away.” He began to walk again.



She pulled the phone away from her ear as his location appeared on the screen. “I’m coming to get both of you.” She hung up, tossing the phone back in the bag. Lily had to be sedated.

Ellie pulled up as close to the treeline as possible. Vision emerging seconds later. Her heart lurched seeing Lily nearly lifeless over his shoulder. Quickly opening the door, she gestured for him to lay her down in the back. “Was there a magick blocker in the sedative?”

The man looked offended. “Of course there was.” 

Great, she can’t contact Agatha. Nodding, she smoothed the young girl's hair. “This will be easier if she’s awake. It will take her a while to come to. I have an antidote.”

“What about her magick?” Vision narrowed his eyes.

“It’s only to wake her up, the magick stays,” she lied. Reaching into her bag, she took out another syringe and administered it. “What’s the plan then?”

“We have to cut the main power, get to the air strip, and fly the prop plane to the mainland.” Vision walked around to the passenger side and slid in. “You’re driving, I’ll disable cameras.

“Who’s flying the plane?” Ellie asked warily, watching for any sign of movement from Lily, the girl’s fingers twitching.

“You know how to fly.” 

“Enough to not die.” She retorted, catching Lily’s arm when she attempted to take a swing. “Whoa, Lilith. Let me explain.” 

“Ellie! You’re working with him!” Lilith screeched.

“No, of course not.” The older woman soothed, looking up at Vision and winking.

“Then why is he sitting in the passenger seat and not tied up?” Lily struggled to sit up with Ellie’s help, glaring at Vision weakly.

“Because he knows I’ll throw him to your mother if he doesn’t. Now, just sit quietly and let the antidote run its course. We’ll get everything sorted, okay?” 

During their short conversation, Ellie held her phone in her hand, screen up. Her notes app opened to a message she had typed before meeting them. 

Lily read it before nodding, voice trembling. “Yes, Ellie.”

Ellie returned to the driver’s seat. She had been aware of the storm rapidly spreading across the island. It was no surprise to her when the rain began to fall in heavy sheets.

A creepy, calculating smile crossed the kidnapper’s face. “Perfect.”

“I can’t fly in this, Viz.” Ellie paid close attention to where she was driving.

He turned to see the girl in the back. “Little Hark, can you manipulate the weather?”

“Of course I can.” Agatha’s spawn responded confidently.

Ellie smiled internally. She knew that was something the teen couldn’t do yet. Like her mother, she was going to ‘yes, and?’ her way through this. 

They both were. 

🦕🦖🦕

Wanda Maximoff was having a fantastic time. She and Zora conversed easily, comfortably. They were parked on a lookout over a valley when clouds began to move in. Sitting on the hood of the vehicle, leaning back on her elbows, she groaned. “Of course there’s going to be a storm.”

Zora smiled crookedly at her. “You don’t seem surprised. It wasn’t in the forecast, and from the sounds of it, it’s going to be a nasty one.” 

Wanda nodded. “Knowing those two, I’m sure it will be.” 

“What do you mean? Those two? It’s science, Vela.”

“Shit.” Wanda cursed herself. “Look, we’ve had a great time today, and I really like you.” Her eyes drifted to Zora’s lips before darting back to her eyes. “I really want to see you after we leave the Island, which is going to be much sooner than we thought.” 

Confusion was written all over Zora’s face, not understanding what Wanda was trying to say. “What are you trying to tell me?”

Wanda took her hand, gazing into her eyes. “Promise me you won’t think I’m crazy.”

The operative squeezed her hand, laughing playfully. “Wanda, we’re surrounded by species that have been extinct for millions of years! I think I can handle anything.”

Wanda’s expression said otherwise. “Yes, well, that was science, this isn’t.”

The blonde leaned forward and kissed the corner of her mouth. “Just tell me, Vela.”

Zora’s breath tickled her lips, and oh, how she wanted more than just that small contact. She had to tell her everything first. Something in her gut told her that this woman needed to know. Now. “Agatha and I, we’re witches, and Rio is the physical embodiment of Death.” 

Zora’s brows furrowed, sliding off the hood of the car. “That pocket dinosaur that Rio has certainly isn’t normal, and I swear I saw her take a dinosaur ghost somewhere once. But no, that doesn’t make sense.” With a harsh exhale, she blew a piece of hair out of her face. “So you’re telling me Agatha and Rio are conjuring a storm?”

“Well, Rio really. Death is technically The Original Green Witch. Growth and decay, something poetic like that. Anyway, she can control the weather, grow things, very nature-y. The storm, however, is from the argument and make-up session she and Agatha are having. I told you that story.” Wanda clambered off the hood, prepared to demonstrate her power.

“Okay, and Agatha?”

“Communes with the dead, but that contract she had with her mother, being controlled by her? It was very much a magickal one; there was a ritual, it was very dramatic. Now she can do basically anything. She’s also brilliant and taught herself. That’s a story for another day, also why we’re here.” 

Zora looked at Wanda skeptically. “...and you?”

“Well, that ritual? I’m…it’s hard to explain. I can read minds, show memories, warp reality, and that’s not even the tip of the iceberg.”

“Is this a test? Some kind of trustfall?” Zora tilted her head.

Wanda knew she wouldn’t believe her. “Zora, look around. What’s happening to the trees? Down in the valley?”

“There’s wind, it’s blowing everything everywhere.” She stopped and looked at the redhead. “I can hear the thunder, see the lightning, but the wind isn’t touching us. That tree is maybe ten feet away and affected.” Gasping, she watched the witch’s eyes and hands glow scarlet as she lifted a few feet in the air.

“Not a trust exercise.”

“Okay, I may believe you.” The guard grinned. “Will you come down here? You’re a good ten feet up now, and something is going to eat you.”

“Catch!” Wanda’s hands returned to normal, and she quickly plummeted to the ground. 

Zora yelped and caught her bridal style, eyes wide, face pale from the shock.

Wanda grinned. “That was the trust exercise.” Turning her head to the car, she was filled with a sense of dread. Something told her that she should have felt it earlier, and she had blocked it.

“Phones. Also, it’s about to rain. We’ll be fine, but car.” Flinging open the car door, not even sure how she got there so fast, she dug around for her phone, calling Ian as soon as she read the texts.

 

“Wands, thank God!” The man sounded relieved just at the sight of her name.

“Have you heard from anyone?” Her hand found Zora’s, gripping it tightly.

“No, you’re the first one. Is Zora with you?”

“Yes,” The redhead mumbled sheepishly.

“Well done, kid. Now put me on speaker.”

Wanda did as she was told, setting the phone on the center console. “One minute, Ian, she knows, but I have to spell her first.” 

Chewing on her thumbnail and making eye contact as best as she could, she nervously asked, “Obviously, this is all a big secret. May I put a spell on you to ensure that you don’t say anything to anyone? It’s mainly for when there are cameras or other people around. The cameras cut out, and anyone eavesdropping won’t hear what you’re saying.”

“Of course, Vela. Whatever you need to do. I trust you.” 

Placing two fingers on a blonde temple, her eyes flashed scarlet once again. “Finished.”

Ian cleared his throat. “Aw, your first spell together, and you have a nickname. Does this mean you’re official?”

“Well, these are obvious grounds for divorce…” Wanda’s fingers traced Zora’s profile.

“That’s an understatement,” He grumbled. “Here’s what we know. He’s been able to make the cameras go haywire; we found an abandoned bike with a sidecar; and we can’t trace Lily’s phone. I assume Ags and Rio are fighting, which is causing this storm.”

“You could call it that, yes,” Wanda confirmed, now running her fingertips over the other woman’s collarbone.

“Ellie is still missing. She was last seen at the vet building. You don’t think…” Ian trailed off.

“She wouldn’t. If she did run into them, then we know Lily is safe. We still have to find them. I’ll connect with Agatha. We’ll contact you with the plan. You’re going to have to tell Ralph.

The line went dead without a response. 

“He knows Ralph will send in the cavalry, which will only make things worse,” Wanda explained. “I have to send Agatha a message.” She announced, then immediately scrunched up her nose. “I did not need to see that.” Seeing Zora’s expression, she rushed to explain. “Mind meld, we can speak telepathically.”

“Now that’s convenient.” Leaning forward, she slid a hand into silky red hair, eyes searching for anything that looked like dishonesty. “I thought the dinosaur thing was insane, but damn little raptor. You’re full of surprises.”

“You haven’t seen anything yet, trust me, muscles.” Wanda leaned forward, her hand now gripping Zora’s shoulder, the other still holding her hand. “Thank you for taking this all in stride.”

Zora hummed, closing the distance between them, pressing her lips to Wanda’s.

“I always knew you could do better, Wands. Your mother will be so proud.” Rio winked from where she appeared in the backseat.

Wanda groaned, dropping her head onto Zora’s shoulder. “This means I have to go help Aggie.”

“Death does have the worst timing.” Zora quipped. 

“You kids go say your goodbyes. Zora, you and I will figure out a plan when you’re done. Be quick.” 

The younger women stepped out of the vehicle, moving to the side. Wanda took Zora’s hands in hers and sighed. “I really do have to get to Ags, and while it’s not the best idea, I am going to apparate and hope I don’t miscalculate.” She bit her lip nervously. “Hopefully, this isn’t a common occurrence after tonight.”

A bolt of lightning split the darkened sky in two.

“I’M GOING!” Wanda shouted at the vehicle before turning back to Zora. “She may seem cheerful, but from the conversation in my head with Agatha, it was a lot.”

“We’ll get her back, save the world, kill the villain. You’re nervous, my fierce little velociraptor. I have your number, and I’m sure I’ll see you later. Something tells me it’s going to be a long night.” With that, she pulled Wanda into her, enveloping her mouth with hers. It was sweet, a promise of more to come, not a goodbye.

Wanda vanished mid-kiss, knowing she wouldn’t be able to stop and look at the blonde before leaving.



Zora stared at the space Wanda had previously occupied. “Do you witches do this often?”

Rio stepped closer from where she had appeared. “It’s not uncommon. These witches are just very dramatic.”

“Wanda told me everything, by the way.” She finally turned, somewhat surprised to see the scientist's human form, also known as Death. 

“Good, then I won’t have to fill you in. Get in the car. We have people to save.” Rio commanded, glancing toward the mountain. Of all the planets, in all the universes, she had to be on this one.

No matter how badly things may end, she couldn’t imagine being anywhere Agatha Harkness didn’t exist.     

🦕🦖🦕

Agatha was mumbling to herself as she foraged in the rocky outcroppings near the foot of the mountain: Bennettitales, a truly extinct prehistoric plant. Clipping a few leaves, she pulled out her ‘field potions’ kit as she deemed it, carefully mashing the leaves and adding the juices to the already prepared vials. 

“You’re not going to do that surrounded by dinosaurs, are you?” Wanda asked, appearing beside her.

“I thought I told you no magick?” She asked, putting everything away and taking a few clippings of a few nearby plants. 

“We don’t have time.” Wanda protested.

“Good girl, using your head.” Agatha praised her daughter with a smile. “I got lucky finding the ‘tales down here, with nothing around. The storm has driven most things back into their dens. Which does not help with our next errand.” Agatha closed her eyes, using her mind to look for the nest.

Wanda chewed her thumbnail nervously. “I’m distracting them, right?” 

“Yes, angel.” She bit her lip, calculating how far up they would need to be. “Well fuck. I knew this was going to be hard, a little dangerous…”

“How bad?” Wanda’s eyes honed in on Agatha, watching for anything that could tell her she wasn’t being honest.

“We can spell ourselves and the packs against the storms, even better, throw them in a pocket dimension, but no complete shields. We will be affected by the wind.” The spirit witch worried at her lip, blood pooling there. “At least we won’t get wet.”

“It’s a narrow ledge, isn’t it?” Wanda pinched the bridge of her nose.

“Isn’t it always?” With a reassuring smile, she reached out and placed a comforting hand on Wanda’s shoulder. “I need you to promise me something.”

The redhead's shoulders sagged under the weight of the hand that should have been comforting. “Don’t.” She warned.

Agatha smiled gently, softening the blow. “If anything goes wrong, get the fuck out of there, save Lily, and go.”

Wanda pulled away, eyes burning scarlet. “Absolutely the fuck not! I’m not leaving without you!”

“You have two young sons to raise, dcéra.” Agatha reasoned.

Wanda scoffed. “What about us, matka? You have four children and two grandchildren!” 

“And I am so proud of the amazing people you have grown into, and your boys? They are surrounded by love, anjelik.” Her eyes softened, knowing this was the best plan of action. It didn’t matter that Wanda could withstand almost anything. Agatha already had one child in danger; she didn’t need to fail another one. 

 

 

“You have a husband, a very stubborn cosmic entity to win over, a Malcolm, a coven, an Ellie!” She gathered her hands on one hip. “I think I have a girlfriend, and I absolutely need you around for that.” Her eyes filled with tears at the mere thought of losing the only mother she had ever known, even as a hypothetical. 

“We need you!” She shouted. 

“I need you.” She whispered, falling into Agatha’s arms.

 

Agatha held back tears of her own. “Yes, dievčatko, I’m sorry. If anything goes wrong, freeze them in place, and we’ll run or something. I just don’t want to harm the animals. We’re visitors on their lands, they aren’t used to us, and we’re stealing something from them. I’m trying to be nice.”  

Wanda blinked, accepting the handkerchief and bottle of water Agatha handed her. 

“Are you ready for this?” She asked gently, rubbing Wanda’s back. 

“I think so.” Removing what they needed before stowing their bags in another dimension like a locker, they stepped onto the ledge. Wanda closed her eyes, bracing herself. “Zora kissed me.”

“Well, not the first time you’ve kissed a girl. You’re not exactly inexperienced in that department, Angel.” Taking Wanda’s hand, they suddenly found themselves on the rocky side of the mountain, sixty feet up on a path just wide enough for pack animals. 

Rain still fell in sheets that couldn’t touch them, the occasional bolt of electricity lighting up the sky, thunder causing the ground beneath their feet to vibrate. 

The worst part was the wind that pushed against them, back down the path.

Time to use the link, kid. We’re going to get as close as we can. You’re going to create an illusion that makes everything look the same. I will sneak, slowly and silently, to the nest and extract the DNA. We get out, back to this spot, and apparate to the others. Easy as I was in college.”

The older witch squeezed the redhead's hand, looking back over her shoulder for a smile of confirmation.

Disgusting. Now, back to Zora. I felt a spark with her. I even told her everything! She doesn’t think I’m crazy. I flew into the air to demonstrate, and she caught me when I deactivated my flight.”

They dug their fingertips into the rocky cliffside, calluses forming in their fight against the wind. Their feet slipped on the water-slickened ledge despite the grips on their boots. Agatha was acutely aware of every extraneous noise they created, desperately praying they did not alert the threat ahead.

Do you want to pursue some type of relationship with her once we get back home? Vision will be out of the picture. She knows about the boys?

Agatha yanked Wanda into her side, pressing them into the jagged rocks when a particularly harsh gust of wind whistled past. 

It didn’t matter how much magick Wanda contained; she was still Agatha’s child first, and according to the state of California and her poorly guarded heart, it was her duty to protect her, even if it meant a few cuts and bruises to the face.

Relaxing after the gust passed, they slowly closed in on their destination, mere feet away from the scaly defenders of the treasure she was seeking. 

I do, Aggie. I really do. Viz and I ended long ago, so I feel ready. The boys? Yes, I told her, and she wants to meet them. I just hope she’s serious and it wasn’t the hormones and adrenaline talking.

 

You know I’ll be there for both outcomes.

The woman in the lead stopped so abruptly that Wanda collided with her back. Agatha held out a hand, surveying the scene. She was thankful they were at least dry and couldn’t feel the rain. Small miracles. The glorious creatures before her, unfortunately, looked miserable. 

Showtime. Stay out of sight, create the illusion with your mind, not with your hands. I’ll see what’s real. Be careful, crouch down to shield from the wind if you need to.

 

You’re good to go, hurry! 

 

Oh, Superstar?

 

Yes? Wanda’s internal voice wavered slightly. 

 

We only have one shot at this. If any of these beauties even sense something is slightly off, it’s over.

Agatha heard the gulp and the shake of her voice.

I’ll keep you safe, maminka.

Agatha kept her breath steady. She knew this was risky, not just that, but stupid. Idiotic even.

Reckless.

She should take the egg, zap across the island, extract the fluid, and send it back.

However, that could harm the creature inside that, and she wouldn’t do that to any parent, human or otherwise.

This was the safest, most humane option for the tiny reptile inside that shell. 



Wanda was afraid even to breathe, watching the great Agatha Harkness silently sneak past ancient creatures to a well-guarded nest. She could only hope this was over quickly. She couldn’t lose another parent because of Evanora Harkness.

Not Again.



Agatha Harkness always loved a good heist. Growing up as an heiress, she often tested her boundaries, like rich kids, all kids really, tend to do. However, it wasn’t just to see what she could get by with. She wanted to know what her evil mother was up to: robbing museums to return stolen artifacts, paying off houses and debt anonymously. Maybe she kept a few things for herself. She wasn’t all good.

She had also been a victim of abuse, and she did everything she could to get back at her mother. If that meant stealing from a few of Evanora’s business associates, so be it.  

It was those skills that she was falling back on. If she couldn’t use magick, then pure skill it was, careful not to make a single noise. Thankfully, she didn’t have security systems and armed guards to deal with. 

The nest itself had been built far enough back on the slippery, rocky outcropping to prevent anything from blowing away or being easily knocked off. The eggs themselves were perfectly aligned, facing up like giant teeth, ready to tear her apart.

Selecting an egg placed close enough to an escape route, she pulled the first syringe from her cargo pocket. Uncapping it, the needle plunged through the shell as quietly as possible, into the sac just beyond. She quickly filled, removed, recapped, and replaced it in the opposite pocket: one down, five to go. 

The ever-alert Troodons didn’t suspect a thing, allowing Agatha’s body to relax slightly.

There were only two left when another gust of gale-force wind whipped past them. Agatha stilled, hands gripping the nest so tightly that her knuckles went white, praying to the Divine Mother that they would make it through. 

Two things happened.

Wanda’s focus faltered for seven point two seconds. Seven point two seconds too long.

One of the branches in Agatha’s grip snapped, the sound echoing against the mountainside around her.

NO NO NO! Agatha! I’m sorry! Wanda’s words repeated in her head.

A nearby adult troodon, always attentive, snapped its head around to her and tilted it. Reality warped again, the human disappearing from its sight. Plunging the last two needles in the egg, she worked as quickly as she could, feeling them trot closer.

I’m perfectly okay, Wands. We’re okay. She soothed, trying to keep her thoughts calm and clear.

She could hear their breathing, feel them sniffing the air around her.

Angel, go. Now. I’ll be right behind you. 

Hurriedly, she removed the syringes, capped them, and slowly placed them in her pocket when she felt the breath against her skin. 

Chompers needed a breathmint.

No, we’re getting out of here!

To Agatha’s horror, she heard Wanda’s voice yell out. “Over here!” She knew Wanda well enough to know that she was jumping around and casting magick haphazardly.

Just like in one of the sitcoms she loved so dearly.

GO!

Confirming Agatha’s fears, it worked. The predators, though smart, went to assess the new potential threat, one they could see and hear. 

A tingling sensation shot through her, every hair standing on end. This was her chance to get both of them out, though the chances were still reasonably low.

Don’t hate me, I love you. Adopting you and Pietro was one of the easiest decisions I ever made, moje dieťa.

She snapped, sending Wanda to the others, right as the lightning hit the drenched, rocky ledge—the perfect conductor for the electric current. 

Agatha flew back like she weighed nothing. Chunks of rock exploded around her, bursts of white light filled her head, and a loud whine echoed within her skull. 

This was her chance. The second snap was weak, but she urged it to be enough.  

🦕🦖🦕

Lily sat in the back of the Jeep, willing her power to come back faster. She knew the complicated history between her mom and Dr. Sattler; however, she also knew the blonde scientist would never intentionally harm her. Ellie was the person who let her ‘help’ with projects around the lab when Agatha was away, and Uncle Ian had kid duty. The summer she was six, and her mom was in South Africa, Ellie made sure Lily had all of her favorite snacks wherever she was.

The history was between Ellie and her mom. She would never admit it, but deep down, Agatha Harkness trusted Ellie Sattler with her kids’ lives, and that meant something.

When she saw Ellie with Vision, she almost burst into tears until she read the note on her phone.

 

Don’t worry, Lilypad, he only thinks I’m working with him. The antidote is for everything. Play along until you link with your mom or Wanda.

Be a Harkness sweetheart.

I’ll get you out of this.

 

Lilith never truly liked Vision from the moment Wanda brought him to dinner all those years ago. When she received Nicky's text with the evidence that Vision was working with Evanora, that sealed it.

Wanda didn’t need to divorce him. They needed to let a T-Rex use him as a chew toy. If she had learned only one thing from Agatha Harkness, it was that you didn’t go against the family.

They stopped at a power station, Vision climbing out. Ellie looked at him skeptically. “You know how to do this, Viz?”

“Yes, I know how to do it, thank you.” He spat, stomping off toward the door.

“How’s the magick coming along, sweetie?” Ellie asked, turning around in her seat.

Lilith wiggled her fingers, the magick sputtering. “Nothing yet. I don’t understand why.”

“So no little mailbox dimensions yet, I’m guessing?”

“How’d you know about that?” Lily asked, mouth agape.

“I know a lot more than you think. At least he used his badge, which will ping before he cuts the power.” Ellie found a Sharpie in her bag and hopped out of the Jeep. On the door, she drew an arrow and a plane—a message. 

What was taking this man so long? The teen thought.

Climbing back into the car, she looked back at Lily. “Did he hurt you?”

“I’m a little nauseous and shaken up, especially after the motorcycle.”

“Good, because if he had hurt you…”

“I know.” Her eyes squinted at the message on the door. “Aren’t you worried he’ll see it?”

“Are you?” Ellie quirked a brow.

Lily laughed quietly. “Not at all.”

The sound of the power being deactivated silenced her laughter. The lack of the floodlight above plunged them into total darkness, the rain seeming to grow louder without the constant hum in the air.

Vision slammed the car door closed, blonde hair slightly singed. “To the airport,” He mumbled angrily, burns on his hand. 



Ellie held back her laughter as they slowly drove toward the airport, aware of the mud and storm. 

“Can’t you go any faster?” His voice was tense, clearly losing his temper.

“I can barely see through this rain, and it’s dangerously muddy.” Ellie kept her voice calm. Even if it were clear, she’d stall as much as she could, buying every witch on the island as much time as they needed.

He turned to the backseat. “Can’t you do anything?”

“You drugged me, remember?” The look on Lily’s face revealed every thought she had about the man.

“FUCK!” He shouted, rummaging through the bag at his feet. “Evanora didn’t give me anything to help regain magick.” He turned to the driver’s seat. “Do you have anything useful?” 

“I’ve got a few potions in case of emergency. Usually takes thirty minutes though.” Ellie supplied. 

“If I take twice the dose, it will cut the time in half.” Lily offered, cutting her eyes to Ellie. A look that clearly said I am winging something I probably should not wing. 

A look she had seen from Agatha thousands of times.

“Do it.” Viz ordered.

Lily reached for Ellie’s bag, conveniently placed on the seat next to her, knowing precisely what she was looking for. 

“Are you sure you can trust her to only get what she needs?” He asked, suspicion in his voice.

Ellie huffed. “I’ve known her since birth, of course, I can.” 



Lilith knew Ellie trusted her, not just to use her quick wit and stupidly high IQ, but to use her common sense and everything she had learned from the adults around her. She quickly took the two capsules that would not only shorten her healing time but also amplify her power. Ellie kept them on her primarily for Agatha. The youngest Harkness was currently grateful for the sapphic inability to let go of an ex. 

Using the sleight of hand that had to be a family trait at this point, she slipped a knife into her pocket before taking a bottle of water and swallowing the pills. “Just give me a few minutes, and I’ll be ready to go.” She smiled, ready to cause her own Harkness-style chaos.   

🦕🦖🦕

Rio had cheated. While she knew the dangers of haphazardly portaling and poofing about the island right now, she also knew they only had so much time. It’s why she had called Dr. Malcolm and asked for his location before appearing right next to him.

Zora had nodded in approval at the new method of travel.

“Get in, Doc.” She instructed.

“Agatha and Wanda?” He asked wearily, clambering into the back.

Rio’s jaw tightened. “Errands.” The disapproval radiated from her in waves.

“Ah. Evanora related, no doubt.” He attempted to wipe his glasses off with his drenched shirt before the blonde in the passenger seat handed him a dry towel.

“Power’s out, your storm is slowing everything down. Viz’s badge was swiped at the power station before he shut everything down. I told Ralph that the boy was no good.” He shook his head like the disapproving father he was. “Thankfully, we have generators for most buildings and parts of the island, but not for some of the enclosures. I’ve sent people to various areas for containment.” He quickly explained, as if the cosmic being cared. “Power being out means no cell service. I say we go there first, see if we can tell where they’re—”

The Jeep lurched, settling in front of the darkened power station. “...headed.” Ian finished. “Well, that’s one way to get places.”

“We don’t have time. We have to find Lily, get all of us back together.” Rio stated, words laced in concern and something else she refused to label.

“You know, if you would just accept the bond, accept her for who she is…” He began, the concerned sister-wife he was.

“Cosmic beings aren’t supposed to have soulmates!” Bristling as she stepped out of the car. The movement was comforting, needed. 

Malcolm followed closely behind. “There aren’t supposed to be dinosaurs running around in 2020, either! You don’t see me being a whiny little bitch about it!” 

“Or witches!” Zora added, looking at Dr. Malcolm in shock.

“SHIT HAPPENS! You should be used to it!” Malcolm yelled at Death herself.

The three bickering adults stopped in front of the door where Ellie had left her rough drawing, the rain not touching any of them. 

“Airport!” Zora exclaimed as they scrambled back to the vehicle, Rio apparating them once more.

This time, they didn’t appear next to the building; they were tucked among the trees on a hill, looking down at the airport. Close enough to see and form a plan.

They quietly exited the vehicle once more. Standing in front of it, arms crossed, staring at the airport sitting below them.   

Ian Malcolm tilted his head at Death. He had worked with the woman in different settings for years, though he hadn’t known her true nature until five years prior.

It never changed how he viewed her or how he spoke to her. He practically shared a husband with Agatha Harkness. The man had very little fear left. “Is that why you won’t accept it? Create the bond? Because you’re not supposed to have a soulmate?” He turned to face the so-called ‘entity’ skepticism etched on his face. “Ralph and I, everyone in that house, really, have listened to her go on and on for years. You are a cosmic being, god-up, damn.”

Rio clicked her tongue. “It’s not that easy, there are rules, a balance, a—”

He flicked her skull like an upset mother. “None of that should matter. Entity or not. You are in a metaphorical time-out until you come to your senses.”

Zora was impressed. “Did you just put Death in timeout?”

“I work with dinosaurs, Zora. This isn’t a big leap. Now, a plan.”

It didn’t matter what the plan was; it was thrown out of the window when Wanda appeared, falling to her knees, screaming Agatha’s name. Her eyes were swollen with tears, her face littered with cuts and bruises, clothing smeared with dirt, hair blowing wildly in the wind.

Zora closed the distance quickly, skidding to her knees in the drenched grass and wrapping her arms around her.

Malcolm was a second behind. If Wanda was back sans Agatha, it could only mean one thing. 

All four of them looked to the mountain when the sky lit up from the explosion.

“No!” Wanda wailed, nails digging into Zora’s arm, head leaning against Malcolm’s chest.

Malcolm shook his head. “She’s alive unless it was a direct hit.”

Rio nodded, confirming, “She’s alive.”



A groan from a few feet away alerted them all. The group turned, and there, head in her hands, was Agatha. “Turn down the lightning, will ya, Sparky?” She smirked at Rio.

Wanda broke free from the arms wrapped around her, scrambling to Agatha. The woman nearly slammed her head into Agatha’s chest to make sure her heart was beating normally. “I said I wouldn’t leave,” she sobbed. “You even said you would find another way.” The younger witch began to hiccup violently. “Then you MADE ME. You SENT ME AWAY.” She was filled with anger and hurt, though she knew full well that she would have done the same damn thing.

 

 

“I couldn’t let you get hurt, too. I knew you would never leave on your own.” She pressed her lips to the top of Wanda’s head. “I will always choose you kids over myself. Every single time.”  

She focused on the generator-powered airport nestled below them, the storm still raging. Their presence had definitely been noticed by now. “I believe we no longer have the element of surprise going for us. We’ve got to get moving.” Her eyes moved to Rio. “Can I get some assistance, please?”

All she needed from Rio was healing, yet what happened was completely unexpected. Wanda rose and stepped away, back to Zora’s arms.
