Death helped the witch to her feet, a tear rolling down her cheek. She leaned in, kissing Agatha with a certainty and heat neither had felt before. 

Agatha’s lips parted, allowing Rio to probe the inside of her mouth, their tongues curling around each other, when the feelings crashed over her in waves.

In that moment, it was just them. Everything and everyone else fell away. Agatha carded her hands through Rio’s hair. They gasped into each other’s mouths when it happened. 

Everything Rio felt, her thoughts, her memories, images, all of it flowed through her.

Best of all? Her voice.

I’m so sorry, Agatha. I never should’ve held back from you.

 

What changed your mind? She was shocked that this hardheaded skeleton of hers had accepted their fate, and damn if it didn’t make her melt.

 

Ian and Zora. Seeing how you do put others before yourself. The amazing mother that you are. How I felt when I thought you were gone. How I felt when I knew you weren’t.

Wanda cleared her throat before Agatha could even form her reply. “I love this for you both, I really do, but I think I heard a plane engine start. There’s still a crazy storm, and we have to go.” 

Agatha pulled away, conjuring her bag from thin air, taking the syringes from her cargo pocket, and placing them in a metal box in the backpack. She took out the vial of potion and mixed the DNA into it. With a few tendrils of magick and words from the Darkhold, it was nearly ready to be delivered to Evanora, only missing one more ingredient. She placed it back into the bag and stood upright. 

“What have you all been waiting for? Why aren’t you all down there?” She pointed impatiently.

“The plan, your highness.” Ian deadpanned.

She opened her mouth to explain when the aircraft began rolling out of the hangar.

“New plan, don’t let the plane leave the island.” She directed, taking to the sky with Wanda close behind.

The two humans and Death clambered back into the Jeep and took off. 

“You’re not taking flight?” Ian questioned.

“Too many near misses.” Death did not elaborate.  




Vision’s head turned the millisecond he heard his wife’s big mouth. “She always was excessively loud.” He muttered, uncovering the Cessna 172, owned by one Evanora Harkness, and loading it with their gear.

“Because she was faking it.” Lilith spat. 

Vision did something he had never done before in his life.

He struck her across the face, his wedding ring slicing her cheek open.

Lily’s eyes burned the same purple as her mother’s, her weakened magick shooting forward, and dying centimeters away from him.

He sneered, showing off the magickal blocker. “I came prepared, you little brat.

Ellie stepped forward, ready to lunge. To murder him in cold blood. 

Her feet felt heavy, as if slowed. She whipped her head toward the determined teenager who shook her head just slightly and knew in that moment they were still playing this sick twisted game.

He descended upon Lilith quickly, grabbing her by the hair. “I am so tired of you insubordinate, disobedient women. Ever since I met you, I’ve had to deal with your sass.” He threw her to the floor. “You’re lucky I need you, because your grandmother gave me permission to kill you.” He kicked her in the side for good measure, knowing the cavalry had yet to head their way.

“VISION!” Ellie yelled, finally gripping his shirt collar. “She’s just a kid! You’re wasting time! Find the fucking keys and start it up!”

Vision stomped away, much like a scolded child, as she rushed to the child curled up on the concrete. “Lilypad, I’m so sorry.” Her hands hovered over the teen, biting back tears.

Spitting out blood, Lilith painfully raised to a sitting position. “I’ll be fine. I’m sure I’ll get to Aunt Jen eventually.” 

The look she gave Ellie nearly broke her. 

“You couldn’t do anything, he still thinks you’re working together, help me to the plane.” She took a breath and then hissed. “There’s a green potion in your first aid kit. I need that. Then, once in the plane, since it belongs to my cunt of a grandmother, I need the silver flask.”  

Helping the injured girl over and into the plane, she found the elixir and flask and handed them both to her. “What are they for?”

“The potion is a painkiller and healing potion; the flask is a painkiller of the grain-based variety.” Lily’s eyes held a spark of mischief, even in her current state. 

Ellie shook her head at the younger girl’s shenanigans while quickly taking in the plane. Ellie wasn’t an expert, but she knew the Cessna wouldn’t hold up in this weather. She could only pray the party would be crashed before she had to take to the sky.

“What about Vision?” Lilith’s words lightly slurred.

Ellie was starting to wonder how safe all this elixir mixing was. 

Ellie’s mouth curled into a sneaky smile. “The keys are right here,” she held up the keys and let them jangle. “Flip those switches, please.”

Vision ran out of the office of the small hangar, swearing and waving his arms in an attempt to flag them down.

“Check the link, let your mom know we’re on the plane and Vision isn’t. We’re safe for now.”

 

 

Lilith nodded, reaching out to her mother.

Nothing.

“Not yet. It’s like all I hear is static.”

The door to the plane slammed shut, Vision’s voice sending shivers down her spine. “Where were you two going?” He asked furiously. 

 

 

Damn, didn’t think he’d have the guts to climb up without a stepladder. She thought to herself. “Didn’t you hear me say I found the keys?” Ellie asked in confusion. I was pulling onto the runway to start the propeller. Lily is my copilot because she’s learning to fly.” The older scientist explained. 

Vision nodded. “No funny business.” His eyes narrowed in suspicion.

Ellie continued to pray to every god she knew of that this would keep working and that the baby witch beside her would finally reboot. Taxiing down the runway, the small aircraft was pummelled by rain and buffeted off course by the wind. 

Ellie was surprised when she took to the air, even though they were dangerously low. She snuck a glance at the girl next to her. The younger Harkness’s eyes were half-lidded, feeling the effects of everything she had been through. She was trying, but it wasn’t her. 

Ellie had a flashback to college when Agatha had taken several ‘quick-fix’ potions to stave off a hangover and a night of mischief. That hadn’t gone so well either.

Great, they had yes, anded too close to the sun.

 

 

Agatha watched in horror from where she hovered above the ground as the plane careened along the slippery runway, afraid to interfere. She didn’t want to make it worse, especially when she had no way to communicate.

Static filled her brain, like the CB radios and walkie-talkies used across the island. The connection between her and Lilith was weak, as though her magick was coming back online. 

Mom. Plane. Ellie. Good. Help.

Wanda’s brow furrowed beside her. “Did you get that?” 

“Not really, but we’ll figure it out. Just take it down safely. Vision’s the only one we hurt.” Agatha sliced through the rain toward the quickly, though wobbly, ascending aircraft.

Brutally. Wanda added.

Agatha smirked, silently landing in the center of the wing.

Sweetheart, I’m on top of the plane. Only broadcast to me. Circle back to the runway. Land.



Glancing back at Vision, Lily caught Dr. Sattler’s attention and spoke in a whisper, “Turn around, land. Mom is on top of the plane.” She hadn’t heard her mom’s entire message, but she had heard enough.

 

 

Ellie nodded, banking hard to the left to make a U-turn in weather she had no business making one in.

Vision yelped, landing hard against the side of the plane.

“Did we forget to activate the seatbelt sign?” Lily smirked.

“WHAT ARE YOU DOING!” he shouted, face bright red, veins protruding from his neck. The plane straightening and descending simultaneously.

“It’s too dangerous, Vision! Lily will have to portal us!” Ellie stressed, throwing her hands in the air and playing to her audience.

“I don’t think we want the same things, Ellie.” His lip curled, removing the gun from his holster.

“This plane is runed.” Lily gasped, finally seeing the markings covering the interior around them.

Ellie took that as her cue. 

She didn’t land the plane. She crashed it.



The witch launched herself from the plane as soon as she sensed the runes.

Landing, Agatha sprinted toward the flaming plane, hoping the rain would dull the heat. Help them apparate Lily and Ellie out of there. Yet they were tethered to the vehicle itself.

The actual seatbelts must’ve been runed as well. Only Evanora Harkness would be this thorough. 

Agatha felt in her soul that Lily was struggling, perhaps dying. She turned back to Rio, begging. “Please, my love! Don’t do this!”

Rio raised her hands and shook her head. To Agatha, it meant she refused to do anything. 

“I can’t put it out!” Wanda screeched.

“RUNES!” Agatha wailed, running towards the flames, coming to a halt just beyond their reach.

Witches walk out of flames, not into them.

It was a biological fear, one she tried in that moment to overcome.

Instead, she was shoved aside by Ian as he sprinted into the fire, winking over his shoulder.

Agatha’s wails were primal. Lily, Ian, Ellie.

Zora ran into the wreckage behind him, Wanda protesting, stopping next to Agatha. The same biological fear, mixed with her own childhood trauma, keeping her from going further.

Silhouettes appeared in the flames, four figures apparating far enough away from the wreckage to be safe. Agatha was there to catch Lily, both of them gently collapsing onto the wet ground.

“Hi, Mom.” She greeted her.

“Oh, my sweet girl.” She smoothed her smoke-filled hair. “Are you okay?”

“Of course I was, Mom. Uncle Ian and Ellie always make sure I’m okay.” She coughed, grimacing as she placed a hand to her rib. “Wanda really needs to divorce Viz. I have evidence he’s working with Evanora. On my phone. It’s in a pocket dimension.” She coughed again. “I need water.”

As if summoned, Zora handed her a bottle from her pack, smiling softly at her.

Lily looked her up and down. “When we’re not in danger, we’re having a discussion about your intentions with my big sister.” 

The mercenary laughed. “Okay, kid, deal.”

Agatha held her youngest tightly, careful to mind her ribs. She smiled through her tears. “Good work.” The older witch, the woman people said was heartless, cried into her daughter’s hair. Looking over at Ian and Ellie, she mouthed her thanks.

“Okay,” She scrambled to her feet, pulling Lily to hers. “We need to find Vision and dispose of him, and then I have an errand to run. You can now turn the storm off, my love, and restore power.” She sent Rio a hard look, unhappy at her lack of intervention.

The storm finally came to an end, the clouds disappearing to reveal the clear night sky. 

Wanda waved her hand, restoring all electricity across the island.

“Ian, radio or call base and tell them we found Lily and that Vision is still missing. You will call Ralph before the end of the night.”

“Rio, I need you to take these two,” she gestured between Ellie and Lily, “To base and get them to the medbay. Take Zora with you. Red, with me.”

“Where are you going, Ags?” Death’s voice was neutral. 

“To find Vision, of course.” The witch shrugged. “Get them settled, and then you can find us.” They both knew she was only partially telling the truth.




Growing tired of listening to adults, Lily had wandered back to the plane the moment her mother had pulled her to her feet. She was still running on adrenaline and picked up one of the extinguishers that Zora had found to put out the flaming wreckage. Circling to the rear of the plane, where Ellie’s backpack would be, protected and untouched, she began spraying it down with foam, being careful of her ribs. She wanted to retrieve the bags, especially her abductor’s. 

She felt him before she saw him, her senses finally returning. Delayed due to mixing the various tonics. 

Whirling around with a hiss of pain, she raised the canister to connect with his jaw and allowed the momentum to carry her around until she was at the right angle to sprint around the tail of the plane.

Suddenly, all she felt was the urgency to get back to safety. 

She made a wide arc to bypass the wing. Opening her mouth to call out to the adults, the deranged man tackled her from behind, clapping his hand over her mouth.

Slipping in the rain-soaked grass, she curled in on herself, reaching for the hidden dagger and bit into the flesh attempting to silence her.

She refused to apparate away, attacking her abuser instead, focusing on causing pain instead of feeling it.



Ellie knew Vision had stayed close. He was too angry not to. She didn’t want to hang around for Agatha and Rio’s spat. The bruises all over Death’s neck were entirely more than she needed to know, and she didn’t need to witness any more being created. 

Instead, she joined the effort to put out the wreckage, ignoring her own injuries that she knew needed to be checked out. Nothing was getting done until Agatha and Rio came to an agreement, or fucked again. 

A flash of wild brunette hair in her peripheral caught her attention. Her feet were moving before she even realized it. Watching in horror as she propelled herself toward Lily, she screamed out as the young girl went down. The blade in her hand gleamed as she twisted and caught Vision in the chest with it before hitting the ground. 

Lily regained her footing and closed the distance between them when the scientist saw Vision move and reach out.

The shot rang out as she shoved Lily out of the way, only to hear two more shots and feel herself spun around and pushed to the ground.

Blinking her eyes open, breathless from hitting the ground so hard and knowing she and Lilith would be sporting matching cracked ribs, she was greeted by the sight of Agatha above her.

“I always thought I’d be happier to see you above me again. Now I’m a little afraid of Rio.” She wheezed.

“I just took a fucking bullet for you, and that’s what you say to me?” The witch smirked.

“MOM! ELLIE!” Lily screamed. “Those were WITCHKILLER bullets!” The teen had gone pale, tears streaming down her face.

“No, they aren’t. They were fake. Evanora didn’t trust him not to kill Agatha accidentally.” Ellie scoffed. The entire scene proving her point.

Agatha rose to her feet and helped the scientist up, wrapping her arms around her. “I just saved your life, though, so are we good? Did I prove that I do still love you? That I care about you?” Agatha whispered into her ear. “You’ve been worse than one of the L Word lesbians for the past…” She tried to calculate and simply couldn’t at the moment. “Too fucking long. We can hash things out later, but I know what you are to my kids and family. Stop trying to die on me, okay?”

Ellie nodded into her shoulder before pulling away, “Just, make sure Rio knows I’m not a threat.” Looking around, she tilted her head. “Where’d the other two shots come from?”

“He kidnapped Lilypad,” Malcolm replied gruffly, placing his weapon back in the hidden holster. 

“Had to make sure he didn’t get away again. Agatha, he’s losing blood quickly; you and Wanda don’t have much time to interrogate.” Zora added.

Ellie took her cue and turned to Rio. “Dr. Vidal, I think it’s time we head back to base.”



Agatha had almost lost entirely too many people in a span of very few hours. She wasn’t entirely sure how it was only eight pm. This would make the perfect time to strike at Evanora’s mansion in Munich, but first, she needed to see a dinosaur about a man.

Hugging her youngest child one last time, she turned to Rio. “My love…”

“I am taking everyone to the compound, and then I will be there.” She flexed her jaw, looking away from Agatha.

“Thank you for protecting me. I barely even bled.”

“Perks of being connected.” She bit into her lip, as though she were physically restraining the words, before walking away.

Agatha made sure everyone had either left or was about to go before turning to Wanda. 

Wanda took Agatha’s hand. “Let’s get the information from Vision that we need.”

“I have a better idea.” Her smile was violent as she turned and stalked toward the man writhing in pain. 

Rio had slowed the bleeding for them.

Thank you, feathers. 

 

I do NOT condone this, nor do I believe what you’re about to do is right. Rio’s voice sounded defeated.

 

I know.

She used her foot and nudged him over onto his back. “Someone has been a very naughty boy, Vision.” She mocked.

Vision’s eyes widened as he struggled to speak.

“Oh, don’t bother, dear son-in-law of mine. I will allow you to speak when I need you to.”

 

Wanda knelt on the other side of him, faking pity. “I got to thinking, divorce is so damaging to kids. Dad going down in a plane crash? Not as bad. Quick, sudden. Who tries to fly in a storm?” 

“Zora is a much better fit for Wanda anyway.” She raised Vision’s bracelet-clad arm. “But first, we have to remove this thing. Thankfully for you, it’s partially cracked and not welded shut. We don’t even have to chop your wrist off.” 

Easily snapping it with her fingers, she let the remains of the bracelet fall to the ground in pieces. “Well, we should get going then. I have some apologies to make.” Both witches placed a hand on Vision’s shoulders, finding themselves back on the mountainside, except on a broader ledge this time.

The mother-daughter team propped Vision against the mountainside, making sure he was still on his feet and that his weight was on the knee Zora had shot. Agatha rendered him immobile, standing inches in front of him, Wanda slightly to her side.

“Now, I am going to ask you a few questions, and we’ll see how useful you can be. If you provide information, you might live.” Agatha dragged a nail down Vision’s forehead to the end of his nose and tapped it. “Also, I made it impossible for you to lie; you can only move your head, and you’ve been stabbed, shot twice, and in a plane crash. You will not survive without our help.”

Vision nodded, terrified.

“Good boy.” She patted him on the head.

“First, why did you betray my oldest daughter?”

“I was tired of being the joke of the family. I knew she didn’t love me anymore. She married me only because she had to. Evanora told me.” He spat.

Wanda shook her head. “That’s only partially true. I was in love with you once. When we were young. I’m not in love with you anymore, and I was keeping up with appearances until we kill Evanora tonight. However, you’re not the best husband or father. I do prefer women, AND you sided with pure evil.”

Vision scowled. 

“Next question,” Agatha continued. “Why side with Evanora?” 

Vision scoffed. “She was going to let me run her archaeology endeavors. Leave the fortune to me.”

“She was going to kill you once she could safely kill me, dumbass.” She deadpanned. “Well, whatever helps you sleep at night. Why did she want Lily?” 

“She didn’t. I did that on my own.”

Wanda sighed, eyes raised to the night sky, “Gods, you’re a moron.” 

“I know you’re not lying, so you really are just that stupid. I can tell Ellie wasn’t in on any of it. She crashed that plane on purpose and took a calculated risk.”

Wanda cut her eyes to Agatha.

Lily said the plane was runed right before Ellie crashed it. She knew that if Evanora had been the one to rune that plane, she would have put a protection rune in there so no one would die in something she owned. She also knew that the non-witches would come in to get them because we rely so much on magick and have a genuine aversion to fire.

Wanda laughed slightly under her breath.

Turning back to the man in front of her, she asked what she wanted to know most. “What’s Evanora’s plan with Eternity?”

“Who?” Vision looked between his captors, genuinely confused. “I know she’s trying to take over the world, but she only tells me what to do. Not her plans. You can check my communication with her.”

“FUCK! That gives me NOTHING!” She shot a bolt of Magick into the rocks next to his head. They exploded outwards, cutting the side of his face, even though he had tilted his head to the side. “I am losing TIME, VISION. Evanora sure as shit isn’t going to tell me much!”

Wanda put a comforting hand on Agatha’s shoulder. “Aggie, it’s time. Do you want to siphon?”

She tilted her head, considering it. “No, I’d rather offer him as an apology and then capture the reaper soul I need since Death will not be the one collecting tonight.”

The archeologist in front of them went ghostly pale as he tried to speak. 

“Oh, we don’t need your words anymore, son. You will be more useful dead than alive. Up we go.” 

Wanda tilted her head, “We wanted to give the boys a really cool story, for when they’re older, of course.”

Taking flight, with Vision in tow, they flew around to the Troodon nest from earlier. 

Angel, do you want to witness this? You can wait for me at the top of the mountain.

She didn’t want to force Wanda to watch her husband be ripped to shreds. While educational, it would still be traumatizing. She was even willing to find the least traumatic thing to tell her grandsons. Whatever was best and the least traumatic.

I’m staying, I’ll cast an illusion for when the reaper comes to make sure you’re not interrupted by any curious predators. 

They hovered slightly out of sight from the creatures, now even more alert than before, still shaken by the earlier lightning strike. Agatha glided closer, inch by inch, floating her gift in front of her. All heads turned at once, curious about this injured prey that was being given to them. 

“Sorry about earlier, guys. Thanks for the assist.” She offered, as they pounced upon their prey the moment he hit the ground and screamed.

The predators' calls quickly overtook the screams. It wasn’t an attack necessarily. They were defending the nest that had been disturbed earlier. 

It was the third bite that killed him. Agatha was thankful that the dinosaur bodies shielded the view, and all Wanda saw from her distance was a bit of blood spray. 

Agatha, however, was closer, waiting for the reaper. The grin on her face when one of the pack swallowed Vision’s head whole was terrifying.

Agatha Harkness’s main rule? Don’t fuck with her family. Especially her kids.

She took pure delight in punishing those who did. 

The reaper didn’t even register Agatha’s presence before it crumpled to the ground, her soul-reaping dagger, much like Rio’s, having severed its soul from its body. She had gotten lucky, catching the other recently deceased soul in the process. She sealed them both in reinforced, runed vials. 

Time to go, Red.

 

 

The view near the top of the mountain was breathtaking and perfect for what they needed. Agatha plucked their backpacks from the dimensional locker she had stored them in and retrieved her special concoction for Evanora. Handing it to Wanda, she brought out the two soul vials. “I love cargo pockets.” She mused. “Heat that up, please.” 

Wanda did as she was told, floating the vial and wiggling her fingers to warm it, the ingredients liquefying. 

Agatha removed the top of the runed vial containing the reaper soul, knowing it wouldn’t be able to escape. Carefully mixing the heated elixir into it, vapors rose from the vial. She prepared three syringes. Making sure they would have backup if needed. Handing one to Wanda, she sent their bags away and steadied herself. “Rea—” 

“You don’t have to do this, Agatha.” Death’s voice cut through the night like a double-edged sword.

Agatha clutched her arms around her stomach. “Yes, I do, Rio. You don’t understand.”

“The connection burned out the rest of the potion. The bullet barely damaged you. You’re IMMORTAL. Abandon this and just be with me.” Rio pleaded.

Agatha spun to face Rio. “It’s not that simple! FUCK I wish it was that simple! I have to do this, Rio, and you know it! Everyone else will tell you the same thing.” 

Wanda looked between both of them, lip quivering as though she was about to lose her soulmate.

“And the whole immortal clan?” Rio pressed.

Wanda’s eyes widened.

“YOU made me immortal! Yes, the family becomes immortal.” Agatha confirmed.

“Agatha was going to be immortal either way through the Darkhold.” Wanda offered.

They ignored her.

Green magick swirled around Agatha, attempting to prevent her from leaving the island.

“I can’t let you do this, Agatha! I have to protect the Sacred Balance, the natural order, to the best of my ability, no matter the cost.”

Agatha cackled. “Did you think I wouldn’t be at least one step ahead of you? That I hadn’t PREPARED for your inevitable betrayal? You’re so caught up in your values, you were going to let Lily DIE!”

“I knew she wasn’t going to, Agatha!” Rio defended.

“It doesn’t matter!” She shrieked. “You didn’t tell me that! You let me believe she might!” Agatha’s entire body was shaking. She craved the Rio she knew still existed. The one she had caught glimpses of. “Whatever. I am immune to your party tricks, Lady Death. I knew you had no intention of letting me kill Evanora.” She pulled out her pendant and held it up. “Remember this? Your magick flowing through my veins? Means I’m immune, my love. I can still affect you, but damn does it suck for you.” She held out her hand and touched the swirls of green, skin glowing as she absorbed them into her body. “Thanks for the boost.”

Rio gaped, sputtering on her words. “That, that should be impossible.”

“Did you doubt me?” Agatha asked before shooting a bolt of magick at the ground, activating a pentagram with Rio in the middle. “Now this, my dear, is a trap.”

Fury burning in her eyes, Death looked at her lover once more. “What are you doing, Agatha?”

Agatha Harkness stepped as close as she dared, closed her eyes, and swallowed past the lump in her throat. Her eyes burned from holding back tears; she knew this would be much harder to recover from. “Rio Vidal, Lady Death. I love you more than I even imagined possible, but I cannot allow you to stop me from doing this.” 

Turned to Wanda, the younger witch’s face blotchy, eyes swollen from crying, Agatha had to ask her something she never wanted to ask. “Sweetheart, she’s a cosmic entity, and your magick will hold her longer than mine. Reinforce it.”

“Aggie, I can’t do that, it’s Rio!” Wanda argued, one of the few times in her life she had ever disobeyed Agatha.

“DO IT, WANDA!” She barked.

“Do this and I will hate you forever!” Venom dripped from Rio’s every word.

Agatha’s eyes shut, letting that promise sink in.

“NOW, WANDA!”

Wanda looked over at Rio, defeat in her eyes as she raised her hands.

“It’s okay, Wanda, I won’t hold it against you,” Rio said gently, eyes boring into Agatha’s back. “She’s just like her mother. Forcing you to do something you don’t want to. Just like Evanora did to her. Continuing the generational trauma. Here I thought you were a good parent.”

Wanda’s anger flared, reinforcing the pentagram, eyes almost black. “Don’t you ever say that about her again.”

Rio hadn’t broken the bond, but Agatha’s heart disintegrated. The woman who always had to have the last word was silent. 

“Let’s go, Wanda.” She whispered, conjuring a portal, not wanting to apparate all the way to Munich.

🦕🦖🦕

Munich, Germany

Right before dawn

Dawn had yet to break in the German city of Munich when Agatha and Wanda stepped out of the portal, leaving Isla Dunblar behind them for the time being. 

Thankfully, their clothes had changed, and they both looked refreshed, even if they were anything but. Thank the Divine Mother for magick. Agatha buttoned the front of her blue, ankle-length coat, runes embroidered into the lining. “These are not cargo pants.” She said, looking down at the plum-colored trousers. She felt around for the potion she needed, locating the syringes in the inside pocket of her coat, and let out a sigh of relief before feeling for her locket, still safe around her neck. 

“Why do you look like a witchy superhero?” She asked Wanda, taking in the scarlet leather ensemble the universe had gifted her with. 

“I don’t know, why is Rio’s Death outfit so forest nymph-y?”

Agatha winced at the mention of the name.

“Aggie, I’m so sorry, what she said…”

Agatha held her hand up to silence her. “No, she was right. I should never have forced you to do something against your will. I’ve taught all four of you the importance of consent, and I am so sorry, Angel. If you don’t want to do this, if you want to go back right now, I understand.”

“No. This has to be done. Evanora will never stop until she’s dead. Sokovia no longer exists because of me. If she had never learned about Pietro and me, never known we existed or what we are? Things would be drastically different.”

Agatha shook her head vigorously, gathering Wanda in her arms. “No, Angel. I’ve told you this for most of your life. Nothing would have changed it. Lilia saw it. The Sorcerer Supreme saw millions of timelines. It was the same outcome. Now, we’re going to right as many wrongs as we can. I can do this myself if you want to sit out, though.”

“No. I want to watch the bitch suffer.” She wiped her face on her leather sleeve. 

Agatha looked around the alley, knowing they were near the gated mansion. “Follow me, Red. We need to sneak in the back and cut the power. We have to pass a few cameras and guards first.”

“I thought you said this was going to be hard?”

 

Shutting down power and knocking out the guards had been relatively easy. Agatha and Wanda had both warded their minds and were protected against Evanora’s Magick. 

What Agatha hadn’t planned on was Evanora being awake.

“Have the sinners come to repent?” She rasped from a corner in the darkened living room.

“Did you know that funding genocidal war is much higher on the ‘thou shalt not’ list than homosexuality, mother?” Agatha’s words were just as caustic as her mother’s.

“I knew that Ellie would betray me.” Evanora mused. “She never could get over you.”

“She was still in my life, mother, and more loyal to me than you.” Done with pleasantries, she looked at her nails, as though they were more interesting. “As much as we would love to stay and chat, we have more important things to do, so let’s make this quick.” Agatha glanced at Wanda, who transferred right behind Evanora and put her fingers on the old woman’s temples.”

Evanora looked bored. “Please, surely you know I’m protected.” 

“Surely you know that one’s a Cosmic Entity.” Agatha rebuffed.

It’s taking me longer to break through the wards. Wanda panted in her head.

Agatha stepped forward, stroking her locket and chanting in Latin. “Tell me, Mother, what are your plans with Eternity?”

“A snag in your plan? You’re stalling.”

Agatha circled Evanora, her finger barely moving.

Appearing quietly next to Wanda’s feet, Señor Scratchy, Agatha’s light brown and white rabbit familiar, provided her an extra boost. Cracking whatever extra protection Evanora had.

“No, mother, I just have questions before I kill you.”

Evanora barked a laugh. “Adding matricide to the list of your sins? You’ll never get away with it.”

The spirit witch smirked, nodding at Wanda to step back. Arching a brow, she apparated Evanora to her bed, Wanda and herself following.

“You see, mother, I already have. The headline will write itself. ‘Famous Billionaire Evanora Harkness Found Dead In Her Munich Home,’ it’ll say you died peacefully in your sleep from a heart attack. An autopsy and the toxicology reports will even back it up. Except that’s not what’s going to happen here.”

“I should’ve killed you the moment you left my body.” Evanora spat.

“Probably.” Agatha shrugged. “Now, what were your plans?” 

“You’re going to kill me, Agatha. I’m not telling you.”

Agatha stepped closer to the immobilized woman, “Why do you hate me?” She whispered desperately, the question of a hurt child.

“You were born evil. You cannot be good. You want to know why I did all those things? I was punishing the universe for giving me you.” Evanora hissed. “The pollution, the wars I funded, every backhanded dealing, not to mention everything I did to you—I did because I was cursed with you.” 

Evanora bared her teeth, rolling her eyes to look at Wanda. “I enjoyed hunting you, enjoyed murdering your parents. Your mother wasn’t actually dead when she was pulled from the rubble. I snapped her neck myself.”

 

Wanda’s eyes flared scarlet, her accent bleeding through. “I will fully enjoy seeing you experience everything you did to her. You thought you were burdening Agatha with us? It was the best thing you could’ve done in the situation.” 

Wanda stepped forward, her hand phasing through Evanora’s chest and gripping around her heart. “Agatha isn’t evil, you abomination. She’s the best person I know, despite being related to you.” She watched, gleefully, as Evanora’s breath slowed, finally releasing her heart and removing her spotless hand. No damage done.

 

“Teach me that,” Agatha stated, before turning back to her mother. “As much fun as this has been, mother, it’s time for you to die.”

“You’re not getting a penny, you know.” The old crone rasped. “I left it all to my corporations.”

 

Agatha’s lip curled, leaning into her mother, breath hot on the aged woman’s ear. “Good thing I learned from the best, mother. I made sure it’s all mine. I’m going to undo everything. Even open queer community centers with your blood money.”

The needle cleanly pierced the leathered skin, injecting the serum directly into the bloodstream. “This is going to hurt a lot.” The younger Harkness grinned, removing the syringe and putting it back in her pocket. Stepping back, she nodded at Wanda. 

 

Red magic sparking from her fingers, she tethered the bitch to her body, ensuring she felt everything.

Evanora’s jaw gaped open in a silent scream, frozen in a natural sleeping position, unable to thrash around. 

In her mind, she saw memories that didn’t belong to her, felt pain she inflicted either directly or indirectly. Every physical punishment she gave to Agatha, every time she starved her for days, she felt it.

Every verbal slur, putdown, method of psychological damage, cut her to the bone.

Iryna Maximoff’s neck snapping. The people she had ordered to be murdered.

Every casualty of a war she had funded because it had benefitted her.

Every person who had died from a weapon that one of her secret warehouses had produced.

Every victim of a cartel or trafficking ring in which she was directly or indirectly involved, or funding.

As the poisoned serum worked its way through Evanora and stole her final breath, Agatha took her dagger, severing the wretched soul and placing it in a reinforced vial to add to her small collection of souls that did not deserve to pass on.

 

Wanda’s eyes widened in horror. “That’s why she was trying to stop you! That’s what she meant!”

The older witch’s lips pressed together in a thin line, making sure to clear the room of fingerprints and any evidence. Silently walking through and out of the house, she put everything back in order, her familiar hopping behind her, nose twitching.

Restoring the power and cameras, complete with zero missing footage, she raised the guards one by one, wiping their memories and replacing them with new ones, urging one to check on their boss. 

Once back inside the alleyway, slumped against the wall, whole body shaking. “Yes, Wanda. That’s what she meant by disrupting the Sacred Balance. She knew I wouldn’t truly let Evanora pass on. I didn’t let Vision pass on either.”

 

Wanda looked down, considering the situations they had been put in by both of the deservedly deceased. “Aggie, the world is shades of gray. If she can’t see that right now, then she needs to get back to where she can before you can truly be together. I want you to be happy. I truly do, but the two of you are only going to be highly toxic until she can get away from this rigid line of thinking.” 

 

Falling into her second oldest, she sobbed. “Fuck, you’re so grown up.”

“Listen to the kid.” Lilia Calderu’s voice filled the alleyway. “Rough night?”

“You could say that, ya’ old bat.” Agatha wiped her tears away. “‘Bout time you showed up.”

“You had it handled.” She took Wanda’s arm. “Junior and I are going to do island clean-up and set Rio free. Do you want to come?”

Agatha looked away from the two sets of eyes that bore into her, nodding slowly. “I, I have to try.” 

 

🦕🦖🦕

 

Lilia and Wanda had gone to check on and oversee the proceedings at the compound. Agatha wanted to get her family home and try playing with dinosaurs another day.

She, however, portaled straight to where she left Lady Death, only to be greeted by one furious bearded dragon. 

“Victoria, you’re going to be picked up by something with wings and treated like a tasty snack. Where’s Lady Death?”

Swearing the reptile roared at her and called her a few select names. She watched, amused, as Scratchy hopped close to the oversized lizard and sniffed.

“Clearing, thanks, Scratch. Have fun with your new friend, I guess.” 

 

 

Rio dragged her hands through her hair, scraping her nails against her scalp. She knew she could have intervened once Victoria had set her free. The thing was, she couldn’t bring herself to do it.

She had felt everything Agatha was going through. The agony, fear, heartbreak. She had wept for her love. Yes, she had said terrible things before the witch left, but she hadn’t actually meant them, had she? 

What was happening to her? She was at war with herself, fighting against the very code she was made to carryout and protect.

The mark over her heart that had seared itself into her flesh the moment she had accepted her fate throbbed. Tugging the moment her witch had returned. She set her eyes on the clearing, waiting for the other brunette to appear.

“Agatha,” She breathed, the second the woman stepped into the clearing.

Rio hurried to her, stopping feet away from the woman, as if stopped by an invisible wall. “You said things that can’t be taken back, Rio.”

“I…” Rio inhaled deeply and lifted her chin. “You purposefully broke the Sacred Balance, and for what? Revenge?”

“It’s more than that, and you know it.” The much younger scientist said through clenched teeth. 

“Why did you come back, Agatha?” Rio had made up her mind. She had a duty to the universe. Cosmic beings weren’t supposed to have soulmates. Agatha would move on. 

“To tell you, we need to wait until you’re more you. The old you.” Agatha’s eyes filled with panic the second it happened. “I can’t feel you! Why can’t I feel you?” Her hand flew to her chest, to her mark. The pain of it fading made her scream out.

“Good-bye, Agatha.”

With that, Rio was gone. 

 

🦕🦖🦕

March 19th, 2021

Soria, Spain

 

Lilia Calderu and Jennifer Wu-Kale were at her bedside throughout the night. Wiping the sweat from her brow, holding her hand, monitoring her vitals.

They were the only ones who knew, spelled to secrecy. Sharon and Alice suspected, but didn’t dare say anything. 

A storm raged across the Spanish countryside, seemingly out of nowhere. The wind blew in the opposite direction, the rain warm and out of season.  

Random natural phenomena occurred globally.

Not quite the spring equinox, but right at the changing of the seasons. The promise of something new hung in the air, still held by winter’s icy grip. 

A waxing moon, full of promise, but not yet full.

Even through the pain, physical and emotional, there was no screaming. Only heavy, focused breathing, deep concentration, and the sheer will of one horse’s ass of a cosmic entity.

Rio squeezed Lilia’s hand tightly enough that the Sicilian witch was convinced it would break. 

Jen looked up from her position, nodding, “One more push.”

The storm peaked, the wind rattled the shutters, and the rain pelted the windowpanes.

A small cry cut through the air and silenced the world.

The storm dissipated immediately, clouds rolled back, and the night sky cleared. Moonlight filled the room, as though the Divine Mother herself was blessing the new life. 

Right before dawn, Lady Death gave birth to her and Agatha’s daughter.

Jen smiled down at the pink, bloody bundle and laid her on Rio’s naked chest. “She has her hair and chin.” The medical witch whispered.

“She’s beautiful. You did a wonderful job,” Lilia squeezed Rio’s shoulders, glaring at Jen.

“She has her eyes,” Rio’s voice was watery. “Amelia Jade Harkness-Vidal,” she whispered. “Out of death, life.” 

 

Chapter End Notes

So, how's everyone doing? Hannah says welcome to the afterlife, she's been waiting for other readers.

I am already working on the new chapter! Two weeks tops? Hopefully?

Come see me over on tumblr! DeathByLesbianWitches for updates, and to yell at me.

Chapter 5: Roar at Your Dinosaurs

Chapter Summary

The aftermath.
The reunification.

What happens now that Agatha has finally healed? Moved on?
It's been five years.

Enter Rio with the child she's kept a secret.

Chapter Notes

It's a rollercoaster.
Walk with me.

Thank you for trusting the process, and I hope this answers a lot of your questions!
So, originally this was 5 chapters; now there will be 7.
Also, I'm sure you've noticed, but the English teacher in me has decided all the punctuation will be used.

Thank you again to my dearest Hannah! I have now traumatized her enough that she has been contributing lines of her own, so...blame her for some of this.

Comments, kudos, and reblogs on Tumblr/threads give me life and motivation!

*Disclaimer: Despite taking five years of Spanish over twenty years ago, it's not the best. I know I mixed dialects (Spanish/Castilian and Latin American), which I did on purpose. I did use Google Translate, and I apologize if it's terrible or off.
I did, surprisingly, remember more than I thought.

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Harvard University, Cambridge, Massachusetts

August 18, 2025

“This is Maria Hill with CNN, reporting from what seems to be the epicenter of Mother Nature’s fury,” The reporter said excitedly into the camera, hair blown astray. “I’m here with one of the witnesses, Dr. Agatha Harkness, professor of Paleobiology at Harvard University. Professor, what was it like?” The award-winning journalist shoved the microphone into Agatha’s face for her response.

Agatha coolly pushed loose hair behind her ear, speaking clearly into the mic before her. “That’s world renowned expert in every field of paleontology, actually.” She straightened her collar, signature locket perfectly in place. Her hair was sticking up wildly, sweat dried on her brow, blood and dirt smeared across her face, clothes ripped.

She looked feral. 

She looked as though she were in her element.

With a tooth-baring smile, she looked straight into the camera, “You know, Ms. Hill, it was just the wildest thing.” She pressed a hand to her chest, feigning shock. “Another colleague and I were discussing a few matters regarding the upcoming semester when the entire building began to shake, quickly followed by tornado sirens.” Her eyes darted to Rio, talking to another newscaster across what had been the quad.

“Well, imagine my surprise when an alert came across my phone about a hurricane popping up unexpectedly! In the middle of August on a clear, sunny day.” She faked her surprise.

Lilia whispered something into the cameraman’s ear and nodded before raising her hands.

A translucent hourglass appeared, freezing time in its place.

“Hey! I was about to be a global sensation!” The witch cried out. “Women, sliding into my DM’s left and right.” She tossed a glare over at Rio, still seething and aiming to hurt her however she could.

“Absolutely not, we have to fix this. Now, you overpowered Etsy Witch.” Lilia shook her head firmly before calling Rio over. “Overrated Halloween decoration, get your ass over here!”

 

Rio carefully stepped over the rubble toward them, feeling like a scolded child, knowing they were both in trouble, deservedly. They had nearly leveled multiple historic landmarks and killed a few people, which was a problem for her.

Lilia rewound the storm, the buildings around them being built in reverse as she gave them a look that said, ‘fix this, you incompetent idiots’.

 

Agatha glanced over her shoulder at the traitorous bitch and new coworker, trembling in pure murderous rage once again, remembering what had truly been stolen from her. It was unfair that she couldn’t kill Death, make her suffer, or feel even an ounce of the agony that ran through Agatha’s soul. Instead, she looked away. “I can’t speak to you right now, oh almighty Death,” she spat ruefully. “I will be meeting her. Soon.”

Spinning away from the woman she would have once gone to the ends of the universe for, she flounced toward the parking garage. She would text Lilia later. Right now, she hoped the drive and extremely loud music would calm her down before she returned to her new home outside of the city.

She liked her new house, and it would be a shame to destroy it.

There was always bourbon.

She had just healed, and now the scars had been ripped wide open.   

🦕🦖🦕

2020 - Harkness Mansion

Los Angeles, California

Right After the Events on Isla Dunblar

She didn’t step out of the portal as much as she collapsed through it.

Broken. Defeated. Devastated.

She was hoping the cobblestones of the enchanted basement would break her fall if she had her way, her skull. An amnesia-inducing concussion would be preferable to this since dying was no longer an option.

Fucking immortality.

Much to her dismay, Ralph caught her just in time. 

He always knew when she needed him most.  

She woke an hour later, screaming for the Cosmic Entity who had left her.

Ralph climbed into the bed, moving Scratchy over, and held her close. “Remember the first time you and Ellie broke up?” It was soft, not meant to harm, just a gentle reminder.

Agatha sniffed. “I played Mariah Carey and Paula Abdul on repeat for a week,” her voice broke on a sob.

“You also ate your weight in ‘Half Baked,’ and I had to hide every form of payment you had.” He smiled wryly. “What did I tell you then?” He mopped her face with a tissue, grabbing another when she let out another sob.

“That women are just as bad as men, and hogs don’t discriminate.”

“Yes, but what else?”

“That you’ll never break my heart or make me cry like that.” Her eyes were red and puffy, but she smiled anyway.

“And have I?” 

“I gave birth, vaginally, to twins that are biologically yours, Ralph.” There was a small amount of laughter mixed with a sob.

“Childbirth doesn’t count, Aggie.”

“You’ve never broken your promise, Ralph. You’ve never destroyed me, but this is different.” She burrowed her head in his chest, one of the few men she would ever seek comfort in.

“I never said it wasn’t.” He tucked her head under his chin. “What did we say when we found each other as teenagers? What we’ve always told the kids?”

The witch pulled back and looked up at the person who had always understood her. The first person who truly chose her. “Family is forever.”

“Right. That mark on your chest? That bond she broke? It will be repaired one day. Until then, we’ve got you.” He pulled her closer and hummed. “So, which one of us gets parent of the year? Ian told me everything.”

“Technically, I saved everyone.” Agatha wiped her face on his shirt.

Ralph laughed. “I wasn’t even there, I think I win.”

“No way! We still have half a year to go!”

Leaning his head back, he gasped. “Are you planning anything else death-defying this year, dear wife?”

“You know who you married.”

 

He chuckled, thankful for his ability to cheer up the menace lying next to him. 

 

Sighing heavily, she felt as though the weight of the world was crushing her. “What now?”

“We do what we always planned. Fix the broken things we can, continue raising amazing children, set your mother on fire, and get your living corpse back.”

“She’s not a corpse, Ralph.”

“Fine, sack of bones.”

Agatha slightly swatted at him. “Be nice. She’s still my…” her voice began to tremble again.

He hugged her. “I know.”

Sniffing, she crinkled her nose, finally using her other senses. “Why am I in clean clothes? Did you bathe me?”

“Just like after every breakup when you were blacked out drunk. Even used the rejuvenating serums from Jenny.”

“You’re the best husband I’ll ever have, Ralph.”

“I’m the only husband you’ll ever have, and Fury is already preparing divorce papers.” He smiled into her hair. 

“Fine, but I’m keeping the ring, and we’re splitting custody with Ellie.”

 

The news of Evanora’s death broke out soon after, causing a media firestorm and a volatile market as the future of all Harkness-held companies was uncertain. 

Agatha had already chosen the empire's next rulers. She had been headhunting for years, with her lawyer doing the research. Several were already running smaller companies Evanora hadn’t known about.

Pietro took over the pharmaceutical division, and though she offered positions to others in the family, they declined. She didn’t blame any of them.    

The next several months proved to be a media circus with the death of her mother and the takeover of the business. Turning them into corporations to benefit people instead of harming them. She cut all ties with corrupt organizations and politicians—creating clean water and reforestation initiatives instead.

She stepped away from her position at the University of Southern California, knowing that, for now, she needed to put whatever energy she had into her current initiatives.

Agatha had always been an academic. Devoting her life to researching, finding answers, developing, and proving theories about prehistoric species. She was stepping out of her comfort zone, away from a world of creatures and plants that should no longer exist, and into a world of correcting the mistakes humans had made.  

It was a lot in such a short span of time, but she was doing everything she could to prove that she was mostly good. Generating and preserving life. For the most part.

Fostering the fucking Sacred Balance.

Willing her love to come home to her. 

Leaving the proverbial porch light on. 

She respected the boundaries. Never reached out. Only waited.

Six months passed, and the world had moved on from the keys to the kingdom of Salem Seven Holdings being handed over. The business headlines were still filled with her and the company's every move. Now focusing on the changes, the good, the growth, the growing stocks. Factories were already being refitted with more efficient, clean equipment, reducing their carbon footprint. 

She hadn’t been shocked to find out that, in liquid assets, her mother had enough money to fund multiple small countries for decades. In combined assets, she was a trillionaire.

It made her sick.

What surprised her was that her mother had paid her taxes.

Which, of course, was a pitiful sum considering how much she didn’t have to report. 

Agatha had gotten to work immediately. Raising and evening out wages, initiative upon initiative, and scholarship funds.

It had been a busy six months, and she still had so far to go.



December 2020

Minas Gerais, Brazil

 

Agatha sat on the partially collapsed oversight platform where the dam had failed, poisoning the surrounding towns. 

An iron ore mine formally owned by her mother. Allowed to take shortcuts until one day, the earth gave way, and toxic sludge found its way into the river, poisoning the water that sustained the region.

The water used by multiple villages and towns for cooking, bathing, praying to gods she knew existed but didn't believe in.

Salem Seven had tied it up in litigation for years. No one saw a dime, and the mine continued operating. Continued poisoning the town. 

It was one of the first places Agatha wanted to see boots on the ground.

Her inner circle, noticing a pattern of where Agatha had been going, didn’t say a word.

Agatha and Ralph had been keeping detailed plans and a timeline for years, consistently updating them. 

Recent events may have affected some of that, but as long as the cleanup still happened, what did that matter?

Agatha had hired local crews, found world-renowned inspectors and engineers. Nothing would falter on her watch, even if she and Wanda had to reinforce it.

During the days the mine was closed, she made sure every employee was paid. No one would go without because her mother had been pure evil.

She had done that enough throughout her life. 

Looking out over the valley, at the rivers, the rios, she considered the life they had once provided. It was the last day before their holiday break. The first time since the mine opened that it had ever closed for any holiday, let alone a paid one. 

A lot of changes had been made since she had taken temporary control of the entire company. While most of these towns and villages hated her at first, or saw her as someone just trying to repair the damage, they eventually learned that she really was trying to change the tide.

Yet she hadn’t convinced the one person that mattered—hadn’t seen her, hadn’t heard from her. She knew Lilia was still in contact, but that was all. 

All that time trying to stave off Death, and now she couldn’t lure her for anything.

Taking a large drink from the sustainable water bottle she now carried, she moved over to make room for Wanda before she even heard the sound of her footsteps.

“Thinking about her again?” Wanda asked, lowering herself to sit next to Agatha.

“Only every second of the day.” She scoffed.

“Anything yet?”

Agatha shook her head, setting the bottle back down. “No. Lilia said she’s fine, just abroad. Won’t tell me where. Threw herself into protecting the Sacred Balance.” 

“Did Jen find a way to return her to how she was?” Wanda squeezed her hand.

“No. Nothing in the Darkhold, Hela doesn’t know anything either. We just have to wait it out, I guess.” Keeping her gaze on the horizon, she added. “Nothing about removing or breaking the bond, either. I’m starting to think that would be better.” Her voice dropped, worrying at her lip. Guilt automatically seeped in at the mere thought of no longer being the one made for Death. 

Wanda bit her lip, knowing what she was about to say was not what Agatha wanted to hear. “You deserve to be happy.”

The laugh that came in return was harsh, cruel, but not aimed at the young woman. “No, I don’t. I made my choices, and I knew the consequences, honey.” She stood offering Wanda her hand. “I know I’m not innocent in all this, and what’s worse is I would do it all again if it meant keeping you kids safe, healthy, alive.”

“You fucking martyr.” Wanda blew a piece of hair out of her face, lightly scolding Agatha.

“Dievčatko, it’s not martyrdom, it’s accepting my place in life. I may have been born into a life of luxury, but it was a cursed one. Whatever sins I committed in this and every single past life, I’m paying for them now. That’s mine to bear, to fix.”

Wanda knew there was nothing she could say to get through the hard-headed skull belonging to the woman beside her. “She was wrong about what she said, you know.” 

Agatha shrugged, turning to walk back to more solid ground. “Not completely. I know what I’ve done, especially when it comes to you kids.” She looked Wanda up and down. “Now, you need to get back to the states for the boys. I still have work to do.”

Wanda’s eyes widened. “Agatha! The wedding! Christmas!”

“I’ll be there, I just have a few loose ends to tie up here.” She explained, leading Wanda back to their vehicle to drive to the edge of town, while everyone else had left the site.

Wanda looked her over cautiously. “I’m not letting you go alone; you’ve been too reckless lately.”

“Absolutely not, it’s dangerous. I’ve already put your life at risk enough.”

“I’m the Scarlet Fucking Witch. Besides, I’ll tell Ian you're taking Ralph to a gay strip club for his bachelor party.”

“Fine. Ruin my fun. Let’s go take care of the inspectors who let this happen.”

 

Agatha had been a shell of her usual self since June. Watching her kids open their presents during the holidays hadn’t brought her the sense of joy it usually did. Instead, she moved on autopilot, counting up the ways she had failed.

Even the visit to the so-called safety inspectors in Brazil had done little to cure her mood. In fact, she had done several of those visits in recent months, none of which had made her feel anything.

No, it was like a light had been permanently burnt out.

 

She thought that on New Year’s Eve, it would come back. Their divorce was finalized, and on New Year’s Day, Ralph and Ian would finally marry. Something she had personally promised them when they were younger.

She had sworn she would throw Ralph an epic bachelor party, which is why it made complete sense that, at forty-seven, they sat on a balcony at their beach house in Malibu, drunk and watching fireworks.

“I’m so sorry, Ralph,” She whispered the apology under her breath, eyes red and glassed over.

He shook his head. “Don’t apologize, Ags. There’s nowhere I’d rather be the night before my wedding than here with you.”

“You’re lying.” She laid her hand on his forearm and turned her head. “Do you remember the night before our wedding?”

His eyes rolled upward. “I’ll never forget it. Evanora tried to give us the sex talk.”

“In front of five hundred people at the REHEARSAL DINNER.” Agatha howled with laughter, mostly due to the amount of alcohol she had consumed.

“Then she led everyone in prayer for you to quickly be blessed with a male heir.” He recalled.

“Again, but at the reception.” 

“The look on your face was perfection. I’m taking the framed picture, by the way. So glad that was preserved forever.” He laughed.

“Yours wasn’t much better. Ian and Ellie both looked sick.” She added.

“Well, her prayers worked; we did have two amazing children shortly after.” 

Agatha smiled sadly. “Only because she was already planning it.”

“If it was inevitable that she committed that heinous act, I’m glad we were the fortunate assholes to raise them.” He poured two more shots of what he hoped was rum and handed her one, raising his in the air. “To our oldest kids. Thank gods we didn’t fuck ‘em up too horribly.”

“We also kept ‘em alive, they’re successful, and Red gave us two amazing grandsons,” Agatha added.

They knocked back their shots, trying to focus back on the fireworks and whatever band was playing on the pier.

He took her hand in his and squeezed. “Do you think you’ll—”

“In the words of the great Ani DiFranco, if it isn’t her, it isn’t here.” Reaching for her water, she gave him a tight-lipped smile. “And I think I’m finally witch enough to admit that I’m depressed as fuck about it.”

“Have you reached out to a therapist?”

“Yes, I have.” She smiled proudly.

He raised an eyebrow, “Does this therapist have a name that isn’t printed on a bottle?”

Huffing, she slumped in her chair. “I’ll call first thing Friday. Everything’s closed tomorrow, and you’re getting hitched.”

He turned to fully look at her, “I’m being serious, Agatha. You’re starting to worry me, and it’s only been six months. You haven’t even really talked about it. You’re not wallowing in music, you aren’t going on excessive shopping sprees, you aren’t doing stupid, reckless things—killing sprees aside. You haven’t been able to sleep, and when you do, it’s curled up beside one of your people. The Agatha Harkness I know rarely sleeps with someone beside her unless it’s a certain bunny.”

“I’ve never felt this type of pain before, Ralph.” When she finally looked back up at him, what he saw broke his heart. The strongest person he knew looked more lost than she did when they were at the conversion camp as teenagers. 

He sobered immediately, standing and pulling her up with him. “Alright, dear, it’s time to shower, get ready for bed, and watch movies from our youth until we fall asleep. I can’t get married tomorrow if you’re miserable.”

Ralph Boehner assisted children every day, and often helped extend their lives, so they lived to be old and gray. He used to have a healthy fear and respect for death.

Now? Now, if he ever saw Rio Vidal again, even though he was not a violent person, he would get in as many punches as he could.

After asking her to switch to her true form, of course, he would never hit a woman.



March 18th, 2021 (19th in Spain)

Manhattan, New York

 

Agatha had finished her part in Brazil, with the long-term cleanup now in place and the infrastructure rebuilt. Currently, she was staying at her Manhattan penthouse while visiting sites and reviewing plans for a new refugee outreach program in the city. Wanda would be taking over the project; however, she was currently teaching in her new position at NYU.

Dr. Maximoff had been offered Rio’s previous position shortly after Isla Dunblar, when Rio had resigned. It had been a difficult decision for her, not wanting to move away from the family, mainly Agatha, though Pietro's move to New York for his new position at the family company softened the blow. 

Agatha had deeply missed Wanda’s sons and worried about them, though Lilia and Sharon made a point of being in New York more often, and Wanda now had Zora.

The Harkness children were growing up and finding new places to land away from LA and the main nest.

Agatha was finding more reasons to be near those she cared about, desperate not to be alone, as the family quietly took turns keeping an eye on her. Sure, Nicky and Lily were still home, but Ralph and Ian were travelling more now that they were married.



The scientist sat on the couch in sweatpants and a Yale shirt that either belonged to Ian or Ellie. She wasn’t sure anymore. Her eyes were fixed on the window as it stormed over the city. A storm not previously forecasted.

“I still think of her every time it storms.” She mused to her longtime friend before taking a sip of wine.

“I think we all do, Aggie.” Ellie settled next to her, curling into her side. “Should you be drinking? It’s nearly midnight.”

Agatha lay her head against the other woman’s. It wasn’t romantic, simply a comfort from knowing each other as long as they had and finally burying the things they had let fester for far too long. “Can’t sleep. I’m hoping it helps.”

“You’ve been restless tonight, and you keep rubbing that fucking scar.” The blonde swatted at her friend’s hand for the hundredth time that night.

“It’s pulling harder than it ever has. I texted Lilia,” She grimaced at another sharp tug. “Said everything’s fine, but it’s normal for it to get a bit wonky. Should dull after a year.” 

Ellie stole a sip of the bitter liquid. “I’ve never witnessed a group of people so willing to fight somebody before, yet here we are.”

“I appreciate it, but as I keep saying, I’m not innocent in this, Ellie. Just like with you.” 

Ellie put her hand over Agatha’s mouth to silence her. “You and I were young, stupid, backed into a corner, and not a good match romantically. You don’t switch enough.”

Agatha scrunched her eyes at the thought. “Ever.”

“Exactly. This is Rio, an all-knowing, cosmic fucking entity, being an idiot. You are trying so hard. I will always side with you, unless it involves the kids.”

“Figures,” Agatha grumbled.

Ellie silenced yet another breaking news alert on her phone. “Damn. Another volcano today, underwater again. What is that psychotic bitch doing?”

“Maybe someone is stabbing her in the heart, but they can’t find it.” Agatha stood, having finished her wine. “Huh.” She stared out at the night sky. “It stopped.”

“...what about?” Ellie asked cautiously.

Careful not to stumble toward the kitchen, she washed out her glass and placed it in the drainboard. “It settled. Maybe she was having one last breakdown. If that’s even possible.” Running her hands through her unruly mass of hair, she looked at one of her many anchors shakily. “Take me to bed, please.”

 

If Agatha had said those words years ago, she would’ve jumped at the chance. She knew now, though, that they held a different meaning. The pillar of strength before Ellie Sattler had shattered.

Every single adult member of the ragtag family had taken turns holding her together. Harkness had thrown herself into her new passions: philanthropy and murder. Her way of upholding the Sacred Balance. She was still collecting souls for her plot to attain immortality for all of them, her fingertips growing blacker beneath her glamour.

Yet she couldn’t sleep alone, and was slowly, platonically, attaching herself to Ellie. Not that she minded, she had done Agatha wrong for years and owed it to her. Dr. Sattler just wasn’t sure how much longer she could watch this happen. Witness this all-powerful witch fade away underneath a mask of power and control.

“I’m big spoon, someone has to be your glue." She responded quietly, taking Agatha’s hand in hers. 

The blonde scientist was starting to consider the ethics of a supernaturally enhanced velociraptor with an appetite for cosmic entities.

 

End of May 2023

Los Angeles, California

 

The youngest Harkness children were graduating high school. They could have graduated early if they had wanted to, but they chose to graduate with their dual high school diplomas and associate's degrees instead. They wanted the high school experience, and they had gotten it.

They would still graduate from college early.

It had been nearly three years since that summer, and life was finally getting back on track for Agatha and her family. She wasn’t fully healed, but she was healing. Sleeping on her own more often, not needing someone by her side consistently. 

She had even entertained the idea of dating someone. 

For exactly one minute and seventeen seconds. Therapy was going well, but she wasn't ready to be with anyone else yet.

 

Jurassic Park was open and operating successfully, Ian supervising from afar. Ralph had taken a position as the Chief of Pediatric Medication at Yale New Haven Children’s Hospital. 

Pietro was living in New York, running a division of the company. He hadn’t married, though was beginning to date more seriously. His true love was the commitment to his work and leaving a legacy for the company, now named Harkness Holdings. 

Jen had been pulled into the company to enhance the pharmaceutical and medical division alongside Pietro. 

Zora had been cleaning up and taking over the darker side of the business, while Wanda still taught at NYU. The now ten-year-old twins were thriving and growing like weeds.

Wanda and Zora had married in the fall of 2022, and all of her parental figures had cried with joy rather than slight disappointment as they had at her first wedding. They had never told her that, but secretly she knew. Her three siblings had all given Zora a warning, and while the former combat operative was dangerous in her own right, she had a healthy fear of their combined powers.

Standing in the high school football stadium one last time while the graduates found their seats, the proud mother felt it. The pull, lighter than in the past, but there. Her hand wrapped around Wanda’s upper arm and squeezed lightly.

“What’s wrong?” The younger witch whipped her head around in alarm, sensing the panic in Agatha’s grip.

She’s here. Hissed through Wanda’s head, as not to alert anyone else.

Where? I’ll send her to another universe. Her lips curled back into a snarl, looking for the one who had broken Agatha.

I can’t sense her location, I just feel she’s here. The older woman slowly looked around, trying to appear subtle.

Last time you felt the pull, Lilia confirmed she was on another continent, just not which one. 

No, she’s here. She definitely came to support the kids. Agatha confirmed, looking down at Lilia in the row below her.

As if on cue, Lilia turned around and, with a sad smile, nodded.  

Agatha braced herself, praying to the Divine Mother that Death had enough sense to leave her the hell alone.



Lilith and Nicolas Harkness had graduated from high school. Gathering her cap from the ground and hugging her brother and friends, she felt the chill down her spine. Judging from the look on her brother’s face, so had he.

“What does she want?” She hissed.

“A beating, obviously.” He said through clenched teeth. “Nearly three years, and she comes here? Of all places?”

“Nonna didn’t even say anything, surely she knew?” Lily’s eyes scanned the crowds, looking for the raven-haired woman she had so desperately missed and despised at the same time. 

Their eyes widened, staring at each other when their phones buzzed. Neither twin had been able to delete or block her number all those years ago. Holding out hope just like their mother, desperately hoping for another secret friendship.

Texts and calls that never came. Until now.

 

☠️💔

Locker Room

 

They looked back at each other, solid resolve in their eyes. Nicky spoke first. “Well, we have to tell Mom, but let’s hear what the bitch has to say.”

Lily sighed, making eye contact with their mother in the stands. 

We’ll meet you soon. 

Agatha nodded from her position. Go. 

Lily knew it was taking Agatha everything she had not to go with them, and she was proud of her mother for her restraint. Taking her older brother’s hand, she dragged him into the tunnel underneath the stadium, the locker room forming around them as soon as they were out of sight from others.

“RIO, YOU UNDEAD TWAT! What do you want?!” Lily yelled angrily the millisecond the room was fully formed around them.

For all of the bravery the twins had mustered, they broke the minute they saw the look on the woman’s face.

She was just as conflicted as they were.

Nicky still held his sister back, just in case. She may be crying, but it didn’t mean she wouldn’t swing. 

The hug was watery and snotty—arms, loud sobs, cracked voices, when finally Nicky formed a full sentence.

“She’s right above us, respecting YOUR stupid boundaries. Go see her.” 

“I…” The cosmic being gathered her composure. “I can’t. I’m only here for a moment. I have a graduation gift for you both.”

Lily’s eyes flared a royal purple. “Rio fucking Vidal, do you know how much…”

Nicky had been right to regain his grip on his twin after the hug.

Lady Death held up a hand. “I didn’t come to fight or argue, but I had to do this in person. I don’t live in New York anymore, and Alice is getting tired of house sitting. I know you two are going to school there.” 

Boxes appeared in their hands, each containing a set of housekeys. “I know your mom can buy you an entire building, but the apartment is paid for since I’ve lived there…well, a long time. Two separate floors, two separate entrances. Everything you need. With it being magickally warded, well…”

Nicky looked at the keychain curiously, a small replica of Rio’s dagger. “What’s this?”

Rio shrugged. “Just a reminder of me.”

Lily tilted her head to the side, running her fingers over it. “Sure it is.”

The woman before them dropped her head. Stepping forward, she hugged them again, careful to keep an eye on Lily and her clenched fist. “I’m proud of both of you. Everything you need is at the apartment.” 

“Do you want to see…” Nicky began, but the only person who could really, truly heal their mother had left.

“You already knew the answer, Nicholas.” Lilith placed her hand on his shoulder. “You should have let me hit her, at least once.”



The graduation party was in full swing back at the mansion that Agatha knew would always be hers, even if she did resent the damn thing. 

It held too many secrets.

“Did you know?” She questioned Lilia, holding a glass filled with a very strong cocktail.

Lilia pinched the bridge of her nose. “Yes. She swore me to secrecy. She wasn’t completely sure she’d do it today, but she really wanted to be there and watch them cross the stage, listen to their co-valedictorian speeches. She’s proud of them.”

“Probably thinks it’s all Ralph and Ian’s influence.” The not exactly sober woman grunted.

“You’re a fucking good mother, Agatha Harkness, and you know that. So does she. There are four successful humans out there,” Lilia gestured out at the party. “That will agree with me.”

“I will never forget the words she said to me.”

Lilia wrapped her in a half-hug. “She would have said anything in that moment to get you to stop and reconsider.”

“Doesn’t matter, Lils.” She drained her drink and exchanged it for another one from a passing waiter. “You know I haven’t even completed the immortality rituals?” She laughed. “Of course you do, one of them is yours.” With a shake of her head, she rambled on, “I mean, I’ve extended everyone’s lives, but the only immortals are still Wanda and me.”

Lilia’s eyes were sympathetic, yet held no pity.

“Did you tell her?” Agatha’s voice was laced with something like desperation.

“You need to tell her yourself.”

“You won’t even tell me where she’s been, and she’s blocking me from finding her.” Agatha looked down at the shorter woman, narrowing her eyes, tempted to search her mind.

“My mind is warded, even to you, missy.” Lilia lightly scolded. “You’ll get your chance, one day.”

🦕🦖🦕

Harvard University, Cambridge, Massachusetts

August 18, 2025

Agatha had moved to New York shortly after the twins graduated, wanting to be closer to Harkness Holdings headquarters. Nicky, who decided to go into business instead of the medical field, aimed to take over the Harkness empire one day. He shadowed Agatha and the woman who would eventually take the helm. 

Zora Bennett herself. 

Wrongs had been righted and continued to be. Alice sent Agatha lists of criminals that had gone free to keep her siphon fed, and the powerful witch knew it was time to monitor the kingdom from afar.

Nicky still had a few years to earn his degree and his position, but he was learning. Agatha also felt she was finally in a place where she could fully live on her own again. The nightmares didn’t happen as often, and she wasn’t as dependent on Ellie keeping her stable.

The two scientists were best friends in only the way lesbian exes could be, but after five years, Agatha knew it was time to return to academia. No longer worried she’d run into Rio, or something that would remind her of the woman. 

She had her choice of positions and chose a tenure-track position at Harvard on Lilia’s insistence. Several professors had just retired. Even the dean was retiring in another year or so. Agatha didn’t need the money or the job, but she craved it. 

It was also close to her family.

She found a house outside the city with room for the family to visit, a large yard and a pool, quiet enough that no one would disturb her. For the first time since shattering, she was finally looking forward to this next chapter of her life.

She still had the reminder of what should have been etched into her skin, right over her heart; now she had a few tattoos as reminders of what she survived, a very attractive and prominent white streak, and a house that only she and Scratchy lived in. But she was starting to feel happy again.

She was mostly sober, consistently seeing a therapist, and her family was less worried about her.  

She had even been seeing someone for six months: Darcy Lewis, a brilliant computer scientist. Darcy had worked alongside Ralph and Ellie on the park endeavors and now handled much of the tech for Harkness Holdings. She wasn’t scared away or startled when she stumbled into Agatha’s New York office to see her portal in. Merely explained the science and went on with her day.

Agatha had asked her to lunch an hour later.  

The shell of Agatha Harkness was beginning to fill with life again.

Until the day she stepped onto the prestigious campus to prepare for the semester. Her new office was filled with furniture and boxes delivered from the storage unit where she had kept them. The office itself had hardwood floors, a large arched window framed by bookshelves, her large L-shaped mahogany desk already dusted off and placed where she had specified. The office chair behind it, with the large area rug in shades of purple, placed perfectly beneath it. Two purple wingback chairs in front of her desk, a seating area to the side.

It was perfect.

She would magickally unpack the boxes later. Sliding her phone into the back pocket of her jeans, her eyes zeroed in on the campus phone list. It had been carefully placed on top of the desk, ready for the new professor in case she needed to coordinate with anyone. Agatha picked up the stapled papers and flipped through them, one name immediately jumping off the paper.

Vidal, Rio.

Next to the name was her building and office number. Rio was right next door.

Slamming the paper down, she stormed out of her office and looked to her right, surprised she hadn’t noticed.

She hadn’t noticed because their nameplates hadn’t been hung yet.

Three demanding knocks caused the slightly ajar door to swing open. Not a soul inside, or Rio for that matter.

“Well, it was open,” Agatha mumbled to herself, stepping inside the office. The space was the same, though the furniture was more in line with Rio’s taste and style. Hues of green, plants, and half unpacked boxes filled the space. 

A drawing lying on a shelf caught Agatha’s eye. Curious, she walked over and looked down at it, not touching, just observing.

White drawing paper, a crayon picture clearly drawn by a young artist. Nicky and Lily had decorated her office with thousands of them when they were young. There was a spiky mountain range, grass, a house, a young girl with wild brown hair, and a woman with black hair.

Agatha’s hand flew to her mouth, scanning the room for a picture. This had to be a drawing from a kid who admired Rio. There was no other explanation.

 

“The pull is stronger today than it’s ever been…”

“She’s in another country, overseas…”

“She doesn’t want to be found…”

On Rio’s desk, facing inward to where only Rio would see it, was a small painted frame. Trembling, Agatha picked it up, careful not to drop it.

The little girl had her hair, her eyes, her chin.

That smile? The gap in her teeth? All Rio. She looked similar to Lily at that age. Four. Agatha didn’t know how, but she knew her age.

She carefully traced her glamored fingertips over the glass, reverently, wishing to hold her daughter.

Her daughter.

No one told her. No one had to. A mother knows. That night, on Isla Dunblar. Something had happened, and there smiling at her from the picture frame was the result.

Over four years of not knowing she existed. Agatha didn’t know it had been possible for them to create life together, but if she had known, she would have been there every step of the way.

Had Lilia known? Jen? Anyone?

She hung her head, one hand gripping the desk to keep herself from falling back into the void she had just clawed her way out of. Silent tears fell, splattering on Rio’s desk.

She had been so close to healing, only to shatter once more.

She truly was an awful parent.

“Agatha?” 

Agatha’s head snapped up, eyes swirling a shade of purple so dark they were almost black. Carefully setting the photo down so it wouldn’t be damaged, she advanced around the desk and toward her ex. “Were you ever going to tell me?” Her voice was sharp, meant to cut.

Rio’s lips were a thin line, almost non-existent.

“I…”

“She’s my daughter too, Rio!” The sheer amount of pain in Agatha’s voice caused the earth beneath them to shake violently, registering as an earthquake.

Agatha was furious. Raising her hands, purple magick blasted Death herself through walls and out onto the grass.    

🦕🦖🦕

Beginning of September 2020

New York City

Dr. Rio Vidal had resigned from her position at NYU. She hated to do it, but she couldn’t bring herself to teach; in fact, she needed to take some time off. She had connections at other universities and planned to teach in Madrid beginning in the fall of 2021.
