Darcy shook her head and stepped back with a playful laugh. “We have too much to do before everyone gets here. You are not avoiding your feelings with sex. We both smell like sweat and sex as it is and need to shower.”

Agatha tilted her head. “So what’s one more round?”

“Talking. Agatha.”



Agatha had recounted every conversation she had had with Rio in recent days to Darcy. She had already shown her memories and trusted Darcy enough to let her in.

In all honesty, if she had been in Rio’s position, with that version of Agatha? Well, she could see why Rio did what she had.

But this Agatha? Her Agatha? She was different, softer. She was no longer fighting for the lives of her children, for her freedom, for her life. Rio hadn’t had the same experience.

The three women who had loved Agatha Harkness each had vastly different versions of her, and Darcy was the one teaching her what a real, healthy, loving relationship looked like.

And she knew it could fall apart at any moment, because Rio Vidal, the personification of Death, had something Darcy did not. It wasn’t Amelia; it was the fact that no matter how much Agatha denied it, she was still in love with the entity.

It was in her nightmares, and the way she cried out for Rio. Agatha swore up and down she didn’t have those when she shared a bed with Darcy. The truth was, she didn’t remember. It was the way she had looked up Lily’s summer program and lit up with a tear slipping down her cheek when she saw Rio’s name and picture. Agatha, though, had kept true to her word and respected the boundaries her ex had set. Telling her girlfriend, “She didn’t want me to find her. This is Lilia’s doing”.

Darcy saw how her girlfriend had watched Death and Amelia interact. There was a small glimpse of love and admiration behind the forced coldness in her eyes. The what-ifs that went unsaid.

The young woman also knew the most dangerous secret of all. Agatha still, very reluctantly, accepted that she was made for Death. She had never closed her side of the bond. It was something Darcy decided she could live with. She wouldn’t have been able to sever that connection either.

Yet she chose Agatha every day. She knew that the witch truly loved her, which was why she stayed. Whether it lasted another week, month, or fifty years, she’d love her as long as she could. Not from pity or desperation, but because they truly loved and respected each other.

Despite how hard Darcy wanted it, she couldn’t stand in the way of someone else’s fate. 

Getting ready for the family to arrive, Darcy smiled lovingly at Agatha, who had shared moments before about the nightmare and her concerns about the upcoming visit. “You know I’ll support you no matter what, right?” 

Agatha pursed her lips to the side. “What do you mean?”

“I grew up with divorced parents, and their coparenting sucked. New significant others, constantly fighting, bickering. It was rough. I know neither of you would do that, I’m just saying, whatever you need. I’m here. Just talk to me.”

The witch took her in her arms again, “I really do love you.”

“I know.”

🦕🦖🦕

“Mom, what are we supposed to call her?” Tommy Maximoff asked, possibly for the five hundredth time.

Zora’s mouth twitched, a hint of a smirk as she looked over at Pietro. “The ADHD is strong with that one.”

“Family trait.” He smiled back.

Wanda rolled her eyes at both of them, adjusting in her seat to get comfortable. It wasn’t a long flight, and it was a comfortable private jet, but at seven months pregnant, none of that mattered. “Just Amelia, or Milly.”

“But she’s our aunt.” Billy protested.

“She’s four, Billy. You don’t call Lily and Pietro aunt and uncle. They’re eight years older than you.” Zora pointed out.

“Maybe you should. You might actually respect us.” Nicky teased.

The younger boys stuck their tongues out at their uncle. 

“Little Gabrielle here,” Lily reached over and rested a hand on her sister’s stomach. “Will probably call her Amelia or Milly, too. But I’m absolutely Aunt Lilith this time.” 

“Does that make me Uncle Nicholas, since we’re using full government names, apparently?” 

Pietro snorted, “Makes you sound like Santa Claus.”

Zora scrolled on her phone, “Never use your government name.” She said automatically.

Wanda, Pietro, and Jen nodded in agreement. 

Lily looked at the four of them suspiciously, “Thanks for the advice, criminals. No, you’re still Nicky, my name is just cooler. Anyway, hey rugrats.”

Billy and Tommy groaned and looked at her. 

“What?”

“We’re twelve.”

“Headphones, grownups need to talk before we land.” Their aunt instructed.

They rolled their eyes, turned on their games, and put on headphones.

Wanda made sure the electronics were enchanted, so the boys couldn’t hear any of the conversation as the adults gathered closer to speak. She turned to Jen first. “Where’s Alice?”

“She portaled to see Rio first. I wanted to fly with you for obvious reasons.” She explained, eyes falling to the reason mentioned.

“We thank you for that,” Zora said sincerely. “So Alice really didn’t know?” 

Jen shook her head. “No. I mean, she figured it out, but she knew Rio didn’t want to have more people keep the secret from Agatha, and that when a cosmic being doesn’t want to be found, there’s a reason. Rio really did want to tell each of you. She’s been at war with herself for years.”

Pietro was the first to agree. “Remember after Oxford? We all reached out and supported her. We had all gotten to know her during their time together, over FaceTime or texting. I was really excited to meet her. Wands told me how good they were together. Every single one of us reached out, tried to explain. She seemed very surprised Mom actually risked her life to save mine. Anyway, we were still there for her.”

“That’s how Lily and I formed the bond we did. Even dads got in on it. Ellie not as much, but…” Nicky’s words trailed off.

“It’s Ellie.” Everyone said simultaneously.

“I know I wasn’t around before, and I didn’t know Rio for long before everything happened.” Zora began, looking lovingly at her wife. “But I spent time with her on the island, and she always had a very interesting perspective on the science, dinosaurs specifically. That night, trying to save Lily, every time she looked at Agatha, it was with more love than I thought could fit inside of a human.” She shrugged one shoulder. “Then again, she’s not human. I don’t think any of us can fully understand why Rio did or does what she does, because, again, she’s not human.”

They all went quiet, an uncomfortable silence falling around them. Nicky shifted. “We all saw the pain she caused Mom, though. She wasn’t herself for years. She hid our sister from us.”

Zora nodded. “I’m not saying there wasn’t massive pain at that type of connection being severed, and I’m not saying she’s not somewhat in the wrong. What I am saying is none of us can put ourselves in her shoes. She lives by a set of rules none of us can fathom.” She inhaled deeply, knowing the next part was going to be hard for some of them to swallow. “I’m also saying, your mother isn’t infallible either. She did some pretty terrible things.” She held her hands out before anyone, including her wife, could jump on her. “I’m not saying she was wrong, I know why she did them, but neither of them is innocent.” She lowered her hands and looked around at the family she had married into. “You were absolutely justified in being upset with her five years ago. Even more recently, and I understand the outrage at her hiding Amelia, I’m upset about that too, because it hurt all of you. All I’m saying is, Ags isn’t the only one hurting, and to give grace.”

Jen nodded. “She’s right. Rio has been hurting. She has Amelia and Lilia knew her way before she knew Agatha, but that doesn’t mean she hasn’t agonized over this. Then there’s the magick and Sacred Balance issue.”

Pietro’s head popped up as he stared at his business partner. “What issue?”

She sighed. She hadn’t told Pietro the real reason they were having issues at the company. “When I restored Rio’s magick. Agatha and I overcorrected. The reason people aren’t living through things as they should is because of us. Rio is too perfect in her interpretations, and it’s a bigger problem than we realized.”

“So there’s that, and Rio really has been conflicted. I saw it all summer, Wanda could tell on Monday. I mean, I gave her hell all summer because I needed her to come back, but she really is conflicted.” Lily flipped her phone around in her hands, fidgeting.

Wanda looked away sheepishly. “I wasn’t very nice when I saw her.”

Zora smirked. “The four of you get a little defensive when it comes to Agatha.”

Jen’s eyes darted to the blonde. “Are you just now realizing that? It’s a family trait. They also meddle.” 

Lily sank into her seat, feeling guilty. Agatha and Rio weren’t supposed to be on the island at the same time. That was specifically her and Ian’s fault.  

Nicky let out a low whistle. “Mom and Darcy are in love. Mom is so happy with everything.”

Wanda nodded. “Yeah, and while I will happily give Rio hell and feel bad about it at the same time, I still feel terrible about binding her. But Mom loves Rio, she always will. Gods, I’m so fucking glad soulmates aren’t common.” Her eyes widened, and she looked at Zora. “Not that I wouldn’t love to be universally bound to you, honey.”

Zora smirked. “Sure.”

Nicky leaned back in his seat, chin in his hand. “So, don’t be assholes to Rio, give grace, support mom. Are we Team Darcy or Team Rio?”

“We’re Team Amelia. Mom has to figure out her love life on her own. Amelia is our priority. We set a good example for her, we create a cohesive, loving, supportive family, and we put her first.” Pietro answered. 

“That’s all she wants, it’s all she’s dreamed of for years. Granted, she didn’t plan on Mom dating anyone, but all she wants is all of us.” Lily confirmed, before looking back at them. “She’s four, but she’s a cosmic being. It’s a lot.”

Jen beamed. “Someone raised you kids right. Lilia and the dads, I’m sure.” She laughed when Wanda sent a few sparks of scarlet her way. “Okay, fine, Agatha helped, I guess.”

“Speaking of dads,” Nicky pulled out his phone. “I’ll let them know what we decided. No one needs Ellie coming in with her sass.”

Jen huffed. “I don’t know, I was kind of looking forward to her telling Rio she slept with Agatha for a few years.”

Wanda grimaced, taking a kick to the kidney. “I’m glad we’re landing soon. I need to walk.” She took a pillow her brother handed her and resituated. “As fun as that would be, Jenny, Milly doesn’t need to witness that.”

As the plane began its descent, the Scarlet Witch released her sons’ headphones from their spell. “Okay, boys, get ready to land. Now, when we get to Nana’s…”

“We know!” Billy smiled.

“Billy did a counter spell, we heard everything.”

“We just want to meet Milly and go swimming,” Billy added, putting his games away.

Nicky grinned at Zora. “I’m sure Gabby will be nothing like her brothers.”

“Don’t hex it, Harkness,” Zora grumbled.

🦕🦖🦕

Alice had known for the past five years that Rio had been hiding something important. Jen wouldn’t tell her, which only meant one thing.

Yet again, her wife had been sworn to secrecy.

Fucking Agatha Harkness and Rio Vidal.

Alice Wu had known Rio her entire life. Her mother, Lorna, had been close friends with Lilia and Sharon, and therefore, Rio. Once Alice was older, they became close, best friends, inseparable. Rio would often appear the same age as Alice, learning in the same ways Alice did. 

While the young witch knew her friend was different, older than time, and saw the world differently, she appreciated it. It gave her someone to talk to. To confide in.

Different in her own way, she had trouble making friends, was fiercely overprotective, and knew she would never live up to her mother’s reputation. She saw the world differently, as did Rio. It was what made them such a great pair. 

Quite possibly due to her connection to Lilia, or possibly her birthright, she was also a member of Agatha’s coven. It was how she met Jen, their attraction immediate. Alice and Rio always remained friends, her bond and loyalties lying with the original green witch.

When she found out the role her wife had played in the draining of Rio’s magick, she had been heartbroken. She knew what was at stake for the Harkness children, but had spent years watching Rio fawn over Agatha from afar after that encounter in London. She had been rooting for them. 

She forgave Jen and kept an eye on Agatha after the fallout from Oxford. Trying to play witch’s advocate. After all, she knew one of the Fates. While her mother wouldn’t tell her everything, she knew Rio and Agatha were meant to be. A risky match, if you asked Alice, but no one had. 

Alice wasn’t a cosmic being, fated to remain a blood witch. She did understand Rio’s plight slightly better than the others, however. It was why no one ever saw Lorna Wu. She had chosen to hide away, only emerging when necessary, tired of Cosmic-Mortal politics. 

Surely her mother knew about this and had many opinions on it.

While upset about Rio hiding Amelia, she had also been a hidden child herself, and again, she understood Death’s choices.

It was also why she didn’t seek out Rio. When a cosmic being went to great lengths to avoid being found and reached out to only certain people, they had their reasons; her mother always had. 

Knowing she needed to speak to Rio before everyone converged at Agatha’s, she transferred straight into her best friend’s kitchen on Friday morning, a few hours before they were due at the main Harkness residence.

“Is my niece still asleep?” 

Rio nodded, not startled at all by Alice’s arrival. “She had a hard time getting to sleep last night. Her brain wouldn’t shut off.” She set her coffee on the counter and collapsed into her human friend. “Gods, I fucking missed you.”

“Did you forget how to use a phone for the past five years?” She asked, holding Rio tightly.

“I thought you’d be here sooner.” The entity murmured into Alice’s shoulder.

“I wanted to let things settle first.” Moving to the main room and sitting on the sofa, she picked up a rogue stuffed hippo and held it on her lap. “I understand, you know.”

Rio sat, facing her, knees tucked to her chest. “I knew you would.” She lowered her voice, making sure Amelia wouldn’t hear. “So, what’s your take?”

The protection witch looked around the room, at the pictures already hung on the walls, the toys abandoned on the floor. “Looks like you have a kid.”

“You know what I mean.”

“I understand why you didn’t tell her. I understand your fears. I think you have a few things to tell her about that night, like the truth.”

Rio looked away. “Why do you know me so well?”

“It’s in my blood.” She smirked. “She knows most of this is because of her.”

“Not all of it?” Rio replied. “But now everyone hates me, and she’s in love with Darcy.”

“They hate how she reacted, not you. Yes, she’s in love with Darcy, but Darcy isn’t her soulmate.” Alice reached over and put a comforting hand on Death’s knee. “Don’t worry about that right now. Focus on one thing at a time. Everything works out eventually.”

The very human immortal exhaled deeply. “Right. Amelia’s well-being is what I need to focus on right now.” She uncurled herself, sitting up straight. “I’ve heard you’ve been up to no good.”

“Everyone I sent her after was guilty, committed heinous crimes, and were going to die eventually anyway. I’m like Dexter, but not the one committing the murder.” Alice shrugged.

“I have got to find better role models for Milly.”

Alice grinned. “She’ll be well balanced. Let’s just focus on this weekend. One step at a time.”

 

Both women turned when they heard small footsteps, morning light trickled in through the windows, and Alice suddenly had her arms full of a four-year-old.

“Hi, Aunt Alice!” Amelia greeted, face lit up with a smile.

“Are you like this with everyone?” She questioned the child hugging her.

“Only the people I know and have seen in Mama’s dreams. Plus, you’re Gran’s daughter!”

Alice’s breath caught in her throat as she peered around Amelia, eyes landing on Rio. “She’s met mom?”

“Of course, she’s met Lorna. She was practically one of my neighbors in Soria.”

Milly nodded, her wild hair flying. “She watched me if Nonna was gone and Mama had to go somewhere. You look like her. But you’re not a Fate like she is. You’re a detective and solve crimes.”

“You’re right. What else do you know?” Alice asked curiously.

“That you’re married to Aunt Jen. She’s my doctor and does my checkups because Mama says she’s the only one qualified because I’m an anomaly. Mommy is probably going to have Ralph look over me, too, since he’s a kid doctor.” Milly rambled on.

Rio sighed. “I hate to admit that it’s not a bad idea.” She stood up with a sigh. “C’mon, cariño, it’s time to get dressed, packed, and head over to Agatha’s house.”

“Pack?” Alice questioned.

“I’m staying with mommy this weekend! And my brothers and sisters!” Amelia answered excitedly. “I have my own room and everything! We decorated it over the week! Mommy uses magick for everything!” Her eyes were wide, full of admiration for Agatha Harkness.

Rio felt like she’d taken a punch to the heart. If Amelia knew just how much she personally had sacrificed for Agatha to be able to have all that magick, she might think differently. Then again, Amelia may be an all-knowing cosmic being, but she was still four and half human.

Besides, Agatha was her mother, and what child wasn’t in awe of her parents at that age? Agatha was still a novelty to her. 

Looking at Alice, she knew they’d be having a conversation later.



Milly was about to burst from the excitement. “Mama, do I have my swimsuit?”

“Yes, mija, it’s packed.”

“¿Recuerdas la manta especial que me hizo la abuela cuando nací? The purple and green one?” She didn’t even notice she was beginning to mix her languages.

“Sí, sí, cariño. Está todo aquí.” Her mother knelt and placed her hands on her shoulders. “Breathe, honey.”

Amelia took a deep breath in, held it, and slowly exhaled. “I’m nervous, Mama. It’s a whole weekend and a lot of people.”

Her Mama nodded. “I know, but they all love you, and I’ll be there every day.”

Milly bit her lip and gave a nod. She knew she could do this. She had created illusions of her family, had talked to them for hours on end, and now she was finally getting to do the same thing with the real people.

She had even traveled the multiverse to see other versions of her family and interact with them, not that she’d ever tell anyone that. She had spent time with Agatha every single day since Monday, a few hours with Wanda that first day, and a whole summer with Lily. Darcy wasn’t too horrible. 

She had to go, but she wasn’t horrible.

Now, she was finally getting everyone, and it was going to be great. She reached over and took the stuffed bunny from her bed. He was brown and worn from use, but still soft. She’d had him since birth and used him for comfort. His name was Sir Cuddles, and she decided he was coming with her. 

Hugging him close to her chest, the tiniest of cosmic beings looked at her Mom, now standing and holding her suitcase. “Can Victoria come with me this weekend? You know, so Scratchy can have a friend?”



Rio looked down at her daughter, knowing why she wanted Victoria with her, but she wouldn’t let on. “Of course, I think Scratchy would love that. I can teleport her house and lights since it’s so heavy. Let’s load and get to Agatha’s before everyone else gets there, okay?”

A small smile crept across her face while she followed her mother to the car, Aunt Alice joining them. “Mama?”

“Yes?”

“Why do you always call her Agatha?” 

Rio swallowed, not ready for that question. “Well, I call her Aggie or Ags sometimes.”

“Don’t parents usually say Mommy or Mom?” 

Rio glared at Alice before she could say anything as she loaded the car, Victoria changing into a cat solely to jump in. “Sometimes, but your mother and I haven’t been together for a long time. So we just use first names.”

“Oh. Okay.”

Rio looked over at her friend as they buckled themselves into the front seats and hoped she was doing the right thing.

One step at a time.

🦕🦖🦕

Amelia ran into the house and straight into Agatha’s arms. “Mommy! Is anyone here yet?” She asked, looking around excitedly.

Agatha let out a genuine laugh, “No, sweet girl, though Ralph, Ian, and Ellie should be here soon.”

It was mid-morning, and Agatha had set out brunch-like snacks with Darcy’s help. She ran a hand through her long, dark brown waves. She was dressed simply, in shorts and a sleeveless shirt. Yes, she liked to make a statement, but this was her element, her family. She didn’t need to do that here.

“I brought someone with me!” Amelia exclaimed, eyes wide. 

Knowing that could mean anything, she raised an eyebrow. “Animal, person, or random deity?”

“Animal, silly! Well, and Aunt Alice.” Giggling, the little girl wiggled away from her Mother and attempted to whistle, the grumpy bearded dragon running into the kitchen where they stood.

“Hello, Victoria.” Agatha greeted cooly, one hand braced against the counter, the other on her hip. “Scratch is in the basement. He’s missed you, you wannabe dinosaur.”

Victoria, remembering their last encounter, let out a small flame before finding her way to the basement to reunite with her old friend. 

Agatha rolled her eyes and turned back to her youngest child. “I see you have another friend with you.” Her eyes dropped to the brown bunny clutched securely in the mini entity’s tiny fingers. “Who’s this?”

“Sir Cuddles.” The girl said quietly. “He needed me this weekend.”

Agatha dropped into a kneeling position in front of Amelia. “Is that right, Plumita? Well, Sir Cuddles is very cute, and you absolutely did the right thing by bringing him. Change can be very scary, and I can’t imagine how overwhelming this week has been for him. He definitely needs your snuggles this weekend.”

Running a hand through Amelia’s soft hair, she leaned forward and kissed her forehead, hoping her little girl understood she didn’t mean Sir Cuddles at all. Standing, she took her other hand and walked toward the front room, swinging their hands between them. 

The nickname had rolled off her tongue without thought, and it fit. Rio was Feathers, so naturally, Amelia was Plumita, little feather. It was something she could unpack and drink over much later. 

The miniature Agatha looked up at her, bunny tucked protectively under her arm. “Mommy, what do I call Ralph, Ian, and Ellie?”

Agatha started to answer, but was cut off by a familiar voice.

“Whatever you want, princesa. Uncle, Dad, Aunt, you with the face.” Ralph stopped when his eyes landed on the young girl at Agatha’s side. “You are a perfect mix of your mothers, beautiful. Harkness genes run strong, but you absolutely have your mama’s smile. Well done, Skelator.”

Amelia blushed and hid her face in Agatha’s side, usually not afraid to take compliments. 



Rio averted her eyes, surprised at the compliment from Ralph of all people, but not at the nickname. “Thanks,” she mumbled.

She was still trying to process her emotions from Agatha calling their daughter Plumita, and now the entire gang was rolling in.

Maybe she needed to snuggle Sir Cuddles. 

Alice squeezed her elbow and gave her a reassuring nod. She could do this.

Ian was already discussing chaos theories with the four-year-old, and Ellie was talking to Darcy. That blew Rio’s mind. The changes Agatha had made were shocking, but to see Ellie having a civilized conversation with Agatha’s current girlfriend startled Rio to no end. 

The same woman who had sabotaged them twice was laughing and smiling with Darcy.

Had Rio accidentally reset a timeline? Had she and Agatha fucked things up so much that they threw something completely off track? Had Ellie suffered a major concussion in the plane crash?

Air. She needed air.

“I’m going outside.” She informed Alice before heading through the back of the house and finding a shaded area to lounge in a chair. Despite it being August, it was pleasantly warm, not extremely hot, with a light breeze. 

She reminded herself of what the detective had said earlier. One step at a time. This was what was best for Amelia. She couldn’t focus on who was being nice to whom, or how Agatha and Darcy looked at each other, or how her heart clenched every time her witch interacted with Amelia.

None of that.

Shoulders tensing on instinct, she looked up to see Ellie heading toward her, watching as the blonde woman lowered herself into the seat beside her.

“It’s good to see you, Rio.”

She scoffed. “The way Lily talked all summer, I expected more of a beating.”

“A few years ago, perhaps. Not now.” The blonde watched her closely. 

“Why? Because of Amelia?”

“Partially. I was around the kids a lot when they were growing up. We made sure never to argue or show any weirdness in front of them. It happened, and they knew, but we tried.”

“She makes sure we go to another room, away from Milly, before we argue.” Rio reflected.

“Exactly.”

“Is that what you’re doing with Darcy? Playing nice?” The entity asked, pushing down her jealousy. “All you ever did with me was push me away, sabotage us.”

The younger scientist considered the question carefully before answering. “I saw how she was after everything happened, Rio. She practically lived with me, slept in my bed every night. Not like that, so stop looking at me like you want to kill me. I had never seen her in that state. Not one of the many times we had broken up, or after any of the horrible things Evanora had done. If she and Darcy were to break up? She’d be upset, sure, but not like that.

“The way the two of you hit it off immediately at that summit? How she completely shifted almost overnight? I knew then something was different. After Oxford, she told me you were bonded. Yes, I teased her on the island, but it was there that I realized she and I were always temporary.”

If Rio weren’t a cosmic being, she would think she was hallucinating. “So, that’s why you’re fine with Darcy, because you know she’s keeping Aggie happy for now.”

“Yep.”

A memory came to Death, and her eyes lit up. “She lets me call her Ags and Aggie. She refused to let you do that.”

“Thanks for the reminder.” Ellie grimaced.

“But, why?”

“I truly was awful when we broke up, and we really were toxic for each other romantically. I used her mother against her and for financial gain, but then I double-crossed the old crone.” She shrugged. “We have always made an amazing research team, great friends. Once we buried things, patched old wounds? We went back to truly being us.” 

Ellie considered everything that had happened over the last sixteen years. “Ags knows she was equally, if not more, in the wrong than you were, and so much went wrong in those few hours. She wasn’t even supposed to be there at the same time you were.” She tilted her head to the side, “I do agree hiding the kid was shitty.” The woman rose to her feet and strode back to the house. “You two make beautiful babies.”

Rio leaned back in the chair, thinking over what Agatha had said about being at fault, and now Ellie. Was this witch, who never took responsibility for anything, actually going to own up to her actions?

Had Rio really not been as awful as she had thought she had? And why was Ellie the one telling her that?

What did Dr. Sattler mean when she said the witch wasn’t supposed to be on the island at the same time as Death?

Rio needed a drink.

🦕🦖🦕

Eternity paced in front of her desk. She was an important entity and liked to stay organized; she had an office much like any other CEO. Around her were screens displaying statistics and models of various timelines, populations decreasing. She knew a crisis was brewing, and yet Death had her head in the sand.

Even the human news was reporting on increased death rates. 

She needed to take Amelia soon. The chaotic force would give Eternity what she needed to either leverage with her sister or reintroduce exception. 

She also needed to find out exactly what the child was capable of, what her powers were, and how much of a threat she was. If she were too powerful, then Amelia would simply have to be destroyed. 

She was pulled from her plotting by one of the reapers she had recruited to her side in her efforts to undo the damage caused by her sister. He spoke to her in an ancient language that was more sound than word. 

“Harkness.” She hissed. “Of course, she’s surrounded the little brat with people.” Turning back to the reaper with a pointed look, “Keep an eye on them. I want to know the minute everyone is gone, and there’s an opening to snatch the little demon easily.” This niece of hers was already giving her a headache. “Instruct another reaper to make space for her!” She instructed.

She sneered. 

Agatha Harkness and her damn bonds with people were a pain in her ass.  

🦕🦖🦕

Agatha looked on as Amelia sat atop Pietro’s shoulders in the pool, trying to push Billy off Nicky’s. 

Lily swam up behind her twin and poked him, hard in his sides, bringing them tumbling down. Laughter rang out as they began accusing Lily of sabotage, Tommy making sure Amelia was swimming okay on her own. 

Lilia sat next to her pseudo-daughter, watching how easily everyone had taken to Amelia, and smiled.

“Five years and you didn’t find a way to tell me?” Agatha whispered, not wanting to ruin the moment for herself. 

“I didn’t tell my own wife, Agatha.”

“Yes, but she knew. Milly showed me her special blanket.” The younger woman kept her eyes on the youngest child in the pool. “Convenient color choices for someone who didn’t know.”

“We’ve been married for quite some time, Aggie. She knows how to read me.” She explained. “But, you have to remember, Rio and I see time differently than you do,” She held up a hand to silence her daughter. “Yes, I understand what she hid from you, but Ags, you didn’t see her the way I did. She was torn the entire time. You have to stop playing the victim here.”

“What? I missed out on five years, Lilia. I absolutely am the victim.”

Lilia tried to keep her voice even as Sharon took a seat next to her, giving them knowing looks and taking her wife's hand. “You’re not, you’re just good at playing one.” She placed her hand on the younger witch’s knee. “Neither is Rio. Look at that little girl right there. She should have grown up with this, surrounded by her family. That baby is strong and resilient, but she is the true casualty here. She didn’t ask to be born and hidden. She didn’t ask not to know you. She didn’t ask to enter Rio’s dreams. She didn’t ask to have an inevitable stepmother.” The centuries-old witch’s voice hardened. 

“You and Ralph made sure the four oldest ones were raised in a healthy home, despite your divorce. They grew up with three parents and a weirdly close aunt. They had two grandmothers close by, two other aunts who loved them. Amelia had none of that until now. Fix it, Harkness.” Lilia stood, needing to walk off some of the emotions from the past five years.

Agatha’s lips were slightly parted from the shock of Lilia’s words as the words repeated in her mind. 

Sharon moved closer to the stunned witch. “Keeping that secret from you, me, everyone? It took a toll on her. I knew something was going on, and then I saw a pregnant Rio in the background of a video call. I never told either of them that. I knew automatically who the other parent was.” 

Agatha’s eyes widened. She would never underestimate Sharon Calderu again. She’d always seen her as a little ditsy, a little too ‘good witch’ for her taste.

“It’s why I accepted Lilia being away so much. I knew Rio needed her. It allowed me to put in more work on my crafts. Even started a small business online.” She waved her hand as if to wave that topic away. “No matter. You’re a very intelligent witch, Agatha. I trust you’ll figure it out. Not all problems can be solved with Magick. You know that, dear.” Sharon stood to take the plate of food Wanda was carrying.

“Oh, sit down, honey. Where’s your wife?”

Wanda nodded her appreciation. “It’s fine, Sharon. She was helping with the grill. Go eat,” she shooed Sharon away before she could hover. 

“I should go make Amelia a plate,” Agatha said, leaning forward to stand, but Wanda put a hand on her arm to stop her.

“She has at least five people fighting over who gets to help her. You and Rio should maybe take this chance to eat and discuss co-parenting plans. This isn’t your first rodeo.” Wanda suggested between bites of her kebab.

She looked at the redhead, brow raised. “What has gotten into all of you today? I thought that we were all mad at Rio, and we all loved Darcy.”

“We are all Team Amelia, and you need to have better communication skills.” 

“I have stepped into the Twilight Zone.” She groaned. “I’m going to check on my child.” 

Darcy stopped her from standing this time. “Aggie, I brought you something to eat. We can share. I know you don’t eat nearly enough when you’re moody.”

“I’m not moody.” She replied defensively. 

“The vein in your forehead is showing.” The younger woman sat and leaned forward to kiss the offending area. 

Agatha took a moment to look at what her girlfriend was wearing. Swim shorts, what looked to be a one-piece, and a rashguard. Darcy was covering up her best assets, and Agatha took that personally.

 

“Darce?”

“Yes?”

“What are you wearing?” She could hear Wanda rolling her eyes.

Darcy looked down at herself and back at the older woman. “Family-friendly swimwear. I’m not going to wear one of my two-pieces around your ex and youngest child during family bonding time, Agatha. It’s inappropriate.”

She hated that Darcy was right and reminded herself that this was for Amelia. She would have to be the strongest soldier of the gods right now. 

She leaned over, close to the younger woman’s ear. “Can I have a kiss at least?” 

Darcy turned and planted a kiss on Agatha’s cheek. “G-rated only, Aggie. If you’re good, we can make out before we go to sleep.”

The witch turned sharply to glare at Wanda, who was flushed from holding back her laughter. “I’m going to check on Milly.” She grumbled, storming off.

 

Following the sound of giggles, she turned to see two of the outdoor tables by the pool pushed together. Amelia sat on Lily’s lap, her brothers and nephews seated around the table. Jen and Ralph were glancing at Amelia and talking, Agatha assumed, about her health. Ian and Lilia were engaged in the kids' discussion. 

She looked back to where she had been sitting, noting that Zora was now with Wanda, and Darcy had moved elsewhere. Scanning the area, she saw Ellie and Darcy walking to another part of the yard where Sharon and Alice were. 

Which left only one person unaccounted for.

“Amelia’s enjoyed herself today.”

There she is. Agatha thought to herself. “I noticed. She’s played hard, too. They all have, really.”

Rio stepped up next to Agatha, arms nearly brushing. “I’ve spent a lot of time, uh, reconnecting, I guess, with the family.”

“Oh?” Agatha kept her eyes on the table where most of her children sat.

“It’s been nice. Thank you for doing this. It means a lot to Milly.” 

Agatha knew what it meant for Rio to extend an olive branch, especially after Lilia's lecture. She also knew that Amelia was the reason for all of this, and in order to make things right for her precious daughter, she had to be at least cordial with Death.

“Of course. I want what’s best for her. We both do.” She clenched her jaw, not in anger, but to keep her emotions in line. She swallowed thickly at how Amelia relaxed against Lily. It was early evening, and she was clearly worn out from the day. 

“I’m sorry for overriding everything this week and forcing things on you.” It was barely a whisper, partially in hopes that Rio wouldn’t hear her.

She did. “Excuse me? Did the great Agatha Harkness just apologize to me?”

“You're hearing things, Feathers.” 

 

She tossed her hair back and walked toward the table, resting her hands on Ralph's shoulders. He instinctively covered one of her hands with his. 

“Now that everyone’s eaten,” She began, “I see someone is getting tired and probably needs to get in the bath.”

The youngest set of twins groaned. “Do we have to?”

Agatha bit the inside of her cheek to keep from laughing.

“I wasn’t talking about you two, but yes. Take your showers. Find a mother.” Her eyes landed on Amelia. “You, Plumita, are the one I was speaking about.”

“You’ve been caught, Green Goblin.” Lily tickled her sides to help wake her up a bit more. “We can watch a movie down in the basement when we’re all showered and changed.”

“Yeah!” The little girl lit up, climbing out of her sister’s lap, grabbing her Mother’s hand, and pulling her toward the house. “Will you and Mama both give me a bath?” 

Agatha looked over at Rio. “Yes?”

“Of course.” She turned and followed them inside.

 

Ralph chuckled. “Guess we’re on clean-up duty.”

Jen rolled her eyes. “I can tell you haven’t lived with Agatha or the kids in a while.” She whispered something in Latin and pointed her finger into the air, and the trash and food disappeared. “Leftovers are in the fridge.”

Ian looked toward the remaining kids. “So, which one of you is moving to Connecticut?”



“Amelia, this bathtub is gigantic. How are you still getting the bubbles everywhere?” Rio asked her daughter from her position on the bathroom counter.

“Because I’m a mermaid, Mama!” She giggled, splashing her crossed legs like a mermaid tail.

“No splashing, you’re making a mess.” She scolded, not wanting to anger Agatha. They were actually being civil with each other—no need to ruin it.

“Nonsense, it’s easy to clean up.” Walking into her bathroom with a hooded towel in the shape of a dinosaur, Agatha winked at the mermaid.

Rio huffed. “We have to set ground rules and make sure we’re on the same page.” She whispered where Amelia, hopefully, couldn’t hear.

“Later,” kneeling next to the tub, she reached for the shampoo she had brought from Amelia’s bathroom. “Your Aunt Jen made this. She said it’s the same kind you use at home.”

The mermaid nodded. “I can even do it myself! Aunt Jen made it not to hurt my eyes.”

“Well, have at it, kid.” She stood and moved to lean against the counter next to Rio, lowering her voice. “Today’s been weird.”

The entity nodded. “Tell me about it. Ellie told me you weren’t supposed to be there that night.” 

Agatha straightened. “Talk to Ian and Lilith about that.” Clapping her hands together, she stepped back toward the large bathtub. “Alright, kiddo, time to get out and watch movies downstairs!” 



Lilia and Sharon had said their goodbyes, promising Amelia that they’d return in the morning. Zora, Pietro, Jen, and Nicky had taken over Agatha’s office for emergency business calls. Darcy was on standby in case she was needed, but was in the living room, conversing with Alice and Ellie. 

Agatha sat next to Darcy, a hand on her thigh, which was currently allowed since the kids were watching a movie in the converted basement. Wanda leaned against her mother, nearly asleep. 

In the kitchen, Rio was playing chess with Ian, while Ralph supervised. 

“She’s older than time, she’s going to win.” 

Ian shook his head. “Not necessarily. The odds are in her favor, but she prefers order, predictability, balance. I prefer chaos, surprise.”

“Checkmate.” Rio grinned at her former colleague. 

“Where’s Nicky? I can at least beat him in this game.” Ian mused.

Ralph shook his head. “Stick to creating dinosaurs, dear.”

“Actually, I need to talk to you about something. Ellie told me Agatha wasn’t supposed to be at the park that day, and Ags told me to ask you and Lily.” 

Lily appeared, a sleeping Amelia in her arms. “Rio, do you want to put Milly to bed? I didn’t want her to get crushed in the cuddle puddle of boy she was in.”

Standing, Rio took the sleeping child in her arms. “Yes, I’ll take her. Stay up here, please. I need to talk to you and Papa Chaos here when I’m done.” 

The young woman audibly swallowed as Death walked away with her little sister. She turned back to her dads at the table. “Are we in trouble?”

Ralph nodded. “She knows your mom wasn’t supposed to be there that week. Take a seat, Lilypad.”

“Well fuck.”



Rio was halfway up the stairs when she felt the familiar presence behind her. “Can I help you?”

“Do you know where her room is?” Agatha asked softly.

Rio had expected a snippy comment, not this. It really had been a weird day. “Yes, she showed me earlier.”

“I’d like to say goodnight, too. It’s her first night staying with me, good impressions and all.”

Was Agatha Harkness, self-proclaimed great parent, anxious about Amelia staying? Rio was starting to think the end of the world was nigh.

“Okay, Agatha.”

They stepped into Amelia’s space and galaxy-themed room, Agatha turning down the sheets and switching on the nightlight.

“She doesn’t sleep with a nightlight,” Rio murmured. 

Agatha’s jaw twitched faintly in the dark. “Oh. Not even to see if she gets up in the middle of the night?” 

The entity lay their child down and pulled the covers back over her. “No, she usually uses her magick as light.”

“Right. She already has it.” Agatha ran a hand through her hair before reaching for Sir Cuddles and tucking the bunny in with Milly. “Anything else I should know?” She whispered.

“She’ll end up kicking her blankets off and only sleeping with her special one. Don’t be surprised if you check on her and she’s levitating, or her dreams are projected around the room.” Leaning over, she kissed their daughter on the forehead. “I love you, cariño, see you in the morning.” She stepped aside to let Agatha say goodnight, deciding it was only fair.

Agatha sat on the edge of the bed and lightly stroked Amelia’s face, careful not to wake her. “She’s a deep sleeper.” 

“She gets that from you.”

A tiny smile tugged at the corner of Agatha’s mouth. She leaned over and lightly kissed the girl on the forehead as Rio had done. “Te amo mucho, Plumita. Siento mucho que te hayas visto envuelta en todo esto.” 

Standing, she brushed past Rio and out into the hallway. Rio rushed to stay close and caught her by the wrist. “Aggie.”

“Feathers.”

“What’s going on? Less than twenty-four hours ago, we were fighting. Now, you’re apologizing? Being nice?” Tilting her head, continuing to stare at the back of Agatha’s head, her voice dropped even lower. “Very unlike you.”



The professor froze and yanked her wrist from the Green Witch’s hold, not daring to look at her, trying to keep an even temper. “Don’t read into it. It’s for Amelia.” She tried to keep an edge to her voice, but failed.

“Lilia?”

“I don’t know what you’re talking about.” The witch knew she had to keep her defenses up around the cosmic demon behind her.

“Yeah, me, too. So did Ellie. It’s been an interesting few days.” 

Agatha stayed there, expecting Rio to say something, anything, to cut through the charged silence. Agatha had plenty she wanted to say, but couldn’t. The words wouldn’t climb out of her throat or form in her mouth. There was so much more to say, to apologize for, and at the same time, she wanted to yell and scream. Beg and plead. Pull Death into her bedroom and show her how mad she was. Show her how much she missed her. Show her how much she hated her. Show her how much she loved her.

Darcy.

The name flashed through her mind like a warning signal. She loved Darcy. Darcy loved her.

Fingers trailed down her spine, causing her to shiver on reflex. She turned, expecting Rio, finding Darcy instead.

Why was she excited and disappointed to see her at the same time?

“Aggie? What are you doing up here by yourself? Rio came downstairs a few minutes ago.”

“Oh. Just lost in my thoughts, I guess.” Hands automatically interlocking, she led the way to her bedroom. “I think today went well, don’t you?”

“Yes. Don’t you think we should go back downstairs?” Darcy asked.

Agatha pulled the woman into the room and against her. “I have a better idea.”

“Agatha!” She exclaimed, blushing. “You’ve been insatiable this week!”

“Good stress relief.” Pressing Darcy against the back of the door, their mouths meeting in a heated kiss, her thigh pressing between the young woman’s thighs, when memories flashed through Agatha’s mind.

Sixteen years ago, in a hotel an ocean away. Another dark-haired woman with her back pressed against the door, just like this. 

Agatha released Darcy and staggered back, as though the memory had burned her.

They had done this numerous times; Agatha had never had a flashback until that moment.

Concern flooded Darcy’s face. “Aggie? Is everything okay?”

She blinked a few times, trying to clear the images that kept playing. “Yes, baby, I’m fine. Just got a little light-headed all of a sudden.”

Darcy’s face softened, reaching out and caressing Agatha’s cheek. “You didn’t eat enough. Come with me, I’ll make you a snack.” Taking her girlfriend’s hand, she kissed the back of it and smiled. “Everything’s going to work out, baby.”

Agatha’s smile was tight as she tried to keep herself together. She needed to consult the Darkhold on soulmates and the fated, and to find everything she could in other ancient texts.

Some of their kind thought the Darkhold only held Dark Magick, when really, it had held spells and records of all the rarest kinds of Magick. It’s why she and Wanda had sought it out.

Hearing voices in the kitchen, she pulled the younger woman back. “It sounds like they’re having a rather important conversation. I can conjure something from the kitchen.”

Darcy scoffed. “I’d much rather make something for you; you can’t rely on magick for everything.” 

It was one of the few things they disagreed on. While Darcy did see Agatha for who and what she truly was, and loved her despite it, she wasn’t a huge fan of the witch factor. She couldn’t stand Agatha’s hands in their natural state, and while she was fine with analog Magick, crystals, tarot, and the like. She didn’t care for the type of spontaneous creation Agatha and family did so often. It defied scientific logic.

Still, it was the healthiest relationship Agatha had ever had, and she was mostly thriving. She could attempt to compromise. 

Agatha’s smile loosened into something more authentic when Darcy twirled the white streak she found so enticing in between her fingers and kissed the corner of her lover’s mouth. “Let me take care of you in the ways I can. Go sit down and dote on Wanda. I’ll fix her something, too. Text me about any cravings she has.”

The witch sighed softly, blue eyes sparkling while she gently cupped Darcy’s face. “You’re so good to us.” She pressed a kiss to the young woman’s forehead.

“Of course. I did sign up for the whole package. Now, go, sit.”

Agatha kissed her again and headed to the living room while Darcy made her way to the kitchen. 

Taking a seat next to Wanda, she surveyed the room, noticing Zora, Jen, and the older boys had yet to return. “Are they still dealing with business?”

Wanda yawned and nodded. “Yes. I’m hungry.”

Agatha chuckled. “Darcy thought you might be; she’s getting us both something to eat. Any specific cravings, Angel?”

Wanda tilted her head, thinking. “Celery and cream cheese, black and green olives, and if there are any stuffed mushrooms left, those.”

“I’ll let her know.” Agatha smiled, remembering her own pregnancy cravings. She had wanted vegetables in all forms, especially baby corn. Señor Scratchy had stayed very close to her in case one went rogue. For a quick second, her mind wandered to what Rio’s cravings had been.

“I’ve already got it.” Darcy’s voice floated through the room. “Mushrooms are heating now. I figured you were still having the same cravings as earlier.” Handing the plate and a large glass of water to Wanda, she leaned over to press a kiss against Agatha’s hair. “Aggie told me you craved Swedish Fish and watermelon with the boys, but haven’t wanted any of that with Gabby. I picked some up, though, just in case.” She added before heading back to the kitchen.

Watching her retreat to the kitchen, Agatha poked her tongue into her cheek, thoughts scattered.

Ellie paused her conversation with Alice and looked over at her best friend. “You two are so domestic, it’s sickening.”

Alice hummed in agreement, eyes narrowing. 

Wanda popped an olive in her mouth. “Just remember who your focus is on.”

🦕🦖🦕

Rio left Agatha to her own thoughts, knowing that if she stayed much longer, they would find themselves in a situation they couldn’t come back from. 

She padded back downstairs and rejoined the three people she had left in the kitchen. Ian and Lily were fidgeting, and Ralph sat with his arms crossed over his chest as if he knew what was about to happen. Sitting down across from them, she rolled her shoulders and propped her chin in her hand.

“Start talking. What happened five years ago?”

Ian put a fatherly hand on Lily’s shoulder and began. “I told Agatha to come a week ahead of schedule because we were thinking of opening earlier and needed her to double-check the enclosures. When, really, it was so your visits would overlap.”

Death raised an eyebrow and turned to Lily. “Why?”

“Mom missed you. She’d spent four and a half years trying to explain, and had us explain. Jen had restored your Magick. She hadn’t been the same since Oxford and hadn’t gotten a chance to explain herself. So, Papa here, and I tried to get you in the same place at least.” The young adult shook her head, chewing on the skin around a fingernail. “I didn’t realize your balance changes would affect it so much.”

Ian squeezed Lily’s shoulder. “So many things wouldn’t have happened that night. Now, I don’t know if Vision would have kidnapped Lily or not, but I also know you wouldn’t have Amelia. Maybe you would have later, who knows. I apologize for the part we played, and I take complete responsibility for all of it.”

Rio sensed Darcy enter the room, but continued talking. “I am thankful for Amelia, and wouldn’t change that. I don’t know what would have changed, but you two can’t meddle in things.”

“She was going to seek you out anyway.” Ralph interrupted. “As soon as she was no longer tethered? She planned to see you, tell you everything. She never meant to hurt you, but she knows where she went wrong.” He paused, watching Darcy carefully. “She respected your boundaries these past five years. I know that’s not a long time to immortal beings, but she’s new to this. You told her you would hate her forever, and she took that to heart, Rio. You cut the bond. That felt pretty damn final to her.” 

Ralph made sure Darcy had left the room before he continued.

“I know you think she doesn’t love you, but Death, I know that woman better than anyone else in the world, except maybe Lilia. She loves you. It may take a thousand years for her to admit it, but she loves you.”

Rio scrubbed her palms over her eyes. “Fucking Harknesses.”

No one corrected her.

“Amelia is our focus. Agatha has Darcy. She’s made it very clear where I stand, no matter what the three of you say. Right now, our goal is to be a cohesive family unit for our daughter.” She pushed her chair out, “I do accept the apology, I know you were just trying to help.”



Lily watched her friend walk to the stairs, assumed she was checking on Amelia, and followed her. “Rio, wait.” 

The older woman stopped and half-turned. 

“I didn’t just do it for mom. It was for you, too. We had grown close during that time, and you talked about her so much. You’re happier when you’re around her. The two of you truly belong together.” Pausing for a moment, she wrapped her in a hug. “Thank you for the apartment, and everything. I’m sorry I was a bit harsh all summer.”

Rio smirked. “I needed it.”

🦕🦖🦕

It had been a long day for Darcy Lewis. She didn’t hate Rio. On the contrary, she had known her for years before the woman went into hiding. The cosmic being was wise and clever, seeming to know everything. The young scientist had always looked up to and admired her. However, seeing everyone pull her aside and talk with her today had been rough. She already had to live with the fact that Agatha, HER Agatha, was bound to this woman in ways that the human would never understand.

Thankfully, Darcy had Ellie.

Darcy met her when she was a student at Yale, working in IT on the side. Dr. Sattler could bring dinosaurs to life, yet computers were not her strong suit. Even though the student was a twenty-year-old undergrad, she had been wise beyond her years, leading her to work on the island's IT network. Then at Harkness Holdings. 

It was why she clung to Ellie during the day. They had a few major things in common: Agatha Harkness and knowing how easy she would melt for Rio Vidal, no matter what she said.

Watching Agatha soften towards her ex hours after she had finally professed her love was a punch to the gut. The witch was known to hold a grudge, yet here she was, going soft.

When Agatha rose from the couch to follow Rio upstairs, Darcy had sighed audibly, earning looks from the other women.

Wanda reached over and patted her leg. “She loves you. She’s just doing what’s best.”

“I know. She told me last night.” Darcy watched as they disappeared down the upstairs hallway.

“That’s great!” The redhead exclaimed. “I’m not calling you my stepmom.” She added with a breathy laugh.

Ellie studied her carefully. “You know, this is the longest time they’ve been together without fucking.”

“What?” The young brunette asked hurriedly.

“You’re right,” Wanda confirmed, turning to Darcy. “That’s good for you!”

The blonde scientist nodded. “Usually, they’re going at it within hours, if not minutes, upon seeing each other. But Agatha hasn’t even made a move.”

Darcy leaned into the couch with a huff. “That doesn’t make me feel better.”

Alice looked up from her position on the floor, leaning against the couch Ellie was on. “It should. Agatha always goes for what she wants. She wants you.”



Darcy wasn’t convinced, and when Rio came down without Agatha, she was even less so. 

Agatha, wanting to take her up against the door moments after whatever happened with Rio and then stumbling back as though Darcy’s skin burned her, didn’t exactly comfort her either.

Overhearing that Agatha had planned to get back with Death all those years ago? This was not turning out to be the best of nights, and she was about to make it worse.

Rio, Alice, and Jen had left to go back to Rio’s, and everyone else had finally gone to bed. It had been a long day with the rest of the weekend still ahead of them. Darcy stood in front of her sink in Agatha’s bathroom, brushing her teeth, readying herself for the conversation ahead of her.

As expected, the woman in question stood next to her, washing her face with a frown. “Why are you wearing so much fabric? We’re about to go to bed. Is that a sports bra under your shirt?” 

Darcy spit and wiped her mouth. “I’m sleeping in the guest bedroom this weekend, Aggie.”

Agatha’s jaw dropped as she staggered back dramatically. “Shot. I’ve been shot.”

Darcy rolled her eyes, mouthwash swishing around her tongue and against her cheeks.

The older woman stared at her. “Did I say something? Do something? Is this a punishment?”

She shook her head and rinsed her mouth out. “Of course not, baby. This is Amelia’s first night here. She’s not used to you, let alone us. What if she wakes up in the middle of the night? It’s better if I sleep in the other room for now.” Washing her face clean, she leaned over and kissed her girlfriend’s cheek.

The paleontologist followed her out of the room. “Darce, this is extreme.”

“No, it’s not.”

“Did someone say something? Did Rio?”

She stopped near the door and turned on her heel. “Ags, no. My parents divorced when I was Amelia’s age, and I am trying to show her the same respect I wish my parents and their multiple partners had shown me.”

“Oh.” 

“But, um, is it true you were going to go after Rio if everything had gone according to your plan on the island?” She asked nervously, chewing on her bottom lip. It wasn’t that it mattered, but something inside of her needed to know.

Meeting her eyes, the witch stepped forward, running a hand through Darcy’s hair. “You have to understand, honey, she’s, well, she was, my soulmate. We were fated. It’s very sacred amongst witchkind. So yes, before everything went up in flames, that was my plan. We made some final decisions that night, under very heightened emotions.” Her fingers tangled in the hair at the base of Darcy’s skull. “But things didn’t go right. The fates had a different plan, and now, I’m yours.” 

The gap between them closed, Agatha, moving her hand to hold Darcy’s cheek, kissed her deeply, pouring everything she had into it.

The young woman parted her lips and eagerly welcomed the older woman’s tongue. A hand tangled in the mass of wild brunette curls holding Agatha close to her, the other hand on the small of the woman’s back. 

“Mine.” Darcy moaned into her girlfriend’s mouth. She couldn’t help but wonder how long the woman in her arms would truly be hers.

Darcy broke the kiss and stepped back, eliciting a whine from her older girlfriend, and smirked. “Goodnight, Agatha.”

“Goodnight, Darce.” She replied grumpily, arms crossed. She crawled into the large, empty bed as the heavy wooden door shut. 

Staring at the ceiling fan above her, something felt off. She kept telling herself that was to be expected with kids asleep in the basement, Darcy under her roof but not in her bed, several guest rooms full, and a child she had known for less than a week in a newly designated bedroom.

 

Amelia. She needed to check on Amelia. 

Throwing the covers off herself and tossing her legs over the side of the bed, she reached for her glasses. Entering the hall, she nearly stepped on Victoria, who was hauling tail to the little girl’s bedroom. “Whoa, girl. Timmy fall down a well?”

The witch was thankful the bearded dragon did not shift into something that could eat her.

Quietly, she stepped into the space-themed room and into a memory. Not necessarily Amelia’s, but Rio’s. 

Her daughter was still asleep, albeit fitfully. Curled into a tight ball, shivering, whimpering.

 

Around them, lifelike figures played out various scenes throughout the past few years. Rio in that blasted dinosaur shirt, holding Sir Cuddles and crying. Rio staring at her phone, Agatha’s number displayed, thumb hovering over the screen. Rio watching interviews of Agatha discussing projects, with a small smile on her face. Rio giving lectures about notable paleontologists and doing a unit about Agatha’s discoveries and contributions. 

Then the memories went further back. The night on top of the mountain. Agatha and Wanda walking through the portal. Death automatically causing the cage to disappear and walking off. 

 

Agatha tilted her head. So she hadn’t been trapped?

Death crumbling to the ground after she transferred to her New York apartment. Wailing.

The grief between Oxford and Isla Dunblar.

The questions and light Facebook stalking between London and Oxford.

Agatha saw it all, and what was worse was that their daughter had stumbled upon it by being nosy. Internalized it. Felt it. Now, all of those emotions had turned into a nightmare for her. 

As much as Agatha wanted to know and see more, she needed to wake and comfort Amelia. Stepping forward, she placed a gentle hand on the sleeping girl’s shoulder, careful not to startle her. 

 

“Plumita,” she cooed, “despierta, estás teniendo una pesadilla.”

“La familia es para siempre.” Amelia mumbled in her troubled sleep.

The phrase lodged itself between Agatha’s ribs. Their family motto of sorts. “Sí, plumita.” Stroking her hair, her brows furrowed, she watched the memories swirl around them, when an idea came to her.

“Down, down, down the road…” She began singing the first few words of the ancient song, the memories dissipating. 

Victoria, now a cat, jumped onto the bed and curled up beside Milly, purring, Señor Scratchy close behind.

Agatha knew it would be okay to leave, yet she couldn’t bring herself to do so. Instead, she did something she’d done countless times with her other children. She lay down in the full-sized bed, curled up next to the four-year-old, and drifted off to sleep. 

🦕🦖🦕

For the first time in years, Rio Vidal had the chance to sleep in on a Saturday.

She was awake, dressed, and ready to head back to Agatha’s by six a.m.

“Whoa, slow down,” Alice said sleepily, grinding the sleep from her eyes. “At least drink coffee or something. 

“She’s with her other mother, surrounded by witches. She’s completely safe.” Jen added with a yawn.

“She’s never been away from me this long, and if she has, she’s been with Lorna or Lilia. Not Agatha ‘kill first and ask questions later’ Harkness.”

“Rio. She’s her daughter, too.” Jen reminded her.

Sighing, she made her coffee to go. “Fine, can we go now?”



“I told you no one would be awake yet.” Jen hissed, disarming the alarm and entering the large house.

Lilia chuckled. “We came over to start breakfast. I also figured someone would be impatient.” Turning to Death, she pointed upstairs. “Still asleep.” 

Nodding, she headed for the staircase, taking them two at a time.

Slowly, Rio opened the door to Milly’s room, her breath catching upon seeing her daughter curled up in her witch’s arms.

Her girls.

No. She’s Darcy’s, not yours. She thought to herself.

“Aren’t they sweet?” Darcy asked, drawing up behind her. 

“They are. You’re up early. Couldn’t sleep without Agatha?” Rio didn’t intend for it to come out as snippy as it did.

Darcy’s face remained neutral. “I was helping Lils in the kitchen. I slept in the guest room, just so you know.” She gestured back to Amelia’s room. “I took pictures if you want them.” The younger woman looked to the side, as though she were considering her next words carefully. “May I speak to you for a moment, please?”

Rio was taken aback. This woman was sleeping with the mother of her child. What could she possibly want from her?

“Sure, I guess.” She answered skeptically, following the young brunette into a room across the hall.

 

Darcy left the door cracked, turned to face the older scientist, and took a deep breath. “If you must know, I slept in a guest room out of respect for you and Amelia. There’s a lot of change right now, and she doesn’t need to figure out why Mommy is sharing a bed with me. I know she’s smart, but I also know she wants the two of you together.”

 

Death hated how much sense Darcy made. She wanted to detest this woman, yet here she was, doing what was best for Milly.

Damn this generation and their logic.

Rio cautiously nodded.

 

“You, also, didn’t know Agatha was dating, let alone in love with someone else. I want us to tolerate each other at least, if not truly get along. I’ve always respected you, Rio. Especially when we worked together.”

“Thank you for caring about Amelia,” She filtered through her words, trying to find the exact right ones. “You’re an intelligent woman, and it would be in Milly’s best interest for us all to get along.” Crossing her arms over her stomach, she bit the inside of her cheek. “I do have three questions.”

The other woman quirked an eyebrow. “Okay, sure.”

“Why does this matter so much to you?”

Darcy’s stance shifted. “Because it matters to Aggie. Because her family is my family. Because I was in a similar situation at Amelie’s age and learned a thing or two.”

“I’ll accept that. If Agatha wanted to have more kids, how would you feel about that?” 

Darcy looked away. “We haven’t talked about it, and I’m not sure.”

“Fair. Last one. I saw how much time you spent with Ellie yesterday. What’s the story there?”

Darcy scoffed and shook her head. “Aggie knows we’re just friends, Rio. I understand you want to know what kind of person I am; you won't find any dirt on me. I just want to make sure we’re doing right by Amelia. I’ll send you the pictures.” Turning, she left the room, slightly frustrated. 

 

Rio returned to Amelia’s room only to discover that the bed was now empty. Her phone dinged, pictures from Darcy as promised. 

This was much harder than she had previously thought it would be.



Agatha stood in the enchanted part of her basement, sealed off and hidden from most everyone else. Wanda sat in a recliner specifically for her, feet propped up, munching on pigs in a blanket from breakfast. 

“So she broke free herself? After saying all those things to you?” 

Agatha nodded, books surrounding her, Darkhold in front of her, pages flying.

“Are you going to ask her why?”

Agatha shrugged. “I’m not sure it matters.”

“Of course it matters!” Wanda bolted upright. “If she was going to free herself, why did she say those things? Why didn’t she come after you? Us?”

“Relax, Angel. I will make you go to sleep if necessary.” The older witch smiled lovingly, though she worried about Wanda in her current condition. “Honestly, I don’t know. Okay, found a few things.”

“You’re such a mom.” The redhead rolled her eyes. “Why didn’t you look that up years ago?”

Agatha gave her daughter a look. “Look who you’re talking to, toots. I didn’t think I would need an instruction manual.”

“Right. Ancient, universe-gifted magick definitely doesn’t need instructions. Good call, oh wise one.” The Scarlet Witch rolled her eyes.

“Excuse you, look who’s talking.”

“And that’s why we use the Darkhold. So we know what the fuck we’re doing.” Wanda retorted. “I swear, you’re the dumbest genius I know.”

 

The succubus huffed, looking through the information. She had really only used the information to confirm what she already knew, not to look at the rules or how it worked. That’s what Lilia was for. 

Skimming the page of the first book, glasses slipping to the end of her nose, she jabbed a finger at the words. “This one says that the bearers of the marks can fall in love once the bond is broken, but they will always be drawn to their true love.” Slamming the book shut, she heaved it across the room. “Useless fairytale drivel.” 

Reading from the next one, “Aha! The destined can fall in love and be with one they are not destined with!” Agatha exclaimed gleefully. “As long as they have not met the one they were destined for or the bond was never formed,” she continued. “Well fuck.”

 

Book after book, she read the same damning words after another, only growing more furious, more distraught. Only the Darkhold was left.

Glaring at the sentient book, she muttered to herself, “Show me something I can use, you outdated scroll.”

“Agatha, stop taunting the sacred texts.” Wanda chided between bites of Belgian waffle.

“Where did you get the waffle?”

“Not important. Now see if the spooky self-writing book has anything new, just for you.” The redhead gestured.

 

The grandmother of soon to be three turned back to the book, which was now open to a brand-new page. “Well, it is indeed self-writing.” Pushing her glasses up her nose, she began reading the latest information. “The soulmate bond between a cosmic entity and a high-powered spirit witch and succubus,” Agatha paused and looked back to Wanda. “I feel personally called out.” Returning her attention to the book, she continued, “can be disconnected; not broken. They will always be drawn to each other and never truly happy if not together.” Taking a deep breath, the vein in her forehead popping, Agatha was ready to set the damn thing on fire.

“What else does it say, matka?” 

“Agatha Harkness was made for Death, and Death alone. Their powers combined will restore the true sacred balance, and their children will usher in a new generation of cosmic beings.” She whispered, processing the words.

“Wait, WHAT?! CHILDREN?! Look here, you musty cunt! You'd better mean my current children!” Her hand darted forward to rifle through the pages, the book snapping closed instead. “Oh, fuck you! I’m not done!” Dissappearing into the pocket dimension it called home, she swore the demented book blew a raspberry at her.

“Oh fuck that dusty, crumbling book. There must be another Agatha Harkness somewhere.” Ripping her hand through her hair, she paced through the basement, adrenaline and rage coursing through her veins. “No. I am happy.”

“If there were another powerful witch named Agatha Harkness, we would know,” The young woman’s voice was calm, soothing.

“And what is this ‘with your powers combined’ bullshit? This isn’t Captain Planet!” She spat, face red.

“You’re going to give yourself an aneurysm.”

“At least Death is upstairs to collect my soul!”

Her daughter regarded her softly. “Matka. We all know you love Darcy, but be honest with me. This whole week, what’s it been like having Amelia and Rio around?”

 

The eldest Harkness plopped down onto an old leather couch. “I have loved getting to know Amelia. It’s like when I first met you and Pietro. Learning your likes and dislikes, your fears, your hopes, your dreams, your worries.” Eyes watering at the rim, she continued. “You were already this whole person that we had the privilege to learn about, and it was absolutely a privilege. It’s the same way with Amelia. She’s this whole person. A part of me that I didn’t realize was missing.” The tear fell despite her determination to hold it back. “I have loved having her around, and only wish I could have her around more.”

“And Rio?”

Schooling her expression, Agatha looked away. “Necessary. Getting to know her parenting style is helpful. Allows us to be on the same page. She’s a great mother.”

“What are you going to do about what the Darkhold said?”

 

The professor stood, scoffing. “What I always do, Angel. What I want. I’m not breaking up with Darcy. Everything will settle into a pattern in a few weeks.”

The Scarlet Witch sighed, pushing herself to her feet. “Sure, ignore the book that put your government name on its pages in magickal ink. That sounds like a great idea.”    

🦕🦖🦕

It had always been Ralph’s mission to make sure the kids had everything they ever needed and wanted in life. Which, of course, included clothes and toys. If Agatha had her way, the kids would have worn designer everything and looked like mini-adults their whole lives.

Which was why he and Ian had taken it upon themselves to take inventory of Amelia’s closet. He knew Agatha was going to ask him anyway; he was just taking initiative. He knew Amelia wouldn’t want to leave everyone, but if she thought it was a super fun secret, she would.

Amelia was just like Lily.

Ralph, however, enjoyed living and knew he needed to ask Rio before he took Amelia anywhere. Where Agatha believed in never asking for permission or forgiveness, Ralph believed in living and, therefore, in asking, especially when cosmic entities were involved.

 

Finding the entity in the kitchen speaking with Pietro and Ian, he took a seat and waited for a lull in the conversation, not wanting to interrupt.

Pietro looked tired, turning to Ralph. “I’m trying to tell Rio about how the numbers from preventable deaths are rising.” He stressed, rubbing his eyes.

“It is. Especially among children. It hasn’t been the best time to work at a children’s hospital.” He saw a flicker of guilt in Rio’s eyes. “You gave orders to your employees, so to speak, and they’re following. You are going by the Sacred Balance, Rio. It’s a fine line.”

 

A silence fell over them. Fathers and son exchanged looks while Death seemed to be contemplating what was said.

Ralph cleared his throat and angled toward Rio. “I wanted to ask if Ian and I could borrow Milly for a bit. I know Ags wanted us to take her shopping, and as the gay dads we are, we absolutely claim that right and responsibility.”

“Why are you asking me and not Agatha?” She asked, slightly shocked.

“Because all of this,” he gestured widely, “is built on a foundation of trust and mutual respect. Also, while Agatha won’t admit it, you are her custodial parent and have the final say in everything.” Looking between Pietro, Ian, and the woman before him, he added, “None of you tell Agatha I ever said that.”

 

“You did keep three witches and Speedy over here safe.” Glancing at the other scientist, she added, “You, well, not so much.”

Ian huffed. “There were other factors at play!”

“Our daughter was kidnapped, and our son, shot, no excuses,” Ralph smirked.

“As long as you keep eyes on her at all times, I think she’d enjoy it.” Rio stood and headed to find Milly.

Ian put a hand on her arm. “Wait, there’s more.”

Standing, Ralph nodded. “She’s just like Lily. She won’t want to leave if she thinks she’ll miss something here.”

 

Pietro laughed lightly. “They’re going to make it seem like it’s a secret from everyone else. Everything was always a secret mission for Lily, and Nicky almost ruined it every time. He couldn’t tell a lie, and she was a master of deceit.”

“If she could’ve hexed him at a younger age, she would have.” Ian smiled.

“Also, I’m going to bring Agatha, since she needs to step away from all of this, and she has the ability to wrangle our little chaos princess if needed,” Ralph added, partially expecting Rio to change her mind.

 

Rio inhaled deeply, looking between the two men for what seemed like minutes when it was really seconds.

“Okay.” Rio kept her voice steady. “Age-appropriate clothing, no piercings, no pets.”



Agatha and Ian were already waiting in the getaway car, and it was on Ralph to convince Amelia to go with them. Thankfully, he was well-versed in speaking with the Harkness women. Biological or not.

The youngest cosmic entity was sitting in her room, alone, talking to Victoria and Scratchy. She had Sir Cuddles and her blanket gripped tightly in her lap as she held the attention of both familiars. 

“I really love my family, and I am so glad I have both of you. It’s better than I expected!” She exclaimed, before her voice dropped into something tinged with sadness. “I just expected Mommy and Mama to get back together. Like they’re supposed to be.”

Ralph took that as his cue to enter, not wanting Amelia to dwell on things outside of her control at such a tender age.

“Hey princesa, why are you hiding?”

Small shoulders lifted in a shrug. “There were a lot of people, and I just needed a break.”

“I need breaks from everyone, too, sometimes.” Reaching down, he scratched between Señor Scratchy’s ears before rubbing under Victoria’s chin. “These guys are great listeners, and Scratch gives fantastic advice.”

 

Eyes widening, she looked at the man who was now seated next to her on the floor. “You can hear him too!”

“Well, not quite, but I have known that bunny for thirty-six years. I can tell what he’s saying. I’ve only met Victoria a few times, but she’s a very expressive dragon.”

Amelia tilted her head, questions beyond her age in her eyes. “I know the basics of everything. Why you and mommy were married, my siblings, all of it. I’m not a normal four-year-old.”

“No one is normal in this family. You fit right in.” He inserted.

A small smile formed as she continued talking. “How did you remain friends? Mommy and Mama didn’t.” 

 

Ralph had not expected this conversation. “Cosmic entity or not, that is not a question I can answer for you, princesa. The situations were completely different. Agatha and I have always been, and will always be, best friends. Two kids who needed each other. We love each other in a very different way than how she and Rio do. It’s different when those feelings are involved. Do you know how cuts and wounds work?”

“Yes. Some are worse than others. I broke my arm once. It healed faster than normal, but it was still in a cast for three weeks.” 

“Right. So when Agatha and I get in a fight and hurt each other’s feelings, it’s like a papercut. It doesn’t take long to heal at all. But when someone you’re in love with hurts your feelings, it’s like you broke every bone in your body. It takes a very long time to heal that. They hurt each other’s feelings in a very big way.” Ralph grimaced internally. Not sure if he was making any sense.

“So, they’re still healing?” Her voice had an edge of hope.

“Yes. Your moms will get there. It does not mean they’ll get back together, but they’ll at least be friends.”

Amelia’s lip jutted out in a pout.

 

“In the meantime, can you keep a secret?” His voice dropped into a whisper, hoping he could segway into the entire purpose of his coming to find her.

Excitement returned to her bright blue eyes. “YES!” 

“It’s almost lunch time, and I noticed earlier that you need more clothes to keep at this house. Maybe some more toys. So, Ian and I, and Agatha, are going to sneak you out and take you shopping.”

The anomaly jumped to her feet and disappeared into her closet to find shoes. “Yes! But how are we going to get to the car?”

“Well, we have to be very careful.” 

“I know!” Amelia grabbed Sir Cuddles and her blanket and snapped.

 

Ralph blinked, finding himself in the backseat of a large SUV next to Amelia in her carseat, Ian in the passenger seat, and Agatha behind the wheel.

“Will you warn me next time?” He asked.

Agatha cackled. “Oh, c’mon, Ralph, it’s not like that’s never happened before.”

“Not this young!”

“She’s exceptional.”



Lunch was over, and they had already hit several stores. Amelia was playing in the kids’ area at the mall while Ian checked out a bookstore nearby, and Ralph and Agatha watched her.

“Will you stop making eye contact with men checking you out while aggressively eating that corn dog?” Ralph asked, holding back a laugh.

“But it’s fun.”

He sighed, watching Amelia play tag with a few other kids.

 

“Wanda told me you plan to continue dating Darcy.”

“Of course I do. I love Darcy, she’s amazing, she’s great with Amelia.” Finishing her corn dogs and growing bored of scaring men, she stood and threw away the trash.

Ralph watched her in his peripheral vision, keeping his sights on the small brunette climbing a miniature rock wall. “Right, but what happened to ‘if it isn’t her, it isn’t here’?”

“Time changes things, Ralph. Not everyone can withstand everything like you and Malcolm. I keep thinking things would have been so much easier if Ellie and I had worked out.” Propping her chin on her fist, she tracked Amelia with her eyes. 

“You would have murdered each other.”

“The sex was good though.”

“Disgusting.”

 

Opening her mouth to say something else, she caught a flash of black robes in the corner of her eye, her internal alarm bells going off. 

“Ralph, take Amelia, find Ian, and go to the next store. I have to check on something.” Her voice carried an edge, like a freshly sharpened sword. “If something seems terribly wrong, call Rio.”

“On it.” He didn’t question her, instead calling Amelia over and following Agatha’s instructions.

 

In a world where the population was dwindling, it certainly didn’t feel that way to Agatha as she pushed through the crowds after the two figures she had seen. Finally giving up, she apparated herself to appear right in front of them.

“I just love your costumes! But isn’t it too early to dress up for Halloween? Or is this some type of performance art?”

The reapers screeched at her before trying to flee.

“Sorry, boys, already tied you down to the mortal realm. Let’s go somewhere more secluded, shall we?”

 

The closed and boarded-up Abercrombie formed around them, the reapers now bound.

Agatha quickly used her magick to sear runes into the space around them, as well as a pentagram similar to the one she used on Rio. This one was strong enough to hold two low-level reapers.

Pacing around them, she tapped her chin and hummed as though she were deep in thought.
