“Now, boys, imagine my shock when on this lovely Saturday afternoon, I’m out with my ex-husband, his husband, and the daughter I share with Death herself, when I see you two watching said daughter.” Pausing, she turned on the heel of her stiletto, a little overkill with jeans, but she liked what she liked. “I think to myself, ‘bitch, why are two of Rio’s people here?’ and then I realize, they aren’t working for Rio.” She curled her lips and bared her teeth.

“Who are you working for?” She hissed.

The noises that made up the language of the reapers filled the room. 

“Oh, you’ll never tell?” The smile that adorned her lips was sinister. “I’ll find it myself, and for that, I only need one of you, but just in case I burn through one mind too quickly.” 

 

Violet magick surrounded one reaper, rendering him immobile, ensuring he would be useful, just in case. With the other, she extended her hand, magick working its way through every orifice in the skull of the body before her. The scream-like sound pierced through the air in agony. 

No one outside the store could hear what was happening.

“I do find your agony delightful.” Agatha mused as the memories and information began to flash before her eyes.

 

Eternity walking out of Asgard and returning to her realm, recruiting the reapers who didn’t agree with Rio’s new Sacred Balance. Learning about Amelia and wanting to use her as leverage. Sending these reapers to kidnap her.

 

Agatha was furious. She knew this was her fault. If only she could turn back time in a way that would both fix and preserve some things. There was nothing else of use in his mind, Eternity’s doing, she was sure. Using her rage, she increased the strength of her magick, killing the reaper. 

Thinking quickly, she summoned her dagger and reaped the soul, saving it for later. She did not need Rio to know just yet. Turning to the other, she severed the soul before tightening the magickal bounds, effectively crushing him to death.

Looking down at the bodies, she wrote a message on their robes and portaled them back to Eternity’s realm. “Family drama is the worst,” she mumbled, texting Ralph to see where he was.

 

“Why are you in Build-A-Bear?” Her eyes fell to the bears they were carrying. “Ralph Boehner and Ian Malcolm. How many stuffed animals did you talk this extremely well-behaved child into?”

Amelia giggled. “I said we were only getting one, Mommy, promise!”

Ralph groaned. “Right. Well, the starry one matches her bedroom, and then we had to get the Wicked ones, the Dia De Los Muertos cat, naturally.”

“Naturally.” The witch agreed, winking at her daughter.

Ian continued. “A dragon, two different mothmen, anything Star Wars, oh, and we ordered things online.”

“Well, Plumita, I think you had a very successful shopping trip. Ready to go back home?”

“Can we get ice cream first? Please?” She looked between the three adults, all loaded down with multiple shopping bags.

“Of course, princesa. Lead the way,” Ian walked ahead with Amelia happily chattering by his side.

 

“How bad is it?” Ralph whispered in Agatha’s ear.

“Rio’s gonna be pissed, and I feel like committing more murder.”

🦕🦖🦕

Rio truly enjoyed the afternoon, reconnecting with old friends and learning more about the company Zora headed. Several things stuck with her.

How Nicky switched to a business focus, not solely because he wanted to, but because he felt like he needed to keep an eye on Agatha. Working as her assistant allowed her to do it. While he once blamed Rio, he could now see both sides. She made bad decisions, too, Rio. Look what she did to you.

Darcy had avoided her throughout the day, yet again sticking by Ellie’s side. She hadn’t meant to accuse Darcy, but if Agatha was going to have her heart broken, it was going to affect Amelia. Rio had to prepare for that.

Lilia was making dinner with the help of a few others when the front door opened, and Amelia ran in, excitedly announcing their return. She waved at her Mama, then ran to find Billy and Tommy. 

Nicky hurried into the kitchen, voice low. “Mom’s home, something happened, she’s agitated.”

 

Rio frowned, looking first to Lilia, then to Wanda. Both of whom were just as clueless as she was. 

“Feathers!” Agatha’s voice arrived in the kitchen before she did. “With me, office. Now.”

Following her ex into the office, she sat in a chair and watched as the witch sealed the room. She listened while she recounted the events, and she was just as enraged as Agatha.

 

“Eternity wants to kidnap Amelia? For leverage?” She slumped back in the chair. “This is all my fault.”

Agatha rushed to the chair and knelt beside it—a hand on Rio’s cheek. “Stop saying that my…Rio. Stop saying that, Rio.” Dropping her hand, as if realizing what had happened, she stood and stepped back. “No, this is my fault. We wouldn’t have miscalculated and sent you to this absolute had I not stolen your magick in the first place.”

 

Rio had a lot to process with what had just happened, or almost happened. “I was already becoming human on my own, Agatha. There were so many other things at play.”

“I was still a major factor, Rio. Let’s not pretend I wasn’t. But right now, we have to find a way to fix this. She’s going to try again.”

“Right. So how do we stop someone we can’t kill?”

“You’re Death, shouldn’t you have that figured out?” 

“Surprisingly, I don’t.” She stared at Agatha for a long time, the silence stretching. “Thank you for protecting her, taking care of it.”

“She’s our daughter, Rio. I’d do anything.” Shifting her weight from one foot to the other, she pushed her hair behind her ear. “I saw Amelia’s dreams last night. If you knew you could break out of even Wanda’s bindings, why the big show?”

Rio’s eyes dropped, tracing the patterns in the hardwood. “That’s something I need to talk to both of you about.”

 

“I can accept that. Until then, why is Eternity trying to restore the balance to what it was before?”

“With the Balance being the way it is, survival and life expectancy rates decreasing, it must be affecting her somehow.” 

Agatha groaned and threw her hands in the air. “Oh, so we broke the whole damn planet.” 

“Seems that way.” 

Hands placed firmly on her hips, the witch watched out the window as their family began to eat dinner. She knew they would be scattered throughout the kitchen, dining room, and outdoor areas. No need to plan before they, themselves, ate, she thought. “Let’s get dinner, Feathers. Coven meeting afterwards, and we'll go from there.” 

🦕🦖🦕

Eternity glared down at the bodies, or what was left of them, of the two reapers Agatha had so kindly returned to her. Scrawled in reaper blood, glowing with Magick, was a message.

You lost these.

Stay the fuck away from my family.

“How did she manage to write so much?” Eternity asked no one inparticular, before waving her hand in dismissal. “No matter. So our first attempt failed.” She turned to address the many demons and reapers who surrounded her. “We try again once everyone returns home. Take Amelia from my sister’s house. She won’t expect it.”

She knew Rio, knew she didn’t Magickally protect her house as she should. She’d go herself this time. Surely Agatha couldn’t get through all of the protections and guards to get into the inner sanctum of her realm.   

How hard could kidnapping one four-year-old be? Cosmic entity or not?

🦕🦖🦕

Dinner and the subsequent clean-up were finished. Agatha knew it was time to call an extended meeting of the coven. The plan was formulated. Now it was time to disperse her flying monkeys, so to speak. 

“I need all witches or super-powered humans twenty and older to meet me in the enchanted part of the basement. The rest of you, vaminos.” She announced flatly, making a shooing motion with her hands.

 

Billy and Tommy groaned, each taking one of Amelia’s hands. 

“They never let us in on the fun stuff,” Billy explained.

“Will you show us all your LEGO kits?” Tommy asked excitedly, eyes gleaming.

Amelia nodded excitedly, grinning ear-to-ear. “I have giant ones in the playroom! You can help me!” She ran off, dragging them behind her. 



Once gathered in the basement, Agatha filled everyone in on what happened at the mall and what they already knew. 

“You committed murder in an ABERCROMBIE?” Wanda exclaimed, horrified.

“What? That godawful brand is responsible for more atrocities than what I did.” The witch retorted.

“Teenage self-esteem issues and questionable morals aside, how exactly are we supposed to correct your mess, Agatha?” Jen pointed a finger at her.

“Par-don? You were involved, too, Jenny Kale.” Agatha tossed her hair back.

“It doesn’t matter. We need more information.” Pietro interjected.

“Quicksilver over here is right. So we’re going to split up.”

“What are we, Mystery, Inc?” Jen asked.

“You’re Scooby.” Agatha deadpanned before returning to her explanation. “Alice, I didn’t exactly get a lot of information from the reapers before I got upset.”

“Typical,” Jen muttered under her breath.

Rio smirked.

 

Agatha rolled her eyes, ignoring them both. “I need you, Lily, and Sharon, to interrogate their souls.” Summoning the sealed vials their souls were in, she tossed them to the detective. “I need those back when you’re done.”

“Jen, Pietro, and Nicky. I need the three of you to go to Asgard. Find out anything you can on Eternity, what she might be up to, and confirm she’s out of the dungeons.” 

The first two groups left, Agatha turning to Lilia. “I need you to go see Lorna. She’ll only talk to you.”

“You’re correct in that, but I don’t know if she’ll tell me what we need.”

“It’s worth a shot.” The spirit witch shrugged. 

“I’ll do my best, but you know how she is.” 



Wanda looked up from her recliner at the only other two people left. “So what about us?”

“Well, first,” Agatha turned to Rio, “Skelly here is going to tell us what the fuck happened that night after we flitted off to Munich. Floor’s yours.” The witch stepped aside and leaned against the arm of the old couch.

Death sighed and leaned against an apothecary table. “I do owe you both an explanation.”

“I was going to apologize to you for leaving you there! Part of me has felt guilty for years, even though you said those things to Agatha.” Wanda interrupted, cheeks flushed with anger.

Agatha immediately went to her side and placed a comforting hand on her shoulder, sending subtle pulses of relaxation into her muscles.

“I know, and I’m sorry. The truth is, just as Agatha is immune to my Magick, I’m somewhat immune to both of yours. Wanda, Jen used some of yours while restoring mine, and with the bond, Agatha and I are even stronger. So it wasn’t difficult.”

“Then why wait for us to leave? Or act as though your familiar did it?” Wanda asked, confusion threaded in her voice.

Agatha’s jaw tightened, unable to look at Rio, unsure what she was feeling. “You didn’t want to stop me.” Her eyes lifted to Rio.

Wanda was unable to choose which of them to look at.

“I wanted to. Stopping you was the right thing to do. I needed to stop you, but I couldn’t. Knowing how much it meant to you, how much you had worked and sacrificed for that moment? I couldn’t be the person to stop you.”

 

The Scarlet Witch’s voice was soft, “You had to make us believe you were unable to stop us, and Aggie knew her Magick couldn’t hold you for long.”

“You played on our emotions. You knew just what to say to make Wanda bind you, whether she wanted to or not.”

Death averted her eyes.

“You manipulated us. Me. You left me, broken and bleeding.” The witch stepped toward the woman she was made for, hand leaving her daughter’s shoulder.

The entity whipped around, eyes nearly black. “And that’s your move, right? To manipulate? To use people? To leave them shattered?”

“I’ve taken accountability and apologized for what I did to you. We’re currently trying to fix everything I caused.” Her eyes were a glassy purple, power and emotion swirling together. “Do what you need to do to me, Rio,” she hissed. “But leave my children out of it. Trapping you has eaten at Wanda for years.”

 

The gasp from behind Agatha caught them both off guard. She turned to see Wanda’s face twisted in pain, arms curled around her stomach. At once, the older witch was kneeling at her side, hands on the younger witch’s bump. “It’s okay, my angel. Deep breaths. We need to get you upstairs, Jen said no stress, remember? I shouldn’t have let you stay down here this long.”

“I didn’t have these issues with the boys. I keep forgetting, I’m sorry.” She apologized in between breathing exercises.

“Don’t apologize, it’s been a stressful time, and you were twelve years younger then.” Her hands moved methodically over Wanda’s bump, the way Jen had shown her, eliciting a strong kick from Gabby. “That’s good.” Leaning forward, she kissed the place her granddaughter had kicked and smiled at Wanda. 

“Rio, go get Jen and meet us upstairs, please.” She instructed without turning around.

“She already left, maminka,” Wanda informed, voice tired.

“Good. Let’s get your wife and get you upstairs.”



Zora hadn’t meant to get Wanda pregnant that February. They had used an enchanted strap before, and without a fertility spell, it wasn’t supposed to work. They hadn’t talked about having another child, knowing how dangerous pregnancy and labor were in this new reality. Then, that one time. It worked.

Because the Scarlet Witch, for a fleeting moment, imagined making a baby, so they did.

Zora had adopted the boys shortly after they married. They considered her their second parent. She didn’t need a biological child, but the moment that second line showed up, she knew there was no turning back. Wanda had been hopeful that, because of who she was, everything would be perfect and easy. They had celebrated; Agatha had wept tears of joy; Lily and the brothers had fought for naming rights. The twins had argued about whether the baby would be a boy or a girl. 

The joy had been short-lived after the first appointment with Jen, however. While Jen primarily worked for Harkness Holdings, she also maintained a private practice for a select clientele. It was Jen who told Wanda that, while she was immortal, there was no guarantee that it extended to the baby. The risks were higher, anything could happen, and it was extremely important to remain as stress-free as possible. 

Zora, the coven, and the extended family had done everything they could. Wanda finished out the spring semester and took a leave of absence the following year. Jen had curated several baby-safe potions for Wanda’s anxiety, and while the stubborn witch didn’t necessarily like using Magick for everything, she encouraged her to start doing so. 

Portaling was preferred over transferring, and if Zora could, she would have put Wanda on bed rest for the entire pregnancy.

Now, sitting on the bed, holding Wanda’s hand, she wished she had. Jen’s hands glowed pink as she scanned the witch’s body, brows furrowed. Agatha sat near the head of the bed, trying to give Jen room, but refusing to move far. 

 

“How bad is it?” The blonde’s words were barely audible.

“Surprisingly, it’s not. Yet. It could be so much worse.” Even though she was the one who said it, Jen still appeared unconvinced.

“Tell me the truth, Jennifer. I can take it. I’m a big girl.” Wanda attempted a laugh.

“It’s not you I’m worried about.” Her eyes caught Agatha’s.

“I can be good.” 

 

Returning to Wanda, her face softened as she took a deep breath. “Gabby is beginning to turn herself, rapidly. There’s been a lot of change and stress, and your blood pressure has been raised. You need to be hospitalized, at least until we stabilize both of you.”

Zora felt her heart drop. This was what they had been worried about. “She’s only thirty weeks.”

The doctor nodded grimly. “Normally, that would be worrisome, but not dire. Now? Well, we know what the possibilities are if anything gets worse.”

Zora had always been good at wearing a mask for others, and that’s what she did now as she looked at her wife. The woman she had fallen in love with at warp speed. Pushing her tears back, she gently caressed Wanda’s face and placed her hand where their daughter was preparing for an early exit. 

 

The door clicked softly as the other two women left the room, leaving the wives alone to process the information. 

Zora leaned down and pressed a kiss to Wanda’s cheek. “Seems as though our daughter takes after you, Vela.”

“Flee when things get hard?” Wanda scoffed.

“No, Vela. Protect those she loves when they’re hurting. You’re worried about everyone and everything. Let the family handle saving the world, my little raptor. You finish creating the next generation.” She smiled when she felt movement beneath her hand. “Gabby agrees with me.”

 

The responding laugh was watery, “I love you, muscles. We’re going to make it through this.”

She lay next to the beautiful witch she married, Wanda’s head on her shoulder, thinking about their options.

“Where’d mom and Jen go?”

“Probably to argue about whether to admit you to the hospital or keep you close.” She answered, knowing how much the two older witches argued.

“I just want to be with you.”     

🦕🦖🦕

Too much information. Agatha had taken in too much information in too little time, and she still had to keep it together. She wanted to collapse into Darcy’s arms. Or at least she was pretty sure those were the arms she wanted. Those arms hadn’t cut her deeply today and weren’t part of the reason her daughter and granddaughter were in potential danger. 

Then again, really, the Sacred Balance fuckery was her doing, so Agatha could really only blame herself.

The entire situation had gone completely sideways.

Leaving Wanda’s room, she stormed down the hallway, hair flying behind her like a cape. “Everyone, living room, now. Young kids, basement, movie.” 

There were zero arguments.



She stood in the middle of the living room, seeing that everyone had returned from the errands she had sent them on. They would address that shortly. Running her hands through the tangled mess that somehow still passed as hair, she filled everyone in on Wanda’s condition. 

Noticing a few looks and glances aimed at Rio, Agatha let out a long sigh. “It’s not her fault, so don’t blame her for doing what she was made to do, okay? Want to blame someone? Blame me. Or Evanora for doing what she did in the first place.

Jen cleared her throat to speak. “We didn’t know that restoring the Magick Agatha depleted would make Rio too perfect. We haven’t found a way to counterbalance, we’re trying.”

“Everything that has happened as a direct response to that is my fault, and I shoulder the blame, including this. I don’t want to hear another word.” 

 

A quiet settled over the group for a moment before Lily spoke. “So where will Wands be going? Hospital? New York? Staying here?”

“She hasn’t said yet. She doesn’t need the stress of staying around everyone; at the same time, being at the hospital will be extremely stressful for her.” Jen explained, while Agatha sent the question straight to Wanda.

 

Angel, no stress or pressure, sweetheart. Do you want to go to the hospital?

I need my family. I think our home in New York would be best. Most everyone is close, I can get to the hospital quickly if I need to. I do want to stay here tonight.”

I’ll let everyone know.

Please tell me what you find out. Don’t keep me in the dark on this.

Yes, Angel.

 

Refocusing on her family in front of her, she noticed they were staring, waiting for the answer. 

“Impatient bunch. Wanda’s staying tonight and then will stay at their penthouse in New York.” Raising a hand before anyone got too excited, she added, “There will be rotations for visits and who goes over to care for the boys, cook, fuss over her, and so on. Relieving stress, not causing it.” Lowering her hand, she took a seat on the armrest next to Lilia. “Now, witch business. Non-witches…well, I really don’t care at this point.”

 

Ralph and Ian stood. “We have had enough excitement for today. You’ll fill me in later,” Ralph announced, gesturing for Ellie and Darcy to follow. 

Agatha waited for them to leave before asking for each group to share what they learned. 

 

Alice crossed her arms over her chest. “Not much, thanks to your anger.” She huffed. “There are a lot of reapers that do not like the new terms and conditions. They believe they should go back to how things were in the past, allowing modern medicine to do its job, small miracles, percentages, etc. Many have turned to Eternity’s side.”

“She also believes Amelia is dangerous, with being both human and a cosmic being. A type of demigod no one has ever seen.” Sharon added, as though recalling the memories of the reaper. “She wants to study Amelia.” 

“We don’t even know all of Milly’s powers yet. How could she think she’s dangerous?” Rio asked in disbelief. 

“She’s your sister. You tell me.” Agatha deadpanned. “Jen, what did your group learn?”

 

“The runes failed because time has become absolute; ‘lifelong’ sentences no longer mean anything. With death being so final, the universe now has an end date.” She recited. “It’s what I learned from Chronos. When death wasn’t as final, that’s what allowed timelines to branch, to extend.”

“Eternity is over time, no wonder she’s pissed,” Agatha concluded. “So now, we have to decide how to fix it. Rio, if you just gave the decree to…not be so harsh, would that fix it?”

 

“No,” Lilia interjected, finally saying something. She looked around at the rest of the coven. “Everyone out, this is between these two destroyers of worlds and me only.”

“That means she talked to mom,” Alice told everyone as they stood to move elsewhere throughout the house.

“Better yet, basement,” Lilia added, and snapped.



Lilia Calderu had a lot to say to the two dumbass women before her, but did not have the time at the moment.

For now, she could only relay what Lorna had told her. It was late, and they needed sleep.

“I’ll be quick, and as much as I want to lay into both of you, I’m tired. According to Lorna, you will both have to do something you do not want to do and make a compromise to restore the balance. She said it more eloquently, but fuck it.”

“Did she specify?” Agatha asked, agitated. “I don’t want to do laundry, but I somehow doubt washing a load of underwear without Magick is going to save all of humanity.”

“Agatha’s right. That doesn’t narrow it down.” 

The divination witch sighed. “It’s obviously going to be related.”

“Still doesn’t narrow it down much, Lils.” Agatha flicked her wrist, summoning the Darkhold, her fingers becoming unglamored. 

 

“Anything on how to restore the Sacred Balance?” The keeper of the text asked. 

The pages of the ancient text began to turn rapidly, Lilia and Rio joining Agatha to see what would be revealed.

Agatha let out a frustrated scream when the earlier page was revealed. Lilia cackled. Rio raised an eyebrow.

“Kids?”

“The ones I already have. It’s not happening.” She went to turn the pages of the book, only for it to close on her again and disappear, as it had done hours prior. “Oh, you bitch. Really? You came out for THAT?!” She yelled at the empty space.

 

“No. No!” Her foot came down hard onto the cobblestone floor. “Your cosmic cunt of a sister is trying to kidnap our daughter,” her head turned to Lilia, “your best friend is being vague, which she does best. I don’t care that she’s a fucking Fate.” She walked away from them, pivoting to face them. “My daughter and unborn granddaughter are both in danger from something that WAS preventable, we’re no closer to figuring out how to fix what we broke, and my girlfriend refuses to share a bed with me due to morals, when I really need that right now!”

She vanished in a purple haze.



Rio frowned. “She’s not the only one hurting.”

Lilia put a hand on her shoulder. “I know, and you’ll go back home, and what do you think will happen?”

“Alice will make me talk about it.”

“Right.”

“Agatha has a house full of people, Lilia.”

“And what do you think she’ll actually do?” Lilia headed towards the stairs.

“Feel guilty for causing stress, and bottle it up,” Rio concluded, following the other woman up the stairs.

“Exactly.”

🦕🦖🦕

Unfortunately, Ralph and Ian were already asleep, or else she would’ve gone straight to her ex-husband. Instead, she went to Darcy, who sat next to Ellie on the back porch.

Before she could overthink it, she reached for Darcy to pull her to her feet.

Any normal human wouldn’t have caught the way Darcy quickly glanced at Agatha’s fingers and flinched. Unfortunately for Darcy, Agatha was an immortal being. “I don’t have the energy to reglamor.” She explained quickly.

“Aggie, I’m sorry, I didn’t mean.” Darcy stopped herself and smiled.”You’ve had a long day, it’s fine.” She stood and reached for her girlfriend's hand.

Aggie pulled away. “No, it’s fine. Goodnight to both of you.” Once again, purple smoke filled the air, and she vanished.



Ellie let out a low whistle. “She needs comforting, Darcy. Rookie move.”

The younger woman drew her knees to her chest. “It’s not about the way they look.” For a moment, the only sounds were their breathing, mixed with the gentle breeze of the late summer night. 

Darcy leaned her cheek against her knees, looking directly at her longtime friend. “Did, well, does it ever scare you? The Magick? The unknown?” 

The older scientist smirked, “Darcy, I helped recreate highly dangerous animals. I think the term dangerous is subjective.”

“Misunderstood prehistoric creatures aside, you know what I mean.” 

 

“Follow me, there’s something you need to understand about your girlfriend.” She stood, walking into the darkened house, taking note that they were the only ones awake at the late hour. 

“Agatha has been on a quest for power her entire life, long before I knew her. You know the story, you know everything that happened. Lilia has known her since birth, saw everything Evanora did.” She stopped at a keypad-protected door. “Here it is.”

 

Darcy watched in shock as Ellie keyed in a code and traced a rune into the wood. 

“Wait? You know runes?”

The older woman shrugged. “It’s an old Norse rune.” She gestured for Darcy to enter the room, following behind. “This is the family library, if you will. You haven’t seen it because Agatha hasn’t revealed it to you.”

“I knew this house had secrets.”

Ellie hummed, directing Darcy to sit at a table while she looked for something specific.

 

“Agatha’s whole aesthetic is to appear cold and heartless. She can fool the world, but not us. When I first met her, she was powerful, but not the way she is now.” Taking a few photo albums and journals from the shelves, she placed them on the table in front of Darcy.

“She taught herself not to care about people, and certainly not to love them. Attachment was a weakness she couldn’t afford. She and Jen met when they were young, both witches by birth and lineage, yet not just blood witches.” Plucking an aged scrapbook from the set, she carefully placed it in front of Darcy. “Careful, this thing is older than you.”

 

“I never took Agatha as the scrapbooking type.” Darcy’s jaw was agape as she flipped through the worn photos.

“She was sixteen in the late eighties and early nineties. Of course she was.” 

“This is when she met Ralph? She seems so normal.” Darcy breathed, not stopping on any of them for long.

“Yes, well. She was a teenager who had been cast aside by her mother, Darcy. One who was self-taught in Magick, when she didn’t have the kind you see today. She was my world when I met her. She had expectations to live up to, a legacy to fulfill.

“More importantly, she wanted to create a legacy and family of her own. You asked me if it scares me, and it does. I’ve seen her close to death, Darcy. But at the same time, I’ve seen her grow this family, break free from her personal suffering, and become who she is. I also helped her do it. She will never admit it, but I’m part of the reason she’s in this mess. I’m the reason she stole Rio’s research, when I didn’t even really need it. I needed confirmation, not the actual formulas. I’m the one who drove Rio away the first two times because of my jealousy.” Ellie shook her head with a sad smile.

“The Magick doesn’t scare me, Darcy. Seeing her hurt does. The ink-stained fingers, the skull-scar, the scars from expeditions we’ve been on, or spells gone wrong, the tattoos she has, those are all reminders of what she’s gone through and what makes Agatha the woman and witch she is. Like all of those pictures and letters. Loving Agatha Harkness means loving every single part of her, and not everyone is up to that challenge. You have to decide if you are.”

 

Darcy looked at the pictures, letters, and books full of script in front of her. Pieces of her girlfriend’s life captured in time. A deeper insight than she’d ever had. “Everything’s just been moving so quickly, I feel like something is going to happen at any moment, and I know she’s supposedly invincible, but what if she’s not?” A tear escaped down her cheek.

Ellie smiled softly, arm outstretched to put her hand over Darcy’s. “You need to tell her that, not me.”

Darcy stood, wiping the tears away. “Do you want help putting those away?” 

“No, just go talk to Ags. She needs you.” 



Ellie watched the younger woman leave the room, then stacked the books and looked around the large room. “Victoria!” She whispered, waiting for the bearded dragon to appear. “I have an errand for you.”

Rio’s familiar came into view on top of the albums and journals, appearing groggy from sleep, staring at Ellie suspiciously.

“I need you to take these to Rio. I have a feeling she’ll find them more useful than Darcy did.” She retrieved a pen and a piece of paper from a desk in the corner and wrote a quick note. “Stop judging me, you know it’s for the greater good.”

Victoria side-eyed Ellie for at least five seconds before vanishing with the stack of memories.

 

 

Agatha had transferred to a room where three young children slept, a movie playing softly in the background. Carefully removing Amelia, she gathered the small child in her arms. Burying her nose in the head of unruly curls, she carried her youngest up the stairs to her bedroom. The events of the day replayed through her mind, and she tried desperately to push them away, lest her dream-hopping daughter see them.

“See Wanda?” The mostly asleep entity in her arms asked.

“She might be asleep, but I think you might be a welcome sight. I know the boys saw her earlier.” 

 

They stopped in front of Wanda’s room, the door slightly ajar, and soft light filtering out. “Looks like Ralph is checking on her.” Agatha assessed, pushing the door open and stepping in. 

Zora sat next to Wanda, clutching her hand, whispering. Wanda’s eyes flew to Agatha the moment she stepped in, followed by Ralph and Jen’s.

“Jen? I thought you were at Rio’s?”

Jen glanced at Amelia and seemed to measure her words carefully. “I was, Ralph just called me, and I apparated. We were about to call you.”

“Princesa, do you think you could wait outside?” Ralph asked the young entity.

 

Amelia wiggled out of her mother’s grip and looked around at everyone, suddenly looking much older than four. She rolled her shoulders and walked determinedly towards the bed, climbing up next to her older sister. Carefully laying her hands over her abdomen, swirls of purple and green Magick wrapped around Wanda before settling into her body. The cosmic child stared at her work for a moment, as if daring it to do something, before letting out a contented sigh and curling up next to the Scarlet Witch. She fell asleep immediately, Wanda’s hand smoothing over her hair.

 

“What the fuck was that, Agatha?” Jen’s voice cut through the silence.

Wanda smiled, “She fixed it.”

“What the fuck do you mean, she fixed it?” Agatha sputtered.

“Gabby’s back to where she needs to be,” Wanda explained, as if it were the most normal thing in the world.

Ralph shook his head with a grin. “You always did make exceptional kids.”

“Raised a couple, too,” Agatha added, still trying to process exactly what was happening.

 

Zora hadn’t stopped staring at the four-year-old now, curled up next to her wife like a cat. “She’s the balance.”

"Excuse you, what?” Agatha turned.

“She undid the perfection, had mercy. Returned the balance to what it was. She’s the Sacred Balance. Or at least the answer to it.” 

 

Jen chuckled. “So does that make you Joseph? Or do you prefer Josefina?”

“Technically, I think that makes me God.”

The doctor rolled her eyes. “I am not calling you God.”

“I distinctly remember a time in the nineties…”

“Still minimizing stress!” Wanda cried.

Ralph chuckled. “Nightmare fuel aside, does Eternity know about this, or does she have a theory?” 

“I don’t think Rio knows about this.” The spirit witch rolled her shoulders. “Let’s table this for tonight. Angel, do you want me to take Plumita?” 

Wanda shook her head, a protective hand still on her sister. “No, that was exhausting for her. Leave her be.” She looked up at her mother. “You’re exhausted, go get some sleep.”



Finally collapsing into her giant, albeit empty, bed, Agatha allowed the emotions of the day to wash over her. It was technically Sunday, and everyone would be leaving in a few hours, leaving just her, Rio, and Amelia to figure out this new normal. Lilia and Sharon would be staying in Boston for the foreseeable future, which she was grateful for. Darcy would visit, of course.

Darcy. 

Agatha stared at her hands in the dark. Still unglamored. Still inky, as they would be forever. The way Darcy had flinched earlier played on a constant loop, allowing Agatha’s doubts about their relationship to crawl into the cracks of her heart.

 

The side of the bed dipped slightly. Agatha recognized the scent of Darcy’s shampoo immediately. “I didn’t even hear you come in.”

The young brunette knelt next to her. “It’s not what you think, Aggie.”

“Then what is it?” Her voice was flat.

 

Darcy laced their fingers together. “They remind me that you aren’t as safe as you think you are, Ags. It scares me. I know you’re technically immortal, but look at Wanda. Nothing’s guaranteed. I love you, Agatha. I worry about you anytime witchy things come up, or you travel, or you do anything slightly dangerous. 

“Every part of you is beautiful. The ink on your fingers just reminds me that you control an ancient spellbook that could lead to danger or the end of the world, or who knows what else.”

She held Agatha’s gaze, lifted fingers to her lips, and kissed the blackened digits. 

 

Agatha pulled the young woman to her, eliciting a yelp. “So, tell me again, who needs to talk instead of shutting down?”

Darcy hid her face in the side of her neck. “That’s beside the point right now. I am sorry for not telling you. I didn’t want to add to any of your stress, and before you ask, I am going back to the other room. I just needed to get that off my chest.” She pulled back, cupped Agatha’s face, and kissed her deeply.  

 

Agatha pulled back and dramatically fell against the pillows. “I am being forced to live as a nun.”

Rolling her eyes, Darcy moved off the bed. “Oh, please, as if you were such a womanizer before.” 

 

Lying there, absentmindedly patting the fluffy bunny that joined her, she thought on Darcy’s words and how right they were. She had her fun before meeting Rio, purely for succubus reasons, and then, nothing. After the island, there were a few dalliances here and there, but nothing substantial until Darcy. 

Between that damn contract and Death, she had been ruined, but was that such a bad thing? 

Groaning into her hands, she huffed, pulled her familiar close, and tried to get a bit of sleep.          

🦕🦖🦕

Rio practically collapsed on her sofa beside her friend. “This weekend has been overwhelming.”

“What are you going to do when everyone leaves?” Alice asked, trying to sound nonchalant.

“What do you mean?”

“Well, Darcy will be back in New York, or on the island. Everyone will be back to the grind, it will just be the two of you and Amelia.” Alice pointed out.

“Agatha wants to work out coparenting. We’ll enroll her in pre-k and set up a routine, I guess. Sync our schedules. Lilia and Sharon want to help out as much as possible, plus Agatha is on edge with Wanda and the baby.” She shrugged.

“And?”

“And what, Alice? The Darkhold spelled it out for her, and she denied it. We need to focus on fixing the universe, maybe give Agatha a few millennia.” She tucked her legs beneath her and clutched one of Amelia’s stuffed toys.

 

“Milly seems to have adjusted well.” The other woman said it cautiously, not wanting to upset Death.

“She’s been looking forward to this almost since birth, Alice. Cosmic beings, well, we’re different.” She waved a hand. “Even the half-human ones apparently.”

 

They turned when Jen appeared in front of them, having returned from Agatha’s.

“Rio, Amelia’s powers.” She panted, exhausted.

“What about them?”

“She reversed Gabby’s progression. Wanda had dilated to 5 centimeters, Rio. She was going into active labor. No damage, no harm, just, poof. Fixed it. She can fix the balance.” The words tumbled out of the doctor’s mouth, still in shock from what had happened.

 

Rio stood, beginning to pace. “Did you tell Agatha?”

“Not the details, no. Zora announced that Amelia was kind’ve the answer. But that was all. Agatha just wants to protect Milly from Eternity. Ags isn’t the threat.”

Alice watched the two women in front of her, understanding Rio’s worry, but agreeing with her wife.

Rio nodded. “Well, there’s nothing we can do tonight, I guess. I’ve seen how she is with the kids, how she was with Wanda, how she protected Amelia.”

 

“Your sister is the true threat.” Jen reiterated. “Agatha is sorta claiming to be God, though.”

Rio scoffed. “That’s my Aggie.” She paused, eyes darting between her friends. “I mean, our Aggie.”

Alice and Jen exchanged a look. “Right,” Alice said skeptically. 



Sunday morning came entirely too soon for Agatha. It meant her family returning to their homes, Darcy leaving, and facing a completely new normal with Rio.

Rio.

She still wasn’t entirely sure how she felt toward the woman or where they stood. Without their various family members, how would they fare? They had agreed to put Amelia first, and then there was that reveal about Rio’s manipulation tactics. Maybe they were a perfect match. 

 

Getting dressed, she marched into Ellie’s room to find her packing her bag. “Rio manipulated Wanda and me on the island.”

“Good morning to you, too, Aggie. Isn’t the entire basis of your relationship essentially manipulation?” The blonde raised an eyebrow in question as she zipped her bag. 

“No! Well, yes. That’s besides the point.” She fell onto the bed, sprawled out on her back, and replayed the conversation from the night before. 

 

“So, she gave you exactly what you wanted, but made you believe she didn’t, then broke the bond because she thought you wanted her weak…”

Agatha sat up and blinked at Ellie. “Maybe?”

“This all could’ve been avoided if you two had just talked in the first place.”

 

The witch’s jaw dropped as she stared at her first ex-girlfriend. “Excuse you? You’re the one who had me steal her research! The research you didn’t technically need! And yes, I know!”

“I love our little cons, what can I say? That temple raid that one time was a lot of fun.” She reminisced.

“It was, but that’s not important right now.” She groaned, leaning her head onto Ellie’s shoulder. “Completely random question.”

 

“I have a feeling it’s not, but shoot.”

“Exactly how close were you and Darcy when she was a student?”

“Agatha, she was a student, that’s it.”

The professor leaned back and gave the blonde a skeptical look. “Oh, please. Ellie, neither of us has exactly followed the non-fraternization policies.”

“Darcy and I are friends, you know that. She would never cheat on you. We flirted, that was all.”

“I trust you both. Things are just all over the place, and my feelings are weird. I don’t like this.”

“I know Ags. For what it’s worth, you and Rio make beautiful babies.”

“Baby. One. Singular. No more.” Agatha pushed off from the bed. “Never happening again.”



Agatha made sure not to hug Wanda too tightly, but she was worried for her daughter. Yes, Amelia had saved the day, but no one knew whether that would hold for the next ten weeks. Her oldest daughter had her siblings, the twins, Jen, and Alice, all in New York with her. Darcy would also be there occasionally. 

Amelia hugged her older siblings as they left, looking genuinely sad to see them go. “You’ll come visit soon, right?” Her small lip jutting out.

“Of course, Green Goblin.” Pietro ruffled her hair, adopting Lily’s nickname for her. “If I didn’t have work to do, we’d stay longer.”

“Plus, you have to come see us,” Billy grinned. 

“You’ll love New York!” Tommy added.

“I will visit all the time. You’re still teaching me Spanish!” Lily wrapped her baby sister in a hug and kissed her on the cheek, only to see her tiny twin cross her arms and try to look at her sternly.

“You’re fluent in Spanish, Nicky told me.”

Lilith swatted her actual twin on the arm. “You’re the worst.”

“I’m a terrible liar!”

 

Agatha watched her children interact, smiling as she kept Wanda close and turned to Zora. “Protect her and call me if anything changes, do you hear me?”

“I promise, Agatha,” Zora said solemnly before hugging her and taking Wanda’s hand. 

“I’ll call as soon as we get home, Mom. I love you.” Wanda hugged Agatha once more.

 

Agatha sighed, watching everyone load into the two SUVs, Darcy the last one to bid farewell. The younger woman pulling her inside to do so.

Darcy pulled her close, kissing her girlfriend goodbye. “I’ll see you next weekend?”

“Let me see how the week goes? It should be okay, but Rio and I have a few details to hammer out.” Agatha absolutely wanted Darcy to be a part of everything, but she really did need to consider Amelia's and Rio’s feelings first.

“Of course, Aggie. I love you, and I trust you’ll do what’s best.”

“I love you, too. At least one of us does.”    



Watching the New York group leave, Ralph wrapped an arm around her. 

“We’re heading out as well. Don’t do anything stupid.” 

“Like what?” She scoffed.

He didn’t answer, scooping Amelia up in his arms instead. “Like denying this one anything she wants. Right, Princesa?”

Milly nodded solemnly. “Right, Dad.” 

Ralph sniffed. “Darn late summer allergies.”



With everyone gone, Lilia and Sharon returning to their home as well, Amelia looked between her mothers. 

“Are we staying for the afternoon, Mama?” She asked, climbing onto the couch, already making her decision. 

Rio’s eyes flew to Agatha. “Well, we have to get ready for tomorrow, cariño, and I’m sure Agatha does, too.”

Looking back at Rio, considering the options, Agatha caved. “I think that’s a great idea for the three of us to spend time together, Plumita.”

Rio bit the inside of her cheek, looking back at the witch. “Are you sure?”

 

Agatha nodded, following their daughter up the stairs. “We have to start finding a new normal, Feathers. Don’t you think?” 

Rio took a deep breath and followed as they were led to the galaxy-themed bedroom.

 

“Mama!” Amelia held out a few old books to her. “These used to be Lily and Nicky’s!”

Rio took the worn books, recognizing them immediately, and glanced at Agatha. “These are my childrens’ books.”

“I bought the kids plenty of books; I guess those got in there somehow.” She waved a dismissive hand.

“I signed these, Agatha.” Recognition crossed her face. “Wait a minute! These are from London! I dated them! You didn’t ask me to sign them. Some random kid did! I remember that!”

Agatha scoffed. “You can remember that, but not your sense of humanity,” she mumbled.

“Nicky said they were big fans of yours as kids. They still have the stuffed animals with feathers.” Amelia added.

“Oh, do they now?” Rio quirked a brow in Agatha’s direction.

“Green Goblin, indeed.”

 

“I thought you hated my work? I remember you calling me a fraud on television. Multiple times, actually.” Rio reminisced, amusement in her eyes.

“Oh, absolutely. Kids loved you, though. I blame Ellie and Ian. Terrible influences.” Agatha eyed her miniature look-alike, who was conveniently sitting quietly and focusing on her books. She was unsurprised that the young child could already read.

 

  

Rio tried to hide her smile, knowing that some part of Agatha had respected her work. She watched as the witch sat on the floor next to their daughter, talking with her about her interests, taking in everything the young girl said. While they had spent every day together since Monday, it was finally just the three of them. No Darcy somewhere in the house, or Lilia supervising. 

Rio settled on the bed, easily joining in the conversation, knowing she and Agatha still had several things to discuss. They could do that in their offices, away from Milly. For now, their focus was bonding.

The youngest Harkness took full advantage of being alone with her mothers for the afternoon. They played games, did a puzzle, and read books. Death was in awe of how easily Agatha bent to the will of a four-year-old.

 

It was a side of the stubborn scientist Rio hadn’t seen many times before. Truly happy, smiling, free. She had just started going through the pictures and journals Ellie had sent to her by way of Victoria, and while there were glimpses—there was nothing like this.

 

Aggie’s outright indignation when she was accused of using her Magick to cheat at CandyLand, and then her peals of laughter when she was caught. Rio threw her a look when she started teaching Amelia how to play BlackJack and count the cards.

“Agatha! She’s four!” Rio had pointed out in shock.

“So? It’s a good life skill and good for reasoning and problem-solving.” The younger witch shrugged. 

“Can we save the gambling for double digits at least? Stick to things like Go Fish?” Rio reasoned.

The morally grey witch sighed dramatically and looked at their young daughter. “Fine. Ralph was the same way. It is getting late.”

 

Amelia’s lower lip immediately jutted out into a pout. “I don’t want to go yet. Please, Mama!”

Rio sighed, turning to Agatha and gave her a look. “Maybe dinner and then we can head home?”

“I can handle that, but then, Plumita, we all have to get ready for tomorrow, okay?”

Their daughter grinned, agreeing.



With Amelia watching a movie, Agatha and Rio moved around each other easily, making dinner for three. Agatha made sure to spell the room so little ears couldn’t overhear them.

“So, you allowed me to do what I had to do in Munich?” She asked cautiously.

“Yes.” Death’s reply was short.

“Then why did you cut off the bond?”

“I told you. I thought you wanted me weak. I also couldn’t let you think I was okay with you going against the balance all the time.”

 

Agatha plated the pasta they had made and changed the subject. “I don’t want our past to affect how we parent or treat each other in front of Amelia.” 

“Me neither, I want to do this right, Agatha.” Rio carried the plates to the other room.

“None of this every other weekend bullshit either.” She set drinks down and managed to keep her voice neutral. She had missed out on so much already; she needed to regain equal footing.

“Okay, Agatha.” Rio’s throat bobbed. 

 

Agatha knew Rio was trying to keep her emotions in check, and that this was something she had wanted, just by what she had seen in Amelia’s dreams. She stepped closer and took her hand. “We made a good team in the past, Feathers. I know we can do it again, and she’s more important.” Her voice was barely above a whisper.

Rio’s eyes fluttered closed when Agatha noticed the heat radiating from Death’s body, her eyes glancing to the lips she once swore were the only ones she would ever kiss.

What the fuck was she doing? She stepped back and cleared her throat. “I’ll get Milly.” The witch’s voice was thick with the specific desire she had buried long ago, as she hurried from the room.

🦕🦖🦕

Eternity grinned as she looked down at the youngest entity, safely asleep in her bed. She had waited until everyone had left, and Rio had finally taken the small child back to her place. The entity knew that Death didn’t protect her home nearly as well as she should have. Agatha was going to be pissed.

She picked up the sleeping entity easily and transported them back to her realm, grinning the entire time. 

🦕🦖🦕

Rio sat on her bed, looking through the scrapbooks and journals Ellie had sent her. They held a unique insight into Agatha that she appreciated, and she would have to thank Ellie for it. Some of the journals spanned their time at Oxford and discussed their relationship. Rio hadn’t gotten to those yet.

“I don’t know what it was about.” She said into the phone, speaking with Alice about the earlier moment with Agatha. “I felt the tug on the bond, yes, but it was entirely natural.”

“But she stopped herself?” 

“Of course she did, Alice. She’s probably going to marry Darcy.” Rio reminded.

A sudden chill came over her as her senses heightened, and Victoria ran into the room, frantic.

“Hey, Alice, I’ll call you back.” Not waiting for a response, Death ended the call, put the books away with a wave of her hand, and transferred directly into Amelia’s room.

Where she found Sir Cuddles and her blanket instead of Amelia. 

“Eternity.” She hissed, immediately dialing Agatha.



Agatha continued replaying the moment in her head as she talked to Ralph. “I don’t know what happened. I feel so guilty.”

“You didn’t do anything, Aggie. You stopped yourself, and that’s the important part. Moment of weakness is all.” He explained, attempting to be supportive.

“I don’t even want back with her, Ralph. I want to be with Darcy. Maybe I should propose.”

“Slow down, you are not that type of lesbian.” He warned. “Give it time, see what happens.”

 

A beep interrupted her, and she held the phone away from her ear to see who was calling. “Hey, Rio’s calling, I need to take it.”

She immediately switched over.

 

“Rio?”

“Amelia, Eternity took her.” 

 

Agatha hung up the phone and stared at the idiot she had procreated with, having immediately teleported.

“WHAT?! I specifically told you that your sister was actively trying to kidnap our daughter roughly twenty-four hours ago, and you DIDN’T protect this place like Fort Knox?!” 

 

Rio crossed her arms over her chest. “Lily was kidnapped on your watch!” She shot back.

“She was in Ian’s care, NOT mine. My record is still spotless, thank you!” She ran a hand through her hair and rolled her shoulders.

“Well, c’mon, or are you going to make me get her back myself?” The witch immediately apparated back to her house, Rio following.

🦕🦖🦕

Eternity looked down at the small entity in the room the reapers had created for her. She didn’t look threatening, but she knew that looks could be deceiving. 

She sat in the rocking chair next to the bed and considered her next steps and how, exactly, she would get Rio to comply. 

Turning her head when Amelia stirred, she smiled as Amelia awoke. “Well, hello, dear niece.”

 

Eternity’s blood immediately ran cold when Amelia’s eyes swirled purple and green, and her voice was laced with anger. “Hello, Auntie Eternity. You have made a very bad decision.”

Chapter End Notes

Oh shit. What's going to happen now?

Man. The changes. The threats. Agatha's going to murder an entity if Amelia doesn't do it first.

Aww, baby's first murder.

Chapter 7: If You Forgive Me All This (If I Forgive You All That)

Chapter Summary

A kidnapping, a compromise, a time jump, a new baby, giving in.

Chapter Notes

Hannah and I blasted Celine Dion, and this was the title you got. You’re fucking welcome.

Thank you, my dear Hannah, for everything, reading this, keeping me focused, etc.

Meanwhile, we needed a time jump in this chapter to move things along a bit. The process is finally moving along, and we're getting somewhere!

DeathByLesbianWitches on tumblr, let me know what you think!

Warnings: Smut, light violence, feelings, and childbirth.

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Amelia Jade Harkness-Vidal had thoroughly enjoyed her weekend. She finally met the family she had been dreaming of, and she loved them dearly. She had stayed at her Mom’s house, played with her nephews, spent time with her Dads, met her aunts, Mimi Sharon, and interacted with all four siblings and her sister-in-law. 

Now, she just had to get her moms back together, and she had a feeling she would have help, maybe even her Aunt Ellie’s. 

She had been scheming on Sunday night when her tiny body had succumbed to a deep slumber. Lying safe and sound in her wrought-iron bed at her Mama’s house. Sir Cuddles snuggled tightly against her, wrapped in the special blanket from her Mimi. 

Her dreams had been pleasant, befitting of a normal, mortal, four-year-old. Kittens, unicorns, rainbows, the normal preschooler fare. When she felt a shift. A disturbance in her surroundings. 

No longer in the comfort of her dinosaur-themed room, in her home. No longer snuggled in her blanket, spelled to smell like lavender and rosemary. Sir Cuddles no longer tucked into her arms. 

Amelia was in another realm, one belonging to an entity. She felt the power, the danger, saw the memory of Eternity play back from Death’s own mind. Her power swirled in her eyes as her cosmic abilities activated, though something pulled against them. When her eyes flew open and landed on the offending brunette, technically her aunt, she was no longer a half-mortal four-year-old.

She was a cosmic being with the temper of a Harkness. Her voice sounded detached, the voice of a much older being, not that of a pint-sized entity.

“Hello, Auntie Eternity. You have made a very bad decision.” Eyes swirling with purple and green Magick, her hands darted out, aiming straight at the woman in front of her.

Nothing happened.

She tried again, but again, nothing. That’s when she felt the full force of it. The block. Like her Magick was pushing against something that it couldn’t move.

 

Eternity cackled. “Oh, my dear, sweet niece. I’m not stupid. You can do your little eye tricks, but I made sure to block your actual powers.”

Amelia snarled. “My moms will come and get me. Mommy destroyed the reapers you sent.”

The older entity’s lip twitched. “She told you about that?”

“No, but I’m smart. I knew they were following me, and I heard everything. My sister taught me how to spy.” Amelia had sat up, legs dangling off the bed, ready to strike if needed.

“You really aren’t a typical child.” 

“Duh!” The anomaly spat.

 

“For someone who just met her, you are so much like Agatha.” She chuckled, amusement in her eyes. “Now, if you would listen for a moment, I believe we can help each other.”

Small arms crossed over her chest, brows furrowed. “What do you mean? With the Sacred Balance?”

“Yes. Smart girl.” Eternity stood, holding out a hand to the smallest entity. “I would like to show you something, if you’ll come with me.”

“I need two things before I agree to do anything.” Milly looked at the outstretched hand with disdain. She knew her mothers were devising a plan, and she needed to at least drag this out while she came up with her own ideas.

“What’s that, little one?”

“My blanket and my stuffed bunny,” she sniffed, eyes darting around her surroundings. “And a snack? Please? I like string cheese.” Amelia’s eyes slowly turned back to their normal shade of blue and began to water. 

“I’m sorry if I frightened you.” Eternity softened, taking in the girl's demeanor, and waved her hand. Sir Cuddles and the blanket appearing on the bed. “String cheese is in my office.”

Amelia took the items with a trembling smile and took her aunt’s hand. “Okay, Auntie Eternity. Lead the way.” 

Walking next to Eternity, Amelia carefully ran her fingers through the enchanted threads of her blanket, slowly undoing the block Eternity had put on her, as well as depowering the runes and Magickal locks around the realm.

Never underestimate Sharon Hart-Calderu.

🦕🦖🦕

Agatha arrived in the enchanted basement, Rio on her heels, and summoned the Darkhold immediately.

“What are you doing?” Death asked, confusion on her face.

“Trying to figure out how to incapacitate a cosmic cunt,” Agatha mumbled, pages flying and stopping. “Why did it stop on the soulmate bond?” Her eyebrows were practically in her hair as she stared up at Rio.

“You’re both more powerful when connected,” Lilia explained, stepping into the basement. 

Agatha spun around, eyes wide. “Where the fuck did you come from?”

“Amelia’s blanket. It’s special for a reason. Anyway, when I saw it was in her room, she wasn’t, and you two were gone, I did the math.” The grey-haired witch shrugged. “Eternity?”

“This idiot,” Agatha gestured toward Death. “Let her get kidnapped right under her nose.”

Rio rolled her eyes. “We’re trying to figure out how to get her back.”

“We go in, kill anyone and anything in our way, take her, and put Eternity in the void,” Agatha instructed. 

“You can’t banish Eternity.” Lilia reminded.

 

“We have to stabilize the balance, fix Rio.” Jen was the next to step into the basement, followed by Alice.

Rio cocked an eyebrow at Agatha. “I thought your house, and this basement, were locked down?”

“It is, only coven and family members can get through. If they aren’t recognized, they’re killed, immediately.” Agatha said calmly, narrowing her eyes. “If our daughter had been here, your demon of a sister wouldn’t have been able to STEAL HER OUT FROM UNDER MY NOSE!” She bellowed.

 

Lilith and Nicky nearly tumbled into the basement, breathing heavily. “We know how to fix it!” Lily panted, holding up the dagger keychain from her house key. Her eyes flew to Rio. “I finally figured it out. It’s basically a time knife, and can cut through the multiverse. It’s meant to track down Amelia when she jumps to different universes.”

Agatha’s eyes grew even larger, staring at Rio. “OUR FOUR-YEAR-OLD TRAVELS THE MULTIVERSE?!” She was trying not to implode.

“Occasionally.” Rio’s voice wavered, taking a step away from the angered witch.

Lilia stepped in between them, “You’re behaving like children.” She scoffed. “Explain.” She commanded the twins. 

🦕🦖🦕

When Lilith and Nicky arrived home to their New York apartment, the younger twin had sat on the couch contemplating the events of the weekend. Something about her little sister bothered her, and she was drawn to the old keychain Rio had given her at their High School graduation.

Both of the miniature daggers had been tossed into the junk drawer in the kitchen. She fished them out, laying them on the coffee table in front of her. When they had first received them, she had spent hours studying them, trying to decipher the puzzles they surely contained. 

Now, it was different. Now, she knew Amelia.

 

When they graduated, Amelia had been roughly two. Rio had mentioned a few times over the past summer that she wanted to tell them about their sister's existence. 

Death didn’t do things without a reason. No, she had gifted them this apartment and these keychains for a reason. Her eyes flew over the intricate designs once more, when finally, she caught it.

The runes. They were entangled, but they suddenly jumped out at her. Family, time, wisdom, protection, and stability. It clicked. Her feet were propelling her upstairs before her brain registered it. She burst into Nicky’s half of the apartment, startling him.

 

“Nicky, the daggers, look.” She held them out to him, as if she expected him to understand suddenly.

The older twin tilted his head. “Yes. Those are the replicas Rio gave us for our house keys. What about them?”

“Runes! Look!” A spark of purple Magick went through the hilt of the small object, outlining the separate runes. 

“Neat trick, but what does it mean?” 

“Stability. Zora said Milly was the answer to the Balance. Exception, right? So that explains family. Protection. That’s Rio’s whole thing, protecting the balance. That leaves time and wisdom. Milly can hop into dreams, see things across time, and time is ending. Wisdom is needed to truly fix the problem without over- or under-correcting.” Lily explained excitedly.

“I highly doubt Rio created these specifically to fix the balance.”

“Probably not. It’s probably to track down Green Goblin if she goes running through space and time or something, but this is how it relates to right now. My theory is that we can go back and figure out exactly what needs to happen to restore the balance. Or travel to another universe. Maybe even forward in time. I don’t know exactly, but it’s worth a try.” Swirling a hand over the dagger, she released it, both of them watching as it began to spin in the air.

A portal appeared, shimmering in front of them. Lilith grinned up at her brother. “See! Time travel!” She grabbed his arm and dragged him through the arch with her, holding the other dagger to ensure their safe return.

🦕🦖🦕

“Now we’re here!” Lily explained excitedly. 

“What did you learn? Surely you didn’t come straight here.” Agatha pinched the bridge of her nose. She was very aware of the time being wasted.

“No, we saw a million different timelines. Every possible outcome.” Nicky’s eyes darted to his sister.

“And we can’t tell you exactly what has to be done. What we can tell you is that neither of you will like the correct solution, and there is only one. So, guess right.” Lilith explained.

Nicky picked up from his sister, “Eternity has already opened the window by becoming an active participant. She’s supposed to remain neutral, but because she wants to steal Death’s powers, so that she can be in control, she’s acting out of emotion.” 

Lily nodded. “Rio, you need to do what you did that night on the island, but this time, Mom has to know you’re allowing her to do something you don’t want to do. Allowing the exception.”

 

“So choose wisely,” Wanda reminded from her recliner. 

Agatha’s head whipped around. “Angel, why are you here?”

“I’m so sorry. Jen texted me, and she saw my phone.” Zora explained, holding Wanda’s hand. “She teleported all of us back. The boys are upstairs.”

“I’m going with you.” Wanda stood, eyes glowing Scarlet.

The disagreement was unanimous. 

“But I’m the Scarlet Witch!” The redhead argued.

“I don’t care if you’re the Divine Mother. You’re seven months pregnant. We’ve already had one scare, Angel.” Agatha softened towards her. 

Wanda crossed her arms and grumbled. “Fine, but I’m staying here until I know she’s safe.

“That’s fine.” The matriarch agreed.

“I’m going to let Ralph, Ian, and Ellie know,” Lilia announced, before looking at Agatha. “Before you run off to another realm, you may want to tell Darcy.”

 

Agatha regarded Death. “We should probably come up with a plan first.”

The entity nodded, seeing the others leave the basement from the corner of her eye. “We need to decide exactly what compromise we’re making.”

“We can decide that when we’re there. We’ve wasted enough time, Feathers. Lilia’s right about the connection. We need to reopen it.” Agatha looked at the floor as she suggested it. “We’ll have the mental connection that way, our powers will be amplified.”

“Aggie…” Rio’s voice trailed off.

Agatha’s head snapped up, blue eyes meeting brown. “What are you worried about, Feathers? You’ve already proved how easily you can cut it off. Shouldn’t be too hard a second time.”

“That’s not fair, and you know it.” Death gritted back.

“You have a few minutes to decide. I need to pop in on my girlfriend. Let her know I’m off to save the world.” The familiar purple mist gathered, and Agatha was gone.

 

Rio sighed, knowing that this was going to be even more dangerous than she imagined.

🦕🦖🦕

Darcy stood in front of her closet, trying to pack for her upcoming week on Isla Dunblar. She didn’t leave until the following morning and needed to go to bed soon after her nightly phone call with Agatha.

 

“I love those shorts on you.” Agatha’s sultry voice filled the room, causing her to jump, not expecting the older woman to be in her apartment.

“Ags? What are you doing here?” She turned around, smile immediately disappearing as she took in the agitated look on her girlfriend’s face. “What happened?” 

“Amelia was taken by Eternity. Rio and I are going to get her, but I wanted to let you know. In person.” Stepping closer to the young woman, she wrapped her arms around her waist, burying her face in her neck. Darcy could feel her body shaking. 

“You’re scaring me, Agatha.” Her eyes filled with worry as she held the witch closer.

“For this to work well, for us to communicate silently, and our powers to be heightened,” Agatha took a deep breath, pulling back to look into dark blue eyes. “You know I love you and that you’re the one I want, no matter what happens, right?”

Darcy’s eyebrows knitted together. “What are you trying to tell me?”

“Rio and I. We have to reopen the bond. She’ll cut off her side when we have Amelia back.”

Darcy nodded. While part of her heart ached knowing that her girlfriend would be reconnected to her fucking soulmate, she knew it was necessary. She wanted Amelia back safely and would do whatever her mere mortal self could to ensure it happened. 

Even if it meant Agatha reconnecting with a cosmic love connection to the ex who was still in love with her.

“I just want Amelia back safely, Ags.” Her fingers toyed with the ends of the older woman’s wild hair. “Whatever it takes. I trust you, no matter what. I love you, too.” She pressed her mouth against the witch’s and kissed her urgently, knowing she had to go. “Let me know when you’re safe? Okay?” She whispered, breaking the kiss.

Agatha vanished from her arms, the familiar purple mist evaporating around her. 

Darcy’s tears tumbled down her cheeks as she picked up her phone and automatically called Ellie. She broke down the minute the blonde answered the phone, Lilia in the background.

🦕🦖🦕

Eternity watched the small child sitting in the chair across from her desk. She once again had the demeanor of an actual four-year-old, clutching her blanket and stuffed rabbit while snacking on string cheese of all things. She had felt bad about blocking Amelia’s Magick, but it was for everyone’s safety, mainly Eternity’s.

She pulled up graphs and data on different screens and directed Amelia’s attention to them. “Now, you’re a brilliant kid. These charts show how the death rate on Earth has greatly increased due to Death perfecting the Sacred Balance.”

Amelia raised her eyebrows, still unable to raise just one, no matter how hard she had practiced. “What does this have to do with me?” 

“Your mothers will do anything to get you back, including fixing this. You see, with everyone dying, time will come to a stop very soon. We can’t have that.” Eternity finished.

“I don’t think my moms will have to do anything other than walk in and get me, Auntie Eternity,” Amelia smirked, knowing what she had done.

“This realm is extremely protected, even from Death.”

The words had barely left Eternity’s mouth when the place shook, pieces of the ceiling falling. Her eyes narrowed at the menace before her.

The mini entity lifted her head and locked eyes with her aunt, eyes swirling their familiar purple and green. “Surprise.” She smiled, reaching out her hand and blasting her with her Magick. Knocking her out and sending her flying into another dimension. “Thank you for getting my blanket and Sir Cuddles for me.” 

 

She transported herself out to the main hallway and made her way to her parents, throwing guards and reapers aside with her powers as she did.

It was the resounding crack of bones and the gurgling of blood and ichor that made her stop and turn, eyes wide. It wasn’t just the human sounds—but the language she immediately understood and the pleas that filled her young mind.

The begging to reap their soul and release it into Death’s realm. 

Amelia had never thought of reapers as souls before. She wasn’t really sure what she thought. Her knowledge grew rapidly, but she was still a child. Small feet stepped closer to the body, struggling to breathe, and knelt, pushing the hood back.

A skeletal figure looked back at her, yet as she met its eyes, she saw a young woman. A young woman whose life was cut too short by a car accident. One she should have survived. Amelia reeled back, shocked. 

Though she was young, she understood the tragedy and its implications—the cause. 

“I’m so sorry.” She breathed to the reaper. “You have a son.” She added.

Not much older than you, young one. Yet, you understand so much more.

“My parents are Death and the most powerful witch in existence.” She shrugged. Her voice grew smaller, realizing what she had done. “I am so sorry for hurting you.”

People are dying. Before their time, really. We become reapers instead of crossing over. This allows me to end my journey. Please, release my soul, daughter of Death.

 

Amelia nodded, placing her hand on the woman’s ribcage and, with a pulse of Magick, released the soul. Overwhelmed, she pulled her knees to her chest and wailed with agony. 

A sound no child, cosmic entity or not, should ever make.   

🦕🦖🦕

Rio took a deep breath, knowing she had to reopen the bond with Agatha. It was for the best, it truly was. The entity, cursed with infinite knowledge, just wasn’t sure how it would feel or what would slip between them. Would her witch know that she still loved her? Still yearned for her?

 

“Stop overthinking it. Just block that part off, focus on why you’re connecting.” Lilia advised, re-entering the basement.

“Easier said than done,” She scoffed.

“I know it is, but now isn’t the time. You can disconnect once you have your daughter back.” The wise witch replied, settling on the sofa. 

Rio focused on the many books in the space. “That’s the main issue, Lilia. What if I don’t want to? What if I want to keep it open? It would definitely help with parenting. It benefits us both by strengthening us. Plus, Magickal mood ring for Agatha Harkness? Absolutely.” She glanced back at her long-time friend. “I just don’t want to come between her and Darcy.”

“She’s a big girl, Rio. She can make her own decisions. Now, it’s time to get your daughter.”

Lady Death turned her eyes to the spot her witch had previously occupied as she reappeared, dragging a hand through her hair and rolling her shoulders. “Alright, Skelator, you ready?”

She nodded, inhaling sharply and closing her eyes.

 

Agatha felt her scar flare to life, the skin healing, no longer pale and faded, but a bright mark on her skin. Rio’s voice caressing the inside of her mind. 

Can you hear me?

Agatha smiled instinctively, as if Rio’s voice belonged there, just like Wanda’s did for two very different reasons.

Yes, Feathers, loud and clear. 

The witch knew how to block parts of herself off and knew she would need to do so. There were things Rio didn’t need access to this time around. They weren’t together. They weren’t in love. This was for practicality reasons only.

“Okay, Feathers, let’s go kick your sister’s perfect ass and rescue our daughter.”

Rio grabbed Agatha’s arm, knowing exactly where Eternity’s realm was, and apparated them both. She blinked, looking around at the castle-like structure in front of them.

Agatha snorted, dropping Rio’s hand. “Of course princess has a castle.”

“Yet the inside is an amalgamation of things.” She tilted her head curiously. “I expected this to be harder. It’s usually not easy to walk right in here.

The spirit witch looked around, then up, before pointing to the cracked, broken runes. “Courtesy of our daughter, I’m sure.”

Death smiled and shook her head fondly.  

“Let’s go.” Agatha marched forward, hands glowing purple, obviously tired of waiting. Her clothing had transformed into thigh-high black boots, light purple leggings, a long dark purple gladiator skirt with silver accents, and a silver belt. Her top was sleeveless, with a silver moon-goddess symbol at the neckline, dark purple with silver accents, and side cutouts. Fingerless dark purple and silver gloves completed her battle outfit, her fingers unglamored.

Death’s own outfit had transformed as well. Her dark green vine-like top wrapped around her chest and shoulders, down her stomach, and outlined her hips. Her skirt flowed around her like water, slit up to the hip. 

She hurried behind Agatha, knowing precisely what the witch was going to do, not even flinching when she blasted through the wall surrounding the castle. Reapers and guards flying. 

Kill first, ask questions later—the Harkness way.

Agatha. Slow down.

The faster we get to her, the faster I get to torture your bitch of a sister.

Rio sighed audibly, flicking a hand in the direction of an incoming reaper, causing it to fly backward. 

Agatha stopped when she heard screeching from inside. “What the fuck was that?” She sneered, turning her head toward the sound. “Amelia.”

Rio groaned when Agatha vanished yet again. “A leash, she needs a leash.” 

 

Agatha looked around her, a hall forming, scanning the area for her daughter, when the wailing hit her ears. Looking down, she saw it. There, in a mass of brown hair, Bluey pajamas, and her purple and green blanket, was Amelia. Reaper robes lay crumpled beside her.

“Plumita! What happened? Where’s Eternity?”

“I blasted her three realms over.” Amelia sniffed, using her arm to wipe her face. “Mommy,” her entire body shook violently. “Reapers are people who died too early because of the Sacred Balance.” 

“What?” Rio appeared in a haze of green and black, looking at the pile of robes next to her daughter. “No, they're an ancient species.”

Amelia shook her head. “No.” She pointed to the space next to her. “She died in a car accident. She should’ve survived. She had a son. I, I killed her.” Amelia’s eyes were frantic as she looked between her mothers.

“Well, technically, honey. I killed her with my orders to the other reapers. Did you release her soul?” Rio sat next to Milly, wrapping an arm around her shoulders. 

Nodding, she buried her face in her Mama’s chest, light sobs still tumbling from her lips. “Fix it. Please fix it. I can’t fix it all,” she pleaded.

Agatha’s eyes flew to Rio’s.

So, exactly how do we fix this, Feathers?

First, we get her out of here. Eternity is returning. I can feel her.

 

“So, do you get a family discount at therapy?” Eternity’s footsteps echoed down the hallway, her voice dripping sarcasm. 

So much for that. Agatha stood, shielding Rio and Milly. “We have a fantastic therapist, actually. Would you like to join in a few sessions?”

“Cute. I see you’ve traumatized yet another one.” The entity stopped, eyes dropping to where Amelia was wrapped in Death’s arms. 

Rio had shifted to her natural form.

 

“This wouldn’t have happened if you hadn’t kidnapped her, you time-keeping trollop.” Agatha lashed out, eyes flaring, pinning the caustic bitch to the wall with strands of purple and black Magick.

“Please. You’re the one going around stealing Magick, overcorrecting, pro-creating with entities. I simply borrowed your child for leverage.” Eternity attempted to remain calm.

“Borrowing requires asking.” Agatha held up a Magickally powered hand, ready to put a hole through Eternity.

“Mommy, stop!” Amelia yelled out.

The witch lowered her hand and turned to look down at her daughter, retaining the hold on the entity. “Yes, Plumita?”

“Stop fighting. Just fix it.” She looked at Rio. “Stop ending people’s lives so early. Time is coming to an end because of it.” Looking up at Eternity, her eyes shining with tears, her lip quivered. “You have them both here now. Can I just go home?”

Agatha leveled the time entity with a glare. “I either send her home, or I let her burn the entire place down. You know she can do it, too.”

“By all means, send her home.”

“Wait! I need to do something first! They’re all suffering.” Milly looked up at Lady Death. “Why can’t you hear them anymore, Mama?” She asked, eyebrows furrowed. 

I never could, cariño. Her voice sounded around them. I spoke to them when they crossed, never after.

“That’s my job, Plumita,” Agatha said quietly. Realizing that Milly had powers from both of them. “You learn how to turn it off, block it out.” 

Amelia closed her eyes, taking in calming breaths and raising her hands. Magick swirled around her.

They could feel and see the souls of the fallen rise and vanish into the next realm. Milly’s arms falling and her eyes half opening. “They’re at peace now. I want to go home.”

With a snap, Amelia left in a cloud of green and purple. Death rose to her feet and faced her sister. 

 

Milly’s right, and you just witnessed it. She can repair things, but not everything. Eternity, you broke neutrality. 

“Someone had to. You weren’t listening to reason.” Eternity huffed, pulling against the restraints.

I didn’t realize it was this bad! Mortal souls becoming reapers? That’s not supposed to happen. She turned her half-skeletal face to Agatha, switching to speaking through their link.

Exception. Go with me on this.

 

You need to make the family immortal, and I need to let it happen. Death stated plainly to both of them.

Agatha tilted her head toward her youngest child's mother. “But with choice. The immortal being is allowed to decide if and when they want to crossover with you.” She mulled over the words as she said them, making sure she chose them carefully.

Eternity turned pale, eyes flying between the two of them as though she were watching a tennis match, still pinned, runes keeping her from using her own Magick. “What? You have to be kidding me!”

Rio shook her head. No. We’re restoring mercy and grace. It rewrites the code and sends a message. Restores the balance to what it previously was. We both bend.

“Which causes you to compromise as well.” Agatha pointed a finger at Eternity. “Time continues to stretch, but you return to being neutral, and you do not get to decide finality.” Agatha turned back to Rio. “All I ever wanted was my family, Rio.”

You need to release the souls you still have, you know. Death replied instead.

“All but Evanora’s.” Agatha agreed.

Agreed.

Eternity groaned. “As long as Rio stops people from dying at exponential rates and time no longer has an expiration date, then fine.” The cosmic being thought about it for a moment. “What about Amelia?”

“What about her?” Agatha asked, looking between the entities.

“Her powers are too great for a four-year-old.” Eternity cautioned. “She’s too unstable. Were you even aware she had some of your powers? And Agatha’s?” She asked her sister. “She needs to be des—”

Agatha hummed before pivoting on her heel and punching Eternity squarely in the nose, sending her flying backwards and through a portal leading her into the void. “I’ll show you unstable.” A smile ghosted over her lips. “I know she can get herself out, but she had to have consequences for taking Amelia. The last person who took one of my children was fed to a dinosaur.”

I remember, I’m surprised there wasn’t more torture.

“Milly saw enough.” She traced the structure of Rio’s face, once again committing it to memory. The last time she was fortunate enough to witness this form, they had created Amelia.

Her heart rate increased, the back of her hand instinctively grazing the cool bone of Lady Death’s jaw. I know we’ll never be together again, but I will always find this form beautiful. She thought to herself.

Rio wrapped her bony fingers around Agatha’s wrist, turned her face, and pressed her teeth to the witch’s palm.

A kiss.
