It was then that Agatha realized she had sent her sentiment through the link. Shit. That she knew was a thought to herself.

She stepped back and quickly turned, breaking the moment. “Well, I think it’s high time we get back.”

🦕🦖🦕

Lilia had brought Ralph, Ian, and Ellie back with her from Connecticut, and Amelia appeared squarely in Ralph’s arms. He immediately wrapped her in his arms as she gripped him like a koala.

Her family let out a collective sigh of relief, careful not to startle or overwhelm her. 

The room went quiet, waiting for her to speak, giving her the space she needed, much like they had Lily after the ordeal on the island. 

Finally, she lifted her head and took in the scene around her. “They’re fixing the balance.” Her eyes immediately went to Sharon. “Eternity blocked my Magick. The blanket worked. Thank you, Mimi.” Sighing, she lay her head back on her Dad’s shoulder.

“Do you want to go to bed, Princesa?” He asked softly.

“No, I want to wait.” 

“I know I sent all of you home earlier today.” Agatha’s voice cut through the quiet.

All eyes turned to the women in the center of the room. 

“Like we weren’t going to come back,” Ian sighed in relief for a second time, his hand on Milly’s leg.

Ellie met Agatha’s eyes, communicating silently. 

Lilith and Nicky exchanged a look before turning back to their mother. “Well? What happened?”

“The world is no longer ending,” Rio answered first, now back in human form, rounding the couch to stand behind Ralph and run a hand through Milly’s hair.

“The Balance is also back to where it was before I fucked it up.” She spoke directly to those in the medical field. “Medications will work again.”

“Oh, thank fuck.” Jen let out with a wry smile.

Billy’s eyes lit up. “Does this mean we get to be immortal now?”

“It means I can run as fast as I want! Hell yeah!” Tommy exclaimed.

“Language!” Wanda scolded.

Agatha did not miss the way Rio and Lilia looked at her. “To answer your question, yes, it does. With conditions.” Taking a sharp breath, her cerulean eyes met Death’s amber ones. “A soul cannot be taken while it is still choosing to remain.”

Tommy’s eyebrows knitted together in confusion. “Huh?”

The witch chuckled. “In other words, once you hit about thirty, you will stop aging, unless you decide to grow older. Then, you live until you decide you’re ready for Rio to walk you to the next realm. That decision, however, is final.”

Lily smiled. “You made the right choice, thank the Divine Mother.”

 

With everyone returning home, Agatha sighed, watching as Rio gathered Amelia and her comfort items into her arms. 

“It’s late. Stay tonight.” She offered with a yawn, her arms stretching above her head. They had both showered and were in loungewear instead of their battle outfits.

“I need to get her home, work on the routine.” Rio hid the flush on her cheeks with her hair. Lilith had bathed Amelia for them, and she now wore clean Strawberry Shortcake pajamas.

Milly stirred and looked at both of them. “No. Can we stay here? I want to sleep with mommy.” She requested sleepily.

Death looked up at her witch. “I guess I can leave her here with you tonight?”

The small entity shook her head. “I want both of you,” she whined.

Agatha smiled. “Anytime the other kids had a hard day or bad dreams, they wanted to sleep between Ralph and me. Guess you’re bunking with us, Skeletor.”

Guilty. Agatha felt guilty. She had told Darcy they were back, and she needed to tell her about this situation. Yet she and Rio were grown adults, sleeping on opposite sides of a California King, with a child between them.

The connection was still open.

Rio?

Yes?

Will you turn off your side of the connection? Please. 

Death shifted, turning to peek at the witch over Amelia’s mass of hair. The younger woman was on her back, the moon outlining her features perfectly, giving her skin an ethereal glow. She was beautiful, and Rio couldn’t deny their soulmate bond any longer. No matter how much she wanted to. 

She knew Agatha had made her choice, no matter what the Fates or the Darkhold said. They were coparents, maybe even friends, and that was it. As long as her witch was happy, Lady Death was happy.

It would be the lie she told herself every day.

Her breath hitched through their connection. I don’t think I can, Ags. I’ve accepted this now. I know you chose Darcy, and I understand that. But I can’t break this connection again. 

 

She heard the sharp intake of breath from the other side of the bed—Felt Agatha’s anxiety, followed by a peaceful feeling.

Well, this will be helpful when parenting an entity.

Rio didn’t have to see Agatha’s perfect face to know she was smirking.       



Agatha stirred awake before her alarm, a comforting weight against her chest, her hand wrapped around a familiar hip. Her mouth twitched into a smile. She could get used to this.

Then she remembered. Milly being kidnapped, the reapers, admitting to Death she was beautiful, sharing a bed.

Rio.

Rio’s hip.

She yanked her hand away, jumping out of the bed as if it were on fire. Amelia rolled into her Mama and was pulled into her automatically. 

The witch smiled softly, reached for her phone, and snapped a pic. She set it as her homescreen before she could overthink it.

Following the smell of coffee downstairs, she was greeted by Lilia, who was keeping Amelia while the two of them went to work. “How’d you know they were here?”

“Well, they weren’t at Rio’s, and last night was traumatic. I take it; neither of you will be turning off the bond.” 

Agatha ignored the question.

“All these rooms and you shared a bed?” 

“Amelia needed both of us last night.” Agatha drank her coffee black, refusing to meet Lilia’s gaze.

“Now that the world is fixed, you can focus on what matters.” Lilia poured another cup of coffee.

“Which is?”

“Fixing the two of you.”

Agatha laughed dryly. “There is no us.” She whispered, hearing footsteps on the stairs. “It’s Darcy and me, you old bat.”

She plastered on a smile when Amelia came running into the room, followed by Rio. 

“Mommy! I slept so well last night! Can I sleep with both of you every night?”

Rio choked on her coffee while Lilia attempted to stifle a laugh.

“No, Plumita. That was a one-time deal, I’m afraid. Now, why don’t you eat breakfast? We have to get ready and go to work.”  

Amelia’s smile fell as she trudged to the table. “Fine.” She pouted, placing her chin in her hand, poking the eggs Lilia placed in front of her.

Co-parenting was not going to be an easy feat.

🦕🦖🦕

Agatha sat in her office, planning for the semester and updating syllabi, when Rio knocked and entered, taking a seat without waiting. 

“Can I help you, Feathers?” The witch peered over the top of her thick-framed black glasses.

“Custody. I don’t want to involve lawyers, Agatha. How do we want to do this?” Death chewed on the skin around her fingernail.

She swiveled her chair to face the ageless woman in front of her, leaning back and crossing her legs. Glamored fingers laced together and rested lightly on her stomach. “Rotating weeks? Sunday to Sunday? I assume we’ll be spending holidays together unless we want to do them separately. If one of us has to travel, we can negotiate it then.” 

“I actually agree to that plan. Pre-K?”

“Well, we looked at brochures last week. Can we tour a few this week? It will be good to have her socialize with other kids her age.” Agatha asked, surprised at how easily they were agreeing.

“We’re on the same page so far. What about her Magick? You heard what Eternity said about being unstable.” Rio looked away from the woman across from her.

“She’s a Harkness, we’re all unstable.” The witch replied dryly.

“You know what I mean, Agatha. She’s burdened with all this power and knowledge at four.”

“School will help with some of that, the world not being broken, being with both of us. I wasn’t joking when I told your child thief of a sister that we have a great therapist. Clea Strange. I can give her a call.”

“Therapy? Do you think that’s necessary?” Rio raised a skeptical eyebrow.

“The boys saw her after Vision…died.” 

“After you murdered him?” Rio corrected.

“Semantics. All the kids have seen her at one point or another. There’s nothing wrong with therapy. Our child, Feathers, is a four-year-old cosmic anomaly. Her parents are separated, and she didn’t know me until a week ago. I’m dating a woman who is not her mother. Kidnapped by her aunt, traumatized by her first technical kill. Yeah, I think she needs to see a therapist.” 

“Well, when you put it that way…”

“Were you ever a child?” Agatha asked abruptly.

“Not that I remember. I appeared to Alice as one when she was a child, but I was never one. None of the entities were, I don’t think. If entities have children, they either just appear or don’t remember their childhood.” Rio searched her memories, face contorted in deep concentration. “We don’t have half-human children. We leave that to the Asgardians, generally.”

“So Amelia’s a test subject. Great.” She pinched the bridge of her nose, beneath her glasses.

“Speaking of children, have you and Darcy…” Death’s voice trailed off as she met Agatha’s eyes once more.

“We haven’t. I don’t know if it would work with her, even with spells. That whole human factor and all. I know we could go the science route, but I honestly haven’t given it much thought. We haven’t even talked about marriage.” She tilted her head. “Why?”

“Just curious, since it would affect Amelia.” The entity quickly looked away for a beat before looking back at her witch. “What about you? Would you have another child?”

 

Agatha let out a harsh laugh. “I can’t carry children anymore, Rio. The twins were my only chance. I’m glad I had two.” She tore her eyes from Rio’s care-filled gaze. “Evanora wanted me to give her a long line of heirs for her to shape, of course. She also put a spell on me that went horribly wrong. I began hemorrhaging during labor. Turned out my uterus was infected due to whatever my mother had done. It’s a miracle the twins were born healthy. If Jen hadn’t been my doctor…” A tear slid down her cheek. “Well, that was almost twenty-one years ago. I lost that damn organ that day, and Evanora is dead now. It doesn’t matter.” She looked up to see Death leaning against the desk next to her.

She hadn’t even registered her movement, having been so lost in the memories and emotions. She kept her eyes locked on Rio’s face as the familiar, comforting hand reached down and wiped the offending tears away.

The thrum of energy through the connection hit them both. Agatha instinctively leaned into the touch, like Rio had the previous night, eyes fluttering closed. She would be remiss if she didn’t say she missed this. Crave it in her bones.

The sound of her phone ringing cut through the charged silence, Agatha’s eyes flying open as she pushed back from Rio’s touch. “It’s Darcy. I have to take this. I’ll let you take point on schools.” She rushed out, answering quickly.

“Baby! Did you make it to the island safely?”

 

Rio took that as her cue to leave, watching as her soulmate chattered away calmly with her girlfriend, though she looked guilty.

She had tours to schedule and her own classes to prepare for. She kept thinking back on the conversation and what Agatha had revealed. Death couldn’t help but wonder how her witch felt about having more kids together. Not that it would ever happen, but it was something she thought about from time to time.

Wiping away Agatha’s tears had been a reflex. Really, she had wanted to kiss them away, but she knew that was something she couldn’t undo. No, what she had done was safer, even if the contact had been electrified. 

She didn’t need some supernatural connection for her emotions to be charged around Agatha. Rio had felt that way since the first time their eyes had met. Now that they would be major parts of each other’s lives, and neither was willing to break the connection, they would just have to adjust.   

🦕🦖🦕

“So, I’ll go back and forth?” Amelia questioned her mothers. A hint of disbelief in her voice.

“Sí, cariño. You’ll get to see us for the same amount of time, and we’ll spend holidays together as one big family.” Rio smiled at her daughter.

“I have conditions.” The young girl announced, rising from the couch and turning to face the two women.

“You do, do you?” Agatha attempted to keep her face neutral, though her eyes gleamed with humor.

“We eat together as a family on Sunday nights. I miss our Monday nights with Nonna.” She leveled a glare at her Mama. “Secondly, once a month, we do something, the three of us, on the weekend. Whenever I have something at school, you both have to come.” She put a finger to her chin, deep in thought, to make sure she had thought of everything. “Oh! And each month, one of my therapy sessions is a family session.”

Agatha raised an eyebrow. “Lily told you about those, did she?”

Amelia grinned mischievously and nodded. “She said family therapy was very helpful.”

“I do have a question about the terms of the family outings.” Agatha cleared her throat. “Can other family members come with us?”

Milly pursed her lips and thought over the question. “Family members, including Aunts, yes. Girlfriends, no. Because they aren’t family yet.”

Rio glanced at Agatha from the corner of her eye and registered the jaw clench. “Nicky and Pietro’s girlfriends, I understand, but Darcy—”

Agatha held her hand up to stop Rio. “No, she’s right. I may consider her family, but Plumita doesn’t know her yet, and that’s okay. She needs time.” Holding a hand out to shake on the deal, she smiled at their daughter. “I agree with the terms. You are a very good negotiator.”

“It was a pleasure doing business with both of you.” The pint-sized menace grinned. “Whose house do I start at?”

“That’s for you to decide, cariño.”

“I’ll stay with Mama this week, as originally planned.” 

 

Amelia was excited for the time she had managed to finagle with her moms. The outings, the family meals, the Darcy-free time. She was just trying to make the most of strengthening the relationship between the three of them, mostly. 

Away from that intruder, who could be her sister.

🦕🦖🦕

October 9, 2025, Boston, MA

Over a month had passed since Agatha’s life had changed in the best of ways, and she wouldn’t change it for the world.

Well, most of it.

She was so focused on Amelia, teaching, being involved with the family business from afar, Wanda, and the rest of her family, that she barely had time for the woman she loved.

Darcy Lewis.

Darcy needed to be in New York, and now Ian had charged her with leading the team to set up a second island—Isla Sorna, beginning in January. 

With family like this, who needed enemies? 

Agatha had done a lot of soul-searching over the past few weeks. She had spoken at length with her personal board of directors: Ralph, Ian, and Ellie. 

As much as it pained her and broke her heart to do it, she knew where her focus had to be.

On that Thursday morning in October, she set up online assignments and left for a board meeting for Harkness Holdings. She was leaving for the weekend, Amelia was with Rio, and they would have their weekly dinner on Sunday when they exchanged the kid.

She was uncharacteristically nervous throughout her final walkthrough of the house, ensuring everything was off or secured. Fidgeted on the plane ride while she did some work, and was still trying to settle her nerves when she met Zora at the top of the building in the New York skyline.

 

Zora gave her a polite hug and raised a concerned, blonde eyebrow. “Agatha, you look perfect, but unsettled. What’s wrong?”

The witch glanced down at herself in the dark navy suit she wore. Perfectly tailored, white shirt still crisp, stilettos perfect. “Too much caffeine, I assume.” Her eyes darted around the top floor of the building, looking for any trace of Darcy.

“Darcy’s not here, if that’s who you’re looking for,” Zora smiled wryly. “We have a board meeting. You can hole up at her apartment for the rest of the weekend after we discuss stocks and numbers.”

“Right,” The older woman nodded, smiling at Pietro when he came into view. “I just don’t get to see her as often is all.”

“Which should be a sign, Hagatha.” Jen smiled, not unkindly.

Agatha rolled her eyes, following the others into the room. She loved her family, but didn’t need everyone’s opinions on her next steps.

 

The board meeting went off without a hitch; all sectors of Harkness Holdings were doing well with the rebranding, and the Balance was back in place. Pietro dragged his mother into his office afterwards and studied her face for a moment.

“How’s Green Goblin? It’s been a month, Wanda’s about to have a baby, you’ve been so busy—I just want to check in with you.” He looked away, a slight blush creeping over his cheeks. “I worry about you.”

A smile danced across her lips, her oldest son admitting that he, too, worried about his mother. “That’s nothing to be embarrassed about, syna.”

“I know.” Running a hand through his hair, he met her blue gaze. “How’s she adapting?”

His mother huffed a laugh. “Like she never missed a beat. She has her days where she steps back and has to process, as you did. The circumstances are so much different from those of the two of you. Yet, sometimes it feels like deja vu.” She smiled and sat in one of the chairs across from his desk. “Therapy is helping us stay on the same page.”

Her son lowered himself into the chair next to his mom, watching her facial expressions closely. “How does she feel about Darcy?”

“Ah. There it is.” She scoffed. “Like any child, she wants Rio and me together. She thinks she’s subtle about it, but she’s four.” 

He grinned and shook his head.

“She likes Darcy as a person, but she does not like Darcy dating me. However, it hasn’t been much of a problem lately.” The witch sighed.

“Time?”

Nodding, she changed the subject. “How are things going with you and Emma?”

“Well, it’s one of the reasons I wanted to speak with you, mamička.” He leaned forward, forearms on his thighs, looking away. “How do you manage a family with so many demands on your time?”

“Is she pregnant?” His mother asked quickly.

“No, mom, nothing like that.”

“Thank goddess. I need some time before there are any more children in this family.” Taking a deep breath, she mulled over his question. “Well, you keep the lines of communication open. We’re constantly texting and talking throughout the day to coordinate schedules. You carve out specific time to talk or spend time together, like Sunday dinners, or our once-a-month outings. It all adds up.”

She furrowed her brows. “Why? Trouble in paradise?”

Pietro gave a slight shake of his head. “No, it’s just that I want to ask Emma Frost to marry me, and I want to do it right.”

Agatha gasped, then hugged her oldest child. “I am so happy for you. Don’t fuck it up.”

 

Leaving the office, feeling lighter after Pietro’s confession, she thought back on what she had said. Everything about managing a family with limited time, she had been doing with Rio and Amelia. Amelia came first; her children always had. It ripped at her heart, but she knew what she had to do, despite not wanting to.

With that, she finalized her decision and stepped into the back of the darkened SUV for the drive to Darcy’s.      

Standing in front of Darcy’s door, the renowned paleontologist took a grounding breath and knocked, straightening her suit jacket. She had looked deadly dinosaurs in the eyes, yet she was nervous to see the woman she loved and had been dating for over seven months.

Of course, this wasn’t any normal visit.

Darcy wasted zero time yanking Agatha into her home and melting against her, connecting their lips in a searing kiss. “I’ve missed you so much, Aggie.” She admitted, stepping away. 

“About that.” Walking further into the apartment, she removed her suit jacket and tossed it on the couch. Sitting in an overstuffed chair, she tapped her thigh. “Come here, baby.”

This was something she had to do, no matter how much she dreaded it. Her focus was on her daughter and everything else in her life. Not here, not on Darcy.

The younger scientist looked at her girlfriend cautiously as she took a seat in her lap, wrapping her arms around her neck. “Agatha, what is it?” The look of sadness in her eyes told her she wasn’t going to like the reason Agatha was here.

The witch pulled her closer, caressing her cheek. “Darcy. When we met, I was in such a bad place. You pulled me out, showed me how I deserve to be loved. Taught me how to return that love. I will be forever grateful for that.”

Agatha placed a long, slender finger against Darcy’s lips when she opened her mouth to speak. “Let me finish, pretty girl.”

“With co-parenting, therapy, teaching classes, the business, just everything going on,” her voice broke while she explained her reasoning. “You deserve someone who can dedicate more time and attention to you. I need, have, to be focused on Amelia.”

Strong arms wrapped around Darcy’s waist, pulling her closer. “I am so in love with you, and that’s why I have to let you go.” Dropping a kiss to her shoulder, she drew a ragged breath. “I’ll understand if you hate me.”

 

“I couldn’t if I tried.” A tear slid down the younger woman’s cheek. “You have to do what’s best for your family, Agatha, and I won’t stand in the way of that. I got to be yours for a little while at least, and I’ll always treasure that.” 

 

“That’s so gay.” Agatha laughed, trying to break the tension.

 

“It’s the truth. I think I always knew we wouldn’t be forever,” pulling back enough to cup the witch’s face and tilt it, she kissed her soundly. “I love you, Agatha Harkness, and while you are doing this for all the right reasons, you were never mine to keep. Not really.”  

🦕🦖🦕

“That’s one of the most devastating, but amicable, break-ups you’ve ever had, Aggie.” Wanda waddled to the couch with a bowl of baby pickle spears.

“I just can’t right now. That’s literally the only reason I broke up with her.” Agatha complained from her place, sprawled across a chair.

After breaking up with Darcy, instead of going back to her own Manhattan penthouse, she had gone straight to Wanda’s. One, to check on her, and two, to cry on her shoulder.

“Are you sure that’s the only reason?” A red eyebrow raised in question.

“Yes, I am,” Agatha replied sternly.

Zora smirked, “Not the lingering touches, stolen glances, or almost kisses with Rio?”

“I have no idea what you’re talking about.”

“Oh, please.” Wanda winced when Gabby roundhouse kicked her in the liver. “We were all at the aquarium.”

“You were imagining things.” Agatha refused to think about it. How Sunday evening dinners were becoming Sunday afternoons and evenings together, the three of them. The way the two women often bumped into each other at work, or collaborated.

It was obviously the stupid bond. They were both too stubborn to be the first one to close it. It had nothing to really do with them, did it?

She returned her attention to Wanda, eyes landing on her moving stomach. “Someone is trying to bust through, looks like.

“Don’t give Gabriella any hints.” She muttered, readjusting.

“It’s not Gabrielle anymore?” 

Zora handed Wanda a large cup of ice water and sat next to her, a protective hand on her bump. “No, we thought the ‘a’ on the end fit in better.”

Wanda peered at Agatha beside her. “When are you going to break the news to Amelia and Rio?”

“I don’t think I need to, really. Rio will hear about it, which means Amelia will ‘overhear’ a conversation.” She fully turned to the two women. “This doesn’t change anything.”

 

Billy and Tommy had been hiding on the balcony overlooking the room upstairs, listening to and recording the conversation. 

Tommy grinned at his twin. Did you get that? 

Billy chewed his lower lip. Yes, everything. But do we really need to send it?”

Tommy took the phone from his brother’s hand and composed the message to Rio, pressing send. It’s for Nana and Rio’s only good. Nana is hardheaded and stubborn. He beamed with pride when Rio texted a simple ‘Thank you’ along with a ‘now stop spying’. Our family just keeps getting cooler. We have literal DEATH as basically another grandparent.

Billy had skepticism etched into his face. Witches, entities, scientists, criminals…yet not a dog or cat in sight.

“No, but we could probably ask Grandpa Ian for one of the smaller dinosaurs for Christmas,” Tommy voiced.

“No prehistoric pets, Gremlins.”

 

Both boys slowly turned their heads and looked up at their grandmother. 

“What have I told you two about spying?” She asked, arms crossed, attempting to look stern.

“That if we’re going to do it, don’t get caught,” Tommy grumbled, rising to his feet.

“Correct. Hand me your phone and make yourselves useful. Clean your rooms. I have a feeling they're disgusting.” 

Opening Tommy’s phone, knowing they had been up to something, she went to his recent messages.

“Why was he texting Rio?” Opening the message, she was met with the video, shot from above at a terrible angle. ”Fuck me,” she huffed.

🦕🦖🦕

Agatha had returned from New York early. She had planned to spend the weekend with her children, but wanted to get ahead on work for when she took a few days off for Gabby’s birth. 

Wanda would have plenty of help, and Agatha knew that. However, Wanda and Zora had asked her to be there the first few days. Who was she to deny those wishes? Rio had agreed, though Amelia had protested, wanting to help, too.

It was still three weeks until the Halloween due date that Lilia was swearing by. Lilia Calderu hadn’t been wrong about a single birthday, including Amelia’s, Agatha had learned. 

With that information, Agatha wanted to make sure everything was taken care of before Thanksgiving break. She had two doctoral students ready to take over her courses and labs, the book she was currently writing was ahead of its deadlines, and she wasn’t recording anything for the Science channel until December.

She frowned down at her planner on her desk, chin in her hand. It was a Saturday evening, and she actually had free time. She had thrown herself so completely into her work that she was already weeks ahead of herself without even trying.

“I always have been exceptional.” She scoffed. A warm, furry creature rubbed against her leg. “It is not avoidance. I have nothing to avoid.” She defended.

“What do you know? You’re in a situationship with another demon whose main form is a very judgmental, overgrown salamander.” Reaching down to pet the bunny.

Scratchy nipped her fingers.

“Ow! Did you just bite me?” Hissing, she reflexively brought her fingers to her lips. “You made me bleed my own blood, you sentient pair of slippers.” Leaving her office, she went to doctor the two fingers her familiar had chomped on, glaring down at the overgrown rodent. 

 

Moments later, she settled onto the couch with a glass of wine and turned the TV to a random channel. “Of course it’s some reality show.” 

Reaching for her phone, her thumb hovered over Rio’s number before re-thinking it. Rio was spending time with their daughter. They would see each other for handoff and their Sunday afternoon together in roughly eighteen hours. 

Her mind wandered to what Rio was going to say about the video the twins had sent her, if anything. It wasn’t like she had made any grand declarations or anything. She didn’t want Rio, Amelia, or Darcy to get hurt. 

Agatha Harkness had hurt Rio Vidal enough for a thousand lifetimes.

Her breath caught as soon as she had the thought. Had she subconsciously broken up with Darcy for Rio?

Of course not. This really was something she did out of her love and respect for Darcy, and it hurt like hell. Yet, she had cried fewer tears over this breakup than she had in previous ones. Rio and Ellie. 

Because she had a feeling she would see Darcy on Ellie’s arm soon, and it didn’t bother her at all—the lesbian tale as old as time and all.

Shaking her head with a small smile, she glanced down when Señor Scratchy nuzzled against her knee. “Come to apologize?”

He raised an ear and twitched his nose.

“You stand by what you said, do you?” 

“I really am okay with it.” Draining her wine glass, she took the bottle she had carried in and refilled it. “No, I’m not getting back with her. I’m okay with being single. Maybe I’ll mingle with the women of Boston and siphon through sex again, instead of murder.”

The rabbit nipped her thigh. 

“Ouch! Okay! Point taken. I’m still not getting back with her.” The witch held up her hands in defense, purple Magick sparking from her fingertips. “No more biting, you’re not a piranha.”

 

With Scratchy calmly settling back against her, Agatha looked back on the sixteen years that Rio had been in and out of her life. Their actual relationship had been mere months, yet they followed and studied each other for all those years.

The people in their lives intermingled and were connected to both of them in different ways. While Alice attempted to remain neutral, Agatha knew she would always choose Rio, and that was good. Rio needed that.

Truly, she wanted everyone to form their own opinions, though Agatha knew she tended to play the victim. She had grown since the coparenting agreement had started. While Agatha loved being a paleontologist, a witch, and all the other things that made up Agatha Harkness. Her favorite role was that of mom and caretaker.

Others were always surprised to learn that the world's most powerful and deadliest witch was such a protector at her core when it came to her loved ones. Having another child was an unspoken dream of hers, and for Death to walk back in her life with their child?

Rio would always be ‘the one who got away’. They had been fated, of course. Agatha knew what the damn book said, but refused to follow it. They had hurt each other so deeply, almost caused the end of the world, and put their daughter in harm's way.

Maybe in another life, everything would have worked out for them, but not this one. Sadly, Agatha only got one life, with the whole immortal thing she had so badly wanted.

It was a double-edged sword.

She quietly chastised herself and glanced at the time, deciding she needed to go to bed.

Once her wineglass was in the dishwasher, she settled into bed, freshly showered. Her thoughts still ran wild, and she knew sleep would be hard to come by. 

🦕🦖🦕

Rio had listened to the video from Tommy at least a hundred times through her headphones. She never meant for Agatha and Darcy to break up, but she wasn’t sad about it either. Part of her wanted to call Lilia over to watch Amelia so she could be a supportive friend and offer comfort and an ear. Or any other body part Agatha might need.

No, that wasn’t where the entity needed to put her focus. She needed to have it on the small tornado in front of her, who was trying her hardest to make pumpkin cream cheese muffins to take to Agatha the next day. Even though Rio had been careful, Amelia still heard the video.

Sneaky little anomaly.

She had been quiet before coming up with the idea. Rio could tell something was bothering her daughter, and was grateful she had therapy on Monday. Clea Strange was amazing with Milly, and they could already tell she was processing everything better.

Less traveling the multiverse, at least.

Still, something was off with her cariño, and she couldn’t quite put her finger on it. Instead, she dragged a finger through the cream cheese filling and smeared some on her daughter’s cheek, eliciting a squeal.

With the muffins cooled and packed for travel, the kitchen and kid cleaned up, Milly safe in bed, and protections set, Rio climbed under the covers and collapsed. As had become habit, she summoned one of Agatha’s journals, this one from after the island. Rio had thanked Ellie multiple times for the stack of insights into Agatha’s life. The diaries had been especially interesting. 

 

July 2020

At the risk of sounding like the female protagonist in any romantic drama, I feel as though my heart has been removed. 

I still have my children, my family. I can love in that way. However, once the bond was broken, once I could no longer feel her, a part of me died.

The connection we only shared for a few glorious hours. Not even long enough to truly explore all that it’s supposedly capable of, yet it already felt like home.

She felt like home.

I have always known that I was made for her, even if I have fought it. I have gaslit myself and everyone around me for so long. I have used her in the past, and continued to do so.

I cannot be good.

I am afraid that Evanora was right.

This is who I am, and this time, it has cost me greatly.

 

Rio ran her fingers over the ink, thinking back to that time, how she had unknowingly been pregnant. How upset she, herself, had been. That entire time had been rough for both of them, it seemed. She flipped through a few more entries in the same vein, some of which included the work she had been doing at the time, when she came to one that stood out.

 

March 21st, 2021

Something felt off yesterday and last night. I haven’t felt the pull as much lately, and last night it was nearly unbearable. My scar throbbed. Storms were popping up across the globe, with concentrations in Spain, and volcanoes were erupting across Earth. Mainly underwater.

I’m not sure what was happening with Rio. Part of me was filled with rage and jealousy that she was sleeping with someone else. I will kill them. I have no right to feel that way, but I really don’t care. 

I then wondered if maybe, just maybe, it was because of me. Was she upset? Does she miss me? Is she as utterly shattered as I am?

Or maybe something wonderful happened to her, and that made me happy for a moment. While I may be a drunken disaster, I don’t want bad things to happen to her. Not really. I do still love her, crave her touch, the looks she gave me. 

For two people who were not physically together for very long, we have a very deep history. While part of me hates her, I will always love her. She is literally carved into my flesh.

I hope she’s okay.

 

Rio wasn’t sure how to feel. Agatha would have been jealous? She’s the one who dated someone else! She didn’t even love Rio anymore. Or did she? Because they did have those lingering looks and the touches. 

Her fingers grazed over her three of swords mark when it pulsed, something it occasionally did when her witch let her guard down. She felt the twinge of sadness pulse through, conflict, longing?

Eyes darting to her phone, she did it before she could rethink it.

It didn’t finish ringing before Agatha answered.

“Feathers? Is everything okay?” Her voice was frantic.

“Yes, I didn’t mean to scare you. I’m so sorry.” This was a bad idea, she thought. She shouldn’t have called. “I felt you through the bond and wanted to check on you.”

“Oh,” Agatha’s breathing slowed. “Just thinking over things is all.”

“I’m sorry about Darcy, the twins told me.”

“I know.” Placing a fingernail between her teeth, she tried to find the right words. “It was for the best, really.”

“How are you doing, really?” Rio situated herself to fully sit up and picked at the threads of her comforter.

Relaxing her shoulder muscles, Agatha smiled softly. “Better.” Now that I can hear your voice. She couldn’t say that part, though. “I’m at peace, honestly, as much as it sucks.”

“I really am sorry, Aggie. I know you love her.” As much as it hurt Rio to admit it, she did want Agatha to be happy.

“I do, but you know what they say. If you love someone, let them go be with your ex. Or something like that.” Agatha’s voice had returned to its normal sarcastic tone.

Rio barked a laugh, “You can’t be serious. You really think?”

“Oh, honey, I know. I bet they come together for Thanksgiving. Honestly, they make more sense. Dating someone younger than my two oldest children? What was I thinking?”

Rio smiled out to the darkened room. Yeah, her witch would be just fine. “Get some sleep, Agatha. Milly wants to be there earlier than usual. She made something for you.”

“Goodnight, Feathers. Thank you for checking in.”

“Anytime, Aggie. Good night.”

Rio hung up, putting her phone back in its place, quickly drifting to sleep.  

🦕🦖🦕

At seven a.m. the next morning, Milly sent a gentle shock of Magick into her Mama’s shoulder, effectively waking her up.

“Amelia!” Rio’s eyes flew open as she sat up straight in the bed. “What’s wrong?”

“Nothing! I just want to surprise Mommy with breakfast!” Milly kept her voice light, though she felt nothing but. Being very emotionally aware and intelligent at her age was exhausting. After overhearing the video, she suddenly felt very guilty over her mother’s breakup and wanted to see her as soon as possible. 

Wise eyes searched those of her daughter, a hand ruffling her hair. “Let me get dressed, cariño. Are you sure everything is okay?”

Milly made a sound of reassurance as she scampered from the room, Rio watching her curiously.

As had become a habit in recent weeks, Amelia used her Magick to open the heavy wooden door of Agatha’s house and disabled the alarm with a flick of her wrist. Her pace didn’t slow as she skidded into the kitchen and set the muffin carrier on the counter.

Glancing around and sniffing the air like an expectant puppy, her eyebrows drew together when she glanced back at her Mama, a drink carrier from a local coffee shop in her hand.

“She’s still asleep, mija. She was up late last night.” The words registered after Rio let them slip.

The small entity’s eyebrows rose in question. “How do you know?”

“We talked last night. I was checking in on Wanda and making sure the flight went well.”

“Oh.” Amelia felt slightly hopeful. Her moms talking after the breakup was a good thing, she thought. She took Agatha’s coffee from the tray and floated a plate from a cabinet she couldn’t quite reach. “I’m taking her breakfast in bed.” She announced triumphantly.

“Do you want help?”

“No. I can do it myself.” She responded with a stubborn tone in her voice.

The miniature version of Agatha plated the homemade muffin and carefully climbed the stairs, making her way to her mom’s room, where she set breakfast down on the nightstand. Clambering into the bed, she crawled to where her mother was curled in the middle and shook her. 

Agatha’s eyes cracked open, a lazy smile forming. “You’re early.”

“I made you pumpkin cream cheese muffins,” Amelia whispered, eyes darting away.

Amelia had felt brave this entire time. She had wanted Agatha to break up with Darcy. Her parents belonged together. They were a family, and her moms were soulmates. That’s how it worked. 

Then she spied and overheard the video. Her mom didn’t have enough time for Darcy. Didn’t want to hurt her. Wanted to do what was best for Amelia. 

Didn’t want to get back with Rio.

That wasn’t the problem necessarily. The problem was that Agatha hadn’t wanted to break up with Darcy. She still loved her. But she wasn’t able to spend time with her, and that? That was Amelia’s fault.

Amelia was the one who had said no to Darcy during family time and demanded time together on Sundays. She never wanted to cause her parents pain. She just wanted them to be happy together.

Agatha reached out and cupped Amelia’s face, catching her gaze. “Thank you, Plumita. I appreciate it. These look delicious!” Pinching off a piece, she popped it in her mouth and groaned in delight. “These are amazing, my sweet girl. Will you share with me?”

 

Rio leaned against the door frame, eyes carefully trained on their daughter. She seemed content sharing a muffin with Agatha, but she could tell something still bothered her. 

Agatha’s voice brought her out of her contemplation. “Let’s take this downstairs, shall we?”

Milly scrambled back to her feet and led the way downstairs for family breakfast, her shoulders still entirely too tense for her age.

Has she told you what’s going on with her? Agatha’s voice slid through Rio’s mind like satin, sending a shiver down her spine.

No. I asked this morning. Told me everything was fine, but clearly, something is bothering her.

Sitting with their muffins and coffees, Amelia with her hot chocolate, the witch rubbed comforting circles on her daughter’s back. “Plumita, did you hear the video on Mama’s phone?”

Amelia’s hands fell into her lap, and she curled into herself, hanging her head with a nearly imperceptible nod.

“Ah.” Agatha looked across the table at Rio, waiting for the young child to elaborate.

 

An exhale, too big for her body, caused her to shudder. Looking up at her mom with watery eyes and trembling lips, the words began to flow. “I want you and Mama back together. I wanted Darcy gone, but I didn’t want you to hurt. You do love her, and I heard you did it because you don’t have time. You have to focus on me, and I take up all your time. I’m the one who set up the rules, and it’s my fault. She can come around more and stay here when I’m here. I’m sorry. I didn’t mean to ruin it. I don’t want you to be sad, and—” She choked on a sob.

Agatha gathered her in her arms, carrying her to the living room. Rio followed closely behind. She settled onto the couch, eyes dropping to the seat next to her. Death understood and sat close, their thighs pressed together.

Amelia had buried her face into her mother’s shoulder, now sobbing. 

The mother of five pressed a long kiss into her youngest child’s hair. “My sweet Plumita. Nothing, and I mean nothing, was your fault.”

“Yes, it was.” She squeaked.

 

Well, she didn’t get that from you. Rio scoffed through the connection.

Can it, Skeletor. Agatha growled back.

 

A calming hand landed on Agatha’s knee and squeezed.

The witch continued soothing their daughter. “No, honey, it wasn’t. Darcy and I would’ve broken up eventually. It was better for it to happen now. She deserves someone who can spend more time with her. You and I are still learning each other. Your Mama and I are learning how to be parents together. I wouldn’t change that for anything, Amelia Jade Harkness-Vidal.” Carefully leaning her back to make full eye contact, she continued. “You are one of my children, and my children come first.”

Amelia wiped her arm across her face, Rio reached for a tissue as she did. “You’re sure? It’s, it’s not my fault?” A hiccup caused her to stutter.

“Of course I am, and no, it’s not. We’ve talked about this, Plumita. You are four, and you need to worry about four-year-old things.”

“You should also stop spying,” Rio added.

“If I must,” Amelia sighed dramatically, something she was picking up from Agatha.

 

We have to find a way to help her dwell on more age-appropriate topics. Rio’s concern, again, floated through the link.

I don’t want to hold back her development or intelligence, but I don’t want to give her a complex either. The witch leaned into Death, their daughter now seeking the comfort of both mothers. 

 

Rio hadn’t moved her hand, which Agatha didn’t mind. Maybe Zora had been right about the lingering touches.         

🦕🦖🦕

October 31st, 2025

New York City

 

Wanda was in labor with Gabriella. Zora and Agatha were at her side, coaching her through the process. Her water had broken, though she wasn’t fully dilated yet. Jen was in and out, checking on progress and keeping tabs on the waiting room where Sharon, Lilia, and Ellie were already waiting.

This had been planned, going off of Lilia’s premonitions. Pietro and his fiancée, Emma Frost, had taken the twins trick-or-treating and agreed to bring them to the hospital afterwards. Nicky and his girlfriend, Morgan Stark, were passing out candy and would be joining once the bowl ran dry. 

In Boston, Rio, Amelia, and Lily were doing their own candy hunting, along with Ralph and Ian, who had joined them.

Everyone would converge in the waiting room once Halloween festivities commenced to await Gabby’s arrival. They could decide on overnight arrangements later.

Agatha sat in a chair next to Wanda’s head, spoonfeeding her ice chips, much like she had when the boys were born. “You’re doing beautiful, superstar.”

“Thank you, mama.” A painful contraction caused her to inhale sharply and nearly crush Zora’s hand. 

“Vela, I don’t think you want to break that,” Zora smirked.

The Scarlet Witch’s eyes flared crimson. “We’re never having sex again, it doesn’t matter!” 

Agatha stifled a laugh, though something in her heart stirred with sadness. “Breathe through it, Angel.” A pulse of purple Magick pulsed through Wanda’s veins, better than any epidural.

“Thanks, Mamincka.” Turning to look at her wife, she now smiled sheepishly. “I love you.”

Zora shook her head, “I love you too, but I will be very happy once Gabby is here.”

Wanda glanced back up at her mother. “What am I at?”

“Between eight and nine, based on the scan I did just then. It’s nine p.m. The entire family should arrive soon. I know you’re waiting.”

“I just want them to be able to meet here soon after she’s born,” Wanda confirmed.

The older witch reached out and smoothed her hair. 

Zora cleared her throat. “We’ve all been focused on preparing for this moment for the past few weeks. How are things with you and Rio?” 

There is nothing to report.” Her cheeks reddened slightly.

“Bullshit, Aggie.” Wanda hissed, though this one wasn’t nearly as painful as the last, thanks to Agatha’s Magick.

“What? We’ve stuck to the agreement, had a few extra dinners, the three of us, and that’s it.”

The younger women exchanged a look that said they didn’t believe her.

 

Which was fair, because it wasn’t the exact truth. 

 

Agatha had been writing excessively in a new journal, the past few weekends had been spent with all three of them together. They had been spending a few evenings together after work.

It felt right. Comfortable. Needed. Forbidden.

 

Agatha was still very set on not being with Rio. Not because she didn’t trust Rio. But because she didn’t trust herself.

Neither of them would turn the bond off, and every now and then, they let their guard down, and the connection would do its job as far as sharing their feelings.

Dangerous. 

 

They all looked up when Jen walked in, clapping her hands together. “The family is in the waiting room, sans Alice. She’ll be here as soon as she can. It is Halloween after all.”

 

It wasn’t thirty minutes later when a tiny cry cut through the delivery room. A perfect baby girl with red hair was carefully placed on Wanda’s chest, all three women crying—four, including Jen. 

Agatha was happy for her daughter and overcome with love and emotion, but there was something else—pain, regret, grief. Mourning missed moments and a life she would never have.

She rocked Gabby in her arms for a moment before handing her back to Wanda. She knew she’d have plenty of time with her in the upcoming weeks. “Welcome to the family, sweet girl.” She had whispered before surrendering her, a tear traveling down her cheek.

“I’m going to give you two some privacy. Once they get you settled in a room, and you're ready, let me know.” Leaning forward, she gently hugged Wanda. “You did perfectly, Angel. I’m so proud of you.” Circling the bed, she embraced Zora tightly. “Not bad, Bennett.” 

 

Finally stepping into the hallway, sneakers shuffling softly on the tile, she slumped into a chair across from the door and buried her head in her hands. 

Agatha had been present at the births of her grandchildren. Next to Wanda, holding her hand, encouraging her, feeding her ice chips. She was one of the first people to hold each baby and took time off work to help in the first critical weeks. If any of her other children decided to procreate, she would be there.

This serial killing witch did everything she could to be present for every milestone, performance, game, presentation, hospital visit, anything, for all of her kids. No, she hadn’t been able to be at all of them, but she had tried.

Being in the delivery room that night, doing what she could throughout the pregnancy, finding out about Amelia. Agatha couldn’t help but be filled with rage, along with everything else. The mix of emotion settled in her chest, taking up residence. 

Wiping her face on the back of her hands, she stood as the door opened, Wanda and Gabby being wheeled out in a wheelchair. “We’re going to a room for the night,” Wanda explained, exhausted.

It was Agatha who announced Gabby’s arrival and statistics to the family, scattered across the waiting room. She was also the one who delegated the visitation order.

 

“The boys, Pietro, Ralph, and Ian, first. Then Ralph and Ian can take the boys back to Wanda’s for the night.” Agatha began. “The second group will be Ellie, Lily, Nicky, Amelia, and Rio. Third, Lilia, Sharon, and Alice. Girlfriends, wait, where are Emma and Morgan?”

Nicky turned to his mother, “They went home, knowing you’d tell them they could visit tomorrow.”

Opening her mouth in protest, she snapped it shut. Wasn’t that what she had been about to do? 

Bypassing his comment, she looked down at Amelia, who was tugging at her shirt. “Can I help you?”

“I want to stay with Lily tonight, instead of going home with Mama.”

Agatha’s eyes raised to her middle daughter, who had sidled up behind Amelia. 

“Please? It will be fun! I’ll bring her back up here when you're here in the morning. Or she can just stay for the weekend. I can bring her up to visit and then take her home Sunday.”

“I don’t know, it’s Rio’s weekend. You need to ask—”

“Sounds good to me, have fun, girls.” Rio caught the first group leaving out. “Looks like it’s your turn.”

 

Agatha snuck a look at the entity before her, running a hand through her hair. “You can stay at my place tonight.”

Rio’s lips parted slightly in shock. “Are you sure?”

A laugh slipped from the witch’s full lips. “Of course. Ellie’s staying, too.”

At hearing her name, Ellie turned, a slight blush creeping up her neck. “Uh, about that. I’m going to stay with Darcy.”

Letting out a low whistle, Agatha chuckled. “Damn. The bed isn’t even cold yet, Ellie.”

“It’s not like that, Agatha.” The scientist rolled her eyes.

“Not yet. She’s welcome to Thanksgiving, by the way.” Smirking and pivoting back to Rio, she quirked a brow. “So? Want to stay with me?”

🦕🦖🦕

It was the early hours of the morning when they finally reached Agatha’s Manhattan residence. They were exhausted from the day and the emotional toll it had taken. While it had been a joyous event, Agatha was still processing the feelings she had been trying to keep in check. 

Of which there were many.

They moved around each other quietly as they showered separately and dressed for bed. Words left unsaid hung in the air, growing heavier by the minute. Agatha felt as though heavy boulders had been placed on her chest.

She snickered at the image. Fitting.

Opening her bedroom door to find Rio, she was greeted by the woman, a hand poised and ready to knock. Eyes widening, she dropped her fist. “Ags! I was just, uh, coming to say goodnight.”

Dragging a hand through her damp hair, Agatha blew out a breath. “I was coming to look for you, actually. Do you have a minute?”

“Sure.”

The witch led her deeper into the bedroom and sat on a chest at the foot of the bed. Rio settled next to her.

Blue eyes met amber when the words finally began to flow after years of needing to be said. “Rio, I am so sorry. For everything. For how our relationship ended, for doing what I did, the way I did. For not being there. For how much I hurt you. All of the lies, the backstabbing. We would have been amazing together.” Eyes glimmering with tears, she swallowed hard to hold them back. 

Rio covered Agatha’s unglamored hand with hers. “Agatha, you had to steal my Magick to save the kids. Even if you had asked? I would’ve said no. I know I would’ve. I’m not innocent. Did tonight spur this on?”

“Partially.” Eyes the color of the churning ocean roamed over Rio’s face and body, something hot and angry beginning to bubble inside the witch. “I wasn’t there for Amelia’s birth, and I hate it.” Fisting the hair at the nape of Rio’s neck, she brought their mouths inches apart. Short puffs of breath hot against Agatha’s lips. 

“I hate that I made you feel like you had to hide her from me. I hate how you didn’t trust me.” Closing the gap between them, she pressed a searing kiss to Rio’s lips, drinking her in. Her hand remained fisted in Death’s hair as she reclaimed Rio’s mouth. 

 

Shaking hands framed Agatha’s face, holding her there with parting lips. Agatha, I trust you now, that’s what matters.

A growl ripped from the witch’s throat, her tongue sliding against Rio’s, biting down on her full bottom lip hard enough to draw blood. 

Rising to her knees, she gathered the hem of Rio’s t-shirt and broke the feverish kiss long enough to pull it over her head. Grasping her hips, she effortlessly tossed the cosmic being onto the bed before straddling her, planting her hands on either side of her head.

“I hate that you broke me, Rio. That you ran and hid for five years, and it was my fault.” Dropping her head, she kissed her forcefully, laying her claim, eliciting a moan when she sucked on Rio’s tongue. 

Rio’s hands slid up Agatha’s defined arms, hands grasping her shoulders the moment Agatha bit down on her pulse point. “We both,” she gasped when Agatha sucked her skin into her mouth. “Made choices we can’t take back.”

Clenching her thighs together, Rio craved Agatha in every way possible. I need to see you, Aggie. She hissed at the pinching sensation at her nipple.

“Patience,” Agatha cooed, soothing the sting with a flick of her tongue. Pushing herself up to her knees, she slowly removed the tank top she was wearing before expertly shimmying out of her pajama pants and underwear without leaving the bed.

“You’re more beautiful than I remember,” Rio stared in awe. Taking in Agatha’s beauty, the tattoos, the mark, everything that made her, her. Her witch.

“Well, take a picture, it will last longer.” The scientist scoffed. “Lay back so I can ruin that gorgeous face of yours.”

Gulping, Rio did as she was told, gripping Agatha’s waist as she lowered herself onto Rio’s mouth.

 

Rio licked a filthy stripe through Agatha’s folds. The witch carding her hands through Death’s hair.

“I wrote in journals that entire time, about you.” Her hips set the pace for Rio, Agatha guiding her head. “I hate how I couldn’t stop thinking about you.”

You fell in love with someone else. You moved on! Rio dug her nails into Agatha’s sides to display her annoyance. 

The witch on top groaned at the sharp pain, thoroughly enjoying it. “She wasn’t, wasn’t y-you, my…Rio.” 

Death made one more long pass before circling her witch’s clit with her tongue. Using two fingers, she gently circled her opening. Her eyes were focused on the beautiful woman above her. Then why?

Agatha didn’t want soft and slow. She wanted what they had back on the island, but with even more anger—with her eyes on Rio’s, the corner of her mouth turned up into her signature smirk. 

“To finally forget you.”

Rio’s fingers rammed into her, lips wrapping around her clit. No more build-up, no more coercing, just the rough, rage-driven fucking that Agatha needed.

Rio’s free hand dug into Agatha’s side, drawing blood. 

“Rio! Yes! Fuck me!” Her nails scraped against the entity’s scalp as she cried out and moaned. The sounds of Rio fucking into her filled the room.

I never tried to forget or replace you, Agatha. Rio curled her fingers forward, hitting just the right spot.

Agatha was on the edge, and that one movement sent her over. Her thighs trembled while she continued to ride out her orgasm. 

 

It was quick, dirty, and exactly what she needed.

Slowly removing herself from Rio, she bared her teeth and pulled her soulmate's shorts off. “Did I say I was trying to replace you, Feathers?”

Rio looked up at her, eyes still partially dilated. “You were trying to forget me, so…”

“Yes, because I couldn’t live forever with the what ifs!” Pulling her into her arms, Agatha kissed her again, shuddering as their breasts pressed together. 

I could never truly forget you, Rio, no matter how hard I tried, not with you seared into my flesh, and no one could ever replace you. 

Agatha’s tongue dipped into Rio’s mouth, moving more slowly this time. Exploratory, mapping, savoring. 

Agatha, what happened tonight?  

 

Her kisses grew more possessive as she thought back on how she had felt and on the emotions she had experienced—the jealousy, ire, and remorse. Pulling away from Rio, she practically shoved her face down into the mattress. Kissing the sensitive spot below her ear, she whispered, “I was focusing on what was happening, but kept thinking of you.”

Rio gasped as Agatha dragged her blunt nails down her bare skin, her back arching. “Why?”

The real answer was simple, three words Agatha had said before, but refused to say in the moment. She decided to play it safe instead. “You shouldn’t have done it alone. We should have done it together.”

A heavy hand came down with a loud smack on Rio’s buttcheek, eliciting a moan. “Every step,” another slap on the other side caused Rio to arch up into Agatha’s hand. “Every milestone, I should have been there.” She delivered a third blow before running her fingers through Rio’s slit, feeling her arousal. 

Administering two more firm slaps to Rio’s round ass, she positioned herself directly behind her soulmate. A word that made her heart beat harder and the skull-shaped mark burn. 

Two fingers slipped back between Rio’s legs and through her heat, gently rubbing over her clit. Bracing herself with her other arm, she leaned forward and grazed a bare shoulder with her teeth, savoring the way Rio felt and sounded.

I wanted it all, you know. Agatha’s voice was shaky, even telepathically.

What, what do you mean?” Rio’s inner voice was inquisitive and filled with lust simultaneously.

 

Slowly entering Rio, savoring how her fingers were sucked in, muscles fluttering around them, it hit her. This was the woman she was made for. She knew that, but this was truly where she belonged. Yet all she did was cause Rio pain.

Pulling out, she pushed back in, setting a gruelling pace, trying not to be gentle. This wasn’t gentle. This was rage and fire. Years of hurt and betrayal. Wasn’t it?

Ignoring Rio’s question, she began to ram into Death, determined to make the feelings disappear.

“I missed how greedy your pussy is for me,” the witch growled instead. Flicking her index finger on her free hand, a wisp of violet encircled Rio’s engorged clit, emitting low vibrations.

A high-pitched moan left Rio at the new sensation. “Aggie, what?” Curling her knees underneath her to give Agatha a better angle, her breathing faltered as the vibrations sped up.

 

Agatha’s palm made wet slapping noises against Rio’s cunt each time she slammed into her. She felt her own arousal run down her thighs. “Magick toy, m…muerte.” No, she couldn’t let herself slip. Especially right then.

“C…come kiss me.” Rio stuttered, on the edge once more.

Lying herself against her lover's back, she met Rio’s parted lips and kissed her, not as a woman fucking away anger, but as a woman making love to the woman she was made for. 

Open your eyes, Aggie. Watch as I come on your hand.

Following the command, she reluctantly pulled back from the kiss and, increasing the Magick around Rio’s clit, she brushed against the perfect spot and watched in awe as Rio came. 

Their eyes remained locked on each other, Rio gripping the sheets, back arching into Agatha.

I love you. 

 

The words bounced around Agatha’s mind, wrapping themselves around every receptor. She couldn’t say it back, but her face softened, and she answered in a frenzied kiss.

Pulling back, a string of saliva between them, she gently removed her fingers and vanished the Magick. This was no longer fucking as she was acutely aware of the tears streaming down her face. The dam had broken, and her feelings were out in the open.

“Ags? What is it?” The entity rolled onto her back underneath her soulmate. Blissed out, but concerned, her hand over the mark on her chest.

 

Agatha sat up, motioning for Rio to follow her lead. Positioning them correctly, she pulled Death into her, their sensitive clits touching, and rolled her hips.

Shivers ran up their spines at the contact as they set a rhythm. Agatha cupped Rio’s cheek and looked into her very soul as they moved together. “I wanted it all, Lady Death. Marriage, Kids.” Her words broke on a combination of a sob and a moan. “Tonight was a reminder.”

Death searched the eyes of her lover, seeing regret, the love she tried to hide, and vulnerability. With a hand in thick, chestnut hair, and another around her waist, she pressed her mouth to hers. A declaration, a claim. 

I am yours, Agatha Harkness. Forever. 

Her tongue caressed the witch’s, circling the tip, running against the roof of her mouth, mouths breaking apart only for air. It grew more hurried as their hips stuttered and jerked against each other the closer they got.

Agatha clung to Rio, keeping her as close as possible. Come with me, babygirl.

As they came, screaming the other’s name, a flash of gold lit up the room. Marks flaring to life, carefully concealed feelings no longer hidden.

“I missed you, my love.” Agatha sobbed into Rio’s shoulder.  

🦕🦖🦕

Rio awoke wearing an oversized T-shirt and a sleepy smile. Rolling over, arm outstretched, she was greeted with an empty side of the bed. Worse still, Agatha had already made up her side of the bed.

 

Smile automatically faltering, she thought back to hours before. They had made soft, passionate love. When they were finally spent, Agatha snapped, and the sheets beneath them changed to clean ones.

Agatha had held Rio in her arms as they fell asleep. Where was she now?

Swinging her legs over the edge of the bed, she rose, flicked her wrist to make the bed, and left the bedroom and went in search of her frustrating co-parent.

 

Agatha was supposed to be at the hospital hours ago, but hadn’t even gone to sleep until six am. She needed to return to her mom duties, not entertain the idea of whatever this was. She had been serious three weeks ago when she said she didn’t have the time for a romantic relationship. That included Rio.

Because Rio? Rio deserved so much better than her. Even if they were fated or whatever witchy bullshit the Darkhold said. She had hurt Rio in the past and couldn’t run that risk again. Especially now with Amelia involved. Their daughter would be crushed if things didn't work out between them.

Moving between the cabinets and the refrigerator as she prepared to see Wanda and the rest of the family, she felt Rio’s presence before she saw her.

“Good morning, Feathers.” She greeted her with a casual smile. “I’m about to head up to the hospital. Wanda and Gabby will be discharged later this afternoon, and then I’ll be at the Maximoff household for the foreseeable future, as we discussed. I really appreciate you—” She was cut off by Rio kissing her firmly.

The younger witch felt herself relax against the entity’s body. Rio’s hands on her waist, applying just the right amount of pressure, her tongue exploring her mouth. This was what Agatha had wanted for years, wasn’t it? The domesticity, the love, the ease of familiarity.

Wants and needs were two separate things, she chastised herself, placing her hands on the cosmic being's chest. “I really do have to go. Are you coming?” 

“Of course,” Rio reached for her coat and a granola bar. “Can we talk on the way?”

Agatha nodded, biting the inside of her cheek, leading the other woman down to where a dark SUV was waiting. “When I said I didn’t have time for a relationship, Rio. I meant with anyone.” 

The air around them grew a degree colder; no one else noticed. Stepping into the car, she turned to her apparent destiny. “There’s too much at risk, and I can’t hurt you again.”

So what was last night? Rio hissed through the connection.

We no longer ignored the pull. We gave in like we always do. An apology of sorts. I meant everything.

You used that name. Brown doe eyes landed on Agatha.

It slipped, but I wouldn’t take it back. Rio, when I stepped foot on the island, I was there with a goal. The goal wasn’t to kill my mother, necessarily. It was to break free, then find you and beg you to take me back. I don’t beg. I don’t plead. If someone walks away from me? Fine. Fuck them. But not you. Never you. With her out of the way, we could restore the balance. We could have it all. Then everything went wrong.

I respected your boundaries after the island, even though I so badly wanted to go to you. I knew I had hurt you. Now we’re in a good place with a beautiful daughter, and if we were to get back together, I’d want to do it right. But I can’t risk the past repeating itself.

Tears threatened at her waterline, eyes red. Waterproof mascara was a very good decision this morning.

You already have me, Aggie. I was wrong, too. But, you're right. We are in a good place, and Milly would be hurt too.

 

Death spent the rest of the weekend watching Agatha be the mother she knew she was. The mother she had denied their own daughter. Returning to Boston with Milly, she felt as though she had left part of her heart in New York.

And she had.

Rio truly felt like a part of the family now, and being connected to Agatha had allowed her to see what she had suspected. Her witch was still in love with her.

Now she had to decide what to do about it. She had heard Agatha, and her concerns were valid. They were also Rio’s concerns. However, why should they keep playing it safe? 

This was also the first time they weren’t together, not because they didn’t want to be, but because they didn’t think it was a good idea. Their supportive family surrounded them. Amelia was making great progress. Maybe time was short right now, but surely after the holidays, they could at least go on a date.

Rio was hopeful and determined, having told Alice everything. 

Alice did not share Rio’s sentiments. 

“Did she tell you she loves you?” The detective asked as she stared at pictures of a crime scene. 

“Well, no. But she called me her love, and when the connection sealed, we both felt it.” Rio responded wistfully, grading essays on a Tuesday. 

“It’s not the same, and you know it.” She marked something on a picture. “We all know she loves you. I’m not saying she doesn’t. What I am saying is not to settle just because that hyped-up spell book says the two of you are meant to be or some shit. I want you to be happy, instead of this sixteen-year situationship.”

“I know. This human thing is exhausting.”

Pinning the pictures to her caseboard, Alice had an idea. “Maybe you shouldn’t be one full-time.”

“What do you mean?”

“Be Death during your Milly free weeks. You don’t actually require sleep, and you and I both know you slip out for certain deaths anyway. Start going out more. Check in with Hela and the others more often. Gives you less time to dwell.”

“That’s not a bad idea, wait. You need me to get information from victims, don’t you?”

Alice huffed a laugh. “No. That’s Agatha’s department.  

🦕🦖🦕

Walking into Wanda’s hospital room, she was surprised at the lack of people. “Where is everyone?”

“They’ll meet us at the house,” Zora answered, making sure they had everything packed.

“They really do kick you out right after the kid is born,” Agatha scoffed.

 

“Well, when you heal as quickly as Wanda does, fewer questions.” Jen entered the room, holding discharge papers. “We would have done a home birth, but with everything still stabilizing, we didn’t want to take any chances.” Handing the papers to Zora, she put a hand on Wanda’s shoulder. “You get dressed, we’ll wheel you out, and then sneak into an empty hallway somewhere so we can transfer to your place.”

Wanda thanked her as she left before her eyes landed on Agatha and Death. “Rio, will you hold Gabby while Agatha and Zora help me get dressed?” She smiled sweetly.

Agatha saw right through her but didn’t question it.

Rio happily took the sleeping bundle and sat to the side while the other three women assisted the redhead.

“I already showered this morning, just need help getting out of this hospital gown and into sweats.” She explained.

Agatha quirked a brow while Zora shook her head.

 

You totally had sex with Rio! Came screaming through the link she had with Wanda. 

Outwardly, Agatha grimaced at how loud it had been. Lower your voice, please! Okay, yes.

Are you back together?

No. We can’t. That’s the end of the discussion. She ended the connection before Wanda could say anything else. This was not something she wanted to talk about, especially when Wanda had a newborn to focus on.

 

Transferring into the Maximoff household, they were greeted by everyone, including a very excited Amelia. 

“Wands, can I please hold her?” She asked once everyone was settled.

Wanda patted the space next to her on the couch for Amelia to sit, and carefully transitioned Gabby into the young girl’s arms, making sure the newborn’s head and neck were supported.

 

Agatha thought back to when she had the twins, and two sixteen-year-olds took turns holding them. Then, when Wanda had her twins, Nicky and Lily had taken turns holding and caring for them.

There hadn’t been siblings or other family there with Amelia. 

The room was silent for a moment before people began to speak again, taking in the gravity of the moment. Agatha wasn’t able to dwell for long before fingers curled around her wrist and dragged her to a different room.

 

“Ellie, what the fuck?” She spat.

“I thought the bed wasn’t even cold yet, Ags?” Her voice was filled with amusement. 

“It just happened,” Agatha explained, rubbing her wrist. 

The blonde scientist took her friend’s hand in hers and gave her a genuine, eye-reaching smile. “I’m happy for you, Agatha.”

The other scientist sighed, slumping into a chair in the office Ellie had pulled her into. “Don’t be. I told her we aren’t back together.”

Agatha deserved the slap to the shoulder.

“Why the fuck would you do that?” 

“I can’t hurt her, or Amelia. What if we did get together and it didn’t work out? Amelia would be crushed. I can’t risk it.”

“You’re so fucking blindsided by your own fear and insecurities that you can’t see what’s in front of you. You aren’t that witch anymore, Aggie. You’re so much better than that. It’s not the same situation at all. You’re both in the right place now, and because of Amelia, neither of you will do anything stupid.” She shook her head with a sarcastic smile.

“God, years ago, I would have agreed with you, told you to be with me instead, but I’m not your person. She is, and you are deeply in love with her. Once you realize that, give Ralph a call. He’s better with these things than I am, because I want to strangle you. Gods only know how Lilia hasn’t killed you by now.”

 

Agatha had been read to filth by Ellie Sattler, and she wasn’t sure how she felt about it. Instead, she did what she did best and put everything into her children. The family went back to their homes and continued, while she stayed in New York to help Wanda and Zora adjust to life with two pre-teens and a newborn.

 

Agatha had told herself that if she were going to pursue Rio, she would do it correctly. Date her, sweep her off her feet, communicate, and actually earn the love and affection that Rio was ready just to hand her on a silver platter.

Yet, here they were, on the first Wednesday in November, in Rio’s office.

Agatha had told herself she would just quickly drop by, ask about Amelia, and take Rio a sandwich from her favorite deli. She was being a good coparent. So what if she was wearing fuck me jeans and a tight, low-cut sweater with heeled boots. She could look good with no ulterior motive.

Transporting to her office first, she actually strutted next door and walked into Rio’s office without knocking.

“Exc—Agatha? What are you doing here?” Standing in surprise, her eyes widened, lips slowly curling up.

The professor closed and locked the door behind her. “I brought you lunch.” She set the bag down on the desk before circling it, drawn to the creature staring at her. “You’re fucking beautiful.” Encircling Rio’s waist, she kissed her softly in greeting, surprising them both.

Leaning back, Death ran her knuckles across Agatha’s cheek. “I thought nothing could happen?”

“It shouldn’t.” Agatha agreed, stepping back, letting the hand on her cheek fall away. “I really did want to bring you lunch. I know you don’t need it, but our daughter does require a lot of energy.”

Rio hummed, taking the bag and returning to her seat. “There’s something I’ve been thinking about.”

Leaning against the desk, she watched her carefully. Awaiting some horrible blow. “Yes?”

“I know this all started with me not wanting to be Death. Shirking my duties and responsibilities.” Her shoulders slumped, taking a bite of the sandwich and chewing slowly, giving herself time before swallowing and looking back at Agatha. “On the weeks you have Amelia, after classes, I’ll be fulfilling some of those duties. Remembering what it is to be me. If we’re going to raise a cosmic being, I might as well be one myself.”

 

Agatha Harkness, professional bullshitter and con artist, was at a loss for words. The woman had talked royalty out of priceless artifacts. Assisted in bringing prehistoric animals back to life, and yet, couldn’t process Rio not always being available. “What if we need you?” 

“You won’t. If anything is seriously wrong, I’ll know.” Death focused on her meal.

“And the nights Amelia wants to have dinner as a family? Watch movies?” She tried to search the entity’s face, but found it to be neutral. 

“We can’t keep pretending to be the family we’re not, Aggie. If we aren’t going to be together, we stick to the terms we have and schedule everything else. She’ll understand.” Finally looking up, she registered the torment in her witch’s eyes. “Don’t look at me like that.”

“Like what?” Agatha blinked, attempting to remain neutral.

“Like you didn’t know this was coming. Like you expected me to go along with whatever you had planned.” Placing her lunch on her desk, she took Agatha’s hand and pulled her into her lap.

“Agatha, you were right when you said we have to think of Amelia.” Her arms wrapped around the other woman’s waist, holding her against her. “Maybe once she’s older and not recently traumatized by family.” Pressing her ear to the witch’s chest, she could hear the loud, frantic heartbeat beneath.

Agatha nodded. “It’s for the best, Feathers. I agree.” Years ago, she would have seduced Rio. Dropped to her knees and showed her who was in charge, but things were different now. They had so much more to think about. 

Finally pulling away and rising to her feet, she leaned over and pressed a soft, lingering kiss to Rio’s forehead. “I have to get back. Tell our daughter I love and miss her so much. I’ll call her tonight.”  

🦕🦖🦕

Two weeks passed quickly, and Agatha was back to life as usual in Boston. Amelia had been ecstatic to see her, despite seeing her on weekends. The week before Thanksgiving was Agatha’s, and therefore Rio’s first week back in her true position, part-time.

Their interactions at the university had been charged before Halloween. Now, they were electrified. Agatha updated her on their chaos gremlin, careful to keep her distance. It wasn’t the mark or the connection that drew her toward Rio anymore. It was something else. Something that was all them. 

As Agatha wrapped up for the Thanksgiving break that Thursday, she heard Rio stroll into her office and gently close the door. 

“Aggie?”

“Yes?” She didn’t bother to look up.

“Since neither of us has Friday classes, I’m going to stay away until Sunday.”

Agatha stopped what she was doing and slowly raised her eyes to meet Rio’s. “Oh. Okay. No Turkey Trot on Saturday then?” 

The entity looked toward the ceiling. “No, I can break the news to Amelia. Lily and Nicky will be there. She won’t even notice my absence.”

“Okay, if that’s what you think is best.” Her voice wavered. 

“I saw your tattoos, you know.” 

“I assumed as much. I’m surprised you’re just now bringing it up.” Agatha tried to keep her breathing even, knowing what was coming next. 

“Why do you have a Rio Dipladenia in a line of flowers on your spine?” The other professor whispered the question.

The witch blew out a breath. “Each flower represents a different important person in my life. That’s yours.” Ocean blue eyes met gold-flecked amber ones. “No matter what transpires between us, Lady Death, you will always be important to me.”

“Oh, well. What about the coordinates on your lower back, right side?”

“You know what they are,” Agatha answered, packing her things to leave.

I need to hear it.

Agatha’s heels clicked firmly on the wooden floor as she approached the door where Rio stood. With her hand on the doorknob, she let out a sigh. “Where we met, where we fell in love, and where it all fell apart. I’ll see you on Sunday.” Opening the door, she brushed past Rio and left.

 

Rio had made good on her word and explained to Amelia over the phone that she would be working that weekend. Just like Agatha had said, she accepted it easily, knowing her siblings would be there.

The cosmic entity of Death sat in her realm, thinking about the past few weeks and the explanations of the tattoos. Would Agatha add new coordinates to mark their reconciliation? She did not doubt that she’d add a flower for Milly.

Thinking back to their night together, she remembered what Agatha had said about wanting it all. Marriage, kids, everything. Was that something that was still possible for them? They had a child together and were proving to be a pretty decent parenting team. The Grim Reaper groaned when she was pulled from her thoughts when she felt someone’s soul call for her.

🦕🦖🦕

Wanda and Ellie had curated a fucking playlist and sent it to her. Amelia was sound asleep on the stormy Saturday night before Thanksgiving, and Celine Dion was drifting through her bedroom.

Her family was a bunch of meddling assholes, but the song was fitting.

Agatha sipped her wine as she thought about her dilemma. She had spent sixteen years dwelling on Rio Vidal, so why stop now?

When you touch me like this, when you hold me like that…

They were in a good place, so why not capitalize on it? Why shouldn’t she woman up and go after Rio?

There were things we’d never do again…

“Fucking duh,” Agatha muttered into her wine glass. They had learned so much over the years and had paid dearly for their transgressions. They had forgiven each other. They could start over, couldn’t they?

When you see me like this, and when I see you like that…

That was another thing. They truly did see each other, even if they didn’t always understand. Agatha finished her wine and sent the empty glass to the kitchen. 

It had finally clicked.

Grabbing her phone, Ralph picked up immediately.

“Ags?”

“I’m in love with Rio, and I need to get her back, for good.” She blurted out.

“Fork found in kitchen.” 

Chapter End Notes

IDIOTS.

ONE MORE CHAPTER.

Comments give me motivation!

I'm on Spring Break this week and hope to finish!

Chapter 8: If You're Ready, I Am Too

Chapter Summary

Agatha makes a decision as they navigate the holidays and parenting a cosmic being that is more mature than her four years should allow.

TW: PTSD discussion (child), serious illness, so much trauma, and the repercussions of having a child that is leagues wiser than she should be.

But also, Agathario smut, because time jumps.

Chapter Notes

Okay, so it's a month later than I thought. Ao3 curse, work, life, all the things. Also, we've got 30k+ words.
