“I vow to be your rock and your sounding board, your equal throughout endless lifetimes. To love and cherish you the way you deserve, always. To raise our children together and compromise when necessary.

“Most importantly, I vow to listen, to take the hard times with the good, and to be patient when the dog chews on something of mine.

“I love you, Agatha. I always have and I always will.”

Agatha wiped at a tear with the palm of her hand. “I love you, too, Rio.” She whispered. 

They took a few steps back from each other, hands still clasped, as the ceremony continued. When Ralph finally announced them as Mrs. and Mrs. Harkness-Vidal, Agatha thought she was going to scream for him to hurry up. Her first instinct was to lift Rio in her arms and fly her away, or portal them back home.

Instead, she stepped into the cosmic entity and slotted their lips together as she had done thousands of times before. Though now it was as wives. Agatha didn’t care that everyone was watching and waiting for them, or that Milly was tugging at their clothes, telling them to hurry up; it was time for cake.

The witch held Death close to her and savored the moment, not wanting to part. Finally, she stepped back, Amelia glaring up at them with her hands on her hips. 

“It’s supposed to be a short kiss!” She scolded.

“You only have your first kiss as a married couple once, kiddo.” Lilia came up behind her, the small crowd milling about. “We’re going to head to the reception, let your Moms have a moment. They’ll be down soon.” Lilia gave them a pointed look before steering Amelia away.

“We love you!” Agatha called after her. Taking Rio’s hand, she dragged her away while no one was paying attention and led her to the clearing. 

Rio’s eyes were as big as saucers once they arrived at their destination, letting out a breathy laugh and wrapping her arms around Agatha’s waist. “Whatever are you doing, my dear wife?”

Her breath hitched, and cerulean eyes darkened. “Say it again.” 

“My dear wife.”

Agatha lifted her easily, Rio’s arms moving to her shoulders and her legs wrapping around her hips. Her mouth was on her in a heated, passionate kiss as she carried her to the makeshift bed and carefully laid her down. “Mrs. Harkness-Vidal,” She said reverently, brushing a strand of hair back behind Rio’s hair.

“I hate to ruin the fun, Aggie, but we don’t have long,” Rio warned, vanishing their clothing and pulling her wife down on top of her. 

“I know, my love, I just needed you. A preview of tonight, if you will.” She grinned brightly as the sun set around them.

Death’s tongue darted across Agatha’s bottom lip, immediately gaining entrance. Their tongues swirled and danced together, occasionally retreating to bite the other’s lip. 

Agatha’s hands were quick and precise. While she wanted to take her time, she also knew they had a time limit. The area was enchanted. No one would stumble across them. However, people would soon start searching, and the coven would absolutely walk past a spell.

Unglamored fingers trailed down Rio and through her dripping heat. Fingers traced her entrance, Rio moaning and already extra sensitive. 

My beautiful wife, already so needy for me.

She grabbed the back of Agatha’s head with one hand and shoved her hand between them, running her fingers through Agatha’s own slick. 

So is mine. She retorted.

Agatha smirked against Rio’s mouth, pushing three fingers in without resistance, her walls stretching around her hand perfectly. 

Fuck, baby. You feel so good. 

Rio thrust three of her own digits into Agatha, automatically setting a grueling pace for both of them. 

Come with me, Aggie. She pleaded.

The witch matched Rio's pace, adding her thumb to her clit, and kept kissing her sloppily, her mouth wandering to her jawline and neck.

Rio’s palm ground against Agatha’s clit, desperate for them to come together, when she felt the waves of pleasure begin to crest, and their hips begin to falter.

The kiss was more moaning, gasping, and pleading than anything else. Agatha’s eyes flew open, staring at Rio. “Baby, look at me. I’m about to…”

The entity met Agatha’s eyes as they both reached the peak at the same time.

They lay there for a few moments, Agatha summoning wipes from somewhere and cleaning them up. 

“We should get down there,” Agatha stated. “I couldn’t wait until afterward.”

Rio stood as Agatha followed suit, returning their clothes to their bodies and adjusting their appearances until nary a hair was out of place.

“Neither could I, it is Beltane after all. On a full moon no less.” The entity reminded.

The color drained from Agatha’s face. “Rio, does Beltane affect prehistoric creatures the same way it does us?”

“Do you not hear the mating calls? Of course it does…” Her mouth went slightly agape. “Well, shit, make sure everyone stays at the compound.”

“Do you think all the adults are actually at the reception, or are we going to find people off fucking all night?” Agatha asked, walking them back to the plateau.

“It doesn’t affect non-Majickal folk as much.” 

“Oh, that’s comforting. With you here, the effects are heightened, right?” The witch asked, worry in her voice.

“Obviously.”

“So, we should leave right now.” She scanned the plateau to see that everything had been cleaned up and everyone had, indeed, moved on.

“No, Aggie. We have a party.” She scoffed. “I’m sure our daughter is wondering where we are.”



Portaling to the reception area, Agatha walked straight to Ian, glamored fingers encircling his bicep. “Can the dinos get in this sector?”

“For once, I am several steps ahead of you. Let us frame this moment in history.”

“I was preoccupied, overgrown neanderthal.” 

“You were fucking in a prehistoric jungle, Agatha.” He deadpanned.

“And it was amazing, now, safety protocol?” She asked impatiently.

“When you mentioned the wedding being on Beltane, I knew what that meant. So we increased security measures, made sure to wrangle the extremely dangerous guys to the other side of the island. Tranq guns are loaded, fences are hot. I even looked up the moon cycles, thank you. It’s almost like I’ve been a member of this family for over thirty years.” Ian smiled smugly.

“I’m very proud of you, thank you.”

He beamed and hugged her tightly. “I’m proud of you, by the way. Congratulations, and please find your husband. He’s proud and distraught at the same time.”

“You mean ex-husband?”

“I said what I said.”

Huffing a laugh, she kept walking, saying hello and accepting congratulations from various people. The after-party was much larger. Jen and Wanda went overboard with the guest list. Hearing familiar voices, she turned to see Rio, Amelia, and Darcy in conversation. Rolling her shoulders, she set off in that direction.

 

Rio hadn’t meant to find Darcy; they had just bumped into each other five seconds after she and Agatha had arrived. Her wife went to find Ian, Rio turned to look for a place to sit, and there was Darcy, looking young and extremely nervous. 

Rio thought about baring her teeth and growling, until she remembered that Ellie was serious about her and Ellie was family. Who knew how long Darcy would be around?

“Hello, Darcy.”

“Dr. Harkness-Vidal, congratulations. I really am happy for you both.” Her smile was sincere and, fortunately for the mortal, Rio didn’t sense an ill-meaning bone in her body.

“Thank you. I know that means a lot to Agatha. I, look,” Rio took a deep breath, trying to figure out the words she should say next. She knew how this looked, and it wasn’t great.

Darcy raised a dismissive hand. “It looks bad on both sides, Rio, but we all know the truth. I knew going in she’d be with you one day, and I’m excited she is. The two of you are so happy, and having another baby? That’s amazing! Ellie and I make more sense, and it’s been great. I just want to make sure there’s no hard feelings between us, especially if I’m going to be at family events and holidays moving forward.”

“I accused you of having an affair because I was jealous.”

“I was quite literally trying to shack up with the woman who was made for you, and now that I think about it, I was technically having an emotional affair,” Darcy admitted.

“Well, if I can be friends with Ellie, who literally ruined everything in a villainous way twice, then I think we can make it work.” Rio held out a hand to shake on it.

Darcy hugged her instead.

“Mama?” Amelia asked, voice bordering on rage. 

Darcy stepped back. “Hey, Milly, your mom and I were just talking through some things.”

Her eyes narrowed. “Why are you here?”

“I’m dating your Aunt Ellie.”

Rio laughed under her breath before moving to stand beside her daughter. “It’s okay, mija, she was congratulating me. She’ll be visiting with Ellie sometimes.”

“Oh.” 



Agatha’s arms circled Rio from behind. “Everything okay?”

“Mommy!” Amelia hugged her. “Where were you two? It took you both forever to get here!”

“We went for a walk before we joined everyone,” She answered, hoping it would suffice.

“A lot of people have been doing that! Wanda and Zora, Pietro and Emma, Dad and Papa,”

Agatha raised her hand to signal that she understood.

“Hey, Darce, how’re things?” Agatha attempted to change the subject.

“They’re good. Congratulations, I was just telling Rio…”



Lily surveyed the room, watching the slight chaos that Beltane was causing, debating on how much of a menace she wanted to be. 

“You are not going to cause any trouble,” Nicky answered for her, handing her a cocktail.

“I was just considering taking Green Goblin to visit a few of the less dangerous dinosaurs.” Lily shrugged. 

“That’s a bad idea. Speaking of, where is she? Aren’t you supposed to be her keeper?” 

“She’s with Moms, where’s Morgan? Has Mom met your secret Stark yet?” She turned her attention to finding Milly.

“She’s talking with some friends of hers, all the rich kids know each other, apparently, and no, I have not introduced them. Last I saw, Moms were talking with Darcy.”

“Did I just hear you two lump Rio in with ‘Moms’?” Wanda asked, sidling up to them, Zora’s hand in hers, both of them looking flushed.

“Maybe. Beltane?” Lily smirked.

“Maybe.” Wanda took two drinks from a passing waiter.

“We just collect parents and siblings, don’t we?” Nicky asked. “We’ve known Rio, and it’s just easier. Besides, I love her like a parent. Don’t tell me you guys don’t.”

“You’re right, three moms, two dads, two aunts, two grandmothers, who knows how many siblings we’ll end up with.” Wanda listed off.

“I’m still wondering how many kids we’ll end up with,” Zora only half-joked.

“Speaking of, where’s Gabby?” Lily continued to search the crowd for Milly.

“She’s with Ellie,” Wanda answered. “Boys are somewhere.”

A small brunette wrecking ball crashed into Lily, “Lilith! Why aren’t you dancing! Or taking a walk with someone?” Amelia asked. 

“I’m talking to our siblings and was trying to find you!”

“Nicky!” Morgan, a young brunette with blue eyes, walked to him and took his hand. “Come dance with me, and then I need to meet your mother!” He followed her willingly.

“Oh, that boy’s in love,” Wanda observed.

“Is no one blonde anymore?” Zora asked.

“I hate to be the one to tell you this, but most blondes are evil,” Lily informed her. “I’m also not dancing because other than you, my very short sister, I have no one to dance with. And what do you mean by walk?” 

“Like Zora and Wanda took a walk. Like everyone has been doing.” She answered innocently.

Wanda nearly choked on her drink.

“About that, people are taking special walks because of Beltane, and only the adults are doing that. It’s also why the dinosaurs are making special calls right now.” Lily tried to explain. “Either way, still no one to dance or uh, take a walk with.”

“You mean mating calls? I know what dinosaur mating calls are.” She said, very matter-of-factly.

Wanda hid her laugh behind her hand. “Oh, this is going to backfire, honey. Get your phone out so we can record.”

“Oh, hmm.” Amelia put one finger to her lips, eyes scanning the room before stopping on yet another brunette in a purple cocktail dress. “She’s beautiful,” the youngest Harkness sister breathed out in awe.

Lily followed her sister’s gaze and smoothed down her own lavender halter dress. “You have impeccable taste, hermana.”

“Do you want to dance with her?” Milly asked, beginning to bounce on the balls of her feet.

“Absolutely.” She answered immediately. “I’d mate with her,” she added, muttering under her breath.

“Let’s go, then.” Milly took her hand in hers.

Lily felt her heart stop as her miniature menace of a sister led her across the space to where the goddess stood, talking to an older man. Oh gods, what if she’s straight? She thought to herself.

“Excuse us,” the five-year-old said politely in her sweetest voice. 

 

The older man nodded and walked off. The woman’s shoulders dropped, sighing in relief. “Thank you so much. I kept trying to get him to leave me alone, and he would not.” She eyed the sisters, her mouth curling at the side as she knelt in front of Amelia. “To whom do I owe the pleasure?”

“I’m Amelia Vidal-Harkness, I’m five, and that sweet dog over there is my puppy.” She pointed to where the dog in question was trying to steal something off Ralph’s plate. “This is my big sister, Lilith Harkness. She’s twenty-one and attends NYU. Currently, she’s getting her master’s in paleontology. She thinks you’re very beautiful and would like to dance with you.” Milly gestured for her to lean forward and cupped her mouth so she could whisper in her ear. “She’d like to do more, but that’s our secret.”

Behind her, Lily’s face was bright red, and she wished she could disappear.

“That’s one giant puppy,” The woman looked up at Lily and rose back to her full height. “Well, I can tell you didn’t put her up to this; you wouldn’t resemble a lobster if you had.” 

“No, she does her own thing.” She explained.

“As you should, Amelia.” She held out her hand. “I’m Kate Bishop,” She shook Amelia’s hand first, and then Lilith’s. “I’m twenty-five, I have a master’s in cyber security, and other fancy things that are not important. My mother’s company does all the security on the islands, but I also live in New York.” Her eyes lingered on the woman in front of her. “So, that dance?”

“Oh! Yeah, yes, let’s do that.” She led her to the dance floor, giving Milly a thumbs-up as she did.

“Is that a hellhound?” Kate asked with an air of disbelief.

“Do you know Hela Odinsdottir by chance?”   

🦕🦖🦕

Agatha headed to the table at the front of the space, her wife by her side. “How are you feeling, my love? Can I get you more to eat? Water? Foot rub?” She pulled the chair out for her before taking her own seat, a protective arm around Rio’s shoulders.

“No, Aggie. I’m just tired and ready for a shower and bed. What time is it?”

“The very late hour of eight pm.” She placed a kiss on her cheek and settled her free hand on Rio’s stomach. “A few speeches and then we’ll go home. Honeymoon once the semester is over.”

“Okay, Agatha,” Rio mumbled into her shoulder.

“Hey, Moms,” Nicky approached the table with a girl by his side. 

“Nicholas. Is this the elusive girlfriend you’ve been hiding from us?” Agatha asked, narrowing her eyes, trying to place how she knew the young woman.

“Hi, Drs. Harkness-Vidal. It’s so nice to meet you both finally, Nicky speaks so—”

Agatha held up her hand, interrupting the young girl. “Morgan Stark. Tony Stark’s spawn.”

“Yes, Ma’am,” She darted her eyes to Nicky, panicked.

“I can’t stand your father. He once built a spacecraft just for fun and then sank it in the ocean. I sent my crews to retrieve it and dispose of it ethically.” Her words dripped with acid.

Morgan nodded. “He’s attempting to do better. I plan to earn my doctorate in environmental science, with a focus on marine cleanup.”

Agatha hummed. “Ecological or biological?”

“Both. I studied the river initiative you led, beginning in 2020. Your technology and bioengineering departments are still making incredible advances in the field. I keep begging Nicky to introduce me to his sister-in-law, but he refuses.” Morgan’s cheeks began to pinken. “I apologize, this is your wedding, and I am nerding out. Congratulations to you both.”

“Morgan, tell your mother I said hello, and Nicholas, bring her to the next family gathering.” 

“Yes, Mom.” He leaned forward and hugged her before rejoining his siblings.

“He referred to me as Mom.” 

“They’re all beginning to, my love. You’ve officially joined Club Parent.”

 

The clinking of silver on glass caught the crowd's attention, though several people were still missing. Agatha did a headcount to make sure the people who mattered were there, and they were. However, Lily seemed to have met someone.

“If I could have everyone’s attention, please.” Ralph stood next to Agatha. “We have a few speeches, and I know my ex-wife is very anxious to get out of here. A few people laughed, while some looked uneasy. “It’s okay to laugh at that, really.” Turning to Agatha, he looked at her lovingly before beginning his speech.

“Agatha and I have known each other since we were sixteen, and have been nearly inseparable since. We know each other better than we know ourselves, and when Agatha’s mother told her she had to marry a man, of course, I said yes, because love means making sacrifices, just as these two have done.

“When Agatha and I divorced, it was the most amicable split in history. We’re still practically husband and wife. I just ordained her wedding. She was my best woman, and we call each other at three am.

“You two aren’t allowed to break up. No one will survive. There’s no need for you to do such a thing.”

 

Agatha shook her head, turning to Rio. “Never.” She whispered, placing a soft kiss on the tip of her nose.

 

“See, look at that. Agatha Harkness has a soft side, and it comes out around this woman. Always communicate, something you both have become so much better at. You both are great parents, and Rio, you are now a parent to four other children who come with Agatha. Yes, they’re technically adults, but there are grandkids, extended family, it’s a lot. You’ve handled everything beautifully.

“What I’m saying is, while Agatha and I know each other better than anyone, the two of you have an unbreakable bond, and you fit together perfectly. You can work through anything and compromise. We love you both. Don’t fuck it up.”

 

Agatha stood and pulled him close. “You’re the worst.”

“I learned from the best.” He grinned.

 

They sat and listened to more speeches from Lilia, Wanda, Ellie, and Alice. Once everyone had finished, guests either turned into their rooms on the island or portaled and apparated home. Sharon and Lilia took Amelia and Astraea with them for the night.

 

Agatha helped her overly tired wife to the portal and transported them home, stepping into their bedroom. Rio lay against Agatha’s chest while she bathed them both and washed their hair. Wrapping her in a fluffy towel, she dried her and let Rio lie in the towel while she dressed herself first, wearing purple boxers and her ‘Mother of Six’ shirt. 

She pulled Rio’s Green boxers on her and the dinosaur shirt Agatha had given her all those years ago. 

Tucking herself into Agatha’s side, placing a hand over Agatha’s protective one on her abdomen, she sighed contentedly. “I’m sorry, I’m so exhausted, Ags.”

“Feathers, it was a long, busy day. We’re married, I got to take care of you in multiple ways, we spent it surrounded by loved ones, it was perfect.”

At that moment, there was a small flutter against Agatha’s hand. 

Rio’s eyes flew open. “Did you feel that?” 

She pulled her closer, kissing her joyfully. “Our baby moved on our wedding day!

Rio laughed into her mouth, “I love you.”

“I love you, too.”          

🦕🦖🦕

Late May 2026

 

Time began to fly by, hurtling them toward October. It was on their honeymoon when Agatha finally decided what her future held career-wise. 

They were relaxing on the balcony of their suite in Paris, overlooking the city, when the conversation started. Rio had already decided to take the year off, unsure if she would return.

“Feathers, I’ve decided what my next step is in my career.” Her voice remained steady, certain she was making the best choice for their family.

“Are you not returning to Harvard in the fall?” She questioned, moving closer to her wife.

“I’ll consult, guest lecture, but no. As much as I love academia, I want to be more active in our children’s lives. Our grandchildren’s lives. I’ll be slightly more active in the company and the parks. I’ll still do guest spots on the Science channel, Discovery, what have you—everything I do now. I may work more on research with all the access I have, thanks to dinosaurs being back. I’ll stay busy and engaged in the things I love, but Rio,” she turned, caressing her wife’s cheek. “You and our family are my priority. You, of course, can return to your position or whatever you want to do.”

Rio leaned forward and gently kissed her wife. “It’s perfect.” A laugh escaped her lips, hand moving to her stomach. “Liam agrees.”

“Perfect.” Agatha stood and led Rio back inside.

Late August 2026

Summer continued, Rio celebrating the birthday she had chosen for herself, and Agatha celebrating her actual birthday. School started up again, and Milly began kindergarten.

“Are you sure Astraea can’t come with me?” She whined, eyes darting between her moms.

“How would you make friends if your hundred-and-twenty-pound demon dog is right by your side all day, Plumita?” 

“That’s exactly how I’ll make friends, everyone likes dogs.” She countered.

“No. Besides, it’s the same school as last year, just a different building. You’ll do great.” Rio answered. 

“But we play on a bigger playground, and we don’t get naptime, and we eat in a cafeteria with older kids, and, please?” She looked at both of them with watery blue eyes.

Why did she have to get your eyes?  

Agatha smirked, ignoring Rio’s comment, and handed Amelia Sir Cuddles instead. “You can still have this guy. Most kids have something, even adults have fidgets and comfort items these days.”

“Really?” Taking Sir Cuddles and holding him close, she thought about it, “What’s your comfort item?”

“Your mom.” They said simultaneously.

“You two are weird.” She sighed dramatically. “Fine, if I must go by myself, I will suffer.”

“You’re so dramatic.” Agatha groaned.

Rio stifled her laugh, ushering them both to the car. 

They managed not to cry at drop off, though the tears began once they were back in the car. 

“When did she grow up, Aggie?” Rio whispered. “I blinked, and here we are.” 

“It gets worse, if that’s any consolation. One moment, I had two traumatized kids. The next thing I know, one’s making a name for herself, married, with three beautiful kids. The other? Running one of the family companies and finally married.

“Then the other two? That’s what it feels like. I blinked once, and they went from babies to teenagers, getting kidnapped, doing internships. I blink again, and they’re finishing their degrees for their careers, seriously dating, living their own lives. They grow up so quickly. 

“With Milly, she was already this entire adult, and I’m so glad she made the choice she did. She gets to enjoy it more, but you’re right. It goes by so quickly. We have to cherish it. Thank you for letting me be a part of her life.”

Death took the hand that wasn’t on the steering wheel and kissed the back of it. “Of course, Aggie. We’re in this together now.”

Agatha received a call from the school at nine fifteen notifying her that Amelia’s dog had come to visit.

That was the day they learned that Astraea could apparate and portal on her own.

Mid September 2026

The Vidal-Harknesses had not planned on having a baby shower that September, or ever, but were shot down by Wanda and Emma. 

“Everyone needs diapers, Mom.” Wanda had argued, planning the party with her sister-in-law, roping her sisters, and a few others, into it. 

“Is the dinosaur theme too played out?” Emma asked cautiously, eyeing Agatha.

“Yes,” Agatha sighed, resigning to the fact that they would be having this damn thing.

“No. We’re doing it.” Wanda answered.

“We have to put Rawr in random places!” Amelia added. It was how she and her siblings signed off their FaceTimes every Sunday evening.

“Of course we do, Mills. You and Mimi will be on snack duty.”

In all honesty, Rio deserved to be spoiled and the center of attention. Celebrated for bringing life into the world. Her wife was currently talking to a colleague of theirs, Agatha watching from the side, when Lily and Nicky pulled her aside.

“Yes? Something wrong?”

“No, nothing’s wrong, Mom, it’s just,” Nicky looked down, scratching the back of his neck.

Lily huffed impatiently. “Doofus over here, and I, have fallen in love with very intelligent women, and—”

“Wait, I knew he was in love, but you? She’s older, and it’s only been four months. Can you really say it’s love?”

Lily placed a hand on her hip and gave her mother a look. “I’m sorry, but who are you to give advice? I talked to both dads, Wanda, and Zora.”

“Touché. Continue.”

“They don’t exactly believe the IVF at fifty-three story and are questioning. You and Wanda have let your glamor slip a few times on your fingers and other little things. Can we take them aside and tell them the truth?” Lily’s words came out quickly.

“Yes, just make sure they agree to secrecy first, use the basement. Have fun, kids.” 

Before she could return to the festivities, Ellie joined her, their shoulders brushing. “I still remember the day you told me.” 

The witch laughed lightly. “You nodded, said, ‘That makes sense,’ and no one saw us for two days.”

Ellie’s laugh joined Agatha’s. “You said you had tricks to show me! They were very good tricks.”

“We had a good run, El. A toxic one and we had to break out of jail once or twice, but still.” 

“I’m glad we’re still us, you know.” Agatha took her hand.

“You’d be lost without me, Harkness.” Her mouth curved upward.

“Probably.” Taking a deep breath, she turned to her, studying her face. “You look happy, content. Peaceful even.”

“I am. I was before, but more so now.”

Agatha’s signature smirk appeared. “Good. If she ever hurts you, call me.”

“You know I will. Same for you. Not that I can do much, but I can yell at her.” She squeezed her hand. “Agatha?”

“Yes?”

“Rawr,” Ellie whispered.

“Rawr.” Agatha smiled. “You know, I never have told the kids where that actually originated from.”

“What? Didn’t want to tell them it was our secret code for ‘I love you’?” Ellie’s eyes gleamed mischievously.

“And miss these cute moments where they use it amongst each other? Absolutely not. It’s a Harkness thing now, all of us.” Her smile widened, looping her arm through her best friend’s. “Now c’mon, Sattler, I need to check on my wife, and you need to check on your girlfriend.”

 

Rio took Agatha’s hand when she settled on the couch beside her. “Everything okay, love?”

“Of course. Nicky and Lilith asked to tell their girlfriends everything, and then Ellie and I were talking. Nothing major. How are you?”

“I feel like I’m carrying around a watermelon.” 

Agatha placed a hand on the swell of Rio’s belly, feeling an automatic kick. “Just a few more weeks, my love. You’re beautiful. We’ll get through this, and I’ll draw you a warm bath for you to relax in.”

October 10, 2026 - Evening

Rio had been cramping and having false contractions for days. It was unlike what she had experienced with Amelia, and she was beginning to fear that a home birth might be out of the question. Agatha had done everything she could to put her fears to rest and soothe her pain. 

The go-to medical room was already prepped with everything Jen would need, and a portal was set up in Ralph’s home so he could easily get there to examine Liam once he was born. Agatha had truly thought of everything.

Just as she had promised.

During her pregnancy, and even labor and the first four years of Amelia’s life, Rio felt as though she had worn a mask. Ignoring her pregnancy hormones, not giving in to the pain, carefully curating her emotions, and hiding away from the world.

This pregnancy was different. She had her support system; she had Agatha. If she had a craving, Agatha got it for her. If she cried over a basketball game, Agatha simply handed her a Kleenex and let her. Amelia made her breakfast in bed on Mother’s Day, and she sobbed. Agatha simply explained to Amelia what was happening, and the little girl curled up with her Mama so they could watch playoff hockey together.

If anything hurt, Agatha was right there, massaging the muscles, helping her into a bath, kissing the offending spot. The witch would listen to her talk, read to her, and read to both Amelia and the bump that housed their son.

The nursery next to their bedroom was completed, though their family kept adding their personal touches or modifications. Zora and Kate installed top-of-the-line baby monitors and completed security upgrades throughout the house, with Darcy also assisting—protection runes and spells by the coven.

Amelia had already picked out a light-brown stuffed bunny for Liam, which she named Lady Snuggles. She complimented Sir Cuddles perfectly. Their Mimi even enchanted her.

Thinking about it all brought tears to her eyes. They had reassured Milly that they would never stop loving her, and a baby would never change anything. Since then, the young witch had embraced her future status as a big sister and was now excited. 

Then the discussion had come down to his powers, if his developed as early as Amelia’s had. As much as they hated taking the choice from him, they would suppress them as a toddler, ask him when he was roughly five, and go from there. It felt good to make these decisions with Agatha.

On the evening of October tenth, Rio was thirty-nine weeks and one day pregnant. She had gone to bed fairly early, sharp pains in her back.

 

Agatha put Amelia to bed and joined her wife, wondering if this would be the night their son made his entrance.

 

October 11, 2026

A few minutes past midnight on October eleventh, Rio woke up to use the restroom. Once there, she heard splashing. “That was not water.” She said to herself.

“Agatha!” She cried.

The witch appeared, grounding sleep from her eyes, “Yes?” Taking in the scene, she turned serious. “I’ll call Jen, let’s get you to the other room, my love.”



Amelia was safely with her grandmothers, her dog by her side. The family knew, though Agatha had told them they could come when she said they could. Ralph was there to assist Jen and act as a human Xanax for Agatha.

“How are you already at a seven?” Jen muttered, replacing the privacy sheet and adjusting the IV containing fluids and potions mixture of Jen’s own creation. 

Rio eyed Jen, “I want the same concoction you gave me when I had Milly.”

Jen barked a laugh, “Ri, that was pure cosmic stubbornness to show or process emotion and pain because you wouldn’t admit needing or wanting Agatha. Instead, you took it out on the entire planet. I’ll give you what I would have given you, but with her here? You’re fine.”

“Oh.”

The witch took Rio’s hand, pressing a cool washcloth to her neck. “You’re doing perfectly, angel. Yoga ball?” She chewed on the inside of her lip, attempting to suppress the smirk from Jen’s outburst.

“No, Aggie. I just need you.” Her grip tightened as another contraction rolled through. 

“It won’t be long. Focus on me.” The back of her hand grazed her cheek as she pulled it up into a bun for her. Leaning forward, she brushed their lips together. “You’re gorgeous, my love,” before pressing them together in a gentle, intimate kiss.

“Stop that, she needs oxygen.” Jen scolded from across the room. 

Agatha leaned back, ignoring the potions witch. 

Rio scoffed, “Aggie, I’m in a hospital bed, feet in stirrups, in active labor, and you’re calling me gorgeous? It’s because I’m naked, isn’t it?”

“It helps. But no. Feathers, you’re bringing our son into this world. I’m honored to witness it, and yes, you are gorgeous.” Leaning forward, she kissed her forehead. “I look forward to doing it again.”

“Do not talk to me about having more children right now, Harkness.” Rio grimaced, having ignored previous contractions, distracted by Agatha. This one, however, felt bigger than the others. She cried out, rising into even more of a sitting position than she was already in.

Agatha shifted positions so she could watch what was happening while supporting her wife. “You’ve got this, baby. You’re doing so well.”

Jen sat on a stool, dropping the foot of the bed and readjusting Rio’s legs. Ralph quietly entered the room, followed by a nurse from Jen’s practice. 

“It’s time.” She announced quietly, putting on a fresh pair of gloves, nodding to the others in the room. 

 

Lilia transported in directly beside Agatha and squeezed her shoulders. “I haven’t missed the arrival of one of your children yet; I’m not going to start now. Milly is still asleep at my place.”

Agatha pressed her lips to Rio’s temple. “One more, baby.” 

A cry, louder than Amelia’s had been, cut through the air, a clap of thunder shaking the house. 

“We have a beautiful baby boy,” Jen announced, tears in her eyes. “God damnit, why do I cry when I deliver your kids, Agatha? You fucking demon.”

Jen carefully placed Liam on Rio’s chest before returning to Magickally repair Death.

Agatha carefully sat on the bed next to her wife, “Rio, you did amazing. He’s perfect. I love you so much.”

“We did amazing, Aggie. I love you.”

Lilia kissed them both on the top of the head. “I’m proud of you both. Ags, let me know when I can let the kids come up.”

 

By mid-morning, they had relocated to their room with everything they needed to care for their newborn son. Ralph had deemed him perfectly healthy and had cried when he held him. 

The five siblings came in first, led by Amelia, who gently climbed into the bed with her Moms. “What’s his full name? I forgot.” She asked, clutching Sir Cuddles and a bunny that looked very similar. 

“Liam Astin Harkness-Vidal,” Agatha replied, watching as Rio showed Amelia how to hold her baby brother. 

Amelia considered his name for a moment and tested it out a few times. “No. I’m gonna call him Al.” She whispered a few words in Latin, and the bunny she had for him floated to her. “Don’t worry, it’s just simple analog Magick I taught myself. I thought it would help with big sister duties.” Showing the bunny to her brand new brother, she explained, “This is Lady Snuggles, your new protector. I have a bunny named Sir Cuddles. You’re not allowed to touch him. This one is yours.”

The other siblings laughed, then passed him between them and welcomed him to the family, each one calling him Al.

Once they left, and before the original Coven, both human and witch, came up, Agatha looked down at their son and back to Rio. “AL?! Al. Couldn’t she have picked something like Hark? Or La? No, that one sucks, but something that doesn’t sound like a fifty-year-old man?”

“It’s cute, Ags. Plus, Milly gave it to him. It’s special.”

The name stuck.    

 

🦕🦖🦕

Agatha Harkness fell in love four times in her life.

The first was to Ralph Bohner, a platonic, everlasting love that would save her from herself more times than she could ever repay. They saved each other as kids, burned down a traumatizing place, and took on the world together.

Ralph made sacrifices for Agatha, married her, (scientifically) fathered her children, and would forever be a father figure to all of her kids. The person who knew her almost better than anyone. Her rock.   

The second was Ellie Sattler. Her first romantic love, the one she learned from. It was passionate, highly toxic, and had a rap sheet. It was a love she held on to, never truly able to let go, even though it transformed into one of the greatest friendships she ever had. Ellie would eventually marry Agatha’s other ex, and they would forever joke about being a walking sitcom. 

Agatha wouldn’t change it for anything.

The fourth was Darcy Lewis, now Darcy Sattler. Twenty-two years her junior, she knew exactly what she was risking by loving Agatha and did it anyway. She would always hold a special place in her heart for Darcy, and, while she loved her in some way, Agatha always put her children first.  Darcy was a stepping stone, not her forever. A look at supportive, non-toxic love. They both knew that. It was safe, it was controversial, it wasn’t her best decision, but it happened.  

It was her third love, which some would say was her first, that was her greatest. Some would say it was toxic at times, beautiful, ugly, one for the storybooks. Not everyone is allotted a soulmate, and Agatha was fated to Death of all people. It took them a long time, a lot of heartbreak, backstabbing, a child, and help from their friends to figure it out. But they knew it was right, no matter the way they got there. 

After all, what’s a few decades when you’re immortal?

Agatha had always known dinosaurs had feathers.

Riling up Death was too sweet a temptation to resist.

 

August 2027

London, England

International Paleontological Symposium and Convention

 

“In conclusion, not only did the fossils of the juvenile Tyrannosaurus Rex prove that they had feathers, but now with Jurassic Park, we have definitive proof, and are discovering more species that had them, further linking them to various species of birds previously not thought to have been related to dinosaurs.” Rio wrapped up her portion of the panel, giving the crowd a professional smile.

“Dr. Vidal, surely you’re still not on this feather-brained theory again. The science shows that most larger species of theropods did not have feathers, even as juveniles.” Agatha rebutted with her signature smirk.

It took all of her strength not to use her Magick on her wife at that moment. Too many witnesses. Instead, she took a deep breath. “Dr. Harkness, are you arguing with proven science?”

“I am simply disagreeing with man-created dinosaurs. This isn’t exactly the original recipe we’re working with.” 

 

Once the panel concluded, Rio pulled Agatha aside. “What were you doing, you menace?”

“You’re so cute when you’re irritated. I’ve always known you were right, my love.”

“You are in so much trouble when we get to the room.” Rio hissed.

“I’m counting on it.”

 

🦕🦖🦕









Chapter End Notes
